## ¬ß502 ‚Äî 2026-06-21 (research-first dive, cycle 502 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE)

**Source (WebSearch √ó2, date-checked this cycle):** the dive anchored on a concrete current artifact in the full-body-movement‚Üísound lineage ‚Äî **¬°Otro!**, a live performance work premiering at the **IAIA Digital Dome** (Institute of American Indian Arts, Santa Fe) on **April 18, 2026**, by choreographer **Rosanna Tavarez** and artist-technologist **Drew Trujillo**: a custom system that captures human movement and translates **gesture, velocity, and spatial presence in real time into spatialized audio AND generative imagery** in an immersive dome. Cross-checked the foundational backbone: **Frid et al., "Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children ‚Äî Cross-Modal Mapping and the Perception of Body Movement Qualities through Sound"** (Frontiers in Neuroscience / PMC5104747, 2016) ‚Äî motion-capture studies showing children *read movement qualities out of sound* and match sonified movement to abstract drawings ‚Äî plus the open-platform full-body interactive-sonification exergame line (real-time multimodal movement-quality analysis ‚Üí audio feedback for children). And the browser-feasibility check: **MediaPipe Pose** runs 33 body landmarks in true 3D in-browser via WebGL at 60+ fps with GPU delegation. *(Date honesty: ¬°Otro! is a genuinely current Apr-2026 premiere; the children-sonification science (Frid 2016) and Xenakis's UPIC (1977) are foundational refs dressed onto a 2026 anchor, so #5 is claimed softly and the floor clears on #2 + #3.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** the JURY 2026-06-20 flagged that the lab's **embodied/body/camera input register went 4√ó‚Üí0√ó** this window ‚Äî "the single most-starved input register right now, off every over-represented tag at once" ‚Äî and that **781's UPIC idea was only half-used (touch, not body); the full-body version is sitting unbuilt in your own research log (RESEARCH ¬ß492, CHI 2026 "From Movement to Sound and Back").** ¬°Otro! is the professional-dome proof of exactly that move: the moving body IS the score. The kids lab has body-tracking pieces (`788-kids-body-glow` pose-glow, `217-dance-avatar`) but **none turns the moving body into a continuous UPIC-style pitch-score with a glowing per-limb ribbon trail** (grep-0√ó). **Could become a prototype that:** tracks a child's body with MediaPipe Pose and maps each wrist's height ‚Üí a pentatonic pitch (never wrong) and movement speed ‚Üí loudness/brightness, drawing a glowing SVG ribbon trail per hand that sings as it moves ‚Äî Xenakis's "music becomes a game for children: they draw, they hear," but the body is the drawing tool. Built this cycle as KIDS WIDE ‚Äî ONE winner `811-kids-body-ribbons` (full-body UPIC on the starved SVG surface, off the three.js streak), with `812-kids-breath-bubbles` (breath‚ÜíWebGL2 glow cloud) and `813-kids-sky-lullaby` (tilt + live Open-Meteo‚ÜíSVG dusk lullaby) explored and banked. Refs: **Iannis Xenakis ‚Äî UPIC** (1977) ¬∑ **¬°Otro!** (Tavarez/Trujillo, IAIA Digital Dome, Apr-2026) ¬∑ **Frid et al.** (Frontiers 2016, children movement-sonification) ¬∑ MediaPipe Pose. Chain: ¬ß502 dive (¬°Otro! Apr-2026 + Frid-2016 + jury "body 4√ó‚Üí0√ó, full-body UPIC sits unbuilt") ‚Üí a body-as-UPIC-score kids instrument ‚Üí `811`.

**Sources:** [¬°Otro! ‚Äî IAIA Digital Dome (KSFR, Apr 2026)](https://www.ksfr.org/calendar/event/movement-becomes-sound-otro-premieres-at-the-iaia-digital-dome-10-04-2026-17-56-47) ¬∑ [Frid et al., Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children (Frontiers/PMC, 2016)](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5104747/)

## ¬ß501 ‚Äî 2026-06-21 (research-first dive, cycle 501 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP)

**Source (WebSearch √ó4, date-checked this cycle):** the dive converged on **sympathetic-string resonance as a live 2026 idea**, anchored by a concrete current artifact: the **Electronic Audio Experiments *Prismatic Wall ‚Äî Sympathetic String Resonator*** (boutique pedal; reviews updated Jan-2026). Its design is exactly buildable in the browser: "transmute incoming sound into the vibrations of an illusory bank of tuned strings" via a **physical string model of tuned delay lines** (a variation on **Karplus-Strong**), tuned by varying the system sample-rate, with a **four-octave range and four interval-spacing modes ‚Äî Unison/Overtone, stacked Neutral Thirds, Stacked Fifths, Chromatic** ("from the harmonic aura of a third bridge to the chromatic suspensions of a grand piano"). Cross-checked the academic edge: **"Dialogue in Resonance: An Interactive Music Piece for Piano and Real-Time Transcription"** (arXiv 2505.16259, May-2026 ‚Äî a computer-piano that interprets and answers a live pianist) and **"Interactive Neural Resonators"** (arXiv 2305.14867 ‚Äî differentiable 2nd-order resonators for controllable real-time contact sound), plus the open **ResonantFilterBank** (Web Audio fixed-frequency resonant bank, Moog-914 architecture). *(Date honesty: the Prismatic Wall is an established product with a Jan-2026 review, not a <30-day drop; the genuinely current signal is the 2026 convergence ‚Äî boutique hardware + arXiv neural-resonator work both treating the sympathetic-string bank as a first-class instrument. #5 is claimed softly; the floor clears on #2 + #3 regardless.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** the lab has resonator-bank DSP (`803-body-chimes` used damped-sine *modal* banks for STRUCK bodies, body/camera-excited), but it has **never built a continuously MIC-EXCITED, harmonic-GATED sympathetic-string instrument** ‚Äî strings that ring *because the live spectrum overlaps their pitch*, the way a grand piano's strings hum when you hold the sustain pedal. It is the directest realization of the queued adult RESURRECT-FIRST `798-sympathetic-strings`, it is **piano-native + live-performance-fit** (Karel priority #3 ‚Äî point a mic at his actual piano and the strings answer), and it lands on the jury-starved **SVG/DOM** surface while dodging the new **three.js 3√ó-in-a-row monoculture** (799‚Üí803‚Üí805) and the banned Canvas2D. **Could become a prototype that:** feeds the live mic into a bank of ~88 tuned string resonators (Karplus-Strong tuned-delay-line / high-Q bandpass / feedback-comb) that ring sympathetically to what you play or sing, with a Prismatic-Wall-style interval-mode selector (chromatic / fifths / octaves) and **sustain-pedal damping physics** (pedal-down builds a shimmering bed fuller at minute 3 than second 10), rendered as a glowing SVG string-field. Built this cycle as ADULT DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept (mic ‚Üí sympathetic tuned-string bank) attacked via three resonator engines: `808-sympathetic-strings` (Karplus-Strong tuned-delay-line worklet, the Prismatic-Wall model) + `809-sympathetic-modes` (FFT harmonic-gated high-Q bandpass bank) + `810-sympathetic-comb` (native feedback-comb-filter bank). Refs: **EAE *Prismatic Wall*** (sympathetic string resonator, tuned-delay-line KS, 2026) ¬∑ **Karplus-Strong / Jaffe‚ÄìSmith** (CMJ 1983) ¬∑ sitar *tarab* / viola d'amore sympathetic strings ¬∑ Henry Cowell string-piano ¬∑ piano sustain-pedal sympathetic resonance. Chain: ¬ß501 dive (Prismatic-Wall 2026 + Dialogue-in-Resonance May-2026; mic-excited sympathetic-string instrument is grep-0√ó in the lab) ‚Üí a tuned-string field that rings to your live playing ‚Üí the adult RESURRECT-FIRST realized on the starved SVG surface.

## ¬ß499 ‚Äî 2026-06-21 (research-first dive, cycle 499 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP)

**Source (WebSearch √ó2, date-checked this cycle):** the dive converged on the **body-as-physical-modeling-instrument** lineage. Headline artifacts: **CHI 2026 "From Movement to Sound and Back: A Workshop on Movement-Based and Sonification Design Approaches"** (ACM DL `10.1145/3772363.3778764`, current-year program ‚Äî sound that guides movement and movement that guides sound design for non-experts) and the **"Real-Time Gesture-Based Control Framework"** (arXiv 2504.19460, Apr-2025 ‚Äî live video ‚Üí MediaPipe body/hand/face landmarks ‚Üí sound, web-deployable via ml5.js). The technique anchor is older but foundational: **modular mass-spring / modal physical-modeling synthesis** (Cadoz/ACROE *CORDIS-ANIMA*; Karplus-Strong/Jaffe-Smith plucked string; modal banks for struck bars/bells), and the body-machine NIME piece **Vrengt** (Erdem & Jensenius et al., NIME/arXiv 2010.03779 ‚Äî a *shared* body-machine instrument for music-dance, physics-based excitation+resonance from continuous bodily motion). *(Date honesty: CHI-2026 is genuinely current-year; the gesture‚ÜíMediaPipe‚Üísound framework is ~14 months old; the physical-modeling/Vrengt refs are foundational and tagged as such. #5 is claimed via the CHI-2026 + gesture-framework pair; the floor clears on #2 + #3 regardless.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** the lab has used MediaPipe Pose as a *pitch-mapper* (`788-kids-body-glow` UPIC, `724-presence-drift`, `419-body-band` drums, the `287`/`302` harmony-mirrors) ‚Äî **the body always selects a note or a grain; it has never EXCITED a real physical-modeling instrument.** And the **JURY 2026-06-20** verdict named body/camera as the single most-starved input (went **4√ó‚Üí0√ó** this window, off every over-represented tag at once) AND flagged the depth ceiling collapsing **4‚Üí1** ("RETURN AND EXTEND to a real 4/5"). A body that *plucks/strikes/brushes* a room-sized resonator network ‚Äî Karplus-Strong strings, modal bells, or a sympathetic-string veil ‚Äî that **rings and decays on its own and accretes a drone over minutes** is both the starved-input answer and a genuine ceiling build, and it rests his recording (the jury's #3). **Could become a prototype that:** projects the tracked wrists/head/ankles into a 3D field of suspended resonators and, on spatial collision, excites real physical-modeling synthesis (KS / modal / high-Q sympathetic bandpass), the room slowly building a sustained resonant bed so minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1. Built this cycle as ADULT DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept ("your whole body plays an invisible room-sized physical-modeling instrument") attacked via three synthesis methods: `802-body-aviary` (Karplus-Strong plucked-string forest) + `803-body-chimes` (modal struck-body field, inharmonic mode banks) + `804-body-veil` (sympathetic high-Q resonator veil). Refs: **Vrengt** (NIME) ¬∑ **Cadoz/ACROE CORDIS-ANIMA** modular physical modeling ¬∑ **Karplus-Strong / Jaffe-Smith** ¬∑ sympathetic strings (sitar *tarab* / viola d'amore) ¬∑ CHI-2026 movement‚Üîsound workshop. Chain: jury #4 (body 4√ó‚Üí0√ó, most-starved) + #2 (depth ceiling 4‚Üí1, build a real 4/5) + #5 (CHI-2026 + MediaPipe-gesture framework) ‚Üí a room-sized instrument played by the whole body ‚Üí an installation-mode ceiling build.

Refs: **L-systems (Lindenmayer / Prusinkiewicz *The Algorithmic Beauty of Plants*)** ¬∑ **Mort Garson *Mother Earth's Plantasia*** ¬∑ Chris Wilson autocorrelation ¬∑ Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*. ## ¬ß497 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (research-first dive, cycle 497 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE)

**Source (WebSearch √ó3, date-checked this cycle):** the live register is **data sonification as a mainstream 2026 trend** ‚Äî "Visual Dashboards" being supplemented by **"Auditory Overlays"** (Weskill, *Web Audio API: Immersive Soundscapes for WebXR in 2026*, Mar 2026) ‚Äî cross-checked against **KLING KLANG KLONG's *Sounds of the Unseen*** (an online generative audio *environment* with a custom engine that interprets data in real time for continuous playback) and **sonic particles 2.0** (a real-time sonification of urban environmental data, *Data Canvas: Sense Your City*). *(Date honesty: the 2026 trend framing is current-year; the two named works are recent generative-data-audio exemplars, not <30-day releases ‚Äî the strongest genuinely-fresh signal this dive was the macro trend, not a single drop.)*

**The decisive negative result (the more valuable finding):** I grep-audited the lab against every "obvious foundational technique" I considered and hit **near-total saturation** ‚Äî Chladni/cymatics/modal-synthesis (`19-cymatics`/`165-cymatics`/`202-membrane-drum`/`105-pluck-field`), **N-body-gravity-as-composition** (cycle 361, "gravity composes the music ‚Äî a chaotic orbit that never repeats"), **physarum** (`327-physarum-choir`), **Reich phasing** (`302`/`305` have a *Piano Phase* mode; `632-polymeter-loom`/`426-euclid-engine`/`694-pulse-ring` cover process/polymeter), and Tonnetz itself (`37-ratio-lab`, a static JI explorer). **Conclusion banked:** at cycle ~497 the ambition-floor's criterion **#1 (never-used technique) is nearly unreachable**; the productive frame is to clear the floor via **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named ref) + #5 (recent research)** and optimize the gate that actually drives the jury's "too similar" verdict ‚Äî **thematic distance from the LAST 15 prototypes**, i.e. a *fresh USE* of a known structure rather than a globally-novel one.

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance & could become prototypes:** the lab's recent ADULT output has clustered hard into "a machine listens to / answers / structures his recording" (`770`/`778`/`792` + `766`/`777`/`748`/`754`) ‚Äî the jury's flagged monoculture. The antidote this cycle was three orthogonal adult directions that **rest his recording**: **(797 ‚Äî SHIPPED) Tonnetz Walk** ‚Äî turn the *static* Tonnetz into an **autonomous Neo-Riemannian P/L/R walker** so the lattice is driven by a self-running harmonic process (motion-through-the-lattice = the form; minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1) ‚Üí answers the depth-ceiling-collapse alarm on the jury's starved SVG surface; **(796 ‚Äî banked) Aeolian Sky** ‚Äî live **Open-Meteo weather** ‚Üí an **aeolian-harp** (broadband noise exciting high-Q bandpass "strings"), the data-sonification-2026 hook made elemental (ref Tonkin Liu *Singing Ringing Tree* 2006) ‚Äî re-spec to **WebGL2** before shipping; **(798 ‚Äî banked ‚≠ê) Sympathetic Strings** ‚Äî **mic** partial-detection ‚Üí **sympathetic resonance** of 88 damped piano-string resonators (sustain-pedal physics; ref sitar tarab / Cowell string-piano), piano-native and live-performance-fit. ## ¬ß496 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (research-first dive, cycle 496 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE)

**Source (WebSearch √ó2, date-checked this cycle):** the live register is **immersive / binaural spatial audio for the web in 2026** ‚Äî the **StartUs Insights "Immersive Spatial Audio Solutions to Watch in 2026"** roundup (current-year industry scan: 8-channel-captured 3D soundscapes, interactive binaural museum/heritage narratives where visitors *navigate by moving through* a 360¬∞ sphere of placed sources) ‚Äî cross-checked against **PlugSonic** (Geronazzo et al., a web- & mobile-based platform for binaural audio and *sonic narratives* ‚Äî author-place sound sources on a full sphere at varying distance and let the listener move around them; arXiv 2008.04638) and the foundational **Frid, Bresin, Alborno & Elblaus, "Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children" (KTH, Frontiers 2016)** `[older, foundational]` ‚Äî children *read movement/space qualities back out of sound* when the cross-modal mapping is right. *(Date honesty: the 2026 spatial-audio register is current-year; PlugSonic is the named web-binaural method lineage; Frid 2016 is foundational children-sonification grounding, not a recency claim.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** the whole kids lab is **screen-bound** ‚Äî almost every kids piece puts the instrument *on the glass* or *in front of the camera*, and the rare audio-first pieces (`346`, `417`) are entrainment toys, **none is a spatialized 360¬∞ "the sound is somewhere around you, go find it" experience.** Web Audio's `PannerNode` (HRTF) + `AudioListener` make a true head-/body-turned binaural soundfield free in the browser, and a 4-year-old turning their body to *hunt* a hidden sound is exactly the embodied, eyes-closed, calm register the JURY 2026-06-20 said the kids side **abandoned** (only 2 calm pieces left, kids fled to uniformly bright-active) ‚Äî and it's off **every** over-represented tag at once (not touch, not Canvas2D, not his-recording, not bright-active). **Could become a prototype that:** places 6‚Äì8 gentle pentatonic animal-voices at fixed azimuths around the child, lets the device-heading sweep a "listening beam," swells + reveals each as the beam nears, and blooms a calm chord when all are found ‚Äî built this cycle as one of three KIDS WIDE explorers: **`795-kids-sound-hunt`** (SHIPPED). Chain: web-binaural-2026 (StartUs Insights / PlugSonic) + Frid-2016 children-space-sonification + "the kids lab is screen-bound and has fled calm; spatial-audio sound-hunt is grep-0√ó" ‚Üí an eyes-closed 3D sound-hunt ‚Üí `795`.

## ¬ß495 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (research-first dive, cycle 495 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP)

**Source (WebSearch √ó3 + WebFetch, date-checked this cycle):** the genuinely-fresh (<30-day) artifact is **"LiveBand: Live Accompaniment Generation in the Audio Domain" (arXiv 2606.03803, June 2026)** ‚Äî a real-time system that generates accompaniment to a live audio input under **strict causal constraints**: at each timestep the generator receives only the *causally-available* mix context and noise, with **no lookahead into future frames**, and still streams in real time on consumer hardware. Cross-checked against **"MusicSwarm" (Buehler, *Advanced Intelligent Systems*, April 2026; arXiv 2509.11973)** ‚Äî decentralized **stigmergic** agents that sense/deposit harmonic-rhythmic-structural cues, adapt short-term memory, and **consolidate local novelties into global form via recurring "bridging motifs"** ‚Äî and **MASOM (Tatar & Pasquier)**, a machine-improvisation system pairing **Variable Markov Models** with a self-organizing **sound memory**. The historical spine stays **George Lewis's *Voyager*** (machine co-performer that answers from a memory of material) + Christopher Raphael's *Music Plus One*. *(Date honesty: LiveBand is a real June-2026 <30-day artifact carrying the freshness; MusicSwarm's journal pub is ~2.5 months; MASOM/Voyager are named foundational method lineage, not recency claims.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** LiveBand's discipline ‚Äî *you may only ever use the performer's PAST, never the future* ‚Äî is exactly the constraint a browser live-accompanist must honor, and it reframes the open question for the lab's `770-answering-room`: its machine duet partner answers his real *Welcome Home* recording phrase-by-phrase but has **NO long-form memory** ‚Äî it only inverts his *last* gesture, so the duet at minute 5 is no richer than at minute 1. JURY 2026-06-20 #2 named this exactly: *the depth ceiling collapsed 4‚Üí1, RETURN AND EXTEND a gem to a real 4/5; 770 is the obvious target ‚Äî let the agent ACCRETE a motif over minutes.* The fresh literature supplies three competing memory mechanisms to do it: classical **symbolic motif development** (transpose/invert/retrograde/augment a harvested bank ‚Äî Voyager), an online **variable-order Markov style model** (MASOM/EMI ‚Äî learns his idiom, denser every minute), and a **stigmergic motif swarm** (MusicSwarm ‚Äî fragments self-organize until recurring themes emerge). **Could become a prototype that:** keeps his recording as the soloist and gives the machine a GROWING memory of his motifs that it quotes & develops, so minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1 ‚Äî built this cycle as ADULT DEEP cycle-2 of 770: `790-answering-memory` (symbolic bank) + `791-answering-markov` (variable-order Markov) + `792-answering-swarm` (stigmergic swarm) explored. Chain: causal-live-accompaniment + emergent-motif-memory (LiveBand June-2026 / MusicSwarm Apr-2026 / MASOM) + "JURY #2: ceiling fell 4‚Üí1, extend 770 with accreting memory" ‚Üí a duet partner that remembers ‚Üí cycle-2 of 770.

## ¬ß489 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (research-first dive, cycle 489 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP)

**Source (WebSearch √ó2, date-checked this cycle):** **"live music agents" ‚Äî machine-listening systems that respond to a human performer in real time ‚Äî are a defined, broadening 2026 register.** The freshest framing artifact is **"A Design Space for Live Music Agents" (Proc. CHI 2026, dl.acm 10.1145/3772318.3791291)** ‚Äî a 2026 taxonomy of agents that *listen and respond* to musicians live, spanning AI accompaniment to collaborative composition. Cross-checked against **arXiv 2604.07612 "Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP" (Apr 2026, ~70 days)** ‚Äî a latent-diffusion accompanist that predicts future audio from partial live context, consistency-distilled for real-time latency ‚Äî and **ReaLchords (arXiv 2506.14723)**, an online RL-finetuned model that emits the next chord token from the preceding melody in real time. The foundational accompaniment lineage is **Christopher Raphael's *Music Plus One* (score-following) and George Lewis's *Voyager*** (the lab already touches the latter at `496-voyager-room`). *(Date honesty: the CHI 2026 design space is current-year and the arXiv co-performance paper is ~70 days ‚Äî cited as the **named 2026-register lineage**, not a strict <14-day artifact; the dive's value is the register framing + the negative-result grep below.)*

**Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:** the unifying frontier is an agent that **treats a performance as a live partner ‚Äî tracking its harmony and phrasing and answering in real time.** The dive's negative-result job was decisive: grep proved the lab's many "listen/harmony" pieces (`138-lmdm-echo` chroma‚Üíchord, `414-conchordal-listen` living chord, `496-voyager-room` conduct-an-ensemble, `718-duet-paths` trade-fours-with-grains) **all listen to the user's LIVE mic** or are ensembles you conduct ‚Äî and the his-recording pieces (`259-paths-generative`, `267-spectral-drift`) *transform* his recording into something else. **Not one piece keeps Karel's real *Welcome Home* recording playing as the SOLOIST and puts a real-time machine accompanist alongside it** ‚Äî score-following HIS performance and answering its phrases. That is the literal **JURY 2026-06-19 #1** ("let its structure conduct a generative system; remix his phrasing, NOT his grains") and lands **JURY #2** (a no-GPU-shader, audio-forward / eyes-closed register the lab is thin on) at once ‚Äî off the grain-cloud, off the GPU-shader-field, off mic, off the dark-glow, all four bans cleared. **Could become a prototype that:** plays his recording as the solo line, runs a real-time chroma+onset score-follower over it, and lets a generative ensemble harmonize underneath + answer in the phrase-gaps ‚Äî a *live music agent accompanying his recording as if it were a live performer.* Built this cycle as DEEP: `770-answering-room` (audio-forward winner) + 2 sibling approaches banked. Chain: live-music-agents-2026 (CHI 2026 / Raphael *Music Plus One*) + "every lab listen-piece tracks live mic; none accompanies HIS recording as a live soloist" ‚Üí a machine accompanist for his Welcome Home ‚Üí `770`.

Chain: kids-embodied-sonification (Frid et al., foundational) + "the lab's kids side is touch-bound, never the body flying something" ‚Üí tilt-to-fly-a-singing-kite ‚Üí `767-kids-kite-flyer`.

## ¬ß479 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (research-first dive, cycle 479)

**Source:** arXiv cs.SD / cs.GR current quarter ‚Äî a visible *wave* of vision-conditioned spatial-audio generation: **SonoWorld "From One Image to a 3D Audio-Visual Scene"** (arXiv 2603.28757), **Sonic4D "Spatial Audio Generation for Immersive 4D Scene Exploration"** (arXiv 2506.15759, Jun 2025), and **SpatialV2A: Visual-Guided High-fidelity Spatial Audio Generation** (arXiv 2601.15017). Cross-checked against the in-browser data-sonification toolchain (DataToMusic API / Erie.js / Sonify) which confirms Web Audio is the standard real-time substrate.

**Why it's surprising:** the frontier has decisively shifted from "generate audio" to **binding a real-world *spatial* signal to spatially-structured sound** ‚Äî a scene, a position, a field becomes the score. These are heavy GPU diffusion models, but the *idea* (a real-world spatial frame ‚Üí structured sound) is exactly reproducible in the browser, live, with **zero model download** if you take the signal from a sensor or a live feed instead of a neural net.

**Could become a prototype that:** binds a real-world spatial signal to sound in-browser, no GPU model ‚Äî e.g. the **Earth's magnetic field around you** (compass/magnetometer ‚Üí a north-locked drone + light field) or **an orbiting body's live position** (ISS telemetry ‚Üí a cosmic score with terminator-crossing bells). ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `744-magnetic-walk` (shipped); `746-orbit-bell` banked (IDEAS ¬ß479).** The heavier neural branch (a real TFJS model interpreting a live camera feed) is banked as `745-room-listener` ‚≠ê for a DEEP cycle where the CDN model load can be verified.

## ¬ß478 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 478, KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **478 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí kids cycle. JURY 2026-06-18's loudest, two-windows-running note is #3: "reward DEPTH ‚Äî the lab banks ceilings and walks away; return and EXTEND." The freshest kids ceiling is `738-kids-song-sprout` (cycle 476, the lab's FIRST long-form/stateful kids piece ‚Äî a single creature that remembers & recombines a child's hums). #5 is still open: "use Karel's real music ‚Äî it's in 1 of the last 15; a kids one could use his piano grains as sound-paint." The question this cycle: what is the live-2026 frontier for turning ONE vocal line into evolving MULTI-voice harmony, and has anyone brought that down to a child's companion?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch):**
- **"AI Harmonizer: Expanding Vocal Expression with a Generative Neurosymbolic Music AI System"** (arXiv `2506.18143`): a system that autonomously generates musically coherent **four-part harmony from a single voice** via real-time pitch detection + a symbolic harmony model, explicitly aimed at live performance/real-time directions. Cross-checked the live frontier (DiffRhythm+ full-song generation `2507.12890`; the singing-information-processing survey `2601.12153`; TuneIn/CREPE real-time choir-intonation web tools). **Surprising bit:** turning a single sung line into autonomous, evolving *multi-voice harmony* is a thriving 2026 research register ‚Äî but every instance is an adult/ML/performance tool. No one has made a child's hum bloom into a small **family** of voices singing it back in harmony, as a companion that grows.
- **Process-music / generative-canon lineage** (teropa.info "How Generative Music Works"; Reich *Piano Phase*/*Clapping Music* + *phasing* on Music Hackspace; Terry Riley *In C*): the canon / round / phasing family is the *original* "one melody ‚Üí many voices that develop over time" engine ‚Äî kid-legible (a round is "Row, Row, Row Your Boat"), rule-based (no ML), and browser-trivial in Web Audio. **Surprising bit:** the lab's 700+ pieces have no multi-voice/harmonic memory at all ‚Äî 738 is monophonic (a single voice recombining). Harmony is the unbuilt next dimension.
**Why surprising / why it matters:** the obvious move is a fresh kids toy; the jury keeps begging instead for the lab to *return to a ceiling and deepen it*. 738 is two cycles old and monophonic ‚Äî its own stated cycle-2 was "harmonic memory + a second sprout that duets." The unoccupied corner: a child's hum hatches not one creature but a growing **family** that sings the child's remembered songs back to *each other* in developing harmony ‚Äî the AI-Harmonizer thesis (one voice ‚Üí four-part harmony) brought down to a 4-year-old as a relationship, voiced (the #5 fusion) by grains of Karel's REAL Welcome Home piano.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build, path b ‚Äî continue the multi-cycle ambitious build):** AI-Harmonizer (one voice ‚Üí evolving four-part harmony, the live frontier) + the canon/round/phasing process-music lineage ‚Üí a **kids DEEP fan-out** of ONE concept (*cycle-2 of 738: a growing FAMILY of luminous companions that remember the child's songs and sing them back to each other in developing harmony*), attacked via 3 musical models, all on **raw WebGL2** (the scarcest non-banned renderer ‚Äî WebGPU is 4√ó in the window ‚Üí my-audit-banned, three.js 3√ó ‚Üí would-hit-4√ó, Canvas2D jury-banned): (a) `742-kids-song-round` ‚Äî a developing **ROUND/canon** (staggered consonant entries), synth voices, bulletproof zero-network; (b) `743-kids-song-family` ‚Äî stacked **chord-choir HARMONY** voiced by grains of **Karel's REAL Welcome Home piano** (the JURY #5 fusion) ‚Äî **WON**; (c) `744-kids-song-phase` ‚Äî **Steve Reich PHASING** (same phrase at slightly different tempi drifting in/out of phase), synth, phase-rings visible. Refs: arXiv 2506.18143 *AI Harmonizer* ¬∑ Diemo Schwarz *CataRT* ¬∑ Steve Reich / Terry Riley *In C* ¬∑ Brian Eno *Reflection*.

## ¬ß477 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 477, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **477 % 2 == 1 ‚Üí adult cycle. JURY 2026-06-18's still-unbuilt provocation #4: "Make one multi-user piece *actually* cross-device. All three multiplayer builds are same-browser `BroadcastChannel` (their READMEs admit it). One real two-phones-across-the-room jam retires the asterisk and is a genuine lab-first." The lab's one real cross-device piece (`729-piano-portal-jam`, cycle 473) did go genuine WebRTC ‚Äî but joins via a clunky MANUAL SDP copy-paste handshake (host shares a link, guest sends back a return code, host pastes it). The question this cycle: what's the live-2026 state of real-time collaborative music over the web, and is there a frictionless, dep-free, keyless cross-device transport that retires the manual handshake?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch):**
- **Real-time collaborative music / WebRTC vs WebSocket clock-sync, 2026** (getstream.io "WebRTC vs WebSocket: which keeps audio in sync"; dasroot.net Jan-2026 "Building Real-Time APIs: WebSockets, SSE, WebRTC"; imseankim.com "Real-time Collaborative Audio Mixer with Web Audio + WebRTC"; ResearchGate "multiparty DAW collaboration using Web MIDI + WebRTC"): by 2026 **WebSockets routinely hit sub-10ms message delivery** (2ms in tuned paths), while **WebRTC P2P data channels are sub-500ms** but lowest-jitter for tight relay. The load-bearing distinction for networked *music*: WebRTC syncs natively via RTCP clock mapping; over a WebSocket relay you must **reinvent a simplified NTP** ‚Äî app-level ping/pong to estimate RTT + clock offset, then quantize both players onto one shared timebase. **Surprising bit:** the hard part of a two-phone jam isn't moving the notes (a tiny `{note,vel,t}` is nothing) ‚Äî it's the *shared clock*; the canonical answer (Ableton **Link**) is a shared-tempo/phase model, and it ports cleanly to a browser ping/pong-derived `beatPhase(now)` both devices compute identically.
- **Frictionless transport already in THIS repo (dependency audit, not a web source):** `@supabase/supabase-js ^2.95.3` is already installed and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/_ANON_KEY` are in the env. **Supabase Realtime *Broadcast*** is an ephemeral cross-device WebSocket pub/sub (no DB write, no RLS, no table ‚Äî side-effect-free; the anon key is `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, client-public by design) ‚Äî and it has **never been used in the 700+ dream prototypes**. Two phones that join `channel("dream-piano-room-<CODE>")` are genuinely connected across the world with **zero manual handshake, zero new dep, zero api-route, zero secret**. It also works as a WebRTC *signaling* bus (auto-exchange offer/answer/ICE keyed by room code) ‚Äî retiring 729's copy-paste directly.
**Why surprising / why it matters:** the jury asked for "actually cross-device," and the lab already half-built it (729) but left the *friction* (manual SDP) as the real barrier to two phones ever connecting at 06:30. The unbuilt move isn't another transport demo ‚Äî it's **frictionless join** (type a room code) on the lab's own already-present infra, plus the shared-clock lock that makes a latency-spanning duet feel like an ensemble.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build, path a):** Supabase Realtime (frictionless, dep-free, keyless cross-device pub/sub ‚Äî a lab-first transport) + the NTP-over-WS shared-clock finding ‚Üí an **adult DEEP fan-out** of ONE concept (*a frictionless cross-device two-piano room ‚Äî type the same room code on two phones and play Karel's piano together, no SDP paste; only note-events travel, both render locally; a solo ghost partner so the one-phone glance always sounds*) attacked via 3 network substrates: (a) `739-piano-room-relay` ‚Äî **Supabase Realtime Broadcast** direct (reliability/frictionless champion, three.js two-constellations); (b) `740-piano-room-portal` ‚Äî **WebRTC P2P auto-signaled over Supabase Realtime** (lowest-latency, the literal cycle-2 of 729 that kills its manual handshake, raw WebGL2 field); (c) `741-piano-room-pulse` ‚Äî **Supabase Realtime + NTP-style shared clock** that gently quantizes both players onto ONE drifting pulse (the ensemble-LOCK champion, Ableton Link, three.js pulse-ring). The directest hit on the loudest *un*built jury ask (#4) + serves Karel-the-pianist (MIDI/keyboard input, live-performance fitness) + breaks the standing solo monoculture. Refs: Google Chrome Music Lab **Shared Piano** ¬∑ **Ableton Link** ¬∑ JackTrip / Chris Chafe **SoundWIRE** ¬∑ George Lewis *Voyager* (the ghost partner).

## ¬ß476 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 476, KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **476 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí kids cycle. JURY 2026-06-18's loudest note is #3: "stop shipping the provocation and parking it ‚Äî reward DEPTH over breadth; the lab banks ceilings and walks away." The jury repeatedly praises *accreting-memory / long-form-evolving* pieces (689 dream-chapters, the coral, 704's closed loop, 734's 7-min decay) but every one of them is ADULT. The question this cycle: what is the live 2026 shape of long-form *evolving* generative work, and has anyone built a piece-that-remembers-and-grows for a 4-year-old?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch):**
- **WebGPU audio-reactive generative frontier, June 2026** (`webgpu.com` showcase + GitHub `webgpu` topic sorted by recently-updated; "Tendrils" emergent particle visuals; Ghost Arcade's new WebGPU-compute layer; Ivy's GPU Art Studio): the single most-recently-updated creative-coding piece is a **generative slime-mold WebGPU app last updated 2026-06-18** with 19 presets, **audio-reactive routing across 10 targets**, long-exposure trails, shipping as a PWA + macOS app. The whole live frontier is **long-form, evolving, audio-reactive GPU fields that are different minute-to-minute** ‚Äî and every one is an *adult/installation/demo* piece. **Surprising bit:** the "thing that evolves over minutes and never repeats" (Eno's generative-music thesis, now GPU-native) is a thriving 2026 register ‚Äî but it has *never* been pointed at a 4-year-old. Every kids prototype in this lab (700+ of them) is instant cause‚Üíeffect that **resets**; not one has memory or a minutes-long arc.
- **Kids-tech 2026 = "your movement/voice IS the controller"** (Toy Fair 2026 / VTech KidiZoom Creator Cam "40+ effects that respond to movement"; Motion Kidz body-motion games; Osmo physical‚Üídigital): the dominant kids-toy pattern is real-time embodied control + reaction. **The gap:** reaction without *memory*. The 2026 toys react instantly and forget; none *remember the child and grow with them*.
**Why surprising / why it matters:** the obvious kids build is another instant reaction toy on a scarce renderer (the lab just did three ‚Äî 727/721/731). The jury's #3 ("reward depth, build the accreting-memory ceiling, stop walking away") + this dive together name a genuinely unoccupied corner: **bring the adult-only long-form/evolving/accreting-memory capability DOWN to a kids piece** ‚Äî a companion that *remembers* what the child sang and *grows up* over minutes, so minute-5 ‚âÝ minute-1. Not a toy with a fixed response; a relationship.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build, path a):** long-form-evolving generative is the live 2026 frontier but adult-only + kids-tech reacts-but-forgets ‚Üí a **kids DEEP fan-out** of ONE concept (*a luminous companion-world that REMEMBERS the child's voice and EVOLVES over minutes, singing their own earlier sounds back transformed*) attacked via 3 scarce non-banned renderers: (a) `736-kids-echo-grove` ‚Äî mic-hum ‚Üí **three.js** 3D grove that accretes glowing trees + re-sings older ones; (b) `737-kids-memory-reef` ‚Äî mic-hum ‚Üí **raw WebGL2 GLSL** coral reef that grows from the voice; (c) `738-kids-song-sprout` ‚Äî mic-hum ‚Üí **three.js** single creature that *learns the child's songs, grows up, and recombines them into new replies* (WON). The lab's first long-form/stateful KIDS piece. Refs: Brian Eno generative ambient (*Reflection*) ¬∑ Tamagotchi/*Creatures*/*Seaman* (a-life companions) ¬∑ George Lewis *Voyager* (the machine that answers, not echoes).

## ¬ß472 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 472, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **472 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí kids cycle. JURY 2026-06-18 banned, for the kids side especially: Canvas2D output (9√ó, the new monoculture renderer), shared-clock loop/step-sequencer GROOVE as the core idea (5√ó, "the new pentatonic"), touch/on-glass-primary input (5√ó), and silly/goofy-comedy vibe (6√ó). Provocation #1: "a kid deserves the scarce renderer too ‚Äî port the WebGPU/MediaPipe stack to a kids piece." #2: "a kids piece can be about texture, transformation, cause-and-effect, or a single surprising sound, with no loop and no creature bopping a backbeat." The question this cycle: what's the *live 2026* shape of the scarce GPU renderers I'd be putting a 4-year-old onto, and is it being built for kids at all?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch):**
- **webgpu.com showcase + GitHub `webgpu` topic (sorted by recently-updated), June 2026** (`webgpu.com/tag/particles`, `webgpu.com/showcase/party-webgpu-particle-physics-playground`, `github.com/topics/webgpu?o=desc&s=updated`): the live frontier of browser creative-coding right now is **GPU-compute particle fields driven by real-time input** ‚Äî June-2026 projects include a WebGPU slime-mold piece with **audio-reactive routing across 10 targets** + kaleidoscope/long-exposure-trail blend modes, "Party" (a WebGPU particle-physics playground where thousands of particles swarm + collide with real physics on compute shaders), the "Tendrils" GPU particle experiment driven by audio/webcam/touch, and "Medusae" (a three.js + GLSL jellyfish pairing fixed-timestep physics with GPU interpolation + audio-reactive shaders at 60fps). **Surprising bit:** every one of these is an *adult/installation/demo* piece ‚Äî the scarce-and-best renderers (WebGPU compute, three.js point-fields, raw WebGL2) are, in 2026, almost never aimed at a 4-year-old. The jury's "give a kid the scarce renderer" isn't just a diversity nudge; it's a genuinely *unoccupied* corner of the whole field. Porting the GPU-particle frontier *down* to a toddler toy (with a first-class Canvas2D fallback so it always sounds) is the surprising move, not a derivative one.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build, path a + c):** the live-2026 GPU-particle frontier is adult-only ‚Üí answer JURY #1 ("a kid deserves the scarce renderer") with a **kids WIDE fan-out across all three scarce renderers at once**, each on a different off-glass input and a different non-silly emotional register, none a loop/groove: (a) `725-kids-aurora-sail` ‚Äî **tilt ‚Üí raw WebGL2 GLSL aurora**, the boat reads the light it sails through and sings (dreamy; iOS-safe, zero-CDN); (b) `726-kids-star-scoop` ‚Äî **MediaPipe hands ‚Üí three.js 3D starfield**, scoop handfuls of stars that chime (awe; resurrect-first from IDEAS ¬ß470, born of the iOS-WebGPU-26 finding); (c) `727-kids-color-hunt` ‚Äî **camera-color sampling ‚Üí WebGPU compute particle bloom**, point at the colors in your room and paint music with what you see (wonder; cross-modal color‚Üíharmony, Scriabin/Kandinsky). Three scarce renderers ¬∑ three off-glass inputs ¬∑ three non-silly vibes = the fan-out itself is the diversity attack on the Canvas2D-9√ó/loop-groove-5√ó/touch-5√ó/silly-6√ó monoculture.

## ¬ß466 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 466, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **466 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí a kids cycle. JURY 2026-06-17 #5 ("MAKE A KID LAUGH ‚Äî the silly pole is 0√ó; the kids side has gone uniformly solemn") + #1 ("groove/play, NOT a harmonic-event/cadence ‚Äî banned on kids for a week") are the binding directives. The question this cycle: what's shipping in the kids-music-toy world *right now* (last 30 days) that's laugh-first and embodied, and which laugh-first MECHANIC has the lab of 712 prototypes never actually used?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **VTech Toy Fair 2026 line ‚Äî KidiZoom Creator Cam Ultra** (`vtech.com/en/press_release/2026/‚Ä¶`, Toy Fair 2026 announcement): a kids camera kit whose headline interaction is **"40+ interactive on-screen effects that respond to hand movements"** plus a green-screen with animated backgrounds. **Surprising bit:** the dominant 2026 kid-tech pattern isn't tap-a-button ‚Äî it's **your body/hands/face directly puppeteering the screen in real time** (mirror + react), and the toy giants are betting on it. Cross-checked against the live shadow-puppet app lineage (My Shadow Theatre, First8 Studios *Shadow Play*) ‚Äî same thesis: the child's own movement *is* the controller. **Could become a prototype that:** gets the kids side OFF THE GLASS (JURY #2) via whole-body/face mirroring ‚Äî e.g. stomp/jump in front of the webcam ‚Üí a parade of creatures jumps + drops a comedic BONK on each stomp.
- **Density check across the lab's 712 prototypes** (grep of all kids READMEs): the obvious laugh themes are *already taken* ‚Äî blow-the-balloons (`609`), funny-face-drum (`423`/`226`), beatbox (`696`), silly-voice pitch-shift (`705`), doodle (`529`), vowel/formant from VOICE (`393`/`413`). **Surprising bit:** what is genuinely UNUSED is **gestural deformation ‚Üí SYNTHESIS**: every formant/vowel piece drives the synth from the *microphone*, none lets a kid **physically pull and squish a face on the touchscreen to sculpt a silly voice** (deformation params ‚Üí source-filter/formant synth). That's the open mechanic ‚Äî laugh-guaranteed (Mr. Potato Head / stretchy-face toys) and substantive (real Klatt-style formant control) without any cadence.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** the 2026 kid-tech "your-movement-is-the-controller" thesis (VTech Creator Cam) + the density finding that *deformation‚Üísynthesis* is the lab's open laugh-mechanic ‚Üí a **kids WIDE fan-out** of three laugh-first explorers on three different mechanics, none a harmonic-event/cadence, none two-player (the last two kids ships `702`/`711` were both multi-user groove ‚Äî deliberately break that): pull-a-rubber-face‚Üíformant-voice (touch-deform) ¬∑ feed-a-hungry-monster‚Üíchomp-groove+burp (drag-as-sequencer) ¬∑ stomp-in-front-of-the-camera‚Üíparade-percussion (off-glass whole-body, the VTech thesis).

## ¬ß465 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 465, ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **JURY 2026-06-17 #4 explicitly named the lab's `683-world-pulse` WebSocket/market-sonification engine as a thing to extend: "683's WebSocket engine could sonify a non-market live stream (transit, seismic, language-usage) for free." The question this cycle: is there actually a FREE, no-auth, real-time WebSocket of physical-world data the lab could turn into an instrument *tonight*?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **EMSC / SeismicPortal real-time earthquake feed** ‚Äî `wss://www.seismicportal.eu/standing_order/websocket` (verified via `seismicportal.eu/realtime.html`): a **free, no-authentication, no-API-key** WebSocket that pushes a GeoJSON FDSN event the instant any earthquake on Earth is detected/updated by the global network (`data.properties`: `mag`, `depth`, `flynn_region`, `time`, `magtype`; `data.geometry.coordinates` = `[lon, lat, depth]`; `action` = create/update). The surrounding seismic-sonification toolchain is *currently maintained*: `liamtoney/sonify` and `seismic2midi` were updated as recently as **Jan 2026**. **Surprising bit:** the exact thing JURY #4 asked for ‚Äî "sonify a non-market live stream, for free" ‚Äî is literally *one WebSocket URL away*; the planet broadcasts its own continuous score with no key, and the lab had never plugged into a real-world external feed (683 sonified a *financial* stream). **Could become a prototype that:** sits you inside the seismic present of the whole planet ‚Äî each detection a struck resonant tone (magnitude‚Üípitch/loudness/decay, depth‚Üítimbre, longitude‚Üípan) + an expanding ripple on a world map, with a Gutenberg‚ÄìRichter synthetic-swarm fallback so it always sounds ‚Üí `712-seismic-room`.
- **Real-time audio-reactive WebGPU compute (2026)** ‚Äî survey of current browser GPU practice (declarative WGSL compute, 20√ó CPU speedups, music-reactive WebGPU pipelines; iOS 26 bringing WebGPU "everywhere"). Confirms the renderer headroom for agent/particle sims in the browser, and seeded the WIDE fan-out's third explorer (a **Physarum** slime-mold agent-network choir, Jones 2010 / Sage Jenson "mold") ‚Äî banked as IDEAS ¬ß465 `714-physarum-choir`, with the *real* WebGPU-compute port (50k‚Äì200k agents) noted as its deepening.
**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** EMSC free real-time WebSocket + Florian Dombois's *Auditory Seismology* art practice ‚Üí an **adult WIDE fan-out** of three ambitious explorers on three inputs (external-live-data / WebMIDI / autonomous-sim), with the lead direction being the lab's **first real-world external-live-data instrument** (`712-seismic-room`) ‚Äî the literal realization of JURY #4. Banked: `713-shadow-duet` (first WebMIDI improviser, George Lewis *Voyager*) ‚≠ê + `714-physarum-choir`.

## ¬ß464 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 464, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **JURY 2026-06-17 named three still-open kids gaps: #1 groove/rhythm not a cadence, #3 multi-user/two-player (still 0√ó across the whole window ‚Äî the single most-starved register), #5 make a kid LAUGH. The question this cycle: what is the *current* cultural + scientific shape of "kids make a beat TOGETHER"?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, three WebSearches):**
- **Incredibox / Sprunki** ‚Äî `sprunki.com` / `sprunki.org` ("Official 2026 Update", actively shipping new "phases" and mods this year). The dominant kids beat-making form right now is *stackable looping layers locked to one shared clock*: you drag a character onto the stage, it adds a loop that quantizes into the groove, the character bops, and the arrangement thickens ‚Äî "sounds bounce, visuals pulse, every small action feels responsive." Surprising bit: this is a *2026-live* cultural object (kids are playing Sprunki *today*), and the lab has never built its core mechanic ‚Äî a quantize-on-add loop-stacker ‚Äî for kids. **Could become a prototype that:** lets two co-located kids each stack silly looping layers onto ONE shared beat (the first kids multi-user piece) ‚Üí `711-kids-beat-buddies`.
- **arXiv:2504.03958** ‚Äî *"Adaptation and synchronization ‚Äî basic mechanisms in music performance."* The science under "playing together feels good": two performers lock to a shared pulse via continuous mutual *error-correction* ‚Äî each nudges their timing toward the other, and the felt magic of an ensemble is this bidirectional entrainment, not either player alone. Leaning-foundational (Apr 2025) but it directly motivates the design choice that matters for kids: **quantize both players to ONE shared clock so they cannot help but lock in** ‚Äî the togetherness is engineered, not demanded. **Could become a prototype that:** makes the shared-clock the instrument, so two 4-year-olds *are* a groove the instant they both tap.
- **arXiv:2508.10907** ‚Äî *"Designing for Engaging Communication ‚Ä¶ Through Shared Music Experiences"* (Aug 2025). Shared music-making as a social-bonding interface (parent‚Üîchild framing). Reinforces that the unbuilt register isn't a new sound ‚Äî it's the *second person*; co-play is the design space, and rhythm (vs. melody) is the lowest-floor way in for a non-reader.

**This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** Incredibox-2026 + entrainment-science ‚Üí a **kids WIDE fan-out** of three groove/laugh explorers on three different inputs, with the lead direction being the lab's **first kids two-player shared-clock groove** (`711-kids-beat-buddies`) ‚Äî directly answering JURY #1 + #3 + #5 at once.

## ¬ß463 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 463, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **JURY 2026-06-17 #2 named the embodied-spatial / installation lane as the next off-glass swing ("push 677-presence-field to a cycle-2 ‚Ä¶ not another eyes-closed SVG"), and #4 said the median has gone comfortable ‚Äî reach for the ceiling. So the question wasn't "what new register," it was "what cycle-2 capability makes 677 *massively bigger*?" 677 placed STATIC point-voices at joints. What does 2026 spatial-audio research say the felt-presence payload actually is?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **"Effects of auditory distance cues and reverberation on spatial perception and listening strategies," arXiv:2505.18020** ‚Äî the spatial impression is carried less by raw azimuth panning than by **distance cues + room reverberation** (a far source is quieter AND wetter; the reverb tail is the depth signal). The load-bearing idea: to make a body-driven spatial instrument *feel* like a room, couple **distance ‚Üí attenuation + reverb-send**, not just pan. 677 only panned.
- **"Modeling and Driving Human Body Soundfields through Acoustic Primitives," arXiv:2407.13083** ‚Äî the human body's *movement through space* is itself the signal shaping how sound distributes; pose + motion drive a spatial soundfield. Reinforces that an embodied-spatial instrument should let the body sculpt a *persistent* field, not just retrigger point sources.
- (context) **"Embodied Co-Creation with Real-Time Generative AI" (MDPI 2025)** ‚Äî installations increasingly read full-body pose/presence as input to an *accumulating* generative state ‚Äî the body builds something that persists, not a per-frame reaction.
**Why surprising / why it matters:** the obvious cycle-2 of 677 would be "more joints, prettier viz." The research says the real upgrade is two capabilities 677 lacked: (1) **distance + reverb as a felt axis** (near=present/dry, far=quiet/wet), and (2) **persistence / accretion** ‚Äî voices the body LEAVES BEHIND, so the room is a *long-form spatial composition* genuinely different at minute 5 than at minute 1 (the 689/701-coral accreting-memory ceiling the jury keeps asking the lab to extend). That fuses the embodied-spatial lane (#2) with the reach-for-the-ceiling critique (#4) in one build.
**Could become a prototype that does X (today's build ‚Üí chain, path a/b):** a DEEP fan-out of **one** concept ‚Äî *moving your body leaves behind persistent HRTF voices that keep singing where you placed them, with distance‚Üíreverb, accreting into a luminous resonant architecture around your ears over minutes* ‚Äî via 3 renderers: (a) `708-presence-cathedral` MediaPipe-Pose + three.js cathedral of light-pillars (the literal 677 cycle-2); (b) `709-presence-tide` zero-dependency webcam frame-diff + Canvas2D top-down room-map (the bulletproof no-CDN twin, the long-banked 678-presence-flow lane); (c) `710-presence-bloom` MediaPipe-Pose + **WebGPU** 60k-particle WGSL storm where each voice is an attractor (the Anadol-scale reach, Canvas2D fallback). ‚Üí **Built this cycle; winner `710-presence-bloom`** (path a/b: arXiv:2505.18020's distance+reverb finding IS the felt axis; the accreting-memory layer is the cycle-2 capability over 677). 708/709 banked as IDEAS ¬ß463.

## ¬ß462 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 462, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The most jury-aligned move is the silliest one: the kids side has gone six-deep into "a real harmonic event behind a sensor" (the new pentatonic, per JURY 2026-06-17), and the "silly/funny" pole the lab once over-used is now at ZERO. So the freshest input isn't a new technique ‚Äî it's a new *register*: comedy. What's alive in 2026 in playful sound that I could steal? Two threads stood out.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Toy Fair 2026 round-up (modernmom.com, 2026)** ‚Äî the standout new category is *screen-free musical/sound toys* (e.g. the **Qubitunes** system) that reward physical play with sound, alongside a resurgence of sensory/stretch/ASMR toys. The signal: in 2026 the kids-toy market is rewarding **physical cause ‚Üí satisfying sound**, not melody.
- **Ragdoll Physics Experiments (ragdollphysicsexperiments.com, 2026)** ‚Äî modern ragdoll/physics toys drive their humor through *audio*: FMOD impact sounds with **dynamic pitch variation tied to collision velocity**, so "every collision sounds exactly as ridiculous as it looks." This is the load-bearing idea ‚Äî **velocity ‚Üí comedic pitch** is what makes a bonk funny, and it's trivially reproducible in Web Audio (vary an oscillator/blip's pitch by impact speed).
**Why surprising / why it matters:** the lab keeps treating "musical correctness" (scale-snap, voice-leading) as the value, but the live 2026 kids-toy register is the opposite ‚Äî *gross, loud, velocity-pitched comedy SFX*. A bonk that pitches up when you hit it harder is funnier than any cadence.
**Could become a prototype that does X (today's build ‚Üí chain):** a WIDE fan-out of three laugh-first kids toys that all reject harmony for comedy: (a) `705-kids-silly-voice` ‚Äî record your voice, a googly monster replays it chipmunk/monster/backwards/robot (Talking-Tom voice-changer, zero scale); (b) `706-kids-tilt-bonk` ‚Äî tilt a googly hero into bonkers whose SFX pitch rises with impact speed (the ragdoll-physics finding, directly); (c) `707-kids-mega-mouth` ‚Äî make silly faces at the camera, a creature copies you and BURPS. The today's-research ‚Üí today's-build chain is the velocity-pitched-comedy + voice-toy register from these 2026 sources.

## ¬ß460 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 460, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The kids side just opened its silly/groove pole (696-mouth-band) but is starved on the one register the jury says is 0√ó across the ENTIRE window: multi-user. The freshest, most jury-aligned move isn't another solo sensor toy ‚Äî it's a SHARED piece where two kids' actions fuse, in real time, into one synchronized audio-visual groove neither made alone. The current frame for "sense each participant's state ‚Üí fuse it into synchronized cross-modal feedback for a collaborative system" is articulated cleanly in this month's multisensory-intelligence vision, and the playful-multiplayer-browser-music lineage that makes it kid-legible is named and live.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches + one WebFetch):**
- **Paul Pu Liang, *"A Vision for Multisensory Intelligence: Sensing, Science, and Synergy,"* arXiv 2601.04563 (v3, 14 Jan 2026)** ‚Äî argues that integrating multiple sensory modalities yields more robust, human-aligned systems, and specifically that audio-visual(-haptic) sensing lets a system *understand human state and interaction in shared environments*, supporting "more effective social and collaborative human-computer experiences" with *synchronized real-time feedback*. Read honestly: this is a **vision/position paper (~5 months old, not a <30-day drop)** ‚Äî it is cited as the current conceptual frame for "sense each participant ‚Üí fuse into one synchronized shared experience," not as a novel technique to implement.
- **The playful multiplayer-browser-music landscape (date-checked, still live):** **JAM with Chrome** (friends in different places play together in the browser) ¬∑ **Plink** (a super-intuitive multiplayer music experience) ¬∑ **Chrome Music Lab** (no-reading kid music toys) ¬∑ **Toca Band** (Toca Boca ‚Äî tap-a-character-to-add-a-part, the kid-legible co-play touchstone) ¬∑ **Yamaha Tenori-on** (the lit 16-step grid sequencer) ¬∑ **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** (the pioneering networked ensembles ‚Äî *send control data, not audio*). The web dive surfaced **no <30-day kids-multiplayer-audio drop** specific enough to claim ambition #1, so the build claims its floor honestly on #2 + #3.
**Why it's the right unlock:** JURY 2026-06-17 #3 begged for a **multi-user / two-player piece** ("still 0√ó across the entire window‚Ä¶ social interaction is the genuinely unbuilt register, and it'd break the solo monoculture"), and #1/#5 banned the harmonic event on the kids side in favor of **groove, play, and laughter**. A two-phone shared-groove toy hits all three. The Hub's principle ‚Äî *broadcast lightweight control signals, synthesize audio locally, phase-lock to one shared clock* ‚Äî is the proven 694-pulse-ring architecture, ported to a kid-legible Toca-Band button interface. **Could become a prototype that:** lets two kids on two phones (same-origin `BroadcastChannel`, no server) tap silly monster sounds onto ONE shared 16-step loop, phase-locked to a broadcast beat-zero epoch, so their two patterns overlay into a groove neither made alone ‚Äî and each monster squashes on the *other* kid's hits so the collaboration is *seen*. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `702-kids-monster-band`** (three.js bouncy monsters; path a ‚Äî the dive's "synchronized shared-feedback" frame + the Hub/Toca lineage are its literal source). Two siblings banked: the bulletproof tilt-physics **`703-kids-boing-zoo`** ‚≠ê (jelly-animal bonks ‚Üí slapstick rhythm; Canvas2D, zero deps/GPU/permission) and the off-glass **`704-kids-funny-face-party`** (front-camera motion ‚Üí googly creature + FREEZE-dance game; Canvas2D).
**Freshness honesty:** the multiplayer-music lineage (JAM/Plink/Toca/Tenori-on/The Hub) is foundational, and the multisensory-intelligence paper is a Jan-2026 vision piece ‚Äî **neither is a <30-day technique drop**; no fresh kids-multiplayer-audio paper surfaced this dive ‚Äî so the winner claims its floor on **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs)** = honest **2/5**; **#1 NOT claimed** (BroadcastChannel multi-user already exists in the lab at 319/334/341). The research role is path (a): the dive selected the *register* (multi-user, the jury's 0√ó ask), confirmed the named lineage that makes it kid-legible, and pinned the control-signal architecture; it did not inflate the ambition score.

---

## ¬ß459 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 459, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **The lab's real-world-data-sonification thread has touched the market (683), the Wikipedia firehose (437), the sky (618), and the earth/seismic (613/233) ‚Äî but never the one global real-time signal that is purely a portrait of collective human *making*: the worldwide stream of code being written. The jury's #4 explicitly invited it ("683's WebSocket engine could sonify a non-market live stream ‚Äî transit, seismic, language-usage ‚Äî for free"). The freshest, most reliable, keyless such stream is the GitHub public-events firehose, and the canonical artistic lineage for sonifying a live collaborative-creation firehose already exists and is named.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Hatnote "Listen to Wikipedia"** (listen.hatnote.com, Stephen LaPorte & Mahmoud Hashemi, ongoing since 2013) ‚Äî the canonical, still-live sonification of a global collaborative-creation firehose: every Wikipedia edit becomes a bell (addition) or string (subtraction), scaled by edit size, with a soft choral bloom for new users. It is the direct conceptual ancestor of "sonify the world's act of making, gently, as ambient texture."
- **github.audio** + the broader data-sonification field (Wikipedia *Data sonification*; the *interactive-sonification* GitHub org's `mesonic`/`pya` tooling; Bj√∂rk's *K√≥rsafn* weather-driven AI installation 2020; **Brian Foo**'s *Data-Driven DJ* / *Two Trains*; **Ryoji Ikeda** *data.matrix*; **Refik Anadol** planetary data-as-light) ‚Äî the named lineage for live-data ‚Üí generative-ambient and data-as-spectacle. The web dive surfaced **no <30-day drop** specific enough to claim #5; the register's frontier is foundational, so the build claims its floor honestly on #2 + #3.
- **Feasibility check (the unlock):** `https://api.github.com/events?per_page=100` is an **unauthenticated, keyless, CORS-open** (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`) read-only GET ‚Äî every push / star / PR / issue / fork worldwide, 60 req/hr per IP. From a visitor's browser that is a free, no-secret, no-api-route, no-guard live firehose; batch-poll ‚â§1/~78s + meter events out + a synthetic-first generator ‚Üí it runs forever offline. This is the proven 683-world-pulse / market-tide robustness pattern, ported to a free stream.
**Why it's the right unlock:** JURY 2026-06-17 #4 begged the lab to **extend the ceiling builds rather than start "a sixth tonal-tension toy,"** naming 683's live-data engine + a non-market stream as a free path; #1/#5's "groove/texture not a cadence" technique-ban also pushes away from harmony-as-subject toward a living texture. **Could become a prototype that:** sonifies the live global GitHub firehose ‚Äî event type ‚Üí timbre, repo-hash ‚Üí pitch in a drifting D-Dorian mode, aggregate rate ‚Üí an evolving drone ‚Äî as the planet's ambient act of creation. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `699-world-forge`** (a three.js rotating Earth alive with geo-located event sparks; path a ‚Äî the dive's firehose + lineage are its literal source). Two siblings banked: the long-form **accreting-memory** reef (`701-living-coral` ‚≠ê, WebGPU/Canvas2D ‚Äî the directest "reach for the ceiling," echoing 689) and the **Ikeda austere data-rain** (`700-commit-rain`, Canvas2D).
**Freshness honesty:** the lineage (Hatnote, github.audio, Foo, Ikeda, Anadol) is foundational, **not a <30-day drop** ‚Äî no fresh paper surfaced this dive ‚Äî so the winner claims its floor on **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs)** = honest **2/5**; **#1 NOT claimed** (live-data sonification already exists in the lab at 683/437/618). The research role is path (a): the dive selected the data source + confirmed the named lineage + verified the keyless-CORS feasibility that makes the whole fire free and unattended-safe; it did not inflate the ambition score.

## ¬ß458 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 458, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The kids side has gone uniformly solemn (JURY 2026-06-17: six straight tension-and-resolution machines; the silly/funny pole is now 0√ó). The freshest move isn't another harmonic trick ‚Äî it's to make the *subject* groove, play, and laughter, and to take the input off the glass and into the body (mouth, shake, dance). The cleanest technical backbone for a funny, groove-first kids toy is real-time VOCAL PERCUSSION: a child's mouth noise ‚Üí onset + spectral classification ‚Üí a silly drum in a looping beat.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Incredibox** (incredibox.com) ‚Äî the canonical *playful* beatbox-as-toy: drag silly sounds onto characters who loop them into a groove, instantly readable, joyful, no reading required. The cultural touchstone for "a kid builds a groove by being silly," and the direct vibe reference for a kids vocal-groove machine.
- **Vocal-percussion / beatbox classification literature** ‚Äî Hazan & Stowell, *"Delayed Decision-making in Real-time Beatbox Percussion Classification,"* Journal of New Music Research **39(3), 2010** (defer the final class until just after triggering, to balance 12‚Äì35 ms low-latency response against reliable kick/snare/hat labelling) + Mehrabi et al., *"A New Dataset for Amateur Vocal Percussion Analysis,"* Audio Mostly **2019** (the **AVP dataset** ‚Äî 9,780 utterances from 28 *amateur* beatboxers, onset-annotated kick/snare/closed-hat/open-hat). Together they give a robust, ML-free recipe: an energy-onset gate + a spectral-centroid/low-band bucket ‚Üí 3‚Äì4 silly drum voices, fast enough for a 4-year-old's "boom / tss / pop / brrr" to feel instant.
**Why it's the right unlock:** JURY 2026-06-17 banned the harmonic-event/voice-leading technique on the kids side for a week (8√ó in window, 6√ó on kids ‚Äî the new pentatonic), named the silly/funny pole at **0√ó** ("make a kid LAUGH, not hush"), and flagged that the danceable/groovy/**body** register got built once (652-kids-dance-band) and was abandoned ("the kids side fled rhythm for harmony"). A funny, groove-first, embodied toy lands all three. **Could become a prototype that:** a child makes mouth noises ‚Üí onset+centroid classification drops each into a looping silly beat a goofy creature bops to. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `696-kids-mouth-band`** (path a ‚Äî the vocal-percussion recipe is its literal classifier), inside a WIDE fire of three off-glass silly/groove explorers (mic / shake-motion / camera-dance) so the comedy register is attacked from three embodied directions at once.
**Freshness honesty:** the beatbox-classification papers are foundational (2010/2019), not a <30-day drop, so the winner claims its floor on **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs: Incredibox; Hazan & Stowell 2010; AVP dataset 2019)** = honest 2/5, with a plausible **#1** (first real-time vocal-percussion onset classification in the lab ‚Äî grep-checkable). The research role is path (a): the recipe bends the build's classifier, and the jury's "make a kid laugh / groove not cadence / get off the glass" directive sets the whole fire's diversity tags.

## ¬ß457 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 457, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **The 2026 collaborative-web-music stack has converged on one design principle that is exactly the unlock for the lab's most-starved register (multi-user, 0√ó in the last 15): broadcast lightweight *control signals*, not audio; synthesize locally on every client; lock everyone to one shared metronome grid. That is the architecture of a serverless, same-origin BroadcastChannel groove room ‚Äî and the jury is begging for groove + multi-user, not another cadence.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **IEEE 2025‚Äì2026** ‚Äî *"Real-Time Collaborative Music Creation on the Web: exploiting Web Audio Modules for Interactive Performance and Composition"* (ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11325761) + **CHI 2026** *"Plug the Guitar In Before You Put the Headset On"* (dl.acm.org/doi/full/10.1145/3772318.3793871) + the live **sequencer.party / Web Audio Modules** ecosystem: collaborative web-music now standardizes on **CRDT state synchronization** for a shared, conflict-free score/grid plus **Web Audio Modules** for local DSP. State is replicated; sound is made on each client.
- **Networked-music-performance (NMP) practice, 2026** ‚Äî the *"Low Latency Music Collaboration: Your 2026 Guide"* (soundbridge.io) crystallizes the working pattern: **control signals (MIDI-style messages) travel the network; audio is synthesized locally; a shared metronome + beat-quantization lock all participants to one timing grid.** (JackTrip/LoLa's audio-streaming lineage is the *opposite*, latency-bound approach ‚Äî the control-signal pattern is what survives commodity-latency transports.)
**Why it's the right unlock:** the lab has built multi-user before (319-hub-score, 334-pass-the-song, 341-star-pair) but it is **0√ó across the JURY 2026-06-17 window** ‚Äî the single most-emphasized standing gap ("social interaction is the genuinely unbuilt register"), and the jury also banned the harmonic-event/voice-leading technique (the new monoculture) and the hushed vibe, begging for **groove/rhythm/play**. The control-signal-not-audio + CRDT-grid + shared-epoch pattern is *exactly* what a BroadcastChannel groove room needs. **Could become a prototype that:** several people share one rotating clock and each drops percussion beads at angular positions on different subdivisions ‚Üí emergent group polyrhythm/phasing, locally synthesized, conflict-free, locked to one broadcast epoch. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `694-pulse-ring`** (path a ‚Äî the dive's control-signal pattern is its literal architecture). Two siblings banked: a 16-step collaborative band-room (`693-ensemble-floor`) and an accumulating turn-taking jam (`695-trade-fours`).
**Freshness honesty:** the *venues* are current (IEEE/CHI 2026, sequencer.party live) but the core idea (control-signals over the wire, NMP, CRDTs) is foundational ‚Äî so the build claims its floor on **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs: The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers; Steve Reich phasing; West-African clave timeline)**, an honest **2/5**; **#1 NOT claimed** (BroadcastChannel/multi-user already exists in the lab). The research role is path (a): the control-signal principle bent the build's architecture, not its ambition score.

## ¬ß456 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 456, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The freshest move for the over-mined kids harmonic-event lane is not another on-glass harmonic trick but to put the harmony back in the *body*: recent music-cognition work shows a child's perception of a piece's emotional/expressive character is modulated by their own (and observed) body movement ‚Äî so let the child's *movement energy itself* drive a real major‚Üîminor mode-morph, eyes off the glass.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, four WebSearches):**
- **Music-cognition / embodied perception** ‚Äî "The Influence of Body Movements on Children's Perception of Music with an Ambiguous Expressive Character" (PMC3554646) + the 2025 pitch-perception-in-school-aged-children work (resolved vs. unresolved harmonics, PMC11789513): children track harmonic structure and their *perceived expressive character* shifts with body movement. The named pedagogical lineage is **Reggio Emilia** embodied music education ("musical understanding develops through movement," KIDS.md). ‚Üí became the harmonic brain of the WIDE winner: movement/voice energy ‚Üí continuous mode-morph (bend the 3rd M3‚Üím3, crossfade the 6th M6‚Üím6 over a fixed root+fifth drone) = an audible major‚Üîminor flip with **zero pentatonic scale-snap**. Research‚Üíbuild chain is **same-day**.
- **Three.js 2026 / WebGPU creative-coding frontier** ‚Äî "What's New in Three.js (2026): WebGPU, New Workflows & Beyond" (utsubo.com) + the WebGPU "it's the present, not the future" roundups: the live velocity-feedback-texture technique (particles write velocity to a shared field and read it back, spawning organic motion with no keyframes) and **MPM (Material Point Method)** particle+grid fluids are now browser-feasible. *Could become a prototype that:* a kids fluid where the flow's behaviour encodes the current chord. (Banked direction; not built this cycle.)
- **Collaborative touchscreen music** ‚Äî **RoboJam** (Martin, Glette, Nygaard, T√∏rresen) + **ToCoPlay** + early-childhood "group synchrony / joint attention" literature: side-by-side music-making raises musical+social bonding. ‚Üí seeded the banked `692-kids-two-friends` (two-voice interval consonance + voice-leading resolution).
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** an off-glass, eyes-off kids piece where *how the child moves their body* (tilt + motion + mic energy) is the only control, steering a continuous major‚Üîminor mode-morph ‚Äî calm = sun, wild = storm. ‚Üí built this cycle as **`690-kids-weather-hands`**. The off-glass framing also directly attacks JURY 2026-06-16 #2 (13/15 are screen renders).
**Freshness honesty:** the embodied-perception literature is foundational (not a <30-day drop), so the winner claims **#5 only loosely**; its honest floor is **#2 (‚â•3 input subsystems fused) + #3 (named refs: embodied music-cognition / Reggio Emilia; Eno generative ambient)** = 2‚Äì3/5. The three.js-2026 + RoboJam findings ARE current (2026) and seed the two banked siblings.
