## ¬ß455 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 455, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **The live "real-time audio-reactive AI art" stack (TouchDesigner + StreamDiffusion) is the contemporary frontier of image-gen-inside-AV ‚Äî and the surprising move is to invert its per-frame reflex into a slow, long-form piece that dreams ONE deliberate image per multi-minute movement and KEEPS them as memory.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches + an arxiv probe):**
- Derivative (TouchDesigner) community tutorial **#216 ‚Äî "TouchDesigner & StreamDiffusion: Real-Time Audio-Reactive AI Art"** (derivative.ca) + the **2025 ACM IMX** paper *"An AI-driven Music Visualization System for Generating Meaningful Audio-Responsive Visuals in Real-Time"* (dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3706370.3727869). The state of the art: img2img latent diffusion (StreamDiffusion) driven *per frame* by audio features isolated into frequency bands (kick/snare/bass band-energy ‚Üí noise/movement parameters), so the picture re-renders in lockstep with the beat.
- Context: WebGPU is now "the present, not the future" for browser creative-coding (webgpuexperts.com May-2025 roundup, lisyarus particle sims) ‚Äî the substrate for porting these AV pipelines into the browser exists.
**Why it's surprising / the inversion:** every reference treats AI-image as a *reactive surface* ‚Äî fast, per-frame, disposable, in lockstep. Resonance's vibe is immersive/contemplative/personal, the opposite of a strobing reactive surface. The hook: don't chase per-frame paid diffusion in the browser ‚Äî go the OTHER way. **Could become a prototype that:** runs a slow generative journey and at each multi-minute *movement* boundary mints ONE deliberate flux/schnell image as a remembered "chapter," accumulating a navigable 3D gallery of its own past ‚Äî long-form *memory* rather than reactive *surface*. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `689-dream-chapters`** (the slow inverse of StreamDiffusion). Two adjacent hooks banked: the audio‚Üíimage‚Üí**audio** closed loop with color-feedback (`687-latent-oracle`) and the **re-sonified image-as-instrument** (`688-spectral-seance`, Ikeda/UPIC/ANS lineage). All three keep a procedural fallback so they run with zero network/key.

---

## ¬ß454 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 454, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **A fresh (<30-day) chord-generation paper turns out to describe, almost line for line, the architecture of a child-driven reharmonizer. The jury's named kids local-minimum is the "pentatonic / no-wrong-notes" crutch, and ¬ß452 already found the inverse ‚Äî give full chromatic freedom and let the HARMONY chase the note. This cycle's dive found the *engineering* of that idea shipped this month: He/Li/Sun/Huang decompose chord generation into RETRIEVE ‚Üí EDIT ‚Üí RERANK. That is exactly the loop a "no wrong note, ever" kids instrument needs: the child's tapped pitch class is a hard constraint; RETRIEVE candidate chords that could contain or warmly frame it; EDIT/score by music-theoretic fit (chord-tone > extension > clash penalty); RERANK by minimal voice-leading so the pads glide rather than jump.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches + one WebFetch):**
- **He, Li, Sun & Huang ‚Äî "A Decomposed Retrieval-Edit-Rerank Framework for Chord Generation"** ‚Äî arXiv:2605.07489, **submitted 2026-05-08** (verified via arxiv.org/abs/2605.07489, ICMR '26 Amsterdam). The decomposition "establishes stylistically plausible options (retrieve), enforces music-theoretic constraints through minimal adjustments (edit), and selects among feasible candidates (rerank)," giving explicit control over the diversity‚Üîfeasibility balance vs. end-to-end models. It is symbolic-domain and not itself real-time, but the decomposition ports directly to a tiny client-side reharmonizer.
- **Supporting (older, foundational):** pitch-perception-in-children work (resolved vs. unresolved harmonics, 2025) confirms school-aged children track harmonic structure; the psychophysical "tension and resolution" basis of harmony (Helmholtz roughness; Meyer; Huron) underpins the sibling explorers. Sony CSL *FlowComposer* reharmonization (¬ß452) remains the named lineage for "harmony chases the melody."
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** a kids instrument with the FULL 12-note chromatic palette where every tap is reharmonized live ‚Äî a "wrong" note simply becomes the color of a freshly retrieved chord. ‚Üí became **`684-kids-every-note`** (`harmony.ts` implements `scoreFit` [retrieve+edit] + `voiceLeadingCost` [rerank] + a stay-bias). Chain `today's research ‚Üí today's build` is **same-day** and visible.
- **Freshness honesty:** the lead paper IS a <30-day drop (2026-05-08), so the build legitimately claims **#5 (recent-research, last 14 days bind is comfortably met)** alongside #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named ref). The two sibling explorers (685 mode-glide, 686 suspension) rest on foundational refs only (Cooke; Bach/Helmholtz/Meyer/Huron) ‚Üí honest 2/5 each; 684 is the 3/5 winner.

## ¬ß453 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 453, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **The lab's real-world-data-sonification thread is deep but lopsided: it has sonified the SKY (`618-solar-organ`, live NOAA space weather), the EARTH (`233-earth-pulse`, `613-seismic-choir`), a historic CHESS game (`649-chess-duet`), and the live WIKIPEDIA edit firehose (`437-wiki-pulse`) ‚Äî but never the one real-time signal that is most purely a portrait of *collective human emotion*: the live financial market. And the technical unlock is a transport the lab has literally never opened ‚Äî a `WebSocket` (grep-clean: zero `new WebSocket` / `wss://` across all ~680 prototypes; every prior live feed used one-shot `fetch` or SSE/`EventSource`). A keyless public trade firehose (Binance `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade`) streams every executed trade ‚Äî price, size, and crucially the *aggressor side* (buyer- vs seller-initiated) ‚Äî tens of times a second, a real-time index of the world's fear and greed. The surprising reframe for a meditation app: don't render a trading screen ‚Äî render the market as WEATHER you witness (a tide, a nebula, a data-rain), where collective buy-pressure warms toward consonance and sell-pressure darkens toward a tension that wants to resolve.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, four WebSearches):**
- **Brian Foo ‚Äî "Data-Driven DJ" / "Two Trains: Sonification of Income Inequality on the NYC Subway"** (datadrivendj.com) ‚Äî the canonical named reference for *data ‚Üí music as witness*: the quantity and dynamics of a track's instruments correspond to the underlying data (median household income per subway stop). The browser-feasible reduction here: map live trade aggressor-side ‚Üí chord color, trade size ‚Üí bell register/loudness, rolling momentum ‚Üí consonance‚Üîtension, volatility ‚Üí grain/foam density, over an always-present drifting modal bed.
- **Ryoji Ikeda ‚Äî "test pattern" / "data.matrix"** ‚Äî the pure-sonification-of-data aesthetic (data as surgical white-on-black spectacle) ‚Äî the named anchor for the austere/clinical approach of the DEEP fan-out (`685-ledger-lines`).
- **Wikimedia EventStreams + the broad 2026 WebGPU-immersive-web data-art front** (verified active this year) confirm live-stream ‚Üí audio-visual is a current, living practice ‚Äî but also that the lab's own coverage (solar/seismic/chess/wiki) leaves the **market firehose** conspicuously un-built, and the **WebSocket transport** entirely un-used.
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** an ADULT, long-form, stateful meditation where a live market WebSocket drives a never-repeating weather system ‚Äî momentum bends the harmony between tension and release over minutes, so the piece is genuinely different at minute 5 than minute 0. ‚Üí **DEEP target this fire** (3 approaches: `683-world-pulse` WebGPU data-nebula / cosmic-transcendent ¬∑ `684-market-tide` Canvas2D breathing sea / tender-oceanic ¬∑ `685-ledger-lines` Canvas2D Ikeda data-field / clinical), each with a **synthetic-market fallback** so a silent 06:30 glance is alive even when the socket is blocked.
- **Honest caveat:** the named refs are foundational (Data-Driven DJ ~2016; Ikeda *test pattern* ~2008) and no clean <30-day frontier paper anchors the music-from-markets idea ‚Äî so **#5 (recent-research) is NOT claimed** on freshness. The dive's role is the research-first rule's **path (a)**: it audited the lab's data-sonification coverage, found the market firehose + WebSocket as the genuine gaps, and bent the build there. Ambition rests honestly on **#1 (never-used technique: first `WebSocket` live-stream input, grep-verified)** + **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems)** + **#3 (named refs: Brian Foo ¬∑ Ryoji Ikeda ¬∑ Refik Anadol)**.

## ¬ß452 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 452, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The jury's named kids local-minimum is the "pentatonic / no-wrong-notes" crutch (6√ó of the last 15 kids pieces) ‚Äî and the literature points at the *inverse* of that crutch. The usual trick prevents wrong notes by RESTRICTING the scale (five safe pads). But the deeper musical truth is that consonance is CONTEXTUAL ‚Äî a single note is neither right nor wrong on its own; its quality depends entirely on the chord beneath it. So the bigger move for a child instrument is not "remove the wrong notes" but "give full chromatic freedom and let the HARMONY chase the note" ‚Äî real-time reharmonization, the practice automated by Sony CSL's FlowComposer (reharmonize a melody "in the style of Bill Evans / Michel Legrand"). The other firm ground for a 4yo (carried from ¬ß448/¬ß450): they perceive *closure and its denial* (cadence) and *change itself* well before they map minor‚Üísad (~age 7).**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, three WebSearches):**
- **Sony CSL *FlowComposer* / automatic reharmonization lineage** ‚Äî corpus/style-based reharmonization of a fixed melody (the canonical demo: "Yesterday" reharmonized in the style of Bill Evans / Michel Legrand). The browser-feasible reduction: a small palette of lush candidate chords (diatonic + borrowed colors), each tapped pitch scored for fit, the best-fitting chord chosen and voice-led under the note in real time. The principle it operationalizes ‚Äî *consonance is contextual* ‚Äî is the directest theoretical answer to the jury's "let a kid meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary."
- **"Music and Rhythm as Promising Tools to Assess and Improve Cognitive Development in Children: A Scoping Review" (Acta Paediatrica 2025, PMC12420879)** ‚Äî 23 studies; music/rhythm engagement positively affects executive function, attention, self-regulation. Supports the embodied/sensorimotor framing (rocking, tilting) over button-pressing for a young child, and the developmental caution that *active embodied participation* drives the benefit.
- **Carried-forward anchor (¬ß448/¬ß450, `[older, foundational]`):** 3‚Äì4yos don't reliably map minor‚Üísad until ~age 7‚Äì8; the perceptible harmonic events at that age are (a) *change itself / a moving root* and (b) *closure & its denial* (cadence). ‚Üí ordered curation: cadence (680) sits on firmer 4yo ground than the chromatic-reharmonizer's subtler payload (681) and far firmer than the mode-glide (682, which is therefore framed 6+/family).
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** (a) a kids instrument with **full chromatic freedom where the band reharmonizes underneath to make every note consonant** ‚Äî the literal inverse of the pentatonic crutch (‚Üí built as `681-kids-every-note`, banked winner-adjacent); (b) an **off-glass** lullaby where rocking the tablet drives a **deceptive‚Üíauthentic cadence** so the child *feels* a musical sentence finish (‚Üí **shipped `680-kids-night-boat`**); (c) a 6+/family **continuous major‚Üîminor mode-glide** (‚Üí `682-kids-two-moons`, banked).
- **Honest caveat:** no clean <30-day frontier drop surfaced for the kids-harmony register this cycle (FlowComposer is foundational ~2016; the scoping review is 2025; the developmental anchor is foundational) ‚Äî so **#5 is NOT claimed** on freshness. The dive's role is the research-first rule's **path (a)/(c)**: it bent the fire away from the banned scale-snap toward *embracing* the wrong note (reharmonization) and toward the developmentally-firm cadence, and it consumes the recent ¬ß450 banked kids seeds (676‚Üí680, 674‚Üí682).

## ¬ß451 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 451, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **The lab's single biggest untouched first, named by THREE consecutive juries, is the *embodied-spatial / room-presence* piece ‚Äî and the research front says the payload of such a piece is not the visual but the *spatialization itself*: placing voices in 3D around the listener via HRTF/panning measurably raises the felt SENSE OF PRESENCE. The surprising move is to make where-you-stand-in-the-room literally place the sound in the room ‚Äî body POSITION ‚Üí sound POSITION (binaural PannerNode field) ‚Äî so the instrument you play is the acoustic geometry of the space, not a control on the glass.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **arXiv:2601.22082 (Jan 2026) ‚Äî "Auditorily Embodied Conversational Agents: Effects of Spatialization and Situated Audio Cues on Presence and Social Perception."** Finding: spatializing an agent's sounds (binaural placement) + situated Foley that reflects the agent's position in the environment measurably increases the user's sense of presence and social perception. The generalizable principle: *spatial placement of sound is itself the immersion mechanism*, not a garnish on top of one.
- **arXiv:2412.05486 (Dec 2024) ‚Äî "A Scene Representation for Online Spatial Sonification"** ‚Äî builds an incremental 3D scene from depth sensing and collapses it into a compact 360¬∞ auditory representation in real time. The browser-feasible analogue: read the body's position from a camera (no depth sensor needed) and sonify it as a 360¬∞ field around the listener. Reinforced by **"Sounding Bodies" (arXiv:2311.06285)** ‚Äî modeling 3D spatial sound from body pose ‚Äî and the **IRCAM/ISMM** movement-*qualities* sonification lineage already cited in ¬ß442.
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** an ADULT, off-the-couch **room-presence** instrument ‚Äî step back from the screen and your body in the camera frame conducts a *spatial* ensemble: each voice is placed via Web-Audio `PannerNode` (HRTF) at the screen-x / screen-y / implied-depth of where you (or your limbs) are, so moving through the room sweeps the ensemble around you; presence/spread/motion drive swell, register and reverb. The spatialization IS the instrument. ‚Üí **DEEP target this fire** (3 approaches to body-sensing: MediaPipe Pose skeleton, pure camera frame-diff motion-field, MediaPipe selfie-segmentation silhouette), realizing the long-banked `442-body-orchestra` seed.
- **Honest caveat:** the spatialization‚Üípresence principle is from a Jan-2026 paper (~5 months, NOT a <30-day frontier drop) and the depth-sonification paper is Dec-2024 ‚Äî so **#5 is NOT hard-claimed** on freshness. Its role is the research-first rule's **path (a)/(c)**: the dive directly bends the build (spatialization is the payload, not the viz) AND it consumes the banked `442-body-orchestra` seed, while landing the jury's #1 standing untouched ask. **#1 (never-used technique) NOT claimed via MediaPipe** ‚Äî the lab has used MediaPipe pose/hands/segmentation many times on the KIDS side (217/287/295/419/444/582/617). The genuinely-open frontier is the *register*: an ADULT installation / room-presence piece with **spatialization-as-instrument** has 0√ó builds. Ambition rests honestly on #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs: the spatialization-presence paper + Myron Krueger *Videoplace* + Refik Anadol room-scale presence works).

## ¬ß449 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 449, ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **The tender / generative-ambient register the jury named as the un-built adult corner has a live browser lineage that is *technique-specific*, not vibe-specific: the interesting generative-ambient engines obey real voice-leading ‚Äî minimize each voice's motion, omit minor-9ths, glide rather than jump ‚Äî so the harmony "breathes" instead of stepping. The surprising bit isn't "make it pretty"; it's that the smoothness is a *constraint solver* (nearest-tone assignment) you can hear, and that constraint is exactly what separates a tender music-box from a pad-chord arpeggiator.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch):**
- **Alex Bainter ‚Äî generative.fm + "Making Generative Music in the Browser"** (medium.com/@alexbainter) and the broader Web-Audio generative-music practice: a long-running, actively-maintained (2026) body of browser generative-ambient work. One project in the search explicitly "tries to obey voice-leading rules, omitting minor ninths, and minimizes movements of each voice to maintain smoothness and consistency‚Ä¶ gradually mutating components and shifting into different keys" ‚Äî the precise technique anchor for a tender piece that is *more* than a random arpeggio. Reinforced by **Wotja** (2026, live generative ambient/MIDI) and **SoundTools granular** (browser ambient textures), and the foundational lineage of **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports*, *Reflection*).
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** a tender 3am music-box where the harmony is a tension-weighted random walk over diatonic chords and, on each change, an *exhaustive nearest-tone 4-voice assignment* (minimize total semitone motion, penalize doublings) makes every voice **portamento** to its target ‚Äî and the renderer (inline SVG) draws the voice-leading threads so you SEE the harmony move as you hear it glide. ‚Üí **shipped this cycle as `671-glasswork`** (the realization of the long-banked `660-glasswork` seed).
- **Honest caveat:** generative-ambient voice-leading is **foundational, not a <30-day frontier drop** ‚Äî tagged `[older, foundational]`. Its role here is the research-first rule's **path (c)**: the build consumes an existing IDEAS seed (`660-glasswork`) that itself came from prior research, AND it is the directest hit on the freshest JURY's explicitly-named adult ask (#5: "freeze groove/EDM; the un-built adult register is now tender/intimate ‚Äî go to the banked 660-glasswork"). The visible research‚Üíbuild chain this cycle is: dive (voice-leading is the *technique* that makes tender ‚âÝ arpeggiator) ‚Üí the three explorers' lead ‚Üí 671 ships it. **#1 NOT claimed** ‚Äî generative voice-leading is worked territory in and outside the lab.

## ¬ß446 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 446, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **The directest answer to the lab's most-named local minimum (the pentatonic "no-wrong-notes" crutch, 6√ó of the last 15 kids pieces) is a living interactive *tuning* surface: let a child grab a note and BEND it across a real non-Western scale, hearing it go "wrong" and settle back. The surprising bit isn't the scale ‚Äî it's that the bending IS the lesson: out-of-tune isn't scary, it's expressive.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Ableton's "Tune a Gamelan Yourself" interactive Sundanese-gamelan tuner** (`tuning.ableton.com/sundanese-gamelan/`) ‚Äî a living, hands-on resource where you flip between Javanese *slendro* and *p√©log laras* and **manually drag each note's pitch** to hear how a gamelan is tuned by ear (gamelan tunings are famously NOT equal-tempered and vary per ensemble). This is the cleanest existing demonstration that *a tuning is something you feel and adjust*, not a fixed grid ‚Äî the exact frame the jury asked for ("a non-Western tuning they can audibly bend‚Ä¶ meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary"). Reinforced by the 31edo slendro analysis (`31edo.com/slendrogamelan.pdf`) and microtonaltheory.com for real cent values.
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** a kids metallophone where each glowing bronze bar is grabbed and dragged to bend its pitch ¬±300 cents across slendro/p√©log, with a big toggle that re-tunes the whole instrument + drone between the two *laras* ‚Äî and the signature bronze *beating shimmer* slows as a bar settles back into tune, so the ear is taught consonance‚Üîdissonance pre-verbally. ‚Üí **shipped this cycle as `662-kids-gamelan-bend`** (path (a): research ‚Üí build, same fire).
- **Honest caveat:** the gamelan tuner is a *living reference resource*, not a <30-day arXiv drop, so **#5 is NOT hard-claimed** ‚Äî its role is path (a): it directly seeds the build. The genuinely lab-first piece is the synthesis technique (inharmonic modal bronze, never used in the lab), not the tuning system (the lab has BP / JI / Lydian kids toys already ‚Äî 288/286/280 ‚Äî but never *gamelan* and never *bend-into-and-out-of-tune as the mechanic*).

## ¬ß445 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 445, ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **The live front in interactive audio is *causal, real-time reactive accompaniment that follows a live performer* ‚Äî but a corpus grep KILLED it as a build target (the lab already has it twice: `256-live-duet-harmonist` does live jazz comping + walking bass to your chords, `251-live-duet-trader` trades melodic fours), which redirected the cycle to the genuinely-open adult registers the jury names: the *ecstatic/danceable missing middle* (0√ó SHIPPED on the adult side ‚Äî every ecstatic candidate gets banked and never lands) and *tender*. The surprising move isn't a new ML model ‚Äî it's that the most-cutting-edge research (LiveBand's strictly-causal, never-sees-the-future accompaniment stream) generalizes to a hand-built aesthetic: a process that continuously responds and NEVER resets ‚Äî realized here as a euphoric EDM arc that climbs forever and never lands.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Causal real-time reactive accompaniment is the live 2026 cs.SD front:** *"LiveBand: Live Accompaniment Generation in the Audio Domain"* (**arXiv 2606.03803, June 2026**) ‚Äî a causal transformer in a pretrained causal audio-autoencoder latent space generates accompaniment from only the *causally available* mix context + noise, no future frames; *"Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance"* (**arXiv 2604.07612, April 2026**) ‚Äî a latent-diffusion accompanist responding to a live context-audio stream via MAX/MSP‚ÜîPython over OSC, consistency-distilled to 5.4√ó faster for real-time. Shared idea: **strictly causal, never-landing, continuously-generated music that reacts to a live input** (the Lyria RealTime / "Live Music Models" paradigm, RESEARCH ¬ß443).
- **Browser creative-coding context (secondary):** ShaderVine (WGSL/WebGPU shader toolkit w/ genetic shader evolution) + Noisedeck (browser WebGPU/WebGL2 shader video-synth) confirm the browser GPU-audio-visual stack is mature ‚Äî but WebGPU is jury-BANNED (6√ó) so this only reinforced WebGL2 as the scarce-but-capable renderer.
- **Honest caveats:** LiveBand (June 2026) IS within the freshness window, but it is a **server-side ML codec-LM**, so **#5 is NOT hard-claimed** ‚Äî the dive's role is path (a)/(c): it names the paradigm (continuous causal never-landing stream) and bends the WIDE fire's flagship toward a never-landing euphoric arc. And critically, the headline reactive-accompaniment hook was **killed by a grep** (251/256) before it became a brief.

**Why surprising / the corpus check that drove the build:** reactive accompaniment *looked* like a clean adult build target ‚Äî until a grep returned **`251-live-duet-trader`** (melodic trading-fours to your mic) and **`256-live-duet-harmonist`** (live jazz comping + rootless/drop-2 voicings + walking bass locked to your rhythm). That is the ¬ß437/¬ß441/¬ß444 "grep before claiming 0√ó" discipline again: the headline research hook was already a 2-deep lane. What the grep + the JURY (2026-06-15) surfaced as genuinely open: the **ecstatic register is 0√ó SHIPPED** on the adult side ‚Äî `650-endless-lift` (cycle 441) and `653-endless-lift` (cycle 443) were both *banked and abandoned*, exactly the "missing middle nobody builds" the jury flags ‚Äî and it doubles as **Karel's explicit care #4** ("EDM build-and-drop ‚Ä¶ as alternate journey arcs," still 0√ó). **could become a prototype that does X (this cycle's WIDE fire):** a euphoric **EDM build-and-drop journey arc** whose energy *never lands* ‚Äî riser ‚Üí impact ‚Üí drop ‚Üí climb ‚Üí riser, with a **sidechain pump** that is both audible (bass/pad duck on the kick) and *visible* (the cathedral-nave brightness dips-and-blooms), and a **Shepard-folded bass** so the key reads as forever-rising (the LiveBand "never-landing causal stream," hand-built). Attacked WIDE via two more orthogonal open registers ‚Äî **`660-glasswork`** (tender 3am music-box, SVG, Eno/Budd/Satie) and **`661-murmur-engine`** (boids murmuration AS the music, Canvas2D, Reynolds/Vicsek). Chain `dive ‚Üí build`: the reactive-accompaniment headline failed the grep; its surviving value (the *continuous never-landing causal stream* frame) + the jury's "ship the ecstatic register you keep banking" set the winner.

## ¬ß444 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 444, KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **The live front in embodied audio is the *cast shadow / silhouette* as a whole-body instrument ‚Äî but a corpus check KILLED that hook as a build target (the lab already has three shadow pieces + a gamelan piece), which redirected the cycle to the genuinely-unmined material axis: the kids side has never once been built on Karel's REAL recorded piano, even though the adult side's *granular-on-his-Paths* piece is loved. So the surprising move isn't a new sensor ‚Äî it's bringing a loved adult technique (granular resynthesis of his real recordings) to the kids side for the first time, steered by the body (tilt).**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Shadow-play / silhouette interaction is a current installation front:** *Shadowplay* (Design Research Works ‚Äî interactive silhouette installation touring Shambala / Light Up Lancaster 2024 / EMF Camp, AI image stream controlled by bodies, shadows, objects); *Embodied Visions: Interactive Installations That Reimagine Bodily Presence ‚Ä¶ as Shadows* (**SIGGRAPH Asia 2024 Art Papers**, dl.acm.org/10.1145/3680530.3695436); *Shadow Play* (Burning Man 2026, light-tower silhouette choreography). The shared idea: the visitor's **cast silhouette/contour** (not a skeleton, not motion-energy) is the controller ‚Äî a body-shape instrument.
- **Embodied musical ML / co-creative continuation lineage:** *Understanding Musical Predictions With an Embodied Interface for Musical Machine Learning* (Martin & Torresen, **IMPS** ‚Äî a body-gesture interface that *predicts and continues* the performer's musical gesture in real time, low-latency so response feels simultaneous); *AI-Powered Music Generation from Sequential Motion Signals (LSTM)* (2025). The frame: **the body's movement shapes a living generative response** ‚Äî agency through low-latency embodied control.
- **Honest caveats:** the shadow installations are 2024‚Äì2026 art/exhibition pieces (not <30-day arXiv); IMPS is foundational, not new ‚Üí **#5 NOT hard-claimed.** And critically, **the shadow hook was killed by a grep** before it became a brief.

**Why surprising / the corpus check that drove the build:** the shadow/silhouette lane *looked* like a clean kids #1 ‚Äî until a grep of the tree returned **`268-kids-shadow-theater`, `513-kids-shadow-still`, `617-kids-shadow-monster`** (three shadow pieces already) plus **`463-terra-gamelan`** (so a wayang+gamelan piece would be a 4th-in-lane). That is exactly the "grep before claiming 0√ó" discipline the ¬ß437/¬ß441 dives re-confirmed ‚Äî the headline research hook was the *wrong* build target. What the grep DID surface as genuinely 0√ó: **no kids prototype has ever used `/api/audio` (Karel's real recorded piano)** ‚Äî every kids piece synthesizes its own sound, even `645-kids-piano-creatures` (MIDI synth, not his recording) ‚Äî while the adult side's `227-paths-granular` (granular on his Paths) is one of Karel's loved pieces, and his standing directive is explicit: "incorporate Karel's actual music from the Paths ‚Ä¶ let his existing music be the input." **could become a prototype that does X (this cycle's DEEP fire):** a kids garden whose living voice IS Dad's actual recorded piano ‚Äî **granular-resynthesized** (loved `227` technique, first time for kids) and **played by the body via tilt** (the embodied-control frame from the IMPS lineage, path-(a)-adjacent: the child's movement shapes a living generative response from real material), every grain pentatonic-quantized so there are no wrong notes. Attacked DEEP via two interaction models ‚Äî **`657-kids-piano-garden`** (tilt = a *wind* that scatters his piano into singing fireflies) and **`658-kids-piano-garden`** (tilt = roll a glowing *seed* that blooms his piano into singing flowers). Chain `dive ‚Üí build`: the dive's shadow headline failed the corpus check; its surviving value (embodied control + the grep's "his-real-music is 0√ó for kids") set the build.

## ¬ß443 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 443, ADULT, WIDE ‚Äî 3 jury-named open adult lanes) ‚Äî **The 2026 frontier in music isn't a better one-shot generator, it's the *continuous, live-steerable stream*: Google DeepMind's Lyria RealTime / "Live Music Models" generate music as an unbroken causal flow that never stops and bends in real time to a handful of controls. That paradigm ‚Äî a piece that is *always playing and always listening to one steering input* ‚Äî is the conceptual parent of an ecstatic groove that perpetually rises, and the lab's browser-native, no-network, rule-based ancestor of it is a Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler driven by a single live lever.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, four WebSearches):**
- **Google DeepMind ‚Äî Lyria RealTime / "Live Music Models"** (arXiv **2508.04651**; **Lyria 3** released **2026-02-18**, MarkTechPost; magenta.withgoogle.com/lyria-realtime). A generative model that produces a *continuous* stream of music in real time via **causal streaming** ‚Äî audio is generated in contiguous ~2-second chunks under a ~10-second sliding-window context, "as a function of both user control inputs and past audio output." Live steering of **tempo, density, harmonic context, and timbre** with low latency; designed for professional *live* performance, not offline render. The surprising part for us: the unit of the art has moved from "a track you generate" to "**a flow you steer while it plays**" ‚Äî there is no start/stop, only continuous evolution under control.
- **Honest date caveat:** Lyria 3 is ~4 mo and the Live-Music-Models paper is Aug-2025; both are server-side codec-LM systems ‚Üí **#5 NOT hard-claimed.** Research-first satisfied via paths (a)+(c): the dive names the *continuous-causal-steered-stream* paradigm and bends today's WIDE fire toward it; the lab-feasible ancestor is a **look-ahead scheduler + rule-based arrangement state** steered by one live control, no DNN, no network.
- **Secondary (TouchDesigner feed, for the off-screen lane):** Bileam Tschepe (elekktronaut) is currently teaching **infinite feedback loops** (Cache-TOP-in-a-feedback-loop "repeat motion forever"; POP Copy/Texture-Lookup/Particle/Feedback) ‚Äî the same *perpetual-recirculation* idea as a Shepard fold, in the visual domain. Reinforces the cycle's through-line: **perpetual, never-resetting processes** as the aesthetic.

**Why surprising / the chain `dive ‚Üí build`:** every recent "live music" demo still has a play button and a stop button; Lyria RealTime's bet is that the *interesting* object is a stream with neither ‚Äî always sounding, always bending to control, never landing. That reframes the jury's most-named open adult corner (the **ecstatic missing middle**) as not "a euphoric track" but "a euphoric *ascent that never arrives*." **could become a prototype that does X (this cycle's hooks ‚Üí 3-way WIDE fan-out):** (1) **653-endless-lift** ‚Äî a gospel-house groove that modulates up forever, every voice Shepard-folded so the key climbs without leaving the band, steered live by one PRAISE lever (the browser ancestor of Lyria's "density/harmonic-context" controls); (2) **654-latent-chain** ‚Äî the lab's still-0√ó 2-model AI pipeline CHAIN (audio‚Üíimage‚Üíwords‚Üíimage), Karel's most-wanted "AI image INSIDE an AV piece," resurrected with the chain auto-firing on Start so it's *visible*; (3) **655-breath-sanctuary** ‚Äî the jury's demanded 3rd off-screen/embodied piece: an eyes-closed JI overtone ritual whose continuous causal flow is steered by your *breath* (mic RMS), a continuous-stream meditation rather than a track. All three are "continuous, steered, never-landing" flows ‚Äî the Lyria paradigm in three registers.

## ¬ß441 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 441, ADULT, WIDE ‚Äî 3 jury-named open adult lanes) ‚Äî **Browser generative-composition is a live 2026 practice, and a corpus check against the jury's "0√ó" provocations re-confirmed the ¬ß437 lesson: grep the tree before claiming a lane is empty. Two of the jury's named-0√ó lanes (MIDI/OSC-OUT, WebRTC) are already occupied ‚Äî so the genuinely-0√ó frontiers this cycle are the AI 2-model pipeline CHAIN and structured-data-as-counterpoint with a NEW machine.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **Browser Web-MIDI generative sequencers, shipping now:** **Loopstate** (loopstate.net ‚Äî a free in-browser modular generative MIDI sequencer that sends polymetric/Euclidean/arpeggio/chord/generative patterns to any hardware synth or DAW via Web MIDI, no install); **Riffer** ("Infinity" mode ‚Äî a generative sequencer that constantly evolves the riff each loop, built for live performance, listed among 2026's best); **KOSMOS** (RP2040 generative MIDI sequencer, Euclid/Step-Random/Hybrid + per-step probability + USB-MIDI out, MATRIXSYNTH Mar-2026); Cre8audio **Programm** (Apr-2026, 12-track generative MIDI/CV sequencer). The take-home: *browser ‚Üí hardware generative MIDI is a live 2026 live-performance practice, not a novelty.*
- **Algorithmic-EDM lineage:** **GEDMAS** (Generative Electronic Dance Music Algorithmic System) + **GESMI** (Generative Electronica Statistical Modeling Instrument ‚Äî probabilistic / first-order Markov models generating EDM song-form, chord progressions, melodies, rhythms). The named reference frame for any generative-dance-music engine.
- **arXiv 2603.00576 ‚Äî *SMDIM*** (Feb 28 2026, ~3.5 mo). Symbolic music generation combining efficient global structure construction + light local refinement via **structured state-space models** to capture long-range musical context at near-linear cost ‚Äî the 2026 frontier of *structured* (not loop) symbolic composition.
- **Honest date caveat:** SMDIM is ~3.5 mo (current quarter, not <30 days) + server-side ‚Üí **#5 NOT hard-claimed.** Research-first satisfied via paths (a)+(c): the dive confirmed structured-generative composition as a live front AND drove the **corpus check** that re-shaped the build's ambition.

**Why surprising / the corpus check that drove the build:** the jury (2026-06-15) provocations #3 + #5 name "genuinely 0√ó" lanes to reach for ‚Äî **MIDI/OSC live-performance OUT** and **multi-user/WebRTC** ("a clean #1+#2"). A grep KILLED both, exactly as STATE ¬ß437 killed the jury's prior "MIDI/EDM-drop 0√ó" claims: **`291-harmonograph` already sends MIDI OUT** (`out.send([status, midi, vel])` to external gear), and **`508-accord-call` is a real WebRTC peer-to-peer** piece. So no clean "first MIDI-OUT / first WebRTC" #1 was available. The 0√ó lanes that survived the grep: the **AI 2-model pipeline CHAIN** (audio‚Üíimage‚Üíwords‚Üíimage ‚Äî flagged 0√ó in IDEAS ¬ß427/¬ß433, the lab's repeatedly-named unmined AI frontier, and Karel's most-wanted "AI image INSIDE an AV piece" directive) and **structured-data-as-counterpoint with a genuinely NEW machine** (jury provocation #5's blessed "a live chess game as a duet" ‚Äî distinct from the jury-BANNED 588/613/618 physical-feed sonification template). **could become a prototype that does X (this cycle's hooks ‚Üí builds):** (1) a famous chess game sonified as strict two-voice counterpoint where the game's *structure and events* are the score ‚Üí **`649-chess-duet`** (WINNER, the Immortal Game 1851); (2) the first real 2-model AI chain that narrates its own latent walk ‚Üí `648-latent-chain` (banked, the boldest swing, resurrect with real-`FAL_KEY` verification); (3) the ecstatic missing-middle via a Shepard-folded gospel key-lift ‚Üí `650-endless-lift` (banked). Chain `dive ‚Üí build` is direct: the corpus check named the clean 0√ó frontiers; the WIDE fire shipped the most-verifiable of them.

## ¬ß440 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 440, KIDS, WIDE ‚Äî 3 registers of the jury's "active-wonder middle") ‚Äî **Real-time human‚ÜîAI musical CO-PERFORMANCE is a live 2026 cs.SD front, and its browser-feasible *rule-based* ancestor is exactly what makes a 4-year-old sound like a musician: the child plays, an "agent" answers in real time so there are no wrong notes.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **"Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP"** ‚Äî arXiv **2604.07612** (Apr 2026, ~2 mo). A latent-diffusion model generates instrumental accompaniment in response to a *live* stream of context audio, formulated as a **sliding-window look-ahead protocol** where latency is the binding constraint (consistency distillation buys a 5.4√ó speedup to reach real-time). The key idea for us is architectural, not the DNN: *responsive accompaniment = a look-ahead scheduler that listens to what the performer just did and fills the supporting voices a beat ahead.*
- **"A Design Space for Live Music Agents"** ‚Äî arXiv **2602.05064** (Feb 2026, ~4 mo). A taxonomy of systems that respond to a live performer in real time ‚Äî the conceptual frame for "the invisible band that plays along."
**Honest date caveat:** both are server-side / older-than-30-days, so **#5 is NOT hard-claimed** ‚Äî they're conceptual grounding for a build whose ambition is carried by **#1 (a never-used input on the kids side ‚Äî Web MIDI)** + #2. The lab-feasible version is a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler + rule-based auto-harmony / walking bass / energy-scaled groove** snapped to a pentatonic so every input is consonant.
**Could become a prototype that does X (this cycle's hook ‚Üí build):** a kids instrument where the child **PLAYS** (Web MIDI keyboard / computer keys ‚Äî the lab's first kids MIDI) and a real-time generative **accompaniment agent** answers ‚Äî no wrong notes, an invisible band that builds as they play. Chain `research ‚Üí build` is direct: the dive named the look-ahead-accompaniment architecture; **`645-kids-piano-creatures`** implements its browser-feasible rule-based ancestor. (The WIDE fire also explored the same "active-wonder middle" via camera-conducting [646] and mic-rhythm-parade [647] ‚Äî banked.)

## ¬ß439 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 439, ADULT, DEEP ‚Äî a TRUE **cycle-3** of the `606`‚Üí`630` piano-decomposition spine) ‚Äî **The 2026 source-separation frontier has moved past *masking* (filter the original spectrum) toward *restoration / reconstruction* (re-synthesize a clean source) ‚Äî which is exactly the axis along which `630-piano-refract` can be deepened: stop only masking Karel's piano into register bands, and start RECONSTRUCTING it per pitch-class into something playable/re-voiceable.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, three WebSearches + one WebFetch):**
- **arXiv 2603.16926 ‚Äî *Music Source Restoration with Ensemble Separation and Targeted Reconstruction*** (submitted **2026-03-13**, ~3 months ‚Äî current-quarter, not <30 days; tagged accordingly). Two-stage pipeline: an ensemble of separators makes initial source estimates, then **BSRNN restoration models refine them through *targeted reconstruction*** that reverses production degradations (EQ, compression, reverb). The reframe that drives this cycle: *masking-only separation leaves degraded stems; a reconstruction stage recovers a clean source.* `606` (HPSS mask) and `630` (NMF Wiener mask) are both pure masking ‚Äî the cycle-3 move is to add the **reconstruction stage**: re-synthesize each isolated note from clean tones (a "purity" lever from his real masked timbre ‚Üí fully re-spoken additive resynthesis).
- **arXiv 2409.06513 ‚Äî *Sines, Transient, Noise Neural Modeling of Piano Notes*** (Sep 2024, foundational). A piano note decomposed into sines + transient + noise components ‚Äî the classic spectral-modeling-synthesis (Serra & Smith) decomposition, and the template for the additive-resynthesis reconstruction of a single pitch class.
- Foundational technique anchors that DRIVE the build: **Smaragdis & Brown 2003** (supervised/seeded NMF for music transcription ‚Äî fix the basis to per-note templates), **Fujishima 1999** (chroma / pitch-class profiles), **Fitzgerald 2010** (median-filter HPSS ‚Äî the `606` ground), **Klapuri** (multiple-F0 salience).
- **Honest date caveat:** the freshest *directly-relevant* paper this dive surfaced is 2026-03-13 (~3 months), not within 30 days ‚Äî three searches did not surface a <30-day per-note-isolation/resynthesis paper. So **#5 is NOT claimed.** Research-first is satisfied via path **(b)**: this cycle CONTINUES a multi-cycle ambitious build (the genuine cycle-3 of the `606`‚Üí`630` spine), and the restoration paper supplies the *direction* of the deepening (reconstruction beyond masking), not a fresh idea.

**Why surprising / chain to build:** the jury (2026-06-15) is mechanical and loud ‚Äî "two juries, zero 4/5s, because every banked cycle-2 deepening gets abandoned for a fresh 3/5; finish one for real." `630-piano-refract` (cycle 434) was the adult cycle-2 of the jury-praised `606`, and `630`'s own README explicitly banks the cycle-3: *"run supervised NMF with a fixed basis learned from a clean note dictionary so each voice maps to **true pitch classes** rather than register bands."* That banked deepening, plus the 2026 restoration reframe (mask ‚Üí reconstruct), is the cycle-3. **could become a prototype that does X:** Karel's real solo piano, decomposed past register bands into its 12 actual NOTE-COLORS ‚Äî solo/mute a single pitch class of his own playing, and slide a "purity" lever that re-speaks his exact performance from pure tones. The recording stops being one block and becomes a chord, then an instrument, made of his own touch. ‚Üí **today's build (cycle 439, DEEP cycle-3 of `606`‚Üí`630`).**

## ¬ß438 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 438, KIDS, DEEP ‚Äî a TRUE cycle-2 of `624-kids-day-meadow`) ‚Äî **The 2026 frontier for long-form coherent music generation has independently converged on EXACTLY the data structure `624-kids-day-meadow` invented procedurally for kids: a stored bank of realized motifs that is *retrieved and re-voiced* (not looped, not drifting) as the piece evolves ‚Äî which validates the cycle-2 bet that the way to deepen 624 is to make that motif memory *playable*, not to add more decoration.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, three WebSearches):**
- **arXiv 2509.21714 ‚Äî *MusicWeaver: Composer-Style Structural Editing and Minute-Scale Coherent Music Generation*** (rev. ~Jan 29 2026). Its **Motif Memory Retrieval (MMR)** module "stores compact embeddings of realized motifs and **retrieves them when motifs recur, enabling consistent returns with controllable variation rather than unstable drift or rigid copying**." This is a near-exact conceptual mirror of 624's motif-memory bank (`voiceDegree` re-voices each stored motif into the day's *current* scale; `mutatedDegrees` ages it) ‚Äî independent 2026 confirmation that "remembered-and-re-voiced motifs" is the right architecture for minute-scale coherence, and the principled frame for the cycle-2 deepening: **let the child PLAY the retrieval** (a tilt-steered wind that sweeps across planted things brushes them so they ring in passing order, and that swept gesture becomes a traveling motif the engine remembers and re-voices) ‚Äî a procedural, embodied MMR for a 4-year-old.
- **arXiv 2512.17228 ‚Äî *LUMIA: A Handheld Vision-to-Music System for Real-Time, Embodied Composition*** (Dec 2025). A handheld device turns embodied motion into real-time composition ‚Äî "repositioning generative music as an improvisational practice driven by contextual, sensory engagement." Grounds the tilt-as-instrument move: the iPad held like a tray and *tilted* is the handheld embodied controller, honoring the kids design mandate (sensorimotor, no buttons) and the jury's touch ban.
- Foundational frame (kids design): Reggio Emilia sensorimotor pedagogy + Toshio Iwai *Electroplankton* (2005) ‚Äî musical understanding develops through embodied movement (tilt/sweep), not point-and-click; every gesture is musically valid.
- **Honest date caveat:** MusicWeaver is a server-side diffusion-transformer (not browser-feasible) and ~4‚Äì9 months old depending on revision; LUMIA is ~6 months old. Neither is *implemented* ‚Äî they are conceptual grounding/validation. So **#5 is NOT hard-claimed.** The build's research-first satisfaction is via path (b): it CONTINUES a multi-cycle ambitious build (the genuine cycle-2 of the jury-named kids standout 624), and the dive confirms the deepening direction (playable motif-retrieval over more decoration) rather than driving a fresh idea.

**Why surprising / chain to build:** the jury (2026-06-15) provocation #1 is mechanical and loud ‚Äî "two juries, zero 4/5s, because every banked cycle-2 deepening gets abandoned for a fresh 3/5; finish one for real." Cycle 434 did that on the ADULT side (606‚Üí630). The KIDS standout the jury named ("624 ‚Äî the kids side's one structural reach, a stateful diurnal arc, genuinely different at minute 8 than minute 1") has never had a cycle-2 ‚Äî and it is *built around a motif-memory bank* that the 2026 literature (MMR) independently arrived at. So the cycle-2 isn't theater: it deepens a jury-praised piece by making its core mechanism (the remembered, re-voiced garden) into the *interaction* itself, while swapping touch-to-plant ‚Üí **tilt** (honoring jury provocation #4's touch ban, and pulled by Karel's loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è). **could become a prototype that does X:** a 4-year-old tilts the iPad to steer a glowing wind across their day-meadow ‚Äî the wind plants where it lingers and *plays the garden like a wind-harp* where it sweeps, and those swept gestures are remembered and re-voiced into the current phase, so the child composes by tending. ‚Üí **today's build (cycle 438, DEEP cycle-2 of 624).**

## ¬ß437 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 437, ADULT, WIDE ‚Äî the missing MIDDLE register) ‚Äî **Groove "humanization" is a live 2026 cs.SD front: the contemporary framing is an *expressive drum grid* (per-hit timing deviation + strength) rendered into human-feeling audio ‚Äî which is exactly the browser-feasible thing a microtiming-deviation engine + a grid-vs-actual visualizer makes legible.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **arXiv 2605.10281 ‚Äî *Drum Synthesis from Expressive Drum Grids via Neural Audio Codecs*** (May 2026, ~1 month old). Frames humanization as: a symbolic front-end emits a coarse/quantized drum grid ‚Üí a renderer produces human-like audio with **microtiming, dynamics, and articulation-dependent timbre**. The "expressive drum grid encoding timing deviations and hit strength" IS the data structure this cycle's tender-groove builder implements procedurally (per-voice timing offset + velocity); the cycle-2 deepening hook is *articulation-dependent timbre* (a hit's tone changes with how late/hard it lands).
- **`rolypoly~`** (HAL hal-03015476) ‚Äî a live-performance drum machine that **adapts its microtiming** to a human duet partner (LSTM/Seq2Seq on Magenta's Groove MIDI), able to **morph a rhythm from straight ‚Üí swing** in real time. The browser-feasible ancestor: a global "feel/looseness" lever scaling per-voice offsets from robotic ‚Üí in-the-pocket ‚Üí too-loose.
- **ClaveNet** (ACM 3678299) ‚Äî generating **Afro-Cuban** drum patterns from the clave timeline; grounds the afro/highlife polyrhythm builder.
- Foundational refs that DRIVE the builds: *Does it Swing? Microtiming Deviations and Swing Feeling in Jazz* (arXiv 1904.03442 / Sci. Rep. 2019) ‚Äî bass-vs-drums discrepancy = "swing"; Dan Charnas, *Dilla Time* (2022) ‚Äî the off-grid pocket as a compositional system.
- **Honest date caveat:** 2605.10281 is server-side neural-codec ML (not browser-feasible) and the swing/Dilla refs are foundational ‚Üí **#5 NOT hard-claimable**; the dive is grounding + names the cycle-2 deepening (articulation-dependent timbre, the expressive-grid-as-interface).

**Why surprising / chain to build:** the jury (2026-06-15) named the missing register as "the whole middle ‚Äî ecstatic, danceable, groovy, tender, sensual; nobody is building it," but the only middle entries are `387-drop-engine` (EDM-drop, cycle 341) + `632-polymeter-loom` (hypnotic Reich/Euclidean, cycle 435) ‚Äî and BOTH jury-claimed "0√ó clean lanes" (MIDI, multi-user/WebRTC) turned out occupied (Web MIDI 12 files; `508-accord-call` WebRTC). The 2026 literature says the *mechanism* that produces "groove/feel" is measurable microtiming deviation ‚Äî so the missing corners (tender/neo-soul, ecstatic/gospel-house, joyful/afro-polyrhythm) are each buildable via a *specific* timing/harmony mechanism, not a vague vibe. **could become a prototype that does X:** a neo-soul groove whose laid-back snare / pushed hats are made VISIBLE as a grid-vs-actual onset plot (the expressive drum grid, legible) ‚Äî shipped this cycle as `637-slow-burn`.

---

## ¬ß434 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 434, ADULT, DEEP ‚Äî a TRUE cycle-2) ‚Äî **NMF source-separation is NOT a dusty 1999 technique: it's a live 2026 front (deep non-negative autoencoders for sound *deconstruction + remixing*), and piano is the textbook-ideal subject ‚Äî which makes warm-started NMF on Karel's own recording both fresh-grounded AND the principled fix for the abstract-components weakness that lost the original `607-piano-prism` seed.**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **arXiv 2510.08816 ‚Äî *Audible networks: deconstructing and manipulating sounds with Deep Non-Negative Autoencoders* (Oct 2025).** Extends classic NMF into **deep non-negative autoencoders** for hierarchical sound deconstruction ‚Äî explicitly framed around *taking a sound apart into interpretable parts you can then manipulate/remix*. This is the contemporary frontier of exactly this build's question, and its literal **cycle-3 deepening path**: replace the shallow warm-started NMF with a deep NAE for cleaner, hierarchical register/note separation.
- **Nick Collins, *Live and Non-Real-Time Source Separation Effects for Electroacoustic Music*, eContact! 16.3 (CEC).** Reports that **piano is "particularly amenable to high quality separation, with rather beautiful extraction of key-hammer onsets from the tonal body,"** and that median-filter HPSS is real-time-feasible (a 7-frame median at 1024 hop ‚âà 160 ms latency). Ground-truth that 606's HPSS strings/hammers split ‚Äî the legible *ground* this cycle-2 builds the NMF on ‚Äî is the right primitive, and that piano is the ideal source.
- **Smaragdis & Brown (ISMIR 2003) + Lee & Seung (Nature 1999)** ‚Äî foundational: NMF decomposes a music magnitude spectrogram into `W` (spectral bases) √ó `H` (temporal activations); a **seeded/supervised basis** can lock components onto musically-meaningful structure rather than random clusters. The build applies seeded-basis NMF for *legibility* (stable register voices) rather than transcription ‚Äî the decisive curation differentiator over the random-init sibling.
- *Honest date caveat:* the deep-NAE paper (2510.08816) is ~8 months old and a server-side ML model ‚Äî **not** browser-feasible ‚Äî so it is the contemporary *reference*, not the implemented technique; **#5 NOT hard-claimed.** The build ships the browser-feasible classical ancestor (warm-started KL-NMF on the HPSS harmonic layer). The lab-fresh things are the **first NMF in the lab** (grep-0√ó) and the **genuine cycle-2 deepening** (the structural move two juries say the lab never finishes).
- **could become a prototype:** refract Karel's own solo piano like light through a prism ‚Äî HPSS (606) splits strings vs. hammers, then **NMF fans the strings into 4 stable pitched register voices** (warm-started so they're nameable, not the random-init lottery) that you solo/mute/re-mix instantly. The **first honest cycle-2** in the lab. ‚Üí **today's build (cycle 434, DEEP winner `630-piano-refract`).**

## ¬ß433 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 433, ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **real-time browser binaural / HRTF spatial audio is a live 2026 cs.SD front, and the lab's *off-screen / audio-FIRST* lane is still 0√ó after 600+ builds (only the screen-led `576-presence-room` ever touched binaural) ‚Äî exactly the gap the jury names "every fortnight."**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **arXiv 2601.12950 ‚Äî *ImmersiveFlow: Stereo-to-7.1.4 spatial audio generation with flow matching* (Jan 2026).** Synthesizes spatial/binaural audio by modeling HRTFs; the contemporary reference for "place sound sources around a listener." The browser-feasible ancestor: Web Audio `PannerNode { panningModel:"HRTF" }` + a rotating `AudioListener` ‚Äî true binaural localization, no ML, no asset.
- **arXiv 2508.10924 ‚Äî *ASAudio: A Survey of Advanced Spatial Audio Research* (2025‚Äì26).** Frames the HRTF as a subject-specific filter encoding the binaural cues for 3D perception; confirms the generic-head-model caveat (front/back confusion without personalization) ‚Äî a known limitation to disclose, not a bug.
- **Cross-modal audio‚Üívisual front (relevant to the banked 627):** **Sound2Vision** (audio‚Üívisuals via cross-modal latent alignment) + **Art2Mus** (arXiv 2602.17599, Feb 2026) ‚Äî the live cross-modal lane; but a curate-time corpus check found the lab already has `441-latent-listening-room` + `448-piano-phrase-painter` in exactly that single-model audio‚Üîimage lane, so the unmined frontier is a real 2+ model CHAIN, not a third single-model piece (627 banked, flagged duplicative).
- *Honest date caveat:* the spatial-audio papers (2601/2508) are heavy DNNs and ~4‚Äì10 months old ‚Äî the HRTF/`PannerNode` method is foundational, so **#5 NOT hard-claimed**; the dive is enabling-context + lane-grounding. The lab-fresh thing is the **off-screen / audio-FIRST register** + the **facing-reward mechanic**, not the binaural primitive (576 already did head-tracked binaural ‚Äî said plainly).
- **could become a prototype:** an **eyes-closed, off-screen** piece ‚Äî headphones, near-black screen, invisible presences orbiting you in real 3D binaural sound; you TURN (phone compass / drag) and the presence you FACE brightens and sings while the others recede. Finally gives the perennially-0√ó off-screen/embodied-spatial lane its second real entry beyond 576 ‚Äî **consumes the ¬ß429-banked `620-empty-room` seed**. ‚Üí **today's build (cycle 433, WIDE winner `626-empty-room`).**

## ¬ß432 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 432, KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **long-form coherence is the live 2026 cs.SD problem: keeping a generated piece *structured and non-drifting* over many minutes ‚Äî exactly the gap the lab's kids set has never crossed (every kids "evolving" toy is really a short loop or a one-shot growth, not a stateful multi-minute arc).**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle):**
- **arXiv 2604.05343 ‚Äî *Anchored Cyclic Generation: A Novel Paradigm for Long-Sequence Symbolic Music Generation* (April 2026).** Keeps long autoregressive generation coherent by re-using **anchor features from already-generated music** to guide what comes next, "mitigating error accumulation." The directly-portable idea: a **motif-anchor / memory bank** that re-voices earlier material into the present so a long piece coheres instead of drifting into noise.
- **arXiv 2509.21714 ‚Äî *MusicWeaver: Composer-Style Structural Editing and Minute-Scale Coherent Music Generation* (Jan 2026).** Two-stage: predict a **structured plan**, then render audio conditioned on it; a global path captures long-range progression, a local path the detail. Maps cleanly to a **plan-then-render** arc: a high-level phase plan (the shape of a day) drives moment-to-moment notes.
- **Eno lineage (foundational):** Brian Eno's generative music (wind-chimes ‚Üí SSEYO Koan, 1995; *Music for Airports* 1978; *Bloom* with Peter Chilvers 2008; Gary Hustwit's generative film *ENO*, 2024 ‚Äî "different every screening"). Music as a *system* that is ever-different and never repeats.
- *Honest date caveat:* the 2026 papers are heavy diffusion/transformer DNNs (~2‚Äì5 months, server-side), **not** browser-feasible ‚Äî they are the contemporary *reference* for the problem, not an implementable model. The build implements their **browser-feasible classical ancestor** (an Eno-style procedural generative engine with a motif-anchor memory bank + a phase "plan"), so **#5 NOT hard-claimed**; the engine + memory idea is the lab-fresh thing.
- **could become a prototype:** a kids piece that is **a whole DAY** ‚Äî a ~8‚Äì10 min generative journey arcing dawn‚Üíday‚Üídusk‚Üínight‚Üídawn, where a 4-year-old plants persistent living things that wake/sleep/age with the time of day and a motif-memory engine re-voices them into the current phase's harmony, so the piece is *genuinely different at minute 8 than minute 1*. The lab's first kids **long-form generative journey-arc with state/memory** (and a kids-register answer to Karel's standing "journey-engine alternatives" priority ‚Äî a cinematic/diurnal arc, not the psychedelic 6-phase one). ‚Üí **today's build (cycle 432, DEEP).**
