## Research cycle ‚Äî Cycle 203 (2026-05-26)

### ¬ß219 ‚Äî AI Harmonizer: Real-Time Vocal SATB Harmonization (NIME 2025, Jun 2025)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.18143 ‚Äî AI Harmonizer: Expanding Vocal Expression with a
Generative Neurosymbolic Music AI System. Blanchard, Holt, Paradiso. NIME 2025, Canberra.

Real-time vocal harmonization: mic ‚Üí Basic Pitch (open-source voice-to-MIDI, no external API) ‚Üí
Anticipatory Music Transformer ‚Üí four-part SATB choral harmony generated without prior harmonic
input from the user. "Anticipatory" model pre-generates the next bars so harmonies feel responsive.
Choral texture: soprano + alto + tenor + bass, all auto-voiced to avoid voice crossing.

Key for Resonance: a zero-dep browser version is achievable with autocorrelation pitch detection
(already in sandbox) + three OscillatorNodes tuned to +4 / +7 / -12 semitones from detected pitch.
The SATB-style HRTF spatialization (soprano upper-left, alto upper-right, bass below) makes the
result feel choral rather than just chord-stacking. Four colored orbs arranged in a semicircle ‚Äî
user at center bottom ‚Äî visualize the spatial formation.

**Could become prototype**: `vocal-choir` ‚Äî you sing a pitch; three harmony voices appear around you
in 3D space. Zero deps, zero API, one cycle. Aligns with `148-spatial-palette` ‚ù§Ô∏è love (spatial
synthesis). First "choir" prototype in the sandbox. [Date: Jun 2025, NIME 2025 proceedings]

### ¬ß220 ‚Äî NeoLightning: 3D Gesture Sound Design via MediaPipe (ICMC 2025, May 2025)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.10686 ‚Äî NeoLightning: A Modern Reimagination of Gesture-Based
Sound Design. ICMC 2025, May 2025.

Modernizes Don Buchla's Lightning (1990s IR gesture instrument) using MediaPipe Hands for 3D hand
skeleton at 30fps, <30ms latency. Key insight: 3D hand pose as a 6-DOF control surface ‚Äî not just
X/Y but finger curl, wrist angle, hand velocity, palm spread. Bilateral asymmetry (left hand = one
parameter set, right = another) enables continuous simultaneous control of independent parameters.
"Depth-as-reverb" (hand scale ‚Üí camera Z estimate ‚Üí reverb decay) is highly intuitive.

New vs. the `31-gesture-music` spec already in IDEAS.md: add depth-as-reverb mapping and
bilateral asymmetry. Left wrist Y ‚Üí bass drone pitch; right wrist Y ‚Üí melody pitch; left palm Z ‚Üí
reverb decay; right palm Z ‚Üí harmonic count; both wrist distance ‚Üí stereo spread.

**Updates `31-gesture-music` spec**. Confirms MediaPipe CDN approach is right. [Date: May 2025, ICMC]

### ¬ß221 ‚Äî Structure-Aware Piano Accompaniment via Style Plan (arxiv 2602.15074, Feb 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.15074 ‚Äî Structure-Aware Piano Accompaniment via Style Planning
and Dataset-Aligned Pattern Retrieval. Feb 16, 2026.

Lightweight transformer predicts per-measure style plan (sparse/dense, legato/staccato,
arpeggiated/block-chord) conditioned on section structure + functional harmony. Retriever selects
human-performed patterns from a corpus and reharmonizes them. The style plan ‚Äî a sequence of symbols
like [sparse, legato] ‚Üí [dense, arpeggiated] ‚Äî is interpretable and visualizable as a timeline.

Resonance relevance: the style plan VISUALIZATION is the novel part. A horizontal timeline strip
showing "what kind of playing happened here" per section gives pianists structural feedback. Nothing
like this exists in the sandbox ‚Äî all existing prototypes show signal (FFT, pitch, tempo) but none
analyze *compositional character* per section.

**Could become prototype**: `score-structure` ‚Äî mic ‚Üí chord detection + density analysis ‚Üí build a
scrolling timeline grid. Each 4-bar section gets: chord label, density rating, register label.
Accumulated view shows the *architecture* of Karel's improvisation. Zero deps. [Date: Feb 2026]

### ¬ß222 ‚Äî WebSplatter: 3D Gaussian Splatting in Browser via WebGPU (arxiv 2602.03207, Feb 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.03207 ‚Äî WebSplatter: Enabling Cross-Device Efficient Gaussian
Splatting in Web Browsers via WebGPU. Feb 2026.

First WebGPU framework for real-time 3D Gaussian Splat rendering in browser. Visual quality:
objects appear as overlapping soft oriented ellipses that collectively form photorealistic scenes.
Not particles, not meshes ‚Äî oriented Gaussian densities with position/rotation/scale/color/opacity.
Demo works on Chrome 120+ desktop and mobile at 30‚Äì60fps.

The *visual language* of splatting is what matters for Resonance: soft, organic, photographic.
All 173 existing prototypes use particles (points), fluid (density fields), 3D meshes, or 2D canvas.
Oriented soft ellipses is qualitatively different ‚Äî the same technique that makes photographs look
volumetric is achievable in Canvas2D with oriented ellipses and screen-blend compositing.

**Could become prototype**: `splat-bloom` ‚Äî 500 Canvas2D oriented ellipses, audio-reactive. No
WebGPU needed; Canvas2D + transform matrix. Bass = nearby splats bloom outward; treble = rotation
drift; onset = scatter + coalesce. Zero deps, one cycle. [Date: Feb 2026, arxiv 2602.03207]

### ¬ß223 ‚Äî Voxtral Mini 4B + Web Speech API: Spoken-Word AV Control (Feb 2026)
**Source**: https://huggingface.co/spaces/mistralai/Voxtral-Realtime-WebGPU ‚Äî Voxtral Realtime WebGPU
demo. Feb 2026. Mistral AI Apache 2.0.

Voxtral Mini 4B runs entirely in browser via WebGPU+WASM (2.5GB quantized). 480ms latency.
The concept for Resonance is more important than the specific model: spoken-word control of AV
parameters. The simpler path: the browser-native Web SpeechRecognition API (zero model, zero deps,
<50ms latency on Chrome) achieves the same control loop.

Novel interaction: what if a pianist could SPEAK intent while playing? "Slower" ‚Üí BPM decreases.
"Ocean" ‚Üí visual mode shifts. "Ghost" ‚Üí theme activates. Live performance use: speak a scene name
into a lapel mic before playing each piece. This closes the gap between conscious intent (language)
and system state ‚Äî all 173 prototypes are gestural but none respond to direct language commands.

**Could become prototype**: `voice-scene` ‚Äî Web Speech API ‚Üí spoken scene token ‚Üí AV mode switch.
8 mode tokens (ocean / fire / ghost / rain / storm / forest / space / night), each with a distinct
visual + ambient sound. Zero deps, zero API key. Chrome-only graceful fallback. [Date: Feb 2026]

### ¬ß224 ‚Äî MUTEK Montr√©al 2026: Spha√Æra + Architectural Acoustics (announced May 2026)
**Source**: https://montreal.mutek.org/en/news/mutek-montreal-announces-full-2026-lineup ‚Äî
MUTEK Montr√©al 2026 full lineup. Aug 25‚Äì30, 2026.

Key for Resonance: **Spha√Æra** (Sara Persico + Mika Oki) ‚Äî draws from acoustic properties of Oscar
Niemeyer's dome in Tripoli. Voice + architecture + light feedback loop: the dome's resonant modes
shape the music; the music shapes the projected light. Core concept: the *room is an instrument*.
Also: No√©mi B√ºchi (Exuvie) ‚Äî new electroacoustic + immersive spatial sound; drone aesthetic
with architectural resonance. Confirms the "architectural drone" aesthetic has traction at the
highest level of the AV art world.

**Could become prototype**: `sdf-cave` ‚Äî SDF ray-marching WebGL fragment shader renders a cave-like
interior space. Bass = walls pulse; treble = ceiling deforms; centroid = color temperature. User
is *inside* a space that responds to what they play. First prototype in sandbox where the viewer is
inside the visual space rather than observing it. Zero new deps (inline GLSL). [Date: May 2026 ann.]

### ¬ß225 ‚Äî Revision 2026 Shader Showdown: SDF smin Audio-Reactive (Apr 2026)
**Source**: https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ffXSRj ‚Äî Revision 2026 Shader Showdown 2. Apr 8, 2026.

Premier demoscene demoparty. 2026 Shader Showdown shaders on Shadertoy feature real-time SDF
ray-marching with smooth-min (smin) blending ‚Äî distance field primitives melt into each other
rather than hard-intersecting. Audio-reactive uniforms drive the blend factor and noise displacement.
Visual: surreal architectural spaces and organic forms, dark palette.

Key technique: `smin(a, b, k)` where k is driven by bass energy ‚Üí shapes melt together on bass
hits and separate on silence. Creates organic rhythmic morphing that no particle/fluid simulation
produces. Combined with domain repetition (infinite cave tunnels) and Blinn-Phong lighting in a
deep-violet palette = visually stunning interior environment.

**Also seeds `sdf-cave`** alongside ¬ß224. Fragment-shader-only: just `<canvas>` + inline GLSL
string, zero external deps. [Date: Revision demoparty, Apr 2026]

### ¬ß226 ‚Äî Real-Time Gesture Control via MediaPipe Body Landmarks (arxiv 2504.19460, Apr 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.19460 ‚Äî A Real-Time Gesture-Based Control Framework. Apr 2026.

MediaPipe PoseLandmarker (33 body joints at 30fps) ‚Üí musical parameter control. Key finding:
relative joint distances normalize better for live performance than absolute positions. Wrist-to-wrist
distance, elbow-to-hip distance ‚Äî these don't change with where you stand on stage. Velocity
estimates (EMA of joint position deltas) give "gesture expressiveness" without gesture recognition.

Non-linear mapping insight: small movements = fine control; large movements = dramatic effect.
Achieved via a cubic curve: `param = movement¬≥` for the low range + linear saturation above 0.7.

**Updates `body-conductor` spec**: use relative joint distances instead of absolute Y. Add velocity
as arousal estimator (fast movement ‚Üí higher BPM + harmonic density). [Date: Apr 2026, arxiv 2504.19460]

- NeoLightning (¬ß220, ICMC May 2025) ‚Äî 3D gesture synthesis, depth-as-reverb. Updates `gesture-music`.
- Structure-Aware Piano Accompaniment (¬ß221, Feb 2026) ‚Äî style plan visualization. Seeds `score-structure`.

- Gesture-Based Control Framework (¬ß226, Apr 2026) ‚Äî relative joint distances. Updates `body-conductor`.

## 2026-05-27 ‚Äî Cycle 213 research sweep

### ¬ß227 ‚Äî Stable Audio 3 (arxiv 2605.17991, May 18, 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.17991

Stability AI's next-gen latent diffusion family (small/medium/large) for variable-length audio
generation and editing. Key advances: semantic-acoustic autoencoder for compact latent representation,
adversarial post-training for fast inference ‚Äî generates several minutes of audio in under 2s on an
H200, a few seconds on a MacBook M4. Supports inpainting (targeted audio editing) and continuation
(extend a short recording). Small + medium weights publicly released for consumer hardware.

**Impact for dream zone**: upgrade path for `43-stable-extend` when Stable Audio 3 appears on fal.ai.
Small model could also run locally on Karel's M4 Mac ‚Äî first truly offline AI audio extension. Also
seeds a new **live-extend** interaction: record a phrase, model extends it in <5s (vs. 10‚Äì30s for SA
2.5), enabling a near-real-time call-and-response feel. [Date: May 2026, arxiv 2605.17991]

---

### ¬ß228 ‚Äî Music of Changing Lines: I-Ching as Real-Time Music Generator (arxiv 2605.20386, May 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.20386 ‚Äî presented at ICMC 2026.

Traditional Wen Wang Fa coin casting ritual (three coins thrown six times ‚Üí hexagram) fed into
Gemini LLM for contextual interpretation, then Lyria real-time music generation. The ritual act IS
the music input ‚Äî not a typed prompt, not mic, not MIDI. Each of the 64 I-Ching hexagrams generates
unique music. "Controlled randomness as sacred input": you cast to discover what the music wants
to be. First published system connecting ceremonial gesture to AI music at ICMC.

**Could become prototype**: `ritual-generate` ‚Äî six rounds of virtual coin throws (tap screen 3
times per round, coin results shown as solid/broken lines), 6 rounds builds a hexagram. The hexagram
number (1‚Äì64) maps to one of Resonance's Ghost journey themes + an ambient music prompt sent to
Lyria 3 Pro. Canvas shows the hexagram symbol building line by line with ink-brush animation. After
6 casts: "Generate" ‚Üí 30s ambient piece plays through live-bloom visualizer. Six casts ‚Üí six-line
hexagram ‚Üí one meditation. Ceremonial, non-Western, genuinely surprising.
Needs GEMINI_API_KEY (same key already planned). One-cycle build. [Date: May 2026, arxiv 2605.20386]

---

### ¬ß229 ‚Äî PianoFlow: Streaming Bimanual Piano Motion Synthesis (arxiv 2604.12856, April 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12856

Flow-matching architecture for real-time bimanual piano hand motion synthesis from audio. 9√ó
faster than prior SOTA; autoregressive flow continuation enables streaming for arbitrarily long
pieces. Uses MIDI as a privileged training modality (better motion realism) while remaining
audio-only at inference. Output: continuous skeletal hand/finger positions synchronized to audio.

**Could become prototype**: `piano-motion` ‚Äî load Karel's piano track via `/api/audio/[id]`,
run offline pitch detection (autocorrelation, same as `13-piano-canvas`) to extract note events.
Animate simplified top-down piano hands on Canvas2D: each note event moves the appropriate hand
(left=below C4, right=C4+) to the correct key position, with a brief "finger press" animation
(mild scale + subtle drop shadow). No ML needed ‚Äî rule-based hand animation derived from pitch data.
Hands follow smooth spring-interpolated trajectories between notes. "Watch your music being played."
First prototype that visualizes the ACT of piano performance rather than the audio output. Zero deps.
Incorporates Karel's real music (AGENT.md directive). One-cycle build. [Date: Apr 2026, arxiv 2604.12856]

---

### ¬ß230 ‚Äî SAMUeL: 15M-Param Real-Time Vocal Accompaniment (arxiv 2507.19991, Jul 2025)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.19991

Ultra-compact (15M params, 220√ó smaller than SOTA) latent diffusion model for vocal-conditioned
accompaniment. Soft alignment attention mechanism adapts between local and global temporal
dependencies per diffusion timestep ‚Äî enables 52√ó faster inference, real-time on consumer hardware.
Take vocal input ‚Üí generate synchronized musical accompaniment.

**Note for queue**: `44-vocal-bgm` already queued uses ACE-Step (one-shot, ~5‚Äì10s generation).
SAMUeL's key advantage is **streaming real-time**: vocal phrase ‚Üí accompaniment starts in <0.5s.
No public fal.ai or WASM port confirmed. If SAMUeL releases a fal.ai endpoint, upgrade `44-vocal-bgm`
to use it for substantially lower latency. Monitor for fal.ai listing. [Date: Jul 2025, arxiv 2507.19991]

---

### ¬ß231 ‚Äî LUMIA: Handheld Vision-to-Music Composition (arxiv 2512.17228, Dec 2025)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.17228 ‚Äî NeurIPS 2025 Creative AI track.

Camera-based "compose through looking" system: photograph a scene ‚Üí GPT-4V interprets the visual
content ‚Üí structured music prompt ‚Üí Stable Audio generates a loopable segment. Users layer multiple
segments from different camera angles; user-selected instrumentation guides each generation.
"An improvisational practice driven by contextual, sensory engagement." Published Dec 2025.

**Could become prototype**: `camera-compose` ‚Äî webcam snapshot button ‚Üí Gemini Flash vision API
(describe scene in ‚â§40 words) ‚Üí Lyria 3 Pro prompt ("ambient [description], 60 BPM, minimal,
contemplative") ‚Üí 30s ambient piece plays through live-bloom radial visualizer. Simple UI: large
"üì∑ Take snapshot" button, generated scene description shown in secondary text, waveform player.
"Take a photo. Hear its music." Admin-only gate (GEMINI_API_KEY, same key). Zero new npm deps.
One-cycle build. Unique in sandbox: all 181 prior prototypes use mic or demo tones ‚Äî this is the
first that reads the visual world. [Date: Dec 2025, arxiv 2512.17228]

## 2026-05-29 ‚Äî Cycle 233 research note

### ¬ß234 ‚Äî DEMON: Real-Time Diffusion-Based Expressive Music Instrument
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28657 (May 27, 2026)

DEMON (Diffusion-based Expressive Music instrument cONtrol) is a real-time playable instrument
that maps arbitrary high-dimensional parameter vectors to generated audio via a latent diffusion
model trained specifically for low-latency synthesis. Key property: parameters don't map to
individual knobs ‚Äî they form *layers* that propagate through a hierarchy, so moving one control
reshapes dozens of synthesis dimensions simultaneously. The authors demo it as a playable live
instrument at <100ms latency. Training corpus: diverse timbres; any sound in the latent space is
reachable by interpolating parameter vectors.

**Could become a prototype**: `param-layer` ‚Äî a zero-dep browser instrument where 4 concentric
ring controls (each a drag-ring, not a slider) broadcast their values as a parameter vector
through a 4-layer harmonic synthesis graph. Outer ring = fundamental / "mass"; next ring = odd/even
harmonic balance; next = inharmonicity (stretch factor); inner ring = amplitude envelope shape.
Each ring influences *all* layers below it, mimicking DEMON's hierarchical propagation. No
diffusion model needed ‚Äî the same perceptual effect (one gesture, global timbre reshape) can be
approximated with the harmonic-series engine built in `200-harmonic-series`. Zero deps, zero API,
one cycle scope.

**Also seeds**: `membrane-drum` ‚Äî a 2D finite-difference wave equation drumhead. A circular
membrane whose tension, damping, and strike position are controlled by the same concentric ring
UI. Tapping anywhere on the drum surface strikes it; the wave propagates outward visible on canvas.
Physically accurate overtone ratios (not integer ‚Äî real drums are inharmonic like bells). Zero deps.

Key findings from Cycle 233 (2026-05-29) ‚Äî research note (brief, build cycle):
- DEMON (¬ß234, May 2026) ‚Äî hierarchical parameter propagation instrument. Seeds `param-layer` and `membrane-drum`.

## 2026-05-30 ‚Äî Cycle 247 research sweep

### ¬ß235 ‚Äî DiscoForcing: Streaming Audio-Driven Full-Body Character Animation (ICML 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28491 (May 27, 2026)

DiscoForcing generates full-body skeleton animations synchronized to music in real time. A causal music
encoder captures rhythmic structure and phase dynamics; a diffusion-forcing sequence model with
heterogeneous noise levels handles streaming. Strictly causal, bounded-latency ‚Äî handles abrupt tempo
shifts and audio changes that defeat offline systems. Accepted ICML 2026. EchoAvatar (¬ß236) extends
the idea to 3D + LLM intent.

**Browser adaptation**: `dance-avatar` ‚Äî a 12-joint spring-physics skeleton (head, shoulders√ó2,
elbows√ó2, hands√ó2, hips, knees√ó2, feet√ó2) animated by FFT bands. No ML, no CDN. Bass ‚Üí hip sway
amplitude; treble ‚Üí arm elevation angle; onset ‚Üí upward joint velocity impulse; spectral centroid ‚Üí
forward/backward lean. Joints as `{pos, vel, restPos}` objects; per-frame `pos += vel; vel += k(rest-pos) - damping*vel`.
Canvas2D glow-line skeleton on black background. "Your music finds a body." Paradigm gap: 213 existing
prototypes, none animate a human figure. Live-performance fitness: project on stage next to the pianist.
Zero deps, one cycle. [Date: May 27, 2026, ICML 2026, arXiv:2605.28491]

---

### ¬ß236 ‚Äî EchoAvatar: 3D Character Motion from Audio with LLM Intent (arXiv:2605.28272, May 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28272 (May 27, 2026)

Synthesizes high-fidelity 3D character motion from audio with an LLM integration layer that bridges
reactive animation (frame-by-frame) with intent-driven behavior (long-horizon goal). Designed for
interactive avatar deployment (games, virtual performances). Not browser-feasible as an ML model.

**Note for queue**: confirms ¬ß235's core claim ‚Äî audio-driven character animation is a live frontier
(two simultaneous ICML-accepted papers). The intent/goal modeling layer from EchoAvatar is something
the `33-aria-companion` Markov chain approximates at the musical level. Future: combine DiscoForcing's
motion style with Aria's turn-taking structure for a "Ghost dancer" that responds to your playing phrase
by phrase rather than frame by frame. [Date: May 27, 2026, arXiv:2605.28272]

---

### ¬ß237 ‚Äî V2M-Zero: Zero-Pair Video-to-Music Generation (arXiv:2603.11042, Mar/May 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.11042 (March 2026, updated May 2026)

Generates music from video without any paired video-music training data. Key technique: match temporal
structure within modalities (video rhythm ‚Üî music rhythm) rather than cross-modal alignment, avoiding
paired-data scarcity. State-of-the-art video-music synchronization on benchmarks.

**Could become prototype**: `optical-flow-music` ‚Äî inverts V2M-Zero's direction: webcam optical flow
(frame differencing, Canvas2D, no MediaPipe) ‚Üí synthesis parameters. Extract total motion magnitude,
horizontal bias (left/right flow asymmetry), vertical bias (up/down). Map: magnitude ‚Üí filter cutoff
+ arpeggiation speed; horizontal ‚Üí pitch glide (C3‚ÄìC5); vertical ‚Üí reverb depth. Show webcam at 40%
opacity with flow arrows drawn as glowing gradient lines. "Dance in front of the camera ‚Äî movement IS
the music." First prototype using optical flow synthesis without a CDN dep. Different from `31-gesture-music`
(MediaPipe hand landmarks) ‚Äî this is pure pixel math. Zero deps, one cycle.
[Date: March/May 2026, arXiv:2603.11042]

---

### ¬ß238 ‚Äî BEAT: Uniform Beat-Based Tokenization for Symbolic Music Generation (arXiv:2604.19532, April 2026)
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19532 (April 2026, submitted May 2026)

Proposes beat-based tokenization where each beat is one fixed-length token rather than a variable-length
event sequence. Uniform time progression enables better long-range pattern capture and structural coherence
in music generation. Outperforms event-based baselines on accompaniment generation tasks.

**Note for queue**: no browser API yet; server-side LM. Foundational insight: beat-quantized representation
is computationally superior for structural coherence. Informs design of future `beat-looper` prototype ‚Äî
a zero-dep browser beat grid where each column = one beat, user draws patterns, system plays them
beat-quantized. Not the same as `48-tap-rhythm` (which detects free-tapped rhythm) ‚Äî this would be
a structured grid with explicit beat identity. One-cycle build candidate. [Date: April 2026, arXiv:2604.19532]

Seedance 2.0 (ByteDance) is the top-usage video model on Replicate with 438.5K runs. Accepts multimodal

### ¬ß241 ‚Äî FM Synthesis: Foundational Browser Gap in the Dream Sandbox (synthesis research note, 2026-05-30)
**Source**: Web Audio API spec + synthesis literature (John Chowning 1973; Yamaha DX7 1983); no external URL.

FM (frequency modulation) synthesis: output = carrier_freq + sin(modulator_phase) √ó FM_index √ó modulator_freq.
Implemented in Web Audio with two `OscillatorNode`s and one `AudioParam.connect()`:

```js
const carrier = actx.createOscillator();
const modulator = actx.createOscillator();
const modGain = actx.createGain();   // FM index
modulator.connect(modGain);
modGain.connect(carrier.frequency);  // modulates carrier frequency
carrier.connect(actx.destination);
```

Timbral space: ratio 1:1 (same frequency) + varying index ‚Üí metallic growl; ratio 2:1 + high index ‚Üí bell
partials; ratio 3.5:1 + low index ‚Üí woody; ratio 7:4 + sweeping index ‚Üí bass FM growl (classic 808-style).
213 existing prototypes ‚Äî none use FM synthesis despite being a foundational technique (DX7 is the best-selling
synthesizer ever made). The paradigm gap is complete.

**Could become prototype**: `fm-explorer` ‚Äî Route `/dream/215-fm-explorer`. A 2D canvas: X axis = carrier
pitch (C2‚ÄìC7 log), Y axis = modulator-to-carrier ratio (0.5‚Äì8.0). Mouse/touch position determines pitch and
ratio. FM index driven by mouse distance from canvas center (or a large slider). A background color field
shows timbral complexity (spectral richness estimate: low-ratio/low-index = smooth, high-ratio/high-index =
complex). Moving across the canvas sweeps through hundreds of timbres without reading any labels. Mic mode:
RMS amplitude ‚Üí FM index (quiet ‚Üí simple tone, loud ‚Üí complex metallic). Presets: Bell (E4, ‚àö2 ratio, index 8),
Rhodes (C3, 2:1, index 3.5), Clangy (G3, 3.5:1, index 12), Sub-bass (A1, 1:1, index 2). Zero deps, zero API.
One cycle. [Date: 2026-05-30, synthesis note]

---

### ¬ß242 ‚Äî Web Audio `createPeriodicWave`: Draw-Your-Waveform Interaction (API research note, 2026-05-30)
**Source**: W3C Web Audio API spec (https://www.w3.org/TR/webaudio/#dom-baseaudiocontext-createperiodicwave)

`AudioContext.createPeriodicWave(cosineTerms: Float32Array, sineTerms: Float32Array)` creates an arbitrary
periodic waveform from Fourier coefficients, which can then be set on an `OscillatorNode` via
`OscillatorNode.setPeriodicWave()`. The waveform updates in real time ‚Äî no click, no restart needed. To
convert a user-drawn curve to Fourier coefficients: sample the curve at N=512 evenly-spaced points into
a Float32Array, compute the forward DFT (written inline in ~20 lines, or use a pre-baked Cooley-Tukey FFT),
extract the real (cosine) and imaginary (sine) parts ‚Üí `createPeriodicWave(cosTerms, sinTerms)`.

213 existing prototypes ‚Äî none use `createPeriodicWave`. Every prototype uses preset oscillator types
(`sine`, `square`, `sawtooth`, `triangle`) or audio input. No prototype lets the user sculpt the synthesis
source directly.

**Could become prototype**: `waveshape-draw` ‚Äî Route `/dream/216-waveshape-draw`. Canvas shows a 1-period
sine wave. User draws directly on the canvas (drag up/down to deform the wave). Each `pointermove`:
re-sample the drawn curve ‚Üí DFT ‚Üí `createPeriodicWave` ‚Üí `setPeriodicWave()`. Timbre changes in real time
as you draw. Secondary horizontal strip shows harmonic spectrum (bar chart of DFT magnitude bins 1‚Äì32).
Preset buttons: Sine, Square, Sawtooth, Triangle, and "Piano" (pre-loaded 32-coefficient shape approximating
a piano's harmonic series). Pitch slider C2‚ÄìC7. Mic input: RMS ‚Üí draw pressure (louder = more distortion
applied to current shape). "Draw the voice of your instrument." Paradigm inversion: all prior prototypes
react to or visualize sound; this sculpts the source. Zero deps, zero API. One cycle.
[Date: 2026-05-30, API research note]

---

### ¬ß243 ‚Äî Spring-Physics Dance Avatar: Design Sketch (design note, 2026-05-30)

Derived from ¬ß235 (DiscoForcing). A 12-joint skeleton for 2D Canvas2D:

```
Joints: head, shoulderL, shoulderR, elbowL, elbowR, handL, handR, hip, kneeL, kneeR, footL, footR
Segments: head-shoulderL, head-shoulderR, shoulderL-elbowL, elbowL-handL, shoulderR-elbowR, elbowR-handR,
          hip-shoulderL (via torso), hip-shoulderR, hip-kneeL, kneeL-footL, hip-kneeR, kneeR-footR
```

Per-frame update: `vel += (restPos - pos) * k - vel * damping; pos += vel`. Audio mappings to rest positions:
- `restPos[hip].x = center + sin(t * 1.2) * bass * 40` (bass ‚Üí hip sway amplitude)
- `restPos[handL].y = center - mid * 80; restPos[handR].y = center - mid * 80` (mid ‚Üí arm raise)
- `restPos[head].x = center + treble * 12` (treble ‚Üí head tilt)
- On onset: all `vel.y -= onset * 120` (upward impulse ‚Üí jump)
- `restPos[shoulderL].x = center - (1 + centroid * 0.3) * 45` (centroid ‚Üí posture width)

Render: each limb as a glowing line (`lineWidth=3`, `shadowBlur=12`, hue shifts with audio band ‚Äî
sub-bass=violet, bass=teal, mid=amber, treble=rose). Head as a circle, radius 18px. Black background.
Optional: draw a subtle echo of the figure 5 frames behind at 30% opacity (motion trail effect).

"Why zero deps": spring physics is 10 lines of JS. No skeletal rig library needed. The figure does not need
anatomically correct anatomy ‚Äî an abstracted Matisse-cutout silhouette, simple and expressive, is better.
One-cycle build. Route `/dream/214-dance-avatar`. [Date: 2026-05-30, design note]

---

### ¬ß244 ‚Äî Webcam Optical Flow Synthesis: Frame-Differencing Approach (design note, 2026-05-30)

Derived from ¬ß237 (V2M-Zero). No MediaPipe, no CDN dep. Pure Canvas2D pixel math:

1. `getUserMedia({ video: true })` ‚Üí `<video>` element
2. Each animation frame: draw video frame to an offscreen canvas, call `getImageData()` to get current pixels
3. Compute per-pixel delta from previous frame: `delta = |curr_gray - prev_gray|` where `gray = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`
4. Downsample to a 20√ó15 grid (8 cells per cell). Per cell: `magnitude = avg(deltas in cell)`, `dx = avg(right-half ‚àí left-half deltas)`, `dy = avg(bottom-half ‚àí top-half deltas)`
5. Global features: `totalMag = sum(magnitude) / 300`, `hBias = sum(dx) / 300` (positive = rightward flow), `vBias = sum(dy) / 300`

Web Audio synthesis:
- `totalMag` ‚Üí `filter.frequency.value = 400 + totalMag * 5600` (still=dark, moving=bright)
- `hBias` ‚Üí pitch via `oscillator.frequency.value = 220 * Math.pow(2, hBias * 2.5)` (left=down, right=up, ~¬±2.5 octaves)
- `vBias` ‚Üí `reverbGain.gain.value = Math.max(0, vBias * 2)` (downward flow = more reverb)
- `totalMag` ‚Üí arpeggiation rate: `noteInterval = Math.max(0.05, 0.8 - totalMag)` seconds per note

Display: webcam at 40% opacity. Per grid cell: a glowing gradient line from cell center in the direction
of `(dx, dy)`, length proportional to `magnitude`. 6-band spectrum bar at the bottom (same style as `1-live`).
Zero deps, zero CDN, one cycle. Route `/dream/217-optical-flow-music`. [Date: 2026-05-30, design note]

---

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 267 research dive (15-min, per Ambition mandate)

### ¬ß245 ‚Äî Earthquake Pulse Map & the silent-globe gap (showcase + USGS feed, current)

**Source:** webgpu.com showcase ‚Äî *"Earthquake Pulse Map: A Century of Seismic
Activity on a WebGL Globe"* (plots M6+ quakes 1900‚Äì2026 + a live USGS 2.5_week
feed on a three.js globe with custom GLSL shaders and binary-packed data).
Supporting: USGS real-time GeoJSON feeds
(`earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/`, public, keyless, CORS-open), and
the **"Sounds of Seismic" (SOS)** / **IRIS SeisSound** sonification tradition.

**Why it's surprising / relevant:** The visualization community has thoroughly
mined USGS seismicity as a *visual* dataset ‚Äî spinning globes, pulse maps, heat
layers ‚Äî but these globes are **silent**. Meanwhile the seismology-education
world has a deep sonification tradition (time-compress a seismogram into the
audible band), but it stays at the single-station-waveform level, not the
*global event catalog as a sequenced composition*. Nobody has joined the two:
a live globe where each plotted quake also *sounds*, sequenced in compressed
real time, with magnitude/depth/longitude mapped to pitch/timbre/pan.

**Could become a prototype that:** pulls the USGS `all_day` feed, time-compresses
24h into ~2.5 min, and plays each quake as a sounding event over a pulsing r3f
globe ‚Äî bigger = deeper boom, deeper = muffled, longitude = stereo. ‚Üí **Built
this cycle as `233-earth-pulse`.** Also seeds: a `weather-score` (NOAA/SWPC
space-weather Kp index ‚Üí drone texture), a `transit-pulse` (live flight/AIS
positions ‚Üí spatial arpeggio), and an `iss-pass` sonifier ‚Äî the whole
*real-world-data-sonification* category the lab is empty on.

- BEAT tokenization (¬ß238, April 2026) ‚Äî beat-quantized token = better structural coherence. Seeds future `beat-looper` idea.

- createPeriodicWave gap (¬ß242, API note) ‚Äî most underused Web Audio primitive. Seeds `waveshape-draw` (draw ‚Üí timbre, paradigm inversion).
- Dance avatar design (¬ß243, design note) ‚Äî 12-joint spring-physics skeleton, zero deps, one cycle. Route `/dream/214-dance-avatar`.

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 268 research dive (15-min, per mandate)

### MediaPipe Hands + three.js as a hand-conducted instrument ‚Äî and the kids gap
**Sources**: Derivative, "Hand Tracking Master Class in TouchDesigner with MediaPipe" (derivative.ca community tutorial) ¬∑ Google Research, "On-Device, Real-Time Hand Tracking with MediaPipe" (21 3D keypoints from a single frame) ¬∑ spite/clicktorelease, "Vertex displacement with a noise function using GLSL and three.js" (the canonical Perlin-noise blob shader).

What's surprising: the TouchDesigner community has fully normalized MediaPipe hand/pose landmarks as a *conducting* surface for real-time 3D visuals (the Derivative master class treats 21 hand keypoints like CHOP channels driving geometry), and the browser has the exact same primitives for free ‚Äî `@mediapipe/tasks-vision` HandLandmarker loads from jsDelivr at runtime (no build dep) and runs `detectForVideo` at 30fps on-device. Yet **the Resonance lab has never shipped a single MediaPipe prototype** (all are queued: `31-gesture-music`, `119-body-conductor`, `147-face-synth`) and **no kids prototype has ever used three.js** ‚Äî the entire kids zone is touch + canvas2d. The two silos (TD-style body-conducted 3D, and the kids-music space) have never met.

**Could become a prototype**: a 3D creature a child grows and plays *without touching the screen* ‚Äî hand height/openness (MediaPipe) or voice pitch/loudness (mic autocorrelation) drives a Perlin-displaced blob (three.js) and rings a pentatonic call-and-response. This cycle's build chain (DEEP, 2 parallel approaches: `234-kids-hand-creature` camera, `235-kids-sing-creature` voice) implements this hook directly. First MediaPipe-or-3D piece in the kids zone; breaks the touch+canvas2d local minimum the diversity audit flagged.

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 269 research dive (15-min, per mandate)

### WebGPU/WebGL emergent "Particle Life" has gone mainstream-browser ‚Äî and nobody has *sonified the emergence*
**Sources**: lisyarus, "Particle Life simulation in browser using WebGPU" (lisyarus.github.io blog, 2026) ¬∑ Markaicode, "WebGPU Game Physics: Simulating 1M Particles in the Browser" (markaicode.com, 2026) ¬∑ WebGPU community showcase "Party ‚Äî a WebGPU particle physics playground" (webgpu.com) ¬∑ the "Medusae" audio-reactive GPU-physics piece referenced in the 2026 WebGPU graphics roundups. Lineage of the algorithm: Jeffrey Ventrella's *Clusters*, Tom Mohr / CodeParade's *Particle Life*.

What's surprising: as of 2026, WebGPU compute shaders make **1M+ interacting particles at 60fps** routine *in a browser tab*, and the creative-coding community has standardized "Particle Life" (an asymmetric S√óS inter-species attraction matrix ‚Üí astonishing emergent cells, membranes, chasers) as the go-to demo. But every example is **silent eye-candy** ‚Äî even "Medusae," which is explicitly audio-*reactive*, runs the causality backwards (audio drives particles). **Nobody runs it forward: let the self-organization BE the score.** The Resonance lab has never used particle-life, GPGPU ping-pong textures, or any emergent-simulation technique ‚Äî it's a wide-open, never-touched paradigm. The hook: map each species to a pentatonic voice whose gain/brightness swells with that species' local clustering, so when a swarm condenses into a tight cell you *hear its note bloom*. Music ABOUT emergence, not music visualized.

**Could become a prototype that**: runs ~3‚Äì8k particles via three.js `GPUComputationRenderer`, computes a per-species clustering metric each frame, and drives one Web Audio voice per species ‚Üí built this cycle as `236-particle-life-song` (one of three WIDE explorers; siblings `235-spectral-terrain` and `237-tonnetz-lattice`). Also seeds future `particle-life-conductor` (mic onset perturbs the matrix) and a `reaction-diffusion-voice` companion in the same emergent-sonification family the lab is empty on.

---

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 270 research dive (15-min, mandatory)

### Tilt-as-instrument: browser accelerometer marble physics is mainstream-feasible
**Source**: https://www.kikkupico.com/posts/vibe-discovery/ ("Inertia", kikkupico, 2026) ¬∑ cross-ref embodied-music-cognition (movement reciprocally shapes pitch perception; Reggio Emilia sensorimotor learning).

What's surprising: a full WebGL marble game ‚Äî `ondevicemotion`/`ondeviceorientation` ‚Üí real marble physics over procedural terrain, dynamic camera, browser-only ‚Äî was built on an old Android phone with Claude Code, no native code. Tilt-as-input is now trivially feasible on the web. Yet across the lab's ~110 kids prototypes, **not one uses the device's own motion to play in a 3D world** ‚Äî they are all fingers on flat 2D glass. The one prior tilt piece (`83-kids-tilt-rain` ‚ù§Ô∏è) is a 2D basket-catch. Embodied-music-cognition research says this matters pedagogically: a 4-year-old who *leans the tablet to roll a ball onto a note* is learning pitch through whole-body movement, not abstract symbol-pointing ‚Äî the strongest sensorimotor path KIDS.md endorses.

**Could become a prototype that**: tilts the iPad to roll a glowing marble across a 3D musical hill-world (three.js heightfield with real downhill gravity), ringing C-pentatonic note-pads with StereoPanner spatial audio that tracks the ball ‚Äî built this cycle as `238-kids-tilt-world` (winner of a WIDE 3-builder kids fire; siblings `239-kids-sing-garden` + `240-kids-wave-band` banked in IDEAS.md). Also seeds a future adult `tilt-orbit` (tilt to steer a body through a gravitational soundscape) and a `tilt-fluid` (accelerometer drives a fluid-sim sloshing audio reactively).

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 271 research dive (15-min, mandatory)

### Real-time human-AI musical co-performance + WebGPU has hit browser baseline
**Sources**: arxiv.org/pdf/2604.07612 ("Towards Real-Time Human-AI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP", 2026-04) ¬∑ vr.org/articles/webgpu-baseline-2026-three-js-webxr-default (WebGPU baseline in every major browser, three.js shipping WebGPURenderer, 2026) ¬∑ soundtools.io/granular-synthesizer (browser granular, 2026).

What's surprising on two fronts. (1) The co-performance paper closes the loop the lab keeps gesturing at: a *latent-diffusion accompaniment* that listens to a live human and answers in real time ‚Äî reactive accompaniment is moving from research to MAX/MSP-deployable. (2) The infra surprise that matters more for us *tonight*: WebGPU is now **baseline across all major browsers** and three.js ships `WebGPURenderer` by default, so GPU **compute** (not just render) is finally a safe browser target ‚Äî million-particle physics, on-GPU FFT post, volumetric point clouds. The lab is almost entirely WebGL/canvas2d; we've touched compute exactly once (`130-tsl-particle-compute` ‚ù§Ô∏è).

**Could become a prototype that**: takes Karel's *own* recorded music (file upload, or his Paths tracks) and renders it as a **living, navigable 3D world you fly through** ‚Äî spectral energy becomes terrain / tunnel / volumetric cloud, beats drive the camera, spectral centroid drives color. This is the immersive "data-landscape of your music" lineage (TouchDesigner spectrogram-waterfall; Refik Anadol *data-sculpture*) made personal. Built this cycle as a DEEP 3-builder fire (`spectral-canyon` / `spectral-tunnel` / `spectral-cloud`), directly consuming the cycle-269 spectrogram-waterfall seed and Karel's loved real-music thread (`227-paths-granular` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `163-paths-visualizer` ‚ù§Ô∏è). The reactive-accompaniment paper seeds a future `duet-shadow` (mic onset ‚Üí generative answer) when an in-browser low-latency model is feasible.

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 272 research dive (15-min, mandatory)

### Whole-body pose as a browser play-controller ("Jump to play")
**Sources**: developers.googleblog.com/jump-to-play-building-with-gemini-mediapipe ("Jump to play: Building with Gemini & MediaPipe", 2026) ¬∑ MediaPipe Tasks pose (33 body points / 468 face / 21 hand landmarks at 30+fps on a plain webcam, on-device, WebGPU-accelerated, 2026) ¬∑ cross-ref: child vocal pitch perception/production developmental work (voice as first instrument).

What's surprising: Google is now actively framing **full-body pose** ‚Äî not taps, not hands ‚Äî as the *play controller* for browser games and apps, with Gemini "vibe-coding" lowering the build cost. MediaPipe runs 33 body keypoints at 30+fps fully on-device. The lab has exactly one camera kids piece (`234-kids-hand-creature`, MediaPipe **hands**) and zero whole-body / motion-field pieces; every other kids prototype (~110) is fingers on flat 2D glass. The body-as-controller idea is mainstream-feasible *today* yet untouched here, and it's the strongest sensorimotor path KIDS.md endorses (gross-motor whole-body input for a 4-year-old).

**Could become a prototype that**: lets a child play music by **moving their whole body in front of the camera** ‚Äî dependency-free per-zone frame-differencing (no ML lib) maps motion energy ‚Üí pentatonic voices + glowing particle bursts where they moved. Explored this cycle as `245-kids-body-band` (banked in IDEAS.md ‚Äî input was novel but it shipped a canvas2d output, so it's held until it gets a three.js/WebGL output to clear the diversity bar). The fire's **winner** instead consumed the banked, research-backed `kids-sing-creature` voice seed ‚Äî voice is the *other* embodied non-touch input the dive's developmental-pitch thread points at. Also seeds a future `body-aurora` (full-body MediaPipe pose ‚Üí three.js particle field) and `body-conduct` (pose-driven orchestral swell).

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 273 research dive (mandatory 15-min)

### Audio Gaussian Splatting ‚Äî sound fields as Gaussians from spectrograms
**Sources**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08967 ‚Äî "AudioGS: Spectrogram-Based Audio Gaussian Splatting for Sound Field Reconstruction" (arXiv, **April 2026**) ¬∑ cross-ref: monoton-music/`nibi` (GitHub, 2026) ‚Äî a Three.js **WebGPU TSL-compute** GPU-particle music-video engine with audio analysis; webgpu.com showcases "Party" (WebGPU compute particle physics) and "Tendrils" (audio-driven particle fluid).

What's surprising: 3D **Gaussian splatting** ‚Äî the technique that ate novel-view synthesis in 2024‚Äì25 ‚Äî is now being pointed at **audio**. AudioGS encodes a sound field as an explicit set of *audio Gaussians* derived from spectrograms to synthesize high-fidelity binaural audio from sparse observations. The conceptual leap for us isn't the binaural math; it's the *representation*: a spectrum as a field of soft, overlapping Gaussian atoms rather than hard points or a mesh. The lab's volumetric work to date (`243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `130-tsl-particle-compute` ‚ù§Ô∏è) is all hard additive **points**; nobody has rendered the spectrum as soft anisotropic **splats** that integrate into continuous luminous fog.

**Became a prototype this cycle**: `246-spectral-splat` ‚Äî borrows the *aesthetic* (each spectral atom = a soft `exp(-r¬≤)` Gaussian billboard, additive-blended, anisotropic) and flies the camera **forward through** the accumulating nebula of your own music. This is the "fly THROUGH your music" reading (vs `243`'s orbit), and the splat representation gives the cloud *body* that a point cloud can't. Further hooks seeded: a WebGPU-compute splat field (√Ý la `nibi`/`Party`) once compute lands in the lab; true covariance-oriented splats that orient along the spectral gradient; and AudioGS's actual binaural-reconstruction angle as a spatial-audio room piece.

---

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 274 research dive (mandatory 15-min)

### Interactive Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion as a *stir-able living surface* in a pure WebGL fragment shader
**Sources**: https://github.com/amandaghassaei/ReactionDiffusionShader ‚Äî Amanda Ghassaei's Gray-Scott WebGL shader, with a variant that adds an **underlying vector field** controlling diffusion orientation so users *click to move sinks* and steer the global pattern ¬∑ https://cake23.de/turing-fluid.html ‚Äî "Turing pattern fluid" (Gray-Scott √ó Navier-Stokes mashup, fully interactive in a WebGL shader) ¬∑ cross-ref https://sachinsharma.dev/blogs/fluid-physics-threejs-2026 (real-time GPGPU water at 120fps, 2026) for the perf framing.

What's surprising: reaction-diffusion is usually shown as a *passive* generative-art toy you watch. The current interactive work (Ghassaei's directed-field variant; the Turing-fluid mashup) treats the RD field as a **responsive surface you stir** ‚Äî your input doesn't reset the sim, it *seeds* the chemistry and the pattern grows organically from where you touched. That's the missing affordance for an embodied kids instrument: a living organism a child *feeds by moving*, not a button that triggers a clip. The lab has a passive adult `9-reaction-diffusion`, but nothing where a human body grows the pattern in real time, and (per the diversity audit) the kids zone has zero raw-GLSL-shader pieces ‚Äî it's all touch + flat canvas + three.js.

**Could become a prototype that does X**: a kids "garden" where **whole-body motion (zero-dep webcam frame-differencing) injects RD seed** into a ping-pong Gray-Scott shader, so a 4-year-old grows glowing bioluminescent Turing patterns by dancing, and pattern activity per screen-zone rings soft pentatonic notes. The cause‚Üíeffect is slow-organic (you plant, it blooms) rather than the instant tap-chime the lab is stuck on ‚Äî a genuinely different *temporality* of play. Chain **today's research ‚Üí today's build**: this dive directly seeded WIDE-fire brief B ‚Üí `248-kids-stir-garden` (the predicted winner). It also re-frames the banked `kids-sing-garden` (voice‚Üífbm sky) and `kids-tilt-pour` (tilt‚Üímetaball-fluid) as the same family: child input *stirring* a continuous living shader field, never the discrete tap.

---

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî Cycle 275 research dive (mandatory 15-min)

### Real-time human‚ÄìAI musical co-performance: sliding-window *look-ahead* accompaniment
**Sources**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07612 ‚Äî "Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP" (arXiv [cs.SD], **08 Apr 2026**) ¬∑ cross-ref https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.22105 "Streaming Generation for Music Accompaniment" (streaming/causal accompaniment framing).

What's surprising: most generative-music systems are *offline turn-takers* ‚Äî you give a prompt or a finished phrase, you wait, you get a clip back. This paper formalizes accompaniment as a **sliding-window look-ahead protocol**: a latent-diffusion model predicts the *next* slice of audio from **partial** live context, so the AI plays *alongside* a human in real time, with **latency as the binding constraint** (they apply consistency distillation for a 5.4√ó sampling speedup, and split a MAX/MSP real-time front end from a Python model over OSC/UDP to keep the fast loop decoupled from the heavy model). The conceptual gift for the lab isn't the diffusion math ‚Äî it's the **interaction shape**: a co-performer that acts on incomplete context and *anticipates/settles* rather than waiting for a complete musical thought. The lab's one dialogue piece (`225-aria-companion`) is the opposite ‚Äî a polite fixed-2-second turn-taker; nothing in the lab plays *into the gaps* of a live performer in real time.

**Became a prototype this cycle**: a DEEP 3-builder *live-reactive-accompanist* fire borrowing the interaction shape (not the model) with cheap browser DSP ‚Äî three "band members": **250-harmonist** (chroma‚Üíchord ‚Üí continuous bass+comp bed underneath you), **251-trader** (gap detection ‚Üí live-trained Markov fills that interleave + duck-on-re-entry ‚Äî the **shipped winner**), **252-groover** (spectral-flux onset ‚Üí IOI-histogram tempo inference ‚Üí look-ahead two-clocks scheduled generative groove that follows your pulse). The look-ahead-with-latency-constraint and "act on partial context" ideas map directly onto: a short settle window before switching the comp chord (250), darting into a gap the instant it opens then ducking (251), and a Chris-Wilson two-clocks scheduler that re-derives tempo+phase from your latest onsets every tick (252). Further hooks seeded: variable-order/VLMM Markov for stronger phrase structure (Pachet's Continuator), an AudioWorklet pitch front-end for rock-steady low-latency onsets, and eventually a real streaming model (2510.22105) behind a guarded dream API route if a cheap hosted endpoint appears.

---

## 2026-06-01 ‚Äî cycle 276 (kids ¬∑ WIDE) research dive

### A from-scratch interactive lava-lamp fragment shader, engineered for phones
**Source**: https://damianvandermerwe.com/blog/painting-with-math-lava-lamp-shader ‚Äî Damian Van Der Merwe, "Painting with Math: Building an Interactive Lava Lamp Shader from Scratch" (**April 3, 2026**, ~60 days). Cross-ref: vishald.com "Gooey metaballs using fragment shaders", brybrant/lava-lamp (GitHub), Codrops "Interactive Droplet-like Metaballs with three.js + GLSL" (2025).

**What's surprising / useful**: most metaball/lava-lamp writeups reach for `exp(-d¬≤)` falloffs, three.js, or marching-cubes meshes. This one is a clinic in *making a per-pixel fragment shader cheap enough to run smoothly on a phone* ‚Äî at 1440p√ó2 DPR that's ">7 million fragment invocations every 16 ms" ‚Äî by deliberately choosing the cheap path at every step: a **Hermite smoothstep** metaball falloff instead of an exponential, a **fixed small blob count** (~8 ambient + a capped ring buffer of click-spawned blobs) so the GLSL loop unrolls with no dynamic branch, noise **capped at 2 octaves**, and **devicePixelRatio capped at 2** ("on 3x displays this cuts GPU load in half with no perceptible quality loss"). It's the missing engineering recipe for shipping a gooey-fluid look on a 4-year-old's tablet, and the kids zone had no metaball/smooth-min piece at all.

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: the WIDE-fire winner **`253-kids-tilt-pour`** ‚Äî a candy lava-lamp of 8 blobs you *pour by tilting the iPad*, blobs ringing pentatonic notes when they merge ‚Äî adopts all four of Van Der Merwe's perf choices verbatim and adds a tilt‚Üígravity CPU physics step + smooth-min rim/halo banding (IQ) + a kid-safe limiter chain. Banked siblings from the same fire: `254-kids-blow-bloom` (the lab's first **breath/blow-detection** input ‚Äî blow a dandelion apart, seeds ring notes; distinguished from singing via spectral flatness √ó inverted autocorrelation clarity) and `255-kids-sing-garden` (voice‚Üífbm-domain-warp bedtime sky + pentatonic loopback). Further hooks: an FPS-probe that drops DPR to 1 on weak tablets; true per-blob SDF outlines + split animations; Karplus-Strong pluck voices for the merge sound.

---

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî cycle 277 (adult ¬∑ DEEP) research dive

### Online chord accompaniment that comps *with* a melody, RL-shaped for harmonic+temporal coherency
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.14723 ‚Äî Lee et al., "Adaptive Accompaniment with ReaLchords" (v1 **17 Jun 2025**, date verified via WebFetch). Cross-ref this cycle: arXiv 2604.07612 "Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance" (Apr 2026, the recent anchor that seeded the cycle-275 live-duet family).

**What's surprising / useful**: ReaLchords is the cleanest statement of the problem the lab's "AI band" arc is circling ‚Äî an *online* model that emits chord accompaniment **simultaneously** with an unfolding monophonic melody (no look-ahead at the input it can't yet have heard), trained by **maximum-likelihood pretraining then RL-refined against a novel reward model that scores both harmonic AND temporal coherency**, plus a distillation trick from a teacher that *can* see the future melody. The headline insight for a real-time accompanist isn't the architecture, it's the framing: coherency is two separate axes (are the notes right? are they *on time*?) and they can be rewarded independently ‚Äî which maps directly onto a DSP accompanist's two failure modes (wrong chord vs. ragged pulse). It's slightly older than the 30-day window, but it's foundational for the harmony-member design and a perfect named reference: our template-matcher is the symbolic/DSP cousin of its learned policy, and "replace the cosine matcher with a ReaLchords-style next-chord predictor" is the obvious deepening.

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: arc ‚Üí build)**: the cycle-275 DEEP fire's banked harmony member, rebuilt and shipped as **`256-live-duet-harmonist`** ‚Äî chroma‚Üí36-template chord match ‚Üí 160 ms settle ‚Üí **rootless/drop-2 jazz voicings + a walking bass** placed by a **spectral-flux-onset ‚Üí tempo-inference ‚Üí Chris-Wilson two-clocks scheduler** (it folds the seed's own "next-cycle deepening" ‚Äî jazz voicings, walking bass, onset-synced comping ‚Äî into the shipped piece). ReaLchords' two-axis (harmonic/temporal) coherency reward is logged as the next-cycle hook: split the bed's quality into a harmonic score (chroma‚Üîvoicing fit) and a temporal score (onset-phase alignment) and let a tiny online policy bias the voicing/timing ‚Äî a route from the current reactive DSP toward a genuinely *adaptive* accompanist. The runner-up `250-live-duet-harmonist` (simpler fixed-clock arpeggiated-triad comping, build-verified, more demo-reliable) is banked in IDEAS.md as the fallback if onset-sync proves flaky in a real browser session.

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî cycle 278 (kids ¬∑ DEEP) research dive

### MediaPipe FaceLandmarker's 52 blendshapes as a kid's expression-instrument
**Sources**: https://ai.google.dev/edge/mediapipe/solutions/vision/face_landmarker/web_js ‚Äî Google AI Edge "Face landmark detection guide for Web" (docs refreshed **April 2026**, date verified in result) ¬∑ https://github.com/designerzen/interface ‚Äî designerzen's **InterFACE**, a webcam-based musical instrument whose "expressive mode" maps facial movement to sound (roadmap: mouth controls playback/amplitude of the next MIDI phrase). Lineage ref: Daniel Rozin's interactive *mirrors* (Wooden/PomPom).

**What's surprising / useful**: the lab shipped its first MediaPipe piece only 10 cycles ago (`234-kids-hand-creature`, HandLandmarker ‚Üí three.js), but **FaceLandmarker** is a categorically richer control surface and the lab has never touched it. Beyond 478 3D landmarks it emits **52 ARKit-style blendshape coefficients** in real time in the browser ‚Äî `jawOpen`, `mouthSmileLeft/Right`, `browInnerUp`, `eyeBlink*`, etc., each a clean 0..1 signal ‚Äî i.e. a free, calibration-free, *continuous* expression bus. InterFACE proves the "your face is the instrument" interaction is real and musical; what nobody has done is aim it at a **4-year-old who can't read, can't reach a MIDI keyboard, and shouldn't have to touch the glass** ‚Äî a kid already knows how to open their mouth wide and smile. The blendshape stream maps almost one-to-one onto kid-safe music controls (mouth-open‚Üívolume of a sung note, smile‚Üíbrightness/major-harmony, brows‚Üípentatonic pitch step, head-x‚Üípan), and Canvas2D (deliberately not three.js, which is over-represented in the last 10) keeps it cheap on a tablet.

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **DEEP 2-builder** fire on one concept ‚Äî *"a 4-year-old plays music with their face."* Both builders use webcam ‚Üí FaceLandmarker ‚Üí Canvas2D, diverging on aesthetic+mapping: `257-kids-face-band` (a single glowing creature that mirrors the child's expressions ‚Äî jawOpen‚Üísung note, smile‚Üíbrightness, brows‚Üípitch, head-x‚Üípan) vs. `258-kids-mirror-pets` (a Daniel-Rozin-style *mosaic of small singing "pets"* that light up to form the face ‚Äî a music-box mirror). Shipped winner curated below; loser banked. Next-cycle hooks: two-face duet (`numFaces:2`) so two kids sing together; use the facial transformation matrix for true 3D head-lean ‚Üí richer panning; an adult sibling that maps blendshapes to expressive synth parameters (vibrato from brow micro-movement) ‚Äî the InterFACE "expressive mode" for grown-ups.

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Cycle 279 dive (every-cycle research-first mandate)

**Source / lineage**: Brian Eno, *Music for Airports* (1978) & *Discreet Music* (1975); Steve Reich, *Piano Phase* (1967), *It's Gonna Rain* (1965) ‚Äî re-grounded against the current long-form-generation literature: arXiv **2404.10301** *Long-form music generation with latent diffusion* (a diffusion-transformer on a 21.5 Hz latent, up to ~4m45s) and "Musika!" (arXiv 2208.08706, infinite-length waveform gen via a latent coordinate system). Also captured the **NOAA SWPC** real-time JSON endpoints (`/products/solar-wind/plasma-1-day.json`, `/mag-1-day.json`, `/products/noaa-planetary-k-index.json` ‚Äî no-auth, CORS-open) for the real-world-data sibling.

**What's surprising / useful**: the 05-31 ambition floor lists *"first long-form (>5min) stateful piece"* as a technique the lab has never used ‚Äî and the cheapest, lowest-latency, zero-dependency way to get genuinely **non-repeating** long-form audio is **not** a 2026 diffusion model but Eno's **1978** trick: N tape loops of mutually *incommensurate* length never realign, so the combination never exactly repeats (six loops of 11.3‚Äì37.7 s ‚Üí ~29 h before an exact repeat). Layer a slow state machine on top (movements that change which loops are active, the filter, the reverb, the register) and you have an evolving piece with memory that runs forever in Web Audio with no model, no GPU, no latency. The diffusion papers are the *foil*: they show the field chasing long-form coherence with heavyweight models, which makes the classical generative-loop approach the *right* tool for a browser prototype ‚Äî and it composes perfectly with Karel's loved Paths recordings as the loop source.

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: WIDE 3-builder fire; **brief A shipped** as `259-paths-generative` (file/`/api/audio`-loaded piano ‚Üí 6 incommensurate loops + generative movement state machine + concentric phase-ring viz ‚Äî the lab's first long-form stateful piece, Eno/Reich-referenced). The SWPC endpoints fed **brief B** `260-aurora-drone` (live solar-wind ‚Üí drone+aurora; the 2nd real-world-data sonification after `233`) ‚Äî banked build-verified. Next-cycle hooks: granular re-synthesis of the loop buffer (closer to Eno's actual studio method than `playbackRate`), key-aware transposition constrained to the recording's detected key, localStorage session persistence + WAV export; and for the data sibling, the GOES X-ray flare feed ‚Üí transient flares + a 1-minute estimated-Kp endpoint for tighter resolution.

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Cycle 280 dive (every-cycle research-first mandate)

**Source / lineage**: CymaVis (cymavis.com ‚Äî browser Chladni-plate + 3D water-surface cymatics, WebGL shaders + Web Audio, current) and the broader "make sound visible" canon, re-grounded against the lab's own 260-prototype back-catalogue; plus the **emergent / artificial-life** lineage ‚Äî Sage Jenson's *mold* + Jeff Jones (2010) **Physarum** transport networks, **Bert Chan's Lenia** (2019) continuous cellular automata, Thomas Jakobsen's **Verlet** cloth (GDC 2001). cs.SD/cs.GR recent listings skimmed (mostly diffusion/co-performance ‚Äî already covered cycle 279).

**What's surprising / useful**: the *real* finding this cycle was a **negative result that reframed the build**. The obvious "make sound visible for kids" techniques ‚Äî **cymatics/Chladni, fluid sim, magnetic field lines** ‚Äî all *feel* novel but are already spent in the lab (`19`/`165` cymatics, `3`/`15`/`84` fluid, `192`/`222` magnet). At 260+ prototypes the binding constraint is no longer "find a cool technique" but "find a cool technique **the lab has never touched**" ‚Äî so the Decide step's first move is now a hard grep of every candidate against INDEX+READMEs. That grep surfaced the lab's richest *unused* vein: **emergent self-organization** ‚Äî Physarum agent-transport networks, Lenia continuous CA, Verlet mass-spring physics, plus DLA / Voronoi / curl-noise (all grepped **zero hits**). This vein is the natural deepening of Karel's loved `236-particle-life-song` (music FROM emergence), and none of it had ever reached the kids zone, where every one of ~115 prior pieces is tap‚Üíchime on flat glass.

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **WIDE 3-builder** kids fire on three never-used emergent techniques, all touch-input + raw-WebGL-output (dodging the over-represented three.js+canvas2d of the last 10): `260-kids-slime-garden` (**Physarum** ‚Äî thousands of agents crawl to your touch and weave singing veins; **shipped winner**), `261-kids-lenia-pond` (**Lenia** orbium gliders that sing; banked), `262-kids-light-cloth` (**Verlet** cloth you strum; banked). Concrete next hooks the fire opened: **DLA coral-growth** (tap to seed, glowing branches sing as they aggregate), **Voronoi cell-shatter** music, **curl-noise flow-field** ribbons, and a **predator/prey Lenia** with evolving multi-species texture ‚Äî each a clean-grepped novel technique for a future emergent-kids or emergent-adult cycle. Make "emergent self-organization" a *category* (slime + life + cloth + coral), the way 233/aurora is becoming the real-world-data category.

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Cycle 282 dive (every-cycle research-first mandate) ‚Äî web tools RECOVERED

**Source / lineage** (verified live this cycle): **Flow-Lenia** ‚Äî Plantec, Hamon, Etcheverry, Chan et al., *"Flow-Lenia: Emergent evolutionary dynamics in mass conservative continuous cellular automata,"* published in **Artificial Life 31(2):228 (MIT Press, 2025)**, arXiv [2506.08569](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.08569) (the journal version; original preprint [2212.07906](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.07906)). And **Particle-Lenia** ‚Äî the energy-based / particle reformulation by Mordvintsev et al., Google Research "Self-Organising Systems" ([particle-lenia](https://google-research.github.io/self-organising-systems/particle-lenia/)). Both descend from **Bert Wang-Chak Chan's Lenia** (2019).

**What's surprising / useful**: cycle 280's dive flagged Lenia as the lab's richest *unused* emergent vein and banked a `kids-lenia-pond` seed ‚Äî but it lost to Slime Garden for one honest reason: **classic grid Lenia has a stability cliff.** The orbium glider only survives inside a razor-thin parameter window (mu/sigma/R/dt); off it the creature either dissolves to nothing or explodes the field ‚Äî a genuine **failure mode**, which is disqualifying for an *unsupervised 4-year-old*. The surprise this cycle is that **the artificial-life field already solved exactly this, twice, and recently.** **Flow-Lenia** replaces "add/remove mass each step" with "compute a flow field that *moves* existing mass without creating or destroying it" ‚Üí total mass is conserved ‚Üí the lifeform **literally cannot blow up or vanish.** **Particle-Lenia** reframes the whole thing as particles doing gradient descent on an energy field `E = R ‚àí G` (repulsion ‚àí growth) ‚Üí the swarm self-organizes into a stable, pulsing, crawling "cell" with *no* failure mode and gorgeous additive-glow particles. Both are the missing piece that turns the risky banked Lenia seed into a **spill-proof** kids toy. (Date check: Flow-Lenia journal version is 2025 ‚Äî slightly outside the strict 30-day window, but it's the load-bearing *de-risking* technique for this exact build, and the particle formulation is what makes a 4-yo-safe version feasible ‚Äî foundational-for-this-build, cited honestly.)

**Became a prototype this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **DEEP 3-builder** fire on ONE concept ‚Äî *"a 4-year-old breeds glowing lifeforms that sing"* ‚Äî attacked via the three Lenia formulations so the critic can ship whichever is most stable + beautiful + 4-yo-legible: `263-kids-glow-lifeforms` (**Particle-Lenia**, energy-based particle swarm ‚Äî the most failure-proof + freshest render), `264-kids-lenia-pond` (**classic grid Lenia** / Chan orbium, R8-texture nebula ‚Äî the faithful banked seed), `265-kids-flow-pond` (**Flow-Lenia**, mass-conserving advected goo ‚Äî spill-proof finger-paint). All touch + raw-WebGL2, dodging the over-represented mic + canvas2d of the last 10. This makes "emergent self-organization" a real **category** (Physarum slime `260` + now Lenia life), the JURY's standing ask.

**Next-cycle hooks the dive opened**: **multi-species Flow-Lenia** (parameter localization from the 2025 paper ‚Äî different goo species with different rules that interact/compete ‚Üí evolving texture); per-creature blob detection ‚Üí per-creature pitch (vs fixed spatial bands); two creatures *merging* into a chord; and the still-unbuilt emergent siblings from ¬ß280 (DLA coral-growth, Voronoi cell-shatter, curl-noise ribbons, Verlet `kids-light-cloth`).

## ¬ß283 ‚Äî 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Spectral content *flows*: the frequency-transport reframe (+ TD audio-reactive-tunnel craft)

**The dive** (research-first mandate; web tools healthy this fire after the ¬ß281 529 outage). Two anchors, one conceptual + one practical, for the JURY's flagged "fly through YOUR music" vein.

**(1) Conceptual ‚Äî NSTR: "Neural Spectral Transport Representation for Space-Varying Frequency Fields"** (arXiv [2511.18384](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.18384), submitted **23 Nov 2025**, date verified via WebFetch). Most neural/implicit signal representations assume **globally stationary frequency bases** ‚Äî one fixed spectrum applies everywhere. NSTR drops that: it learns a **spatially-varying local spectrum field S(x)** plus a **frequency-transport network** `F_Œ∏` constrained by a PDE `‚àáS(x) ‚âà F_Œ∏(x, S(x))` that governs *how local spectral composition evolves across space* ‚Äî i.e. the spectrum is a **field that flows/transports**, not a static stack. **Why it's surprising for us**: every spectrogram-visualizer in the lab (and the world) treats a spectrum frame as a frozen slice you stack into a wall/terrain/cloud. NSTR's reframe says the more truthful picture is a *flowing frequency field* ‚Äî spectral content advecting through space. That's a genuinely different aesthetic target. (Date honesty: ~6 months old, so foundational-conceptual, not a 30-day item ‚Äî but it's the load-bearing *idea* that distinguishes today's third explorer from a stacked-wall demo.)

**(2) Practical craft ‚Äî TouchDesigner audio-reactive tunnels + 3D point clouds.** The Interactive & Immersive HQ / Derivative community recipes ([audio-reactive 3D point clouds via instancing](https://derivative.ca/community-post/tutorial/audio-reactive-3d-point-clouds-touchdesigner/68021); [endless audio-reactive tunnel loop](https://alltd.org/ue4-infinite-tunnel-audio-reactive-loop-in-unreal-engine-touchdesigner/)) confirm the robust production pattern: **instanced points + a recycling ring buffer that translates in Z** is the proven, performant way to build a forward-flythrough corridor ‚Äî exactly the motion model the cycle-271/273 banked `spectral-tunnel` seed already nailed, and the reason the tunnel reads as travel rather than pulsing-in-place.

**Became the build this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **DEEP 3-builder** fire on ONE concept ‚Äî *"fly THROUGH your own music as a living 3D world"* ‚Äî the JURY's explicitly-flagged richest vein, extending the loved `243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è (the only 5/5 floor build) and Karel's loved real-piano thread (`163`/`227` ‚ù§Ô∏è) since the source is his own Welcome Home recordings via `/api/audio/:id`. Three render readings: `265-spectral-tunnel` (radial wormhole, rings freeze-on-spawn + translate in Z ‚Äî the TD-craft kinetic reading; resurrects the build-verified banked seed), `266-spectral-canyon` (spectrogram-waterfall terrain with the Z-remap fix ‚Äî the legible reading), `267-spectral-drift` (additive-particle field that **advects** per the NSTR frequency-transport reframe ‚Äî the atmospheric, research-anchored reading). All three.js + audio-file, dodging the over-represented canvas2d + raw-WebGL of the last 10.

**Next-cycle hooks the dive opened**: a true GPU-compute curl-noise advection field (the honest NSTR realization vs. today's cheap analytic flow); stereo L/R splitting the tunnel mirror / dual terrains; UnrealBloomPass across the whole vein; a "freeze + walk" first-person mode to inhabit one frozen moment of the song.

## ¬ß284 ‚Äî 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Co-creative SVG animation (LLM-restructured vector puppets) + the gamelan-tuning anchor

**The dive** (research-first mandate; web tools healthy). Today's build was JURY-driven (the 2026-06-02 verdict: *ban the glow, ban the pentatonic, force a non-luminous output in a non-pentatonic mode*) ‚Äî so the dive went looking for what's fresh in the two lanes the JURY pushed me into: **SVG/vector rendering** (the lab's never-used non-luminous output) and **non-Western tuning**.

**(1) Primary ‚Äî Decomate: "Leveraging Generative Models for Co-Creative SVG Animation"** (arXiv [2511.06297](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.06297), submitted **9 Nov 2025**, date verified via WebFetch). A multimodal-LLM system that takes a *raw, flat* SVG and **restructures it into semantically meaningful, animation-ready components** (groups, anchors, joints), then lets a designer drive motion with natural-language prompts. **Why it's surprising for us**: SVG is usually treated as a static export format; Decomate reframes it as a *riggable, promptable animation substrate* ‚Äî and an LLM can do the rigging. (Date honesty: ~7 months old, so foundational-conceptual not a 30-day item ‚Äî but it's the bridge between today's hand-authored SVG puppets and Karel's stated #1 direction, AI-image/AI-asset *inside* an AV piece.) **Could become a prototype**: a kids "make your own puppet" mode where a child names an animal ("a turtle!"), a model returns an **animation-ready SVG silhouette** with pre-rigged walk joints, and it drops straight onto the `268-kids-shadow-theater` stage already tuned to a gamelan voice ‚Äî AI-asset-generation embedded in an AV experience (not standalone image-gen), which is exactly the empty shelf the JURY flagged.

**(2) Anchor ‚Äî gamelan slendro/pelog as a browser tuning.** Searches surfaced the lineage of microtonal-gamelan-in-software (Greg Schiemer's *Pocket Gamelan* / *Satellite Gamelan* ‚Äî Pure Data + Scala tuning tables exported to mobile ensembles; slendro ‚âà a stretched ~5-EDO-ish set, pelog ‚âà a subset of ~9-EDO) confirming that the honest way to *sound* like a gamelan is **non-equal-tempered ratios + inharmonic partials + slow beating between near-unison strikes**, not a quantized Western scale. This grounded `268`'s synth (detuned twin fundamentals + a ~2.41√ó inharmonic partial over slendro-like ratios 1.00/1.16/1.35/1.52/1.78) as a real tuning choice, not decoration ‚Äî the **first non-Western / microtonal tuning in the lab**.

**Became the build this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **WIDE 3-builder** kids fire, all three deliberately **SVG output + non-pentatonic sound** to obey the JURY's "ban glow + ban pentatonic" cycle. Winner `268-kids-shadow-theater` (wayang silhouettes + gamelan slendro tuning + colotomic gong cycle ‚Äî anchor (2)); banked siblings `kids-paper-parade` (Eric-Carle torn-paper marching band, rhythm-layer groove, major-key I‚ÄìV oom-pah ‚Äî not a note bag) + `kids-paper-score` (Cardew graphic score, shape‚Üítimbre + height‚Üífull-diatonic, a playhead performs your drawing). `svg` grepped clean across 260+ prior prototypes ‚Üí **first SVG-rendered piece in the lab**.

**Next-cycle hooks the dive opened**: the Decomate "name-an-animal ‚Üí rigged-SVG-puppet" mode above (AI-asset-inside-AV, the JURY's highest-value empty shelf); a pelog (7-tone) "grown-up" gamelan companion; a graphic-score piece (banked `kids-paper-score`) that records the child's playhead pass as a shareable loop.

## ¬ß285 ‚Äî 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Real-time audio-conditioned image generation (the streaming-diffusion frontier) + the "meaningful, not spectral" reframe

**The dive** (research-first mandate; web tools healthy). This adult cycle was built to satisfy the JURY's two loudest 2026-06-02 verdicts at once: **(a) finally build AI-image-generation INSIDE an AV piece** (Karel's stated #1 direction, AGENT.md 05-21, and 0√ó in the last 15 builds), and **(b) ban the glow ‚Äî force a non-luminous output**. So the dive looked for what's fresh in *real-time, audio-driven image synthesis*.

**(1) Freshness anchor ‚Äî StreamDiffusion (sd-turbo) real-time pipeline.** Confirmed via search that `sd-turbo` under a StreamDiffusion-style batched-denoising pipeline reaches **~10 fps interactive image generation on a single laptop GPU (RTX 4080)** ‚Äî i.e. true frame-rate diffusion is now a consumer-GPU reality, not a render-farm one. **Why it matters for us**: the *ideal* of this whole vein is a diffusion model painting every frame from the live audio. We can't run that GPU pipeline inside a Vercel/browser sandbox ‚Äî so the honest, shippable approximation is **periodic server-side chapter regeneration (fal `flux/schnell`, 4 steps, ~$0.01‚Äì0.04/image) + client-side audio-reactive transformation of that generated image between chapters.** That two-tier split *is* the design pattern of this cycle's build.

**(2) 2026 corroboration ‚Äî real-time human-AI co-performance with latent diffusion** (arXiv [2604.07612](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07612), 2026; and Live Music Diffusion Models, [2605.22717](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22717), May 2026): both apply **consistency distillation / KV-caching to make diffusion run live** in a performance loop (MAX/MSP + OSC, 5.4√ó sampling speed-up). They're audio-*generation* not image, but they confirm the same frontier move ‚Äî *distill the diffusion model until it's fast enough to be an instrument*. (LMDM is already echoed in the lab at `138-lmdm-echo`.)

**(3) Conceptual reframe ‚Äî "meaningful" audio-responsive visuals.** ACM IMX 2025, "An AI-driven Music Visualization System for Generating Meaningful Audio-Responsive Visuals in Real-Time" (DOI [10.1145/3706370.3727869](https://doi.org/10.1145/3706370.3727869)). *Honesty caveat: full text is paywalled ‚Äî cited from the published title/abstract via search, not read in full.* The load-bearing idea I took: regenerate **semantically meaningful imagery from the musical mood** (a prompt composed from energy/brightness/onset), not just spectral bars or a reactive shader. The generated *content* should mean something about the sound.

**Became the build this cycle (chain: dive ‚Üí build)**: a **DEEP 3-builder** fire on the single concept *"an AI-generated image as a living, audio-shaped participant in an AV piece"* ‚Äî all three: tri-modal audio (mic / file / **his Welcome Home piano by track-id** via `/api/audio/:id` JSON-or-bytes / non-pentatonic synth fallback) ‚Üí live mood analysis ‚Üí `flux/schnell` chapter generation (guarded, 501-degrades to a procedural chapter so it demos with **zero API calls**) ‚Üí an audio-reactive renderer. Three readings: `269-latent-breath` (WebGL domain-warp/displacement of the chapter texture ‚Äî *the image breathes*), `270-dream-chapters` (WebGL ping-pong **optical feedback** so chapters *melt* into each other ‚Äî a long-form memory piece), `271-pigment-mosaic` (a **non-luminous Canvas2D mosaic** the music shatters/settles/re-sorts ‚Äî *the image as matter*). **Winner = `271`** because it uniquely satisfies *both* JURY verdicts ‚Äî AI-image-inside-AV **and** non-luminous (the anti-glow the JURY demanded); 269/270 are luminous and would re-offend the glow ban. All three build-verified `‚óã Static` before banking.

**Next-cycle hooks the dive opened**: the banked `270-dream-chapters` (optical-feedback **melting-memory** piece) is the natural vehicle for the JURY's *"extend 259 / long-form-memory"* ask ‚Äî fuse it with mic-listening (251/256) into a 10-minute piece that listens, remembers, and rewrites its own imagery; `269-latent-breath` (displacement) banked too. The deeper frontier hook: **image-to-image chapter continuity** (condition each new `flux` chapter on the previous frame) ‚Üí the true StreamDiffusion *morph*, the honest realization of anchor (1).

## 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Cycle 286 research dive (kids / non-pentatonic sound)

**Mandate**: every cycle opens with a 15-min dive for one surprising recent piece. This cycle's JURY verdict demanded we finally **audit the SOUND** ‚Äî the lab's ~9/15 builds sing C-major pentatonic and nobody polices it ‚Äî so the dive targeted *sound diversity*, specifically the psychoacoustics that make a sound feel "in tune" vs "rough."

### Consonance / roughness ‚Äî the three-term model `[foundational, recent synthesis]`
**Sources**: McBride, *Musical consonance: a review of theory and evidence on perception and preference of auditory roughness* (arXiv **2510.14159**, Oct 2025); Marjieh et al. timbral-effects-on-consonance (235,440 human judgments, US + South Korea) ‚Äî derived an updated computational model = **liking of harmonicity + disliking of fast beats (roughness) + liking of slow beats**. Verified-fresh last-30-day anchor: **Live Music Diffusion Models** (arXiv **2605.22717**, May 2026) ‚Äî interactive streaming music generation as a "generative delay" running locally; not directly used, but confirms the cs.SD frontier is in real-time interactive territory.

**Why it's surprising / useful**: consonance is not (only) cultural ‚Äî there's a *physical, pre-verbal* layer. Two tones Œîf apart beat at Œîf Hz; ~5‚Äì8 Hz reads as a "rough" wobble, sliding to 0 Hz reads as "pure." A 4-year-old can *feel* that transition in their body before they have any words for pitch. This is the cheapest possible non-pentatonic sound world: pure just-intonation ratios over a drone, with the *beating itself* as the feedback signal.

**Became a prototype this cycle**: `272-kids-tune-purr` ‚Äî drag a detuned creature until the audible beating stops and it locks into a just-intonation interval (4:5:6 triad when all three lock). The roughness‚Üíconsonance gradient *is* the gameplay. Two siblings explored other non-pentatonic sound worlds the same fire: `273-kids-raga-peacock` (Hindustani Raga Yaman + tanpura jvari, just intonation) and `274-kids-clay-clock` (West-African 3:2 + gankogui polyrhythm, percussion-only) ‚Äî both banked build-verified in IDEAS.md.
