## ¬ß287 ‚Äî 2026-06-02 ‚Äî The self-listening musical agent: capture, mosaic, and re-perform your own sound

**Mandate**: every cycle opens with a 15-min dive for one surprising recent piece. This cycle's build was the JURY's #1 adult ask ‚Äî *extend `259-paths-generative` into a piece that **listens, remembers, and rewrites its own structure over 10 minutes***, fusing 259's incommensurate-loop engine with the mic-listening intelligence of `251`/`256`. The dive went looking for the current frontier of **machine-listening co-creative agents**: systems that take in a live audio stream, build a *memory* of it, and generate new music *from* that memory.

### The corpus-based / self-listening agent lineage `[foundational technique + fresh agent framing]`
**Sources**: Lee & Pasquier, *Musical Agent Systems: MACAT and MACataRT* (arXiv **2502.00023**, Jan 2025) ‚Äî **MACAT** = agent-led performance via real-time synthesis + **self-listening** (it monitors its own output to shape what it plays next); **MACataRT** = collaborative improvisation via **audio mosaicing** (corpus-based concatenative synthesis) + sequence learning. Grounded in **Diemo Schwarz's CataRT** (real-time CBCS: segment a corpus into descriptor-tagged units, navigate a 2D descriptor space to resynthesize). Cross-checked against *The Concatenator* (arXiv 2411.04366, Nov 2024 ‚Äî a Bayesian real-time concatenative musaicing engine). Verified-fresh last-30-day anchors confirming the frontier is real-time/interactive: *Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance* (arXiv **2604.07612**, Apr 2026 ‚Äî latent-diffusion accompaniment in MAX/MSP over a GPU server) and *Live Music Models* / Magenta RealTime (arXiv 2508.04651, Aug 2025 ‚Äî continuous steerable music streams).

**Why it's surprising / useful**: the heavyweight 2026 frontier (latent-diffusion co-performance) needs a remote GPU and tens-of-ms round-trips ‚Äî *not* browser-feasible. But the **memory architecture** under all of it is decades-old and runs with zero deps in Web Audio: keep what you heard (raw audio loops, OR a tagged grain corpus, OR a symbolic note stream), let a slow process re-weave it. The surprise is that *the cheapest version is also the most personal* ‚Äî the instrument becomes literally **made of the player's own sound**, which is exactly Karel's "use his real Welcome Home recordings" directive realized as a live, self-listening loop rather than file playback. Three distinct memory representations fall straight out of the literature: **verbatim audio** (Frippertronics/Eno tape loops), **grain corpus** (CataRT/MACataRT mosaicing), **symbolic notes** (Lewis *Voyager* / Rowe *Cypher*).

**Became prototypes this cycle (DEEP, one concept √ó three memory representations)**: shipped `275-memory-loom` ‚Äî captures verbatim phrases from your live mic into an incommensurate loop bank (digital Frippertronics) and reweaves them over a 10-min movement state machine, displacing the oldest memories (it forgets). Two siblings explored the other two representations, both build-verified + banked in IDEAS.md: `mosaic-listener` (CataRT grain-corpus navigation ‚Äî the lab's **first concatenative synthesis**, strongest novelty bank) and `motif-memory` (autocorrelation pitch-transcription ‚Üí symbolic memory ‚Üí Markov/transformational re-performance, the *Voyager*/*Cypher* lineage). Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß287 ‚Üí DEEP brief A ‚Üí `275-memory-loom` (research-first rule path (a)).

## ¬ß288 ‚Äî 2026-06-02 ‚Äî Non-octave tuning: the Bohlen‚ÄìPierce scale (the one repeat-interval the lab has never touched)

**Mandate**: every cycle opens with a 15-min dive. This is a kids cycle (288 % 2 = 0) and the JURY's standing demand is *"audit the SOUND ‚Äî the lab's most monotonous axis."* Recent kids fires have already broadened it (slendro gamelan `268`, just-intonation beating `272`, banked Hindustani raga + West-African polyrhythm). The dive went looking for a sound world that breaks an assumption *every* prior prototype shares ‚Äî even the microtonal ones.

### The Bohlen‚ÄìPierce scale ‚Äî 13 equal steps of a **tritave (3:1)**, not an octave `[foundational technique (1972), active 2026 scene]`
**Sources**: *Bohlen‚ÄìPierce scale* ‚Äî Xenharmonic Wiki (en.xen.wiki) + Elaine Walker / ZIA (ziaspace.com/academic/bp) + Wikipedia. Independently discovered by **Heinz Bohlen (1972)** and **Max Mathews & John R. Pierce** (Bell Labs, ~1984). Freshness anchor that it's a *live* scene, not a museum piece: *"A huge online gallery of microtonal music to listen to"* (The Microtone Fox, **Medium, May 2026**) and Elaine Walker's forthcoming Bohlen-Pierce album. Cross-referenced with the active real-time-resonator literature (*Interactive Neural Resonators*; *Rigid-Body Sound Synthesis with Differentiable Modal Resonators*) for the timbre side.

**Why it's surprising / useful**: every pitched piece in 280+ prototypes ‚Äî pentatonic, gamelan slendro, Hindustani JI, whole-tone ‚Äî still repeats at the **2:1 octave**. Bohlen‚ÄìPierce throws the octave out entirely: its repeat interval is the **tritave = 3:1** (a perfect twelfth, 1901.955¬¢), divided into **13 equal steps** (ratio `3^(1/13) ‚âà 1.0882`, ~146¬¢ each). It was engineered around **odd-harmonic** chords ‚Äî the signature **3:5:7 triad** ‚Äî which is why it has a *natural affinity with the clarinet*, whose chalumeau spectrum is mostly odd partials and which overblows at the twelfth, not the octave. The result is consonant but genuinely *alien*: a tuning that sounds "in tune" yet has no octave anywhere in it. A 4-year-old has no theory to unlearn, so they hear it as simply a new, slightly magical color ‚Äî the purest "huh, I didn't know we could do that" sound the lab can offer. **Could become a prototype**: tilt a paper creature through a sky of balloons, each tuned to a Bohlen‚ÄìPierce degree, with odd-harmonic clarinet-like voices and a few balloons forming a 3:5:7 chord so it's rooted, not random.

**Became a prototype this cycle (WIDE, three fresh non-pentatonic sound worlds)**: shipped `276-kids-balloon-tritave` ‚Äî **the lab's first non-octave / Bohlen‚ÄìPierce tuning** in 280+ prototypes (tilt + inline-SVG cut-paper + odd-harmonic clarinet voices over a tritave drone). Two siblings explored two more never-shipped kids sound worlds the same fire (both build-verified + banked in IDEAS.md): `277-kids-overtone-cave` (hum into the mic ‚Üí a raw-WebGL crystal grotto sings your voice's **harmonic-series overtones** back, khoomei/Lucier) and `278-kids-dream-flock` (wave at the camera ‚Üí frame-diff motion steers a boids flock that sings a **whole-tone / Debussy** dream cloud with no home note). Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß288 ‚Üí WIDE brief A ‚Üí `276-kids-balloon-tritave` (research-first rule path (a)).

## ¬ß289 ‚Äî 2026-06-03 ‚Äî Real-world-data sonification: composing from the live planet (the empty shelf the lab keeps walking past)

**Mandate**: every cycle opens with a 15-min dive for one surprising recent piece. This is an adult cycle (289 % 2 = 1). 271 finally built the AI-image-inside-AV the JURY demanded and 275 extended 259's long-form/memory vein ‚Äî so the loudest *remaining* JURY asks are the **categorical empty shelves the lab keeps walking past** (multi-user/WebRTC 0√ó, MIDI/OSC 0√ó, real-world-data sonification banked-but-unbuilt: `transit-pulse`/`weather-score`/`iss-pass`). The dive went looking for the live, datable frontier of turning a real external data stream into music ‚Äî the one input class the lab has never touched.

### Live earthquake / seismic sonification ‚Äî Earth as a real-time composer `[foundational technique, active 2025‚Äì26 tooling]`
**Sources**: USGS real-time earthquake **GeoJSON feeds** (`earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/summary/all_day.geojson` ‚Äî CORS-enabled, no API key, every quake on Earth in a rolling window, refreshed continuously); **seismic2midi** (PyPI seismic-data‚ÜíMIDI sonification package, **updated Nov 2025** ‚Äî confirms the field is actively tooled right now, not a museum piece); **SeismoDome** (Ben Holtzman, **Seismic Sound Lab**, Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory / American Museum of Natural History ‚Äî seismometer waveforms time-compressed into the audible band, paired with planetarium visuals, each sound carrying strength+depth simultaneously); cross-referenced Sam Nester's *Rifts* (seismic sound art). Also confirmed fresh: **Web MIDI API** MDN docs refreshed **May 15, 2026** (the browser-MIDI empty shelf is current too).

**Why it's surprising / useful**: every one of the lab's 280+ prototypes makes music *about music* ‚Äî it synthesizes audio or reacts to audio. Not one is *about the world*. A live external feed flips the source of authorship: the composition becomes non-deterministic and tied to actual planetary events, so it **never exactly repeats and you can't author it** ‚Äî the Earth does. The USGS feed is the perfect first instance because it is keyless, CORS-open, point-data-simple (mag / depth / lon-lat), and emotionally loaded (every point is a real event somewhere). The mapping writes itself: **magnitude ‚Üí loudness/duration/sub-rumble, depth ‚Üí pitch register & timbre, longitude ‚Üí stereo pan, latitude ‚Üí brightness** ‚Äî and crucially it pairs with a *non-luminous* visual (ink seismograph + graticule map) that directly answers the JURY's "ban the glow" verdict. **Could become a prototype**: poll the live feed, sound + ink-draw each new quake as it arrives, with a time-compressed "replay the last 24h" mode and a graceful bundled-quake fallback when the network is blocked.

**Became a prototype this cycle (WIDE, three empty-shelf explorers ‚Äî research-first rule path (a))**: shipped **`279-tremor-score`** ‚Äî the lab's **first live-external-API real-world-data sonification** (USGS quakes ‚Üí just-intonation/overtone synth + StereoPanner + limiter + ambient drone, drawn as a deliberately non-luminous Canvas2D ink seismograph/map; refs SeismoDome + seismic2midi + USGS). Two siblings explored two *other* never-touched empty shelves the same fire (both build-verified with all-three-present ‚Üí `‚úì Compiled successfully`, banked in IDEAS.md): **`midi-harmonograph`** (the lab's **first Web MIDI** integration ‚Äî held chords retuned to just intonation *and* drawn as a harmonograph/Lissajous ink figure, so consonance becomes visible geometry; degrades to QWERTY + on-screen keys) and **`ensemble-tabs`** (the lab's **first networked/multi-instance** piece ‚Äî same-origin browser tabs join one BroadcastChannel room locked to a wall-clock-derived 90 BPM grid via a Chris-Wilson lookahead scheduler; refs The League of Automatic Music Composers / The Hub). Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß289 ‚Üí WIDE brief A ‚Üí `279-tremor-score`.

## ¬ß290 ‚Äî Real-time browser pitch detection (for a call-and-response singing toy) ‚Äî 2026-06-03

**Sources**: **YIN** ‚Äî A. de Cheveign√© & H. Kawahara, *YIN, a fundamental frequency estimator for speech and music*, JASA 2002 (the autocorrelation-difference family; foundational, still the reference algorithm). **Chris Wilson, PitchDetect** (`github.com/cwilso/PitchDetect`) ‚Äî the canonical Web Audio implementation: `getFloatTimeDomainData` ‚Üí normalized autocorrelation ‚Üí parabolic interpolation; the lab already builds on Wilson's two-clocks scheduling in `256`. **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening: A Composer's Sound Practice*** (2005) ‚Äî listening reframed as an active, *answering* practice (the conceptual spine of call-and-response). **Freshness anchor** (date-verified): MusicalBoard, "How Browser-Based Pitch Detection Works ‚Äî From Web Audio API to WebAssembly", **2026-05-05** (within 30 days), plus the "Voice Composer" Show HN (browser pitch‚ÜíMIDI/strudel) ‚Äî confirms in-browser f0 detection is an actively-tooled 2026 area, not museum tech. Citations checked; no invented arXiv IDs.

**Why it's useful**: the lab's mic pieces all use FFT magnitude or RMS ‚Äî none estimate fundamental frequency, so none can *quantize what you sang* and answer it. Autocorrelation f0 + octave-collapse to a scale degree is the missing primitive that turns "the mic hears energy" into "the toy heard your *note*." Pairs naturally with the JURY's anti-glow / non-pentatonic mandate: snap detected pitch to **Lydian**, answer it with a soft mallet + diatonic third, draw it as matte cut-paper. The O(n¬≤) autocorrelation is cheap enough at a 2048 window throttled to ~130ms; for heavier needs WebAssembly (MPM/YIN) is the documented next step.

**Could become a prototype**: a grown-up **live-vocal harmony companion** (mic ‚Üí f0 ‚Üí chord under your voice in real time); a **pitch-matching game** (sing to "catch" a floating note); the canon/round deepening of 280 (Echo answers while you're still singing).

**Became a prototype this cycle (kids, solo ‚Äî research-first rule path (a))**: shipped **`280-kids-echo-canyon`** ‚Äî the lab's **first call-and-response / canon piece** and first autocorrelation-pitch-detection piece. Child sings ‚Üí RMS-gated phrase capture ‚Üí autocorrelation f0 ‚Üí octave-collapsed C-Lydian degree ‚Üí Echo (far-cliff paper creature) replays the phrase note-by-note + a diatonic third for a round-like shimmer, over a drone+limiter; matte cut-paper Canvas2D (anti-glow), mic input (touch was banned at √ó4 this window), self-playing demo when no mic. Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß290 ‚Üí autocorrelation = subsystem 1 of `280`.

## ¬ß291 ‚Äî Real-time GPU isosurface extraction is now a browser primitive (sound ‚Üí volumetric form) ‚Äî 2026-06-03

**Sources**: **William E. Lorensen & Harvey E. Cline, "Marching Cubes: A High Resolution 3D Surface Construction Algorithm," SIGGRAPH 1987** ‚Äî the foundational algorithm that extracts a polygonal isosurface from a scalar field (three.js's `MarchingCubes` addon is a direct descendant). **Freshness anchor (date-verified)**: **Will Usher, "WebGPU Marching Cubes" (willusher.io, 2024)** + his **Twinklebear/webgpu-marching-cubes** (GPU-parallel) and **webgl-marching-cubes** (Rust+WASM, ~10‚Äì50√ó over pure JS) repos ‚Äî they report WebGPU performance *on average very close to native Vulkan*, i.e. GPU isosurface extraction now runs at near-native speed in the browser. **Secondary**: networked-music-performance research puts the Ensemble Performance Threshold at <25 ms end-to-end (the latency wall that a serverless same-origin tab-ensemble sidesteps by sharing `Date.now()`).

**Why it's surprising / useful**: every one of the lab's 280+ visuals draws **meshes, particles, shaders, or 2D canvas** ‚Äî *none reconstructs a surface from a scalar field.* Isosurface/marching-cubes was historically an offline scientific-viz bake (CT/MRI volumes), too heavy to run per-frame in a browser. The 2024 GPU result flips that: a metaball field driven by live FFT bands can be polygonized **every frame**, so sound literally re-sculpts a single connected 3D form in real time. It's the cleanest "huh, I didn't know we could do that" the lab can offer on the visual axis, and it's a genuinely new *rendering paradigm* (not a new palette) ‚Äî exactly the escape from templates the mandate keeps asking for.

**Could become a prototype**: a sound-driven living volume ‚Äî bass swells a core, mids orbit, highs flick flecks at the edges, loudness breathes the whole form, spectral centroid shifts its hue; fed by mic, a dropped file, or his real Welcome Home piano via the `/api/audio` track-id pattern. Deepening path: a **WebGPU compute** isosurface (the field eval + marching cubes on the GPU) for much higher resolution at framerate; **per-note metaball spawning from onset detection** so individual piano notes bud off the surface.

**Became a prototype this cycle (WIDE, three explorers across three non-canvas2d outputs ‚Äî research-first rule path (a))**: shipped **`283-piano-isosurface`** ‚Äî the lab's **first marching-cubes / volumetric isosurface** (three.js `MarchingCubes(48)` field of 8 band-driven metaballs; file/mic/track-id/synth source; refs Lorensen & Cline 1987 + the 2024 WebGPU lineage + Anadol). Two siblings explored two *other* never-used techniques the same fire (both build-verified with all-three-present ‚Üí `‚úì Compiled successfully`, all `‚óã Static`; banked in IDEAS.md): **`281-midi-harmonograph`** (held chord ‚Üí a **harmonograph** parametric figure in **raw WebGL2**, JI-lock retunes synth+pendulums so consonance becomes a clean closed curve; the *harmonograph geometry* is the novel bit ‚Äî Web MIDI itself already exists at `4-operator`) and **`282-ensemble-tabs`** (the lab's **first networked / `BroadcastChannel` piece** ‚Äî same-origin tabs lock to a `Date.now()`-derived 90-BPM grid via a Chris-Wilson lookahead scheduler; raw-WebGL2 graphic score; refs The League of Automatic Music Composers / The Hub). Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß291 ‚Üí WIDE brief C ‚Üí `283-piano-isosurface`.

## 2026-06-03 ‚Äî Cycle 292 research dive (research-first rule; kids cycle)

**Source**: *nlm: Real-Time Non-linear Modal Synthesis in Max* ‚Äî arXiv **2603.10240** (March 2026) ¬∑ https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.10240 ‚Äî a set of C++ Max externals doing efficient **real-time non-linear modal synthesis** of **strings, membranes, and plates**, with live control of physical parameters and loadable custom modal data. Cross-refs (foundational): circular-membrane **Bessel modes** (Rayleigh, *Theory of Sound*, 1877; the inharmonic drum-head partial set ~1.59 : 2.14 : 2.30 : 2.65 ¬∑ f‚ÇÄ), tympani/drum **tension-nonlinearity pitch-glide** (Morrison & Rossing), **stick-slip friction excitation** of a modal resonator (the physics of a bowed string / a rubbed Tibetan singing bowl ‚Äî Inacio, Antunes & Wright 2008), and mass-spring/Verlet **soft-body** deformation (Provot 1995 cloth).

**Why it's surprising / relevant**: the lab has 130+ kids instruments and the JURY's standing verdict is *"audit the SOUND ‚Äî it's the most monotonous axis; force non-pentatonic."* Almost every kids voice is a **plucked Karplus-Strong string, a triangle bell, or an additive sine** played in **C-major pentatonic**. Modal synthesis attacks that at the root: instead of choosing notes on a scale, you choose a **physical object** (a drum skin, a bell, a sheet of metal, a blob of jelly) and *strike / rub / squish* it ‚Äî the pitches that come out are the object's **eigenmodes**, which are inharmonic and non-pentatonic *by physics*, and a 4-year-old already has a body-level intuition for "hit it harder ‚Üí it bends." Critically, **playable** physical-modeling is absent: the lab has *cymatics visualizers* (`19-cymatics`, `165-cymatics` ‚Äî they *draw* Chladni/Bessel patterns but you don't play them) and *Karplus strings* (waveguide, 12√ó), but **zero playable membrane/plate modal instruments, zero friction/stick-slip excitation, zero mass-spring-soft-body‚Üíaudio**. It's a whole untouched sound family that is maximally kid-embodied.

**Could become a prototype (this cycle, WIDE ‚Äî three physical objects, three never-used techniques, all non-canvas2d, all non-pentatonic; research-first rule path (a))**:
- **`284-kids-thunder-drum`** ‚Äî a big circular **drum skin** (Bessel-mode mesh, three.js): tap/multi-touch to strike; strike *position* picks the modal mix (center = round boom, rim = bright slap), strike *velocity* drives a **non-linear tension pitch-glide** (the "bwooOOWww" of a thunder drum / tympani). First **playable membrane modal synth** in the lab. ‚Üê expected winner (most visceral for a 4yo, most novel *sound*).
- **`285-kids-singing-bowl`** ‚Äî a 3D metallic **singing bowl** (three.js): drag a finger round the rim to **rub** it ‚Üí stick-slip friction builds a sustained inharmonic shimmer that grows with rub speed; tap = a struck bell. First **friction/stick-slip excitation** in the lab.
- **`286-kids-jelly-choir`** ‚Äî wobbly translucent **soft-body jelly** creatures (mass-spring/Verlet mesh, raw WebGL2 or SVG): poke/squish to deform; deformation energy drives a modal "wobble" voice; squish two together ‚Üí they harmonize in just intonation. First **mass-spring soft-body ‚Üí audio** in the lab.

Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß292 ‚Üí WIDE briefs A/B/C ‚Üí ship the strongest.

## ¬ß293 ‚Äî Corpus-based concatenative synthesis / audio mosaicing is browser-feasible now ‚Äî 2026-06-03

**Research-first dive (mandate), cycle 293, web tools healthy.** Topic chosen to load-bear the cycle's build (the queue's #1 bank, `mosaic-listener`): can the lab do real-time **concatenative synthesis** (a.k.a. audio mosaicing) in a browser?

**Findings (dated, verified):**
- **CataRT** (Diemo Schwarz, IRCAM) ‚Äî the canonical interactive corpus-based concatenative synthesis system: slice a corpus into grains, tag each with audio **descriptors**, lay them out in a 2-D descriptor space, and **re-synthesize a target by KNN-matching grains** to a moving target ‚Äî interactively ("play the cloud by hand") or from a target score/live input. This is the exact paradigm the build follows. `[foundational]`
- **"The Concatenator: A Bayesian Approach to Real-Time Concatenative Musaicing"** ‚Äî arXiv **2411.04366** (Nov 2024). A real-time, audio-guided system that concatenates corpus windows to re-create the harmonic + percussive aspects of a target stream. Confirms real-time musaicing is a live, current research area, not a museum technique.
- **Lee & Pasquier ‚Äî "Musical Agent Systems: MACAT and MACataRT"** ‚Äî arXiv **2502.00023** (Feb 2025). MACataRT extends CataRT with a **factor-oracle** temporal model + a self-listening "congruence" parameter for generative live performance. The clearest "where mosaicing is going" pointer (the build's deepen-path: add a factor oracle so it continues a phrase rather than only chasing the cursor).
- **FluCoMa** (Fluid Corpus Manipulation) ‚Äî discourse thread **"Live audio mosaicing on the web"** + `fluid.bufnmfcross~` (NMF-inspired mosaicing, used in album productions since 2021). Freshness anchor that **browser-side** mosaicing is being actively built today.

**Why it fits Resonance**: it is the lab's first *new synthesis paradigm* in a while (the JURY keeps asking the lab to escape its templates with new **techniques**, not new palettes), it is bullseye on Karel's "**use his real Welcome Home piano**" directive (the corpus *is* his recording; you re-compose with shards of it), and the descriptor-space atlas is a legible, novel visual. Browser-feasible: ~600 grains √ó a 256-pt centroid DFT analyzes in <1 s; KNN over ‚â§600 grains is a trivial per-trigger linear scan; grain playback is just windowed `BufferSourceNode`s indexing one decoded buffer (no per-grain copies).

**Became a prototype this cycle (research-first rule path (a))**: `285-mosaic-listener` ‚Äî drag a cursor through an atlas of grains (his piano, or a procedural fallback) and the piece is endlessly re-assembled from the nearest grains. Deepen path: real YIN+MFCC descriptors, PCA/UMAP 2-D layout, a KD-tree for thousands of grains, onset-aligned grains, and a MACataRT factor-oracle so it plays on its own.

---

## ¬ß295 ‚Äî Real-time body-tracking & head-tracked spatial audio are now browser primitives ‚Äî 2026-06-03

**Research-first dive (mandate), cycle 295, web tools healthy.** Topic chosen to load-bear the cycle's WIDE adult fire ‚Äî three empty shelves the lab has walked past for 290+ prototypes: **body-tracking input (0√ó)**, **a 2nd real-world-data source**, and **non-screen / spatial audio (0√ó)**.

**Findings (dated, verified):**
- **MediaPipe Pose Landmarker (Google AI Edge)** ‚Äî `@mediapipe/tasks-vision` runs **fully in the browser**, outputs **33 3-D body landmarks in real time** from a webcam via `detectForVideo()`, GPU-delegated WASM. The web guide + `google-ai-edge/mediapipe-samples-web` are current; loadable as a CDN ESM bundle (`vision_bundle.mjs`) with no npm install. Tellingly, the search surfaced fitness/posture demos everywhere but **almost no audio-reactive / creative-coding** use ‚Äî i.e. body‚Üímusic is a genuinely open shelf, not a crowded one. This is the lab's first chance at a **MediaPipe / body-tracking** piece (the ambition-floor's own example of a never-used technique). `[browser primitive, actively maintained 2026]`
- **Head-tracked spatial audio went mainstream at CES 2026** ‚Äî THX **Spatial Audio+** (dynamic AI head-tracking + virtual 7.1.4) shipping across Razer/Skullcandy/Cleer in 2026. The browser equivalent has existed quietly for years: Web Audio's `PannerNode` with `panningModel = "HRTF"` + a movable `AudioListener`. The fresh insight is the **head-tracker**: you don't need WebXR hardware ‚Äî the phone's own **DeviceOrientation** sensor (or a MediaPipe face landmark) rotates the listener, so any phone with headphones becomes a head-tracked spatial-audio instrument. `[foundational API + 2026 commercial wave]`
- Cross-check: vocal **formant synthesis** (Klatt-style vowel formant tables, bandpass-shaped glottal source) is a palette the lab has *never* used ‚Äî a clean dodge of the over-corrected just-intonation/microtonal default the last-10 window converged on.

**Why it fits Resonance**: all three are immersive, personal, audio-first, and aim at empty shelves the JURY explicitly named (body-tracking / spatial-installation / non-screen). None needs a server or a key ‚Äî camera + CDN model, keyless NOAA feed, and a phone tilt sensor are all client-side.

**Became prototypes this cycle (research-first rule path (a)), WIDE 3-explorer adult fire:**
- **`287-mirror-choir`** ‚Äî camera ‚Üí MediaPipe 33-landmark pose ‚Üí a **vocal-formant choir**; your hands are two singing voices, body-openness morphs the vowel; matte "wooden-mirror" (Daniel Rozin) render, non-glow. **First body-tracking + first formant synthesis in the lab.**
- **`288-aurora-wire`** ‚Äî live **NOAA SWPC space-weather** (solar wind speed/density, IMF Bz, planetary Kp) ‚Üí an evolving cosmic drone + matte aurora curtains, themed to Karel's *Cosmic Homecoming* journey. The lab's **2nd real-world-data source** (after 279's earthquakes), proving the live-API pattern generalizes.
- **`289-still-room`** ‚Äî eyes-closed **HRTF spatial** piece you navigate by **tilting your phone** (DeviceOrientation rotates the AudioListener); find tones by facing them. **First non-screen-first + first tilt-as-controller** piece. Refs Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*, Bernhard Leitner.

## ¬ß296 ‚Äî Head-tracked spatial audio as a KIDS primitive; embodied-movement music pedagogy ‚Äî 2026-06-03
Every-cycle 15-min dive (web tools healthy). This fire targeted the unused half of ¬ß295: head-tracked spatial audio. Confirmed it's a current, ongoing 2026 story, not training-data recall ‚Äî **THX Spatial Audio+ with AI-based head tracking** debuted at **CES Jan 2026** and is rolling out across Razer / Skullcandy / Cleer / Sunplus headsets, laptops and soundbars *through 2026* (businesswire 20260105227559, briefglance coverage). The browser equivalent needs **zero special hardware**: a `PannerNode` with `panningModel="HRTF"` per source + an `AudioListener` whose forward vector is rotated by `DeviceOrientation` ‚Äî the phone's own heading sensor *is* the head-tracker. Cross-referenced the kids angle: embodied-music-cognition research (Dalcroze Eurhythmics; "interactive sonification of children's spontaneous movement / cross-modal mapping," Frid & Bresin et al., PMC5104747 [older, foundational]) says musical understanding in young children is learned through whole-body movement before notation ‚Äî i.e. a kids piece should make the child *move their body in space* rather than tap. Surprising bit: searching "spatial audio + browser + 2026" surfaces fitness/headset demos everywhere but almost **no kids / creative use** ‚Äî body-navigated spatial audio for a 4-year-old is a genuinely open shelf, and the lab had banked the adult version (`still-room`) twice without shipping. **Could become a prototype that does X**: a kids "sound safari" ‚Äî six animals hidden at fixed bearings around the child in HRTF space; the child *physically turns their whole body* to find each by ear; the screen is a footnote. Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß296 ‚Üí WIDE briefs A/B/C ‚Üí shipped **`290-kids-sound-safari`** (research-first rule path (a)). [freshness note: the head-tracked-audio wave is CES-Jan-2026 + ongoing-2026 product rollout; the embodied-pedagogy references are foundational and tagged as such.]

**Became prototypes this cycle (kids WIDE, 3 explorers):**
- **`290-kids-sound-safari`** (SHIPPED) ‚Äî turn your body in a circle to find six hidden singing animals placed around your head via HRTF; DeviceOrientation rotates the AudioListener; iOS-permission + 2s-timeout ‚Üí drag / arrow-keys / hands-free auto-tour fallback. The lab's **first non-screen-first / audio-FIRST KIDS piece** and first DeviceOrientation controller in a *shipped* piece. Refs Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*, Bernhard Leitner *Sound Space*, THX/CES-2026 head-tracking.
- **`kids-shadow-dance`** (banked, IDEAS) ‚Äî camera frame-difference motion ‚Üí a blooming singing meadow; gross dancing, not precise landmarks (technically distinct from 287's MediaPipe). Refs Dalcroze Eurhythmics; Frid/Bresin.
- **`kids-sky-band`** (banked, IDEAS) ‚Äî today's real Open-Meteo weather ‚Üí a generative kids band; first *kids* real-world-data piece. Refs John Luther Adams *The Place Where You Go to Listen*.

## ¬ß297 ‚Äî 2026-06-03 ‚Äî the recent-AV-interactive shelf is thin; harmony-as-geometry is foundational, not fresh (honest negative dive)

**Every-cycle 15-min dive (mandate), cycle 297, web tools healthy.** This was an honest dive that did NOT surface a strong last-30-days hook ‚Äî logging it as such rather than dressing an old result as new (the freshness mandate explicitly rejects re-discovering 2022-era work).
- **arXiv `cs.SD/recent` (listings Jun 1‚Äì3 2026)**: the current crop is music-*generation* ‚Äî *SketchSong*, *SegTune*, *UniVocal* ‚Äî plus speech/enhancement work. **None** is interactive AV / real-time instrument / tuning-visualization. The audio-research front this week is generative pipelines, not playable interfaces.
- **Oscilloscope music / vector synthesis** (`macumbista/vectorsynthesis`, Piksel vector-synthesis workshop, Jerobeam Fenderson *Oscilloscope Music*): a live, healthy creative-coding tradition (audio signals ‚Üí XY beam ‚Üí Lissajous shapes) but **foundational, not new** ‚Äî Lissajous is 1857; the modern scene is years-stable. `[older, foundational]`
- **Adaptive just intonation**: *Playing Music in Just Intonation ‚Äî A Dynamically Adapting Tuning Scheme* (arXiv **1706.04338**) continuously solves a linear system to retune held chords to JI in real time ‚Äî directly load-bearing for a JI-lock feature, but 2017. `[older, foundational]`
- **Web MIDI**: browsers ship MIDI 1.0 only (no 2.0 yet); Chrome 124+ now gates `requestMIDIAccess` behind a user-permission prompt (developer.chrome.com/blog/web-midi-permission-prompt) ‚Äî relevant degradation context, but not a last-30-days finding.

**Why surprising / useful (the negative result)**: the gap itself is the signal ‚Äî *audio-research output is racing on generation while interactive/playable AV instruments are an open, uncrowded shelf.* That validates the lab's whole premise. And it confirms that the cycle's build target had to come from **banked breadth**, not fresh research ‚Äî which is exactly what the 2026-06-03 JURY asked for this fire ("ship the breadth you've already paid for instead of citing new breadth").

**Became a prototype this cycle (research-first rule path (b)+(c) ‚Äî multi-cycle commitment + banked IDEAS seed, NOT research-driven)**: shipped **`291-harmonograph`** ‚Äî the banked `281-midi-harmonograph` resurrected: a played chord (MIDI/QWERTY/on-screen) draws itself as a Victorian harmonograph in raw WebGL2, JI-lock retuning synth + pendulum rates so consonance becomes a clean near-closed figure as the beating settles. **First harmonograph / harmony-as-visible-geometry in the lab; first time the AMBITION criterion #4 (multi-cycle commitment) has ever been claimed.** Refs harmonograph (Hugh Blackburn, ~1840s) + Lissajous (1857). Shipped as the winner of a DEEP 2-explorer fire; sibling `phase-scope` (continuous Pure‚áÑEqual XY-oscilloscope tuning-lock) banked. **Deepen path (cyc 299)**: sustain-pedal figure-hold, mod-wheel‚Üípendulum damping (`d·µ¢`), per-note spectral-centroid color, SVG/PNG export, optional adaptive-JI (1706.04338) so a melody self-retunes; consider folding `phase-scope` in as a "scope mode."

## ¬ß298 ‚Äî 2026-06-03 ‚Äî real-time weather sonification is a current, award-winning vein; concrete param-mappings confirmed

**Every-cycle 15-min dive (mandate), cycle 298, web tools healthy.** Targeted the banked `kids-sky-band` concept to test whether real-world weather‚Üímusic is *current* (the ¬ß297 jury soft-flagged real-world-sonification as recently over-mined; I wanted to know if the vein is live or stale before refreshing it at the kids altitude).
- **DATASONICA** ‚Äî an audiovisual installation turning environmental data into sound, **won the 2026 Data Sonification Award**. Live, award-grade, this-year proof the vein is hot, not exhausted.
- **RIT "Data Sonification Weather Chimes"** (Imagine RIT / Behrend Senior Design, exhibited **Apr 25 2026**) ‚Äî wind chimes that dynamically alter musical parameters from **real-time weather**: wind speed/direction, temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, AQI, UV. A last-30-days build with the exact mapping surface kids-sky-band needs.
- **Glasgow Max/MSP weather sonification** (whiterose dissertation, Ian Baxter) ‚Äî a clean, citable mapping grammar for a "never-ending piece with no beginning or end": **temperature‚Üípitch, cloud cover‚Üíbrightness, humidity‚Üíreverb, rain‚Üíchorus**. Foundational `[older]` but the mapping table is directly load-bearing.
- **arXiv 2408.11461** *Polyrhythmic Harmonies from the Sky* ‚Äî satellite cloud imagery ‚Üí musical compositions via algorithms; an adjacent "sky‚Üímusic" precedent. `[older]`

**Why surprising / useful**: the soft over-mined flag is wrong for *this* form ‚Äî environmental-data sonification is winning 2026 awards and shipping new installs *this quarter*, AND the lab's prior two real-world pieces (`279-tremor` earthquakes, `288-aurora-wire` space-weather, both adult/cosmic) never touched **weather** and never targeted **kids**. "The band is different every day because the sky outside is real" is a genuinely fresh *form* ‚Äî first kids real-world-data piece, first weather source, first piece whose input is literally the world outside the child's window. The RIT chimes (Apr 2026) and DATASONICA (2026 award) make the chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build direct and current.
- **Could become a prototype that does X**: a hands-free kids "sky band" ‚Äî geolocation ‚Üí keyless CORS-open **Open-Meteo** current conditions ‚Üí four sky-friends (Sun/Cloud/Wind/Rain) playing a self-evolving C-pentatonic ensemble + a WebGL2 shader sky (dawn/day/dusk/night palette from `is_day`, drifting cloud noise from `cloud_cover`, rain streaks from `precipitation`, motion from `wind_speed`). Plays with zero touch (jury's kids touch-ban) and zero permission required (geo-denied ‚Üí fixed-location fallback ‚Üí bundled sample ‚Üí always demoable). Ref: **John Luther Adams, *The Place Where You Go to Listen*** (Museum of the North, Fairbanks ‚Äî real-time geophysical data ‚Üí sound + light).

**Became prototypes this cycle (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî research-first rule path (c): consumed a banked, research-seeded IDEAS entry, re-validated current this dive):**
- **`293-kids-sky-band`** (target winner) ‚Äî see hook above. Refs John Luther Adams; DATASONICA 2026; RIT Weather Chimes Apr 2026; Open-Meteo.
- **`294-kids-voice-garden`** (sibling) ‚Äî sing/hum ‚Üí a procedural shader garden grows & sings back (pitch‚Üíflower, loudness‚Üíbloom). Voice input (jury-encouraged alt to touch).
- **`295-kids-shadow-dance`** (sibling) ‚Äî wave your whole body at the camera ‚Üí frame-difference motion energy blooms a singing meadow. Embodied gross-motion (jury "the child moves, not pokes").

## ¬ß299 ‚Äî 2026-06-03 ‚Äî chord‚Üícolor visualization is an active 2025‚Äì26 vein; this week's cs.SD is generation, not interaction (honest dive)

**The dive** (mandatory 15-min, cycle 299, adult/DEEP): targeting the cycle-2 deepening of `291-harmonograph` ‚Äî specifically the "per-note color" feature on the cycle-2 plan ‚Äî I searched for recent chord/tuning visualization work and scanned `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent`.

- **Directly applicable named reference ‚Äî Chord Colourizer** (arXiv **2510.10173**, Oct 2025): a *near-real-time* system that detects musical key/chord from audio via **Constant-Q-Transform chroma** and renders it as color on **Isaac Newton's original color wheel** (red‚Üíviolet), with an Arduino LED display. It's the cleanest modern citation for "pitch ‚Üí hue" mapping. Pairs with **midiVERTO** (arXiv 2203.13158, 2022) ‚Äî DFT-of-pitch-class "tonal Fourier" tonality space ‚Äî for the lineage of mapping harmony to a continuous visual coordinate.
- **Freshness honesty:** this week's `cs.SD`/`cs.GR` (Jun 2‚Äì3 2026) is dominated by *generative* audio-video ‚Äî **Foley-Omni** (unified multimodal soundtrack gen), **JenBridge** (long-form video soundtracking across scene transitions), inference-time scaling for joint audio-video. **Zero** new papers on interactive performance controllers or real-time harmony/tuning visualization. That's itself the finding: the research frontier this week is offline generation, so an *interactive instrument* deepening (path b, continuing a multi-cycle build) is the right move, not chasing a fresh generative paper. (Confirming current-but-hardware: Arturia **KeyStep 37 Mk2**, NAMM 2026, ships generative + expression tooling ‚Äî the expressive-controller direction is live in industry.)
- **Could become a prototype that does X:** the Chord Colourizer pitch‚ÜíNewton-wheel mapping is the seed for `291-harmonograph`'s **cycle 3** ‚Äî color each pendulum thread by its pitch class via the circle of fifths, so a chord's figure weaves visibly from its colored parts (explored in parallel this fire by the `harmonograph-spectrum` builder; banked in IDEAS).
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** path (b) ‚Äî continue the multi-cycle 291 build; the Chord Colourizer finding directly seeds its cycle-3 color layer (banked, not shipped this fire). Cycle 2 itself (pedal/damping/ink/PNG) consumes 291's own banked cycle-2 plan from the README.

## ¬ß300 ‚Äî 2026-06-03 ‚Äî browser pitch-detection is a live 2026 vein; movement-sonification has a current CHI venue (kids WIDE dive)

**The dive** (mandatory 15-min, cycle 300, kids build): two web sweeps targeting the two banked kids candidates ‚Äî voice (sing‚Üíbloom) and whole-body movement (dance‚Üíbloom) ‚Äî to confirm both forms are *current*, not recycled training-data, before building.

- **Browser pitch detection, Web Audio ‚Üí WebAssembly** ‚Äî *MusicalBoard* blog, **"Browser-Based Pitch Detection ‚Äî From Web Audio API to WebAssembly"**, dated **2026-05-05** (last 30 days). Confirms real-time, fully-local autocorrelation/YIN pitch tracking entirely in the browser tab (no server, no app, mic stays on-device) is an active, shipping pattern right now ‚Äî the exact technique the `294-kids-voice-garden` sibling uses. The privacy framing (audio never leaves the device) is itself a 2026 selling point and maps onto the lab's "analysis-only mic" rule. URL: musicalboard.com/blog/2026-05-05-pitch-detection.
- **Movement ‚Üí sound, current academic venue** ‚Äî **CHI 2026** workshop **"From Movement to Sound and Back: Movement-Based and Sonification Design Approaches"** (dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3772363.3778764). A 2026 proceedings entry on a modular, sensor-agnostic toolkit for turning body-movement data into sound ‚Äî confirming embodied movement-sonification is a living 2026 research thread, the lineage `295-kids-shadow-dance` sits in (alongside the foundational **Frid & Bresin** 2016 children's-movement sonification study and **Krueger**'s *Videoplace*, 1985). Mobile-camera + frame-difference (no MediaPipe) is the browser-feasible, dependency-free path.
- **Why surprising / useful**: both kids forms the jury asked for (#2: "the child *moves* or *sings* rather than pokes") are independently validated as *current* ‚Äî pitch-in-browser shipped a how-to **this month**, and movement-sonification has a **2026** CHI venue. So a WIDE kids fire that breaks the touch-poke form along two different fresh axes (voice + whole-body) is on-frontier, not nostalgic. Frame-difference optical-flow specifically (vs. MediaPipe skeleton) is the better fit for a constantly-wiggling 4-year-old ‚Äî gross-motion, model-free, robust.
- **Could become a prototype that does X**: (a) sing ‚Üí a procedural shader garden that grows and sings your phrase back (pitch‚Üíflower hue+height, loudness‚Üíbloom, ~2s silence‚Üícall-and-response); (b) dance whole-body at the camera ‚Üí frame-difference motion energy blooms a singing Lydian meadow with light-trails + self-silhouette; (c) tilt a firefly to wake stars, the sky remembering your path and replaying it as a lullaby (path-memory/state).
- **Became prototypes this cycle (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî research-first rule paths (a) implement this dive's movement hook + (c) consumed banked, research-seeded IDEAS entries):**
  - **`295-kids-shadow-dance`** (WINNER, shipped) ‚Äî frame-difference optical-flow ‚Üí WebGL2 ping-pong-trail Lydian meadow + ghost-dancer fallback. Chains from the CHI 2026 movement-sonification finding above.
  - **`294-kids-voice-garden`** (banked) ‚Äî autocorrelation pitch-detection (Chris Wilson / YIN) + sing-back memory + WebGL2 garden. Chains from the MusicalBoard 2026-05-05 finding above.
  - **`296-kids-firefly-tilt`** (banked) ‚Äî tilt-physics + path-memory replay lullaby (Iwai *Electroplankton* lineage); the bigger "the sky remembers your journey" stateful concept.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** path (a)+(c) ‚Äî the shipped winner implements this dive's movement-sonification hook, and all three explorers consumed banked IDEAS seeds that themselves came from recent research dives (¬ß296 shadow-dance, ¬ß298 voice-garden re-validated current here).

## ¬ß301 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 ‚Äî circle-of-fifths color mapping is the validated way to make a chord's parts visually kindred (adult DEEP dive)

**The dive** (mandatory 15-min, cycle 301, adult build ‚Äî DEEPEN `291-harmonograph` to cycle 3): targeted the cycle-3 "per-note color" plan item with two sweeps (note‚Üícolor mapping + arxiv cs.SD recent) to confirm the *choice of hue mapping* before building, and to verify the form is current.

- **The design question**: should each note's hue come from the **chromatic circle** (semitone = one hue step) or the **circle of fifths** (a fifth = one hue step, `(pc*7)%12`)? The dive answers decisively for circle-of-fifths when the goal is for a *chord* to read as a coherent family: it maps tonally-near keys to perceptually-near colors, so a triad (root + third + fifth, which are spread on the chromatic circle but clustered on the fifths circle's color wheel) renders as **three distinct-but-kindred hues** rather than three clashing ones. Corroborating, current references: **Jack Ox**'s Color/Harmony wheel based on the circle of fifths (researchgate); **maddie lim, "12 Tone Color Theory"** (Observable, interactive notebook) which builds exactly the `(pc*7)` fifths‚Üíhue ramp; **ColorMusic ChordMap** (a shipping circle-of-fifths color tool). Anchored by the lab's freshest named reference, **Chord Colourizer** (arXiv 2510.10173, 2025 ‚Äî CQT chord detect ‚Üí Isaac Newton's 7-color wheel).
- **Honest freshness finding**: this week's `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` is again dominated by *generative* audio-video (synesthesia-viz papers surfaced were 2012‚Äì2023, foundational not fresh). No <30-day interactive-instrument paper appeared ‚Äî consistent with ¬ß299's finding. So this is a **path (b)** dive: it validates and sharpens an in-progress multi-cycle build rather than spawning a new one. The new, dated contribution is the *mapping decision* (fifths over chromatic) backed by current color-theory tools, plus a lab-first artifact direction (vector/SVG specimen).
- **Why surprising / useful**: the "obvious" choice (rainbow by semitone) is the *wrong* one for harmony ‚Äî it makes consonant chords look like noise. The fifths mapping is what makes the polychrome figure legible as a single chord with related voices. This is the difference between a gimmick and a readable instrument.
- **Could become / became a prototype this cycle**: chains directly into **`291-harmonograph` cycle 3** (shipped this fire as the DEEP winner): each held note now draws its own running-composite thread in its circle-of-fifths color (a triad visibly weaves from three kindred hues), and the figure exports as a printable **SVG vector specimen** (`<polyline>` per thread) ‚Äî the lab's first vector export. Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = **path (b)** (continue the lab's only multi-cycle thread, with the dive deciding the hue mapping).

---

## ¬ß302 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 ‚Äî call-and-response BY SINGING is a named, dated 2026 paradigm; browser pitch-detection is the client-side path (kids WIDE dive)

**The dive** (mandatory 15-min, cycle 302, kids build ‚Äî WIDE). The queued kids candidates were (a) reframe the banked `294-voice-garden` out of pentatonic and (b) lift `296`'s remember-and-replay onto a fresher input. The dive targeted the unifying mechanic both want ‚Äî a creature that *answers* the child ‚Äî to find a current, named anchor rather than recycled training data.

- **SingingSDS ‚Äî a dialogue system that responds by SINGING, not speaking** ‚Äî arXiv **2511.20972** ("SingingSDS: A Singing-Capable Spoken Dialogue System for Conversational Roleplay Applications", submitted 2025-11-26, rev 2025-12-24; EAIM2026 @ AAAI). A cascaded ASR‚ÜíLLM‚Üísinging-voice-synthesis pipeline whose contribution is the *interaction form*: the character **sings its reply back** to "foster more affective, memorable, and pleasurable interactions." Surprising because it inverts the assumption that a conversational agent talks ‚Äî it argues melody+rhythm makes a response **more memorable** than speech, which is exactly the case for a pre-reading 4-year-old. URL: arxiv.org/abs/2511.20972.
- **Browser pitch detection, fully local** ‚Äî *MusicalBoard* "Browser-Based Pitch Detection ‚Äî From Web Audio API to WebAssembly" (2026-05-05; at the 30-day edge, re-confirmed from ¬ß300): autocorrelation/YIN monophonic pitch tracking runs entirely client-side, mic never leaves the tab ‚Äî the privacy framing maps onto the lab's analysis-only-mic rule. This is the dependency-free path to implement SingingSDS's *human side* (detect the child's sung phrase) with **no model, no server, no AI call**.
- **Honest freshness finding**: a strict <30-day `cs.SD`/HN sweep is again dominated by *generative* audio-AI (latent-diffusion accompaniment arXiv 2604.07612; Voice-Composer Show HN, ~Jan 2026). The strongest dated *anchor for the call-and-response-by-singing form* is SingingSDS (Nov 2025) ‚Äî foundational for the interaction, used here as the named reference. So this is a **path (a/b)** build: implement the no-AI, kid-sized embodiment of a current named paradigm.
- **Why surprising / useful**: the lab's kids local minimum is "poke a cute thing ‚Üí it drones, no memory, no fail." SingingSDS reframes the goal as **dialogue with memory** ‚Äî the agent *listens, answers, and accumulates*. That's the antidote: a kids piece that is *different at round 5 than round 1* (the JURY's long-form-with-state ask, in a 4-year-old's vocabulary).
- **Became a prototype this cycle (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî research-first paths (a) implement this hook + (b) reframe banked seeds):**
  - **`298-kids-echo-friend`** (WINNER, shipped) ‚Äî sing a phrase ‚Üí a WebGL2 creature detects pitch (YIN/NSDF, snapped to D-Dorian), **sings it back**, and **remembers every phrase**, replaying them as a growing song every few rounds. The no-AI client embodiment of SingingSDS's sing-back; reframes `294-voice-garden` out of pentatonic AND moves `296`'s remember-and-replay onto voice input.
  - **`299-kids-clap-band`** (banked) ‚Äî clap ‚Üí spectral-flux onset detection ‚Üí a 16-step loop that layers woodblock‚Üíkick‚Üíshaker‚Üíbell as more claps land (Steve Reich *Clapping Music*). Build-reviewed.
  - **`300-kids-blow-sail`** (banked) ‚Äî blow ‚Üí breath-envelope detection (RMS √ó spectral-flatness / Wiener entropy, separates breath from tonal sound) ‚Üí wind sails a boat past singing Lydian buoys (breath-controller lineage). Build-reviewed.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a/b)**: the shipped winner implements this dive's SingingSDS sing-back hook with the ¬ß300/¬ß302 browser-pitch-detection technique, and reframes two banked research-seeded IDEAS entries out of their ruts.

## ¬ß303 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî embodied self-layering / loop-as-mirror (adult DEEP fire)

Research dive for the cycle-303 build (extending 287-mirror-choir toward its README-promised "record-a-canon-with-past-you").

- **Strict <30-day, honest finding (again):** the current `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` front is almost entirely *generative* audio-AI accompaniment ‚Äî **LiveBand** (arXiv:2606.03803, *Wed 3 Jun 2026*), **JenBridge** adaptive video soundtracking (arXiv:2606.01703, 2 Jun 2026), Streaming Generation for Music Accompaniment (2510.22105), ReaLchords (2506.14723). All are model-in-the-loop co-performance ‚Äî useful context for "the machine answers you," but not the no-AI, embodied, client-side path the lab builds.
- **Dated named anchor for embodied real-time looping** ‚Äî **LUMIA: A Handheld Vision-to-Music System for Real-Time, Embodied Composition** (arXiv:2512.17228, **Dec 2025**). You *compose by looking*: frame ‚Üí capture ‚Üí **layer** loopable musical phrases through an embodied, camera-based interface. ~6 months old ‚Üí used as a **named reference**, not a <30-day claim. It's the closest current academic articulation of "your body/camera is the loop pedal."
- **Cultural / foundational anchor (ongoing, the real reference):** the **loop-pedal one-person-choir** ‚Äî Jacob Collier and Ariana Grande's BOSS **RC-505** stacking a single performer into an ensemble (BOSS/Guitar World 2026 looper round-ups confirm the form is alive and mainstream) ‚Äî plus the canon/round tradition (**Fr√®re Jacques**, *Sumer Is Icumen In*), **Steve Reich**'s phasing (*Piano Phase*, *Clapping Music*), **Frippertronics**, and **Daniel Rozin**'s *Wooden Mirror* (287's signature). These are the spine of the build.
- **Why surprising / useful:** the lab's adult rut (per JURY 2026-06-03) is "sound makes a picture" with **zero multi-cycle depth** and **three spatial/embodied pieces banked-but-never-shipped**. The loop-pedal-as-mirror reframes the output: you are not making a picture, you are **building a choir of your past selves** and conducting/evolving it ‚Äî memory + state + live agency, the qualities the jury praised in 275-memory-loom. That's the antidote to both the audio‚Üívisual-driver rut (#5) and the orphan-build rut (#1).
- **Became a prototype this cycle (adult DEEP, 2 approaches ‚Äî extend 287's promise):**
  - **`302-mirror-canon-round`** (WINNER, shipped) ‚Äî perform a body-phrase ‚Üí tap to commit ‚Üí a past-you enters in **canon** (offset on a locked bar grid) ‚Üí conduct a 4-voice **round of yourself** in a matte wooden mirror (mute/solo/clear). Fr√®re Jacques / Frippertronics / Rozin.
  - **`301-mirror-canon-phase`** (banked ‚Üí IDEAS, = the planned **cycle-2 deepening**) ‚Äî same engine, **Steve Reich phasing**: each committed voice loops at a slightly different rate so the past-yous drift in/out of phase and the piece never repeats. Build-reviewed; fold in as a Round‚áÑPhase mode toggle.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a):** implement the loop-as-mirror / embodied-self-layering hook (LUMIA + loop-pedal-choir + Reich/canon) as the no-AI client embodiment, deepening the lab's standout 287 into its first genuine multi-cycle adult thread.

## ¬ß304 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî embodied motion ‚Üí scale-constrained musical output is a fresh, dated paradigm; tilt + physics is the no-AI client path (kids WIDE fire)

- **The dive (mandatory 15-min):** targeted the unifying mechanic the two queued kids candidates share ‚Äî a child's *body action* (clap / breath / tilt) caught and turned into stable, always-musical output ‚Äî plus an `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` scan.
- **Dated named anchor ‚Äî *Rhythm in the Air: Vision-based Real-Time Music Generation through Gestures*** (arXiv:**2511.00793**, submitted **2 Nov 2025**). A causal **temporal-convolutional network** maps body/hand landmarks ‚Üí note-level control events at **30 ms latency**, and ‚Äî the key contribution for us ‚Äî keeps the output *musical* via **rhythmic quantization + scale-constrained filtering**. The thesis: an untrained human's embodied motion is rhythmically/tonally messy, so the system's job is to *quantize it onto a grid and snap it into a scale* so every gesture sounds right. That is **exactly** the kids-lane design principle ("no fail, every input is musically correct") stated as a research result.
- **Strict <30-day, honest finding (again):** the `cs.SD/recent` front is still almost entirely *generative* audio-AI (LiveBand 2606.03803, JenBridge 2606.01703 ‚Äî both early-June 2026, model-in-the-loop accompaniment). *Rhythm in the Air* (Nov 2025, ~7 months) is the strongest **dated named anchor for embodied-motion‚Üíquantized-music**, used as a named reference + design principle, not a <30-day claim.
- **Foundational anchors for the build:** the **Aeolian harp** (a stringed instrument played by *moving air*, not fingers ‚Äî the cultural root of "tilt is the wind"); **Karplus & Strong** plucked-string synthesis (1983); **Verlet integration** rope/cloth physics (Jakobsen, *Advanced Character Physics*, 2001); **Steve Reich** *Clapping Music* (the clap-band sibling).
- **Why surprising / useful:** it reframes the kids "no-fail" rule from a UX nicety into the *technical core* ‚Äî the interesting engineering is the quantize+scale-constrain layer, not the input sensor. That licenses swapping the input modality freely (clap, breath, **tilt**) while keeping one robust "always-musical" output stage, which is exactly how to break the kids lane's "poke-a-thing-that-sings" monoculture without losing the no-fail guarantee.
- **Became a prototype this cycle (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî three embodied inputs, one always-musical output):**
  - **`303-kids-wind-harp`** (WINNER, shipped) ‚Äî **tilt** Œ≤/Œ≥ ‚Üí gravity vector ‚Üí **Verlet** string physics ‚Üí swing-threshold **Karplus-Strong** pluck, snapped to **D-Dorian** (the scale-constraint stage). Aeolian harp; Karplus-Strong 1983. The body-motion‚Üíscale-constrained-output paradigm via tilt+physics instead of a camera+TCN.
  - **`299-kids-clap-band`** (re-banked ‚Üí IDEAS) ‚Äî **clap** ‚Üí HFC-onset detection ‚Üí **quantize to a 16-step grid** ‚Üí progressively layer woodblock/kick/shaker/bell. The most literal embodiment of *Rhythm in the Air*'s quantization thesis. Steve Reich *Clapping Music*.
  - **`300-kids-blow-sail`** (re-banked ‚Üí IDEAS) ‚Äî **breath** ‚Üí Wiener-entropy breath/tone discriminator ‚Üí wind sails a boat past **Lydian** singing buoys. Breath/wind-controller lineage.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a/c):** implement the embodied-motion‚Üíscale-constrained-output paradigm (the winner is tilt+Verlet+D-Dorian Karplus) while consuming two research-seeded IDEAS entries (clap-band, blow-sail) re-validated against this dive.

## ¬ß306 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî embodied MOTION‚Üísound is a current CHI 2026 vein, and the phone's own accelerometer is the no-AI client path (kids WIDE fire)

- **Source**: *Designing Interactive Movement Sonification For Hip-Hop Dance*, **Proc. CHI 2026** (dl.acm.org/doi/full/10.1145/3772318.3790703); + the CHI 2026 Extended-Abstracts workshop *From Movement to Sound and Back: Movement-Based and Sonification Design Approaches* (10.1145/3772363.3778764). Both are 2026-dated venue/program entries ‚Äî current, not training-data recall. Foundational kid grounding carried from ¬ß300 (*Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children*, cross-modal motion‚Üísound mapping).
- **Why it's useful (mildly surprising):** the lab keeps reaching for cameras/MediaPipe whenever it wants "the body as instrument" (287/295/302), but the CHI 2026 movement-sonification thread frames the interesting work as the **mapping** (motion *qualities* ‚Üí sound), which is **sensor-agnostic**. That licenses dropping the camera for the cheapest, most-private, zero-AI body sensor every phone already has: the **accelerometer** (`devicemotion`). Grep of INDEX/READMEs: `devicemotion`/`accelerationIncludingGravity`/shake-energy = **0 hits in 300+ prototypes** (303/290 used `deviceorientation` ‚Äî *tilt/heading orientation*, a different sensor and axis from *acceleration/shake*).
- **Could become a prototype:** a kids instrument where SHAKE energy (gravity-high-passed accelerometer magnitude ‚Üí smoothed envelope ‚Üí threshold "hits" with refractory) drives a rainstick/bell shower ‚Äî a beloved 4-year-old gesture, hands-free, phone-FIRST (the review device IS the instrument), and the freshest available input axis. ‚Üí **shipped this fire as `306-kids-rain-shaker`** (D-Dorian bells + matte WebGL2 rain).
- **Honest freshness note:** the strict <30-day cs.SD/cs.GR front remains generative audio-AI (LiveBand/JenBridge family, carried from ¬ß303‚Äì304); the CHI 2026 program is the strongest *dated, named* anchor for embodied-motion‚Üísound as an **interaction** (not generation) thesis. Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = **path (a)**: the dive's "use the phone's own motion sensor" hook is exactly what the winner implements; the two banked WIDE siblings (`304-kids-clap-band` clap-onset, `305-kids-blow-sail` breath) consume ¬ß304/prior dives.

## ¬ß307 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî head MOVEMENT (not just head *position*) is what makes generic browser-HRTF legible ‚Äî the scientific spine for the lab's first non-screen spatial piece (adult DEEP fire)

- **Source**: *"Impact of HRTF individualisation and head movements in a real/virtual localisation task"* ‚Äî **arXiv:2510.09161** (cs.SD, 2025), surfaced fresh this fire alongside the live binaural-rendering front: BSANN personal-sound-zones (**arXiv:2601.06621**, Jan 2026) and *Room Impulse Response Synthesis via Differentiable Feedback Delay Networks* (arXiv:2510.00238). Carries the CES/THX-2026 head-tracked-binaural wave from ¬ß295 (the un-spent half ‚Äî head-tracked spatial audio, DeviceOrientation).
- **Why it's surprising (and load-bearing):** the lab has *avoided* HRTF spatial audio for 300+ cycles partly because Web Audio's `PannerNode panningModel="HRTF"` uses a **generic, non-individualized** HRTF ‚Äî notorious for front/back confusion and in-head localization. 2510.09161 is the empirical answer: **active head movement collapses those confusions** ‚Äî turning the head gives the auditory system the dynamic parallax it needs to externalize and place sources, and the gain from *movement* rivals the gain from *individualizing* the HRTF. So the cheap generic browser HRTF becomes legible **if and only if the listener turns** ‚Äî which is exactly the gesture a phone's `DeviceOrientation` sensor already affords for free. The "weakness" (generic HRTF) and the "interaction" (head-turn) are the same coin.
- **Could become a prototype:** an **audio-FIRST, screenless** room of sustained tones placed around the head via HRTF `PannerNode`s, navigated by rotating the `AudioListener` from `DeviceOrientation.alpha` ‚Äî the motion isn't decoration, it's the disambiguator. ‚Üí **shipped this fire as `308-orbit-choir`** (a head-tracked HRTF spatial field given a 6-minute convergence arc: voices orbit inward + glide from a detuned cluster into a warm A-minor-add9 chord, so the room is harmonically *and* spatially different at minute 6 than minute 0). The simpler fixed-room sibling `307-still-room` (Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening* / Bernhard Leitner) was build-verified and banked.
- **Honest freshness note:** 2510.09161 is ~8 months old (cs.SD), foundational rather than <30-day ‚Äî but it's the *correct* anchor: it's the dated empirical justification for why a generic-HRTF browser piece works at all, and it directly discharges the JURY's standing provocations #3/#4/#5 (ship the spatial breadth banked-but-never-shipped; build on ¬ß295's unused half; for one cycle ban the audio‚Üívisual driver entirely). Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = **path (a)+(c)**: the dive implements ¬ß295's open shelf, and the build is the lab's **first** non-screen / first head-tracked-HRTF piece.

## ¬ß308 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî "reflexive interaction" (the machine mirrors/varies the child's own input) is the validated way to make a kids music piece engaging WITHOUT a no-fail wash ‚Äî it carries the experience on memory + turn-taking, not on endless forgiving noodling (kids WIDE fire)

- **Sources**: (foundational, load-bearing) Fran√ßois Pachet, *The Continuator: Musical Interaction with Style* (IRCAM/Sony CSL, 2002) ‚Äî a system that learns a player's musical style on the fly and answers back in-kind, turning improvisation into a duet; and Anna Rita Addessi et al., the **MIROR** project / "reflexive interaction" studies (Addessi & Pachet, *Experiments with a Musical Machine: Musical Style Replication in 3/5 Year Old Children*, BJME) ‚Äî children sustain far longer, more exploratory musical engagement when the system **mirrors their input with repetition + variation** rather than just making sound. (current/dated touchpoint) *Boosting executive function in children aged 3‚Äì12 through musical training: a three-level meta-analysis* (correction PMC12869707, **Jan 2026**) and the reflexive-interaction CCI literature (PMC5266797, *Effects of Reflexive Interaction on Children's Musical Improvisation*).
- **Why it's useful (and a direct JURY answer):** JURY (2026-06-04) banned the lab's five-fire kids recipe ‚Äî "new sensor ‚Üí no-fail modal noodle" ‚Äî and asked for a kids piece with **memory or consequence** (it flagged `298-kids-echo-friend` as the unfollowed lead). The reflexive-interaction literature is the principled backbone for exactly that: the engagement engine is **turn-taking + the machine remembering and re-using the child's own material**, not a continuous bed that can't be played wrong. It reframes "memory/consequence" from a UX gimmick into the documented mechanism that makes kids stay and explore.
- **Honest freshness note (per JURY provocation #5 ‚Äî stop manufacturing a daily <30-day claim):** this is a **path-(b)/(c) dive**, not a <30-day frontier find. The Continuator (2002) and MIROR (‚âà2009‚Äì2014) are foundational; the Jan-2026 executive-function meta-analysis is the only strictly-recent dated anchor and it's about *benefits*, not technique. I'm calling it honestly: the value here is the **principle** (reflexive interaction ‚Üí memory/turn-taking is what sustains kids), which is the correct theory for discharging the JURY's #2 provocation.
- **Could become a prototype (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî three DIFFERENT memory/consequence FORMS, all breaking the no-fail-noodle recipe; INPUT=touch not phone-motion, OUTPUT‚âÝfull-screen-WebGL-shader, scale‚âÝC-major-pentatonic):**
  - **`309-kids-echo-duet`** ‚Äî Continuator/MIROR homage: child taps a phrase, a creature **remembers + answers in-style** (echo/reorder/extend/transpose the child's recent notes); motifs recur, the duet accumulates. (memory + turn-taking)
  - **`310-kids-sing-back`** ‚Äî Simon (1978) + Kod√°ly echo-singing: a creature sings a melody that **grows by one note each round**; the child echoes on big pads; right ‚Üí song grows + celebration, wrong ‚Üí kind re-teach (no punishment). The lab's first kids piece with genuine-but-forgiving **right/wrong consequence**.
  - **`311-kids-music-box`** ‚Äî Swiss cylinder musical box + **Karplus-Strong** pluck: child places pins on a slowly-rotating 3D cylinder (three.js); the loop **persists** (localStorage) and accumulates ‚Äî a thing the child builds and the box remembers, not a forgettable wash.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)+(b):** the dive's "carry the kids experience on memory/turn-taking, not a no-fail bed" principle is precisely what all three explorers implement; the winner ships, the other two bank to IDEAS.

## ¬ß309 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî the spatial-audio "exploded choir" you stand inside (Janet Cardiff's *Forty Part Motet*) is touring again in 2026 ‚Äî the named anchor for turning Orbit Choir into a head-tracked room of Karel's *own* recordings (adult DEEP-continue fire)

- **Source**: Janet Cardiff, *The Forty Part Motet* (2001) ‚Äî forty members of the Salisbury Cathedral Choir recorded individually and replayed through forty speakers in an oval you walk among, so the choir is **spatially exploded**: you stand inside the music and lean toward one singer at a time before they converge. Currently on tour ‚Äî **Marugame Genichiro-Inokuma Museum (MIMOCA), 2026** and **National Gallery of Canada, 2026** (cardiffmiller.com; mplus.org.hk; gallery.ca). Sits alongside this fire's carried binaural front (arXiv:2510.09161 head-movement HRTF, ¬ß307).
- **Why it's the right anchor (honest path-(b)/(c) dive per JURY #5):** the strict <30-day cs.SD front is still generative audio-AI, not interaction ‚Äî but the JURY's #1 provocation this fire wasn't "find novelty," it was "deepen 308 with Karel's **real** music; the 'use his actual music' directive has never reached the spatial layer." The Motet is the exact precedent for *why that deepening is a bigger idea, not just a timbre swap*: it reframes 308 from "a synthesised resolving chord" into "**his album, spatially exploded into a room you gather with your head**." The 2026 tour is the dated, named, verifiable hook; the technique (per-voice spatialization the listener navigates by moving) is foundational.
- **Built this fire as `308-orbit-choir` cycle 2** (multi-cycle thread #3 ‚Üí cycle 2): the seven synth voices are replaced by Karel's actual *Welcome Home* recordings, fetched live from the public `/api/featured` ‚Üí `/api/audio/[id]` (anon, no-auth ‚Äî the loved `227-paths-granular`/`163-paths-visualizer` path). Each recording is a looping `AudioBufferSource` that starts scattered + detuned (`playbackRate`) + dark (`lowpass`) and, over the 6-min arc, orbits inward + sharpens + settles to true pitch; facing a voice swells/sharpens it and **`navigator.vibrate`s** the instant it locks home (the lab's **first haptic output**); how far you'd gathered the room persists to `localStorage` and resumes across sessions. Falls back to the cycle-1 synth choir if the album can't be reached.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)+(b):** path (b) ‚Äî continues the standing multi-cycle spatial thread; path (a) ‚Äî implements the Motet hook (his recordings as the exploded voices) plus the un-built half of 308's own README (real stems + haptics + persistence). Could-become-next (cycle 3): read each track's `analyses.key_signature` from `/api/featured` and bias the scatter/resolve detune so the *gathered* room is in-key with itself ‚Äî his album tuned to itself in space.

## ¬ß310 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (UTC) ‚Äî game-based, *process-oriented* music learning beats single-minded right/wrong evaluation in children ‚Äî the pedagogy license for a "wrong costs a block but never ends the game" kids consequence loop (kids WIDE fire)

- **Source**: *Effects of Game-Based Learning on Piano Music Knowledge Among Elementary School Pupils: Pretest‚ÄìPosttest Quasi-Experimental Study*, **JMIR Serious Games 2026** (games.jmir.org/2026/1/e80766); alongside the 2026 systematic review *Digital game‚Äìbased learning in music education, 2011‚Äì2023* (J. Research in Music Education / SAGE, 2026, doi 10.1177/1321103X241270819). Both are 2026-dated venue entries ‚Äî current, not training recall.
- **Why it's useful (honest path-(b)/(c) dive ‚Äî per JURY #5, not a manufactured <30-day frontier claim):** the JURY (2026-06-04) banned the kids lane's five-fire "new sensor ‚Üí no-fail modal noodle" recipe and asked for a piece with **memory or consequence** ‚Äî the child can make something *wrong* and fix it. The frontier cs.SD front this fire is still generative-audio-AI (LiveBand/JenBridge family, carried ¬ß303‚Äì309), so I anchor honestly on the current music-pedagogy venue instead. The JMIR 2026 study's finding ‚Äî game-based, **process-oriented** learning outperforms "single-minded evaluation models" that reduce learning to pass/fail ‚Äî is the exact design license for a consequence loop that is real (you can be wrong) but *kind* (wrong is a reversible cost, never game-over): the engagement lives in the **process of rebuilding**, not in a score.
- **Could become a prototype (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî three DIFFERENT memory/consequence FORMS, all breaking the no-fail-noodle recipe; INPUT=touch not phone-motion, OUTPUT‚âÝfull-screen-WebGL-shader, scale‚âÝC-major-pentatonic):**
  - **`313-kids-tone-tower`** (WINNER, shipped) ‚Äî echo a growing melody; each correct note **stacks a glowing block onto a tower that grows + persists**, a wrong note **topples the top block** (gentle "aw", song shrinks by one, re-sung slower after 2 misses). Consequence made *architectural/spatial* ‚Äî the only one of the three that breaks the over-represented Canvas2D-creature-with-pads form. Refs: Simon (1978) + block-tower toys + JMIR 2026.
  - **`312-kids-sing-back`** (re-banked ‚Üí IDEAS) ‚Äî Simon-grows echo-singing with creature "Pip"; right grows the song + confetti, wrong is kindly re-sung; generous auto-shorten. The literal "make something wrong and fix it." (Had a module-init TDZ build error ‚Äî `Cannot access 'G' before initialization` ‚Äî to fix on revival.)
  - **`314-kids-echo-duet`** (re-banked ‚Üí IDEAS) ‚Äî Continuator/MIROR reflexive duet: the creature **remembers + answers in the child's own style** (echo/reorder/extend/octaveTail/motifWeave), motif-weave splices an *earlier* phrase back in as the "it remembered!" moment.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)+(c):** path (a) ‚Äî all three explorers implement the "real-but-kind consequence / memory carries the experience, not a no-fail bed" principle this dive licenses; path (c) ‚Äî they consume the cycle-308 reflexive-interaction shelf (¬ß308) re-validated against this pedagogy finding. Winner ships; the other two bank.

## ¬ß311 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 ‚Äî Real-time SPACE-weather sonification: the Sun as a live composer (the next empty real-world-data feed, distinct from the already-shipped seismic shelf)

**Mandate**: every cycle opens with a 15-min dive for one surprising, datable piece. Adult cycle (311 % 2 = 1). The seismic real-world-data shelf is no longer empty ‚Äî ¬ß289 shipped `279-tremor-score` (USGS quakes). The remaining most-wanted-least-served axes are JURY #4 (AI-pipeline-chain ‚Äî deferred this fire: requires a paid FAL budget I have no explicit per-prototype grant for) and JURY #3's *other* untouched real-world feeds. This dive went looking for the live, datable frontier of an external data stream the lab has never sonified.

### Live space weather ‚Äî the Sun scoring a piece in real time `[live feed + current solar-max event window; named-reference foundational]`
**Sources**: NOAA **SWPC** real-time JSON service (`services.swpc.noaa.gov/products/solar-wind/plasma-1-day.json` ‚Äî `[time_tag,density,speed,temperature]`; `.../solar-wind/mag-1-day.json` ‚Äî `[...,bz_gsm,bt]`; `services.swpc.noaa.gov/json/planetary_k_index_1m.json` ‚Äî `{kp_index,estimated_kp}`). All **keyless, CORS-open, refreshed every ~1 min** from the L1 spacecraft upwind of Earth (DSCOVR/ACE; SWPC is mid-migration to SOLAR-1 + IMAP I-ALiRT, with the legacy RTSW plot operational *until ~2026-06-30* ‚Äî a dated, current detail). Cross-referenced **Terry Riley & Kronos Quartet ‚Äî *Sun Rings* (2002)**, built from Don Gurnett's real plasma-wave recordings captured by NASA's Voyager/space-physics instruments ‚Äî the canonical "music made of the heavens" work ‚Äî and the SeismoDome/Seismic Sound Lab lineage (data carrying strength + register simultaneously).

**Why it's surprising / useful**: `279-tremor-score` sonifies the Earth *beneath* you; nothing sonifies the sky *above* you. We are at/near Solar Cycle 25 maximum, so the feed is currently *active* ‚Äî solar-wind speed swings 350‚Üí700+ km/s, density spikes, the magnetic Bz flips north/south, and Kp climbs during storms ‚Äî which means a piece scored by it is genuinely **non-deterministic, non-repeating, and event-loaded** (a CME arrival is an audible climax you didn't author). The mapping writes itself and is *continuous* (a drone), not discrete-event like quakes: **solar-wind speed ‚Üí register/pitch drift, density ‚Üí amplitude/texture thickness, Bz (north/south) ‚Üí consonance‚Üîdissonance (southward Bz = the storm-coupling = tension), Kp ‚Üí overall intensity + an aurora "curtain" that brightens during a storm.** Pairs naturally with an **aurora visualization** (flowing curtains, not a glow-shader). **Could become a prototype**: poll the three live feeds, drive a long-form evolving drone + aurora curtains, with a "replay the last 24h of the Sun in 3 minutes" time-compression mode and a bundled recent-storm fallback when the network is blocked.

**Became prototypes this cycle (DEEP, 2 approaches to one concept ‚Äî research-first rule path (a)):** the Sun-as-composer concept attacked two ways ‚Äî `314-solar-wind` (aurora-curtain viz + warm additive/just-intonation drone, the immersive reading) vs `315-helios-stream` (Ikeda-leaning data-field + granular/spectral treatment + numeric data readout, the clinical reading). Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build is direct: ¬ß311 ‚Üí DEEP briefs A/B ‚Üí ship the stronger; bank the other.

## ¬ß312 ‚Äî 2026-06-04 ‚Äî Voice-as-controller for very young children: real-time pitch-matching games ‚Äî and the lab already owns the pitch-detection primitive it never pointed at kids (kids WIDE fire)

- **Sources**: **Orpheus: A Voice-Controlled Game to Train Pitch Matching** (HCI in Games / ACM, dl.acm.org/doi/10.1007/978-3-030-77414-1_3) ‚Äî a Greek-myth serious game where the player moves the character *by singing the target pitch*, with continuous visual pitch feedback; **Theta Music Trainer ‚Äî "Vocal Match"** (trainer.thetamusic.com) and **Prodigies' pitch-play for 3-year-olds** (Curwen/Kod√°ly hand-signs ‚Äî pitch height made physical/visible). Live cross-check of the strict-frontier cs.SD front: arXiv:2605.12310 *Poly-SVC* (12 May 2026, polyphony-aware CQT pitch extractor) ‚Äî confirmed <30 days but it's singing-voice *conversion* (generative), not interaction, so per JURY #5 I do NOT force it as the anchor; it's noted, not claimed.
- **Why it's useful (honest path-(b)/(c) dive):** the JURY (2026-06-04) #2 still governs the kids lane ‚Äî break the five-fire "new sensor ‚Üí no-fail modal noodle" recipe; ship **memory or consequence**. The pitch-game literature converges on one robust finding for 3‚Äì5 year olds: **the voice is a legible game controller when the consequence is "the world rises only when your pitch finds the target"** ‚Äî a real, kind consequence (no buzzer, you just hover and wait), and pitch-height made visual is the Kod√°ly hand-sign idea in software. Crucially, the lab **already implemented 4096-point parabolic-interpolated autocorrelation pitch detection** (`13-piano-canvas`) but has **never pointed it at a kids voice game** ‚Äî so this is a no-new-deps, in-house-primitive build.
- **Could become a prototype (kids WIDE, 3 explorers ‚Äî three DIFFERENT input modalities, all with a memory/consequence loop, all breaking the over-represented Canvas2D-creature-with-pads form; OUTPUT‚âÝraw-WebGL-fragment-shader, INPUT‚âÝphone-motion, scale‚âÝC-major-pentatonic):**
  - **`315-kids-sing-up`** ‚Äî *voice*: a child SINGS to make a balloon climb; holding the sung pitch inside a glowing target band for ~1s pops it up a step of a friendly scale, collects a cloud, and remembers the note ‚Üí replays "their song" at the top. Consequence = pitch-match-to-rise (kind, no fail). Refs: Orpheus (ACM) + Kod√°ly/Curwen hand-signs.
  - **`316-kids-seed-garden`** ‚Äî *touch*: tap to plant seeds that GROW + SING in real time and **persist to localStorage**, spreading on their own ‚Äî a long-form evolving generative garden that's different at minute 5 than second 5 and is still there (taller) tomorrow. Memory = persistence + evolution. Refs: Toshio Iwai *SimTunes/Electroplankton* + Brian Eno *Bloom*.
  - **`317-kids-color-bells`** ‚Äî *camera*: show the camera real colored objects from the room; a center reticle samples the average hue ‚Üí rings that color's bell and adds a bead to a remembered "song basket" you can replay. Memory = collected color-song. Novel-for-lab technique: camera region-color sampling ‚Üí sound (no body/ML tracking). Refs: Toshio Iwai *SimTunes* + the color-organ / Len Lye / Fischinger visual-music lineage.
- **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a):** all three explorers implement "voice/touch/camera carries a memory-or-consequence loop, not a no-fail bed"; the WIDE fire ships the strongest and banks the other two to IDEAS.
