## ¬ß313 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 313 research dive (the serverless multiplayer browser instrument)

**Source**: networked-music lineage ‚Äî The Hub (John Bischoff, Tim Perkis et al., 1980s) & The League of Automatic Music Composers (1978) ‚Äî reborn in the browser; corroborated by the current multiplayer-browser-instrument trend surfaced in 2026 creative-coding roundups (PollySynth, a multiplayer polyphonic synth; Tone.js self-playing/generative ensembles). Web Audio scheduling per Chris Wilson, "A Tale of Two Clocks." Also confirmed `BroadcastChannel` is broadly shipped across same-origin tabs.

**Why it's surprising (and honest about freshness)**: per JURY #5 this is a deliberate **path-(c) dive**, NOT a manufactured <30-day arXiv claim ‚Äî the live cs.SD front is still generative-audio *conversion* (carried ¬ß303‚Äì312), so rather than force a frontier novelty I anchored on the named networked-music lineage + the live multiplayer-instrument trend. The genuinely surprising, *underused* technique is the **wall clock as a shared metronome**: if every same-origin browser tab derives the current step/phase purely from `Date.now()`, they all land on the same beat at the same instant with **zero clock-sync handshake** ‚Äî `BroadcastChannel` then only needs to gossip *what* each tab plays, never *when*. That sidesteps the network-music-performance <25ms-latency wall entirely (for co-located tabs) and makes a serverless networked ensemble buildable inside the scope fence with no API route.

**Could become a prototype (DEEP, 2 approaches to one concept ‚Äî "a serverless shared ensemble room; every tab is a player; the music is the SUM of the room; the wall clock is the conductor"; INPUT=multi-tab-presence (a lab-first networked input, not phone-motion/mic/camera), OUTPUT‚âÝraw-WebGL-fragment-shader, scale‚âÝC-major-pentatonic):**
  - **`318-ensemble-room`** *(rhythmic)* ‚Äî each tab edits a 16√ó8 D-Dorian **step pattern** on a wall-clock-shared grid (`barStepAt(Date.now())`, 96 BPM) played by a Chris-Wilson lookahead scheduler; the room renders as a **three.js 3D orbital constellation** of glowing players with a sweeping playhead. Refs: The Hub / League / Chris Wilson.
  - **`319-hub-score`** *(sustained ‚Äî SHIPPED)* ‚Äî each tab is one **continuous just-intonation voice** over a D root; all voices breathe together on `globalPhase(Date.now())` (~30s cycle); any tab can **Take the baton** to conduct the shared harmony field (step a modal chord progression + brightness/density/octave) and everyone glides to match. Rendered as a **no-glow Canvas2D living graphic score** (Ikeda-restrained). Refs: The Hub / League / La Monte Young *Dream House* / Ryoji Ikeda.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (c)**: ¬ß313 consumes the banked, research-chained `282-ensemble-tabs` seed (same lineage), modernized into a DEEP 2-approach fire that dodges the JURY's banned WebGL2-fragment-shader output; the fire ships `319-hub-score` (the no-glow, deeper-multi-user, lower-render-risk approach) and banks `318-ensemble-room` to IDEAS.

**Known limit worth a future dive**: `BroadcastChannel` is same-origin **same-browser-profile** only ‚Äî "the room" is one machine's tabs, not cross-device. The next research thread is **WebRTC data channels + a tiny signaling endpoint + leader-elected epoch + per-peer latency compensation** for a true cross-MACHINE shared room (the lab's first; would need an API route + the `guard` + Karel's nod on signaling infra). That is the banked 318's explicit resurrection path.

## ¬ß314 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 314 research dive (the BOWED string ‚Äî continuous excitation, not a pluck)

**Source**: real-time bowed-string physical modeling ‚Äî **Stefania Serafin & Christophe Vergez**, *real-time friction model of the violin* (IRCAM/CCRMA lineage); **Julius O. Smith III**, *Digital Waveguide Bowed Strings* (CCRMA, `dsprelated.com/freebooks/pasp/Bowed_Strings.html`); the **MDPI two-polarisation physical model of bowed strings with nonlinear contact/friction** (Applied Sciences); corroborated as *current* by 2026 physical-modeling-synth roundups + the SWAM (Synchronous Waves Acoustic Modeling) engine that computes bow friction in real time. The physical heart is **Helmholtz motion / stick-slip**: the bow alternately sticks to and slips from the string, the stick-slip period setting the pitch, the bow *speed* setting the energy/brightness.

**Why it's surprising (honest about freshness, per JURY #5)**: a deliberate **path-(b) dive** ‚Äî not a manufactured <30-day arXiv claim. The bowed-string waveguide literature is foundational, but the *gap it exposes in our lab* is genuinely fresh: in 300+ prototypes **every string is *plucked* Karplus-Strong** (one noise burst ‚Üí decay: 105/108/140/152/184/311/321‚Ä¶). The lab has **never built a continuously-excited string** ‚Äî one where energy is fed in for as long as you move, so the tone SUSTAINS and swells rather than decaying. That single change ("don't pluck ‚Äî *bow*") turns a string from a one-shot into an expressive, breath-like instrument, and it's a different feel a 4-year-old can discover with one drag.

**Could become a prototype (and DID, this fire ‚Äî WIDE winner):**
  - **`320-kids-light-loom`** *(SHIPPED)* ‚Äî a loom of glowing three.js strings of light a child **bows** by dragging; bow *speed* drives a stick-slip-flavored synth (slow = dark/scratchy noise-heavy, fast = bright/pure), the string sustains while the finger moves, multi-touch so two hands/two kids bow together. INPUT=multi-touch-bow ¬∑ OUTPUT=three.js glowing strings with per-frame standing-wave displacement (NOT Canvas2D ‚Äî which hit the 4√ó diversity ban this cycle ‚Äî and NOT a fragment shader) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE=continuous-excitation bowed-string model (lab-first) ¬∑ scale=D-Dorian (not C-pentatonic). Refs: Serafin/Vergez ¬∑ J.O. Smith waveguides ¬∑ Helmholtz motion. Born here.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)**: ¬ß314 ‚Üí WIDE-3 kids fire (one explorer per fresh axis: bow/voice/persistent-growth) ‚Üí ship the strongest novel-technique (`320-kids-light-loom`), bank the voice climb (`322-kids-sing-up`) + the long-form persistent garden (`321-kids-seed-garden`) to IDEAS.

**Future dive worth taking**: the bowed model wants a real **two-polarisation / body-resonance** pass (couple the string to a synthesized violin-body impulse response, and to a transverse+torsional pair) for a less synthetic timbre ‚Äî and an *adult* expressive version where bow pressure (pointer force / contact area) and bow position along the string become playable axes (sul tasto ‚Üî sul ponticello). That is `320`'s multi-cycle deepening path.

## ¬ß315 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 315 research dive (inhabiting the recording ‚Äî data-as-place; + chaotic synthesis as the sound that draws itself)

**Sources** (an adult WIDE-3 fire, so the dive spans the three explorers' roots):
- **Data-as-navigable-place** ‚Äî **Refik Anadol, *Latent City* (BRUSK, 2026)** and **Ryoji Ikeda, *data-verse*** ‚Äî the frontier of turning a dataset into a *landscape you move through* rather than a chart you read. The hook: a *recording* is a dataset too (time √ó frequency √ó magnitude). Nobody in the lab has turned a whole performance into a fixed 3D terrain you *fly through in sync with playback* ‚Äî the spectral pieces we own (`243-spectral-cloud`‚ù§Ô∏è, `267-spectral-drift`‚ù§Ô∏è) are *live-reactive clouds*, not inhabitable worlds.
- **Chaotic synthesis** ‚Äî *Using Strange Attractors to Control Sound Processing in Live Electroacoustic Composition* (Spasov) + *Musical Attractors: A New Method for Audio Synthesis* + the 2026 note that **AudioWorklet is now the assumed substrate for serious browser DSP** (SuperSonic = SuperCollider's engine inside an AudioWorklet, 2026). Hook: integrate a Lorenz/Chua system *at audio rate* in a worklet so the **waveform you hear IS the trajectory you see** in phase space ‚Äî Karel's explicit "strange-attractor visualization" wish, made audible.
- **Silence-as-material** ‚Äî **John Cage *4'33"*, Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*, √âliane Radigue** ‚Äî the anti-reactive lineage. Hook: invert the lab's own monoculture (wave-a-sensor ‚Üí hear-a-wash) into an instrument that blooms only in *stillness* and collapses at the first sound you make.

**Why it's surprising (honest about freshness, per JURY #5)**: a **path-(b)/(c) dive**, not a manufactured <30-day arXiv claim. Each anchor is foundational/named rather than a frontier paper ‚Äî but each exposes a real *gap in our lab*, and the freshest (Anadol *Latent City* 2026, the 2026 AudioWorklet-DSP shift) are genuinely current. The honest framing: the surprise is in the *recombination* (a recording you fly through; a sound that is its own picture; an instrument that rewards quiet), not in a brand-new technique.

**Could become a prototype (and DID, this fire ‚Äî WIDE-3 winner + 2 banked):**
  - **`321-spectral-flight`** *(SHIPPED)* ‚Äî fly through the inside of Karel's **real *Welcome Home*** recording: an offline STFT of the whole track becomes a three.js point-cloud landscape (x=time, y=log-freq, height/brightness=magnitude) and the camera flies forward locked to playback. INPUT=his real audio-file + drag/keyboard steering ¬∑ OUTPUT=three.js point-cloud (NOT Canvas2D ‚Äî the 4√ó diversity ban this cycle) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE=offline-STFT navigable spectral terrain ¬∑ refs Anadol/Ikeda. The standing "use his actual music" directive, finally in the *immersive flythrough* form.
  - **`322-strange-attractor`** *(BANKED ‚Üí IDEAS)* ‚Äî a custom AudioWorklet integrates Lorenz at the sample rate; its x-state is the audio AND the same trajectory is a glowing three.js phase-space sculpture; drag pushes œÅ from periodic ‚Üí chaotic. On Karel's named wishlist; the "sound that draws itself."
  - **`323-stillness`** *(BANKED ‚Üí IDEAS)* ‚Äî the Cage anti-instrument: sustained mic-quiet blooms a drone + a three.js light field; any loud sound collapses it; longest stillness persists across sessions. The boldest answer to the jury's "too similar" ‚Äî it *inverts* the reactive form.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)**: ¬ß315 ‚Üí WIDE-3 adult fire (inhabit-the-recording / chaotic-attractor / stillness) ‚Üí ship the strongest directive-and-love-aligned winner (`321-spectral-flight`), bank the other two.

**Future dive worth taking**: `321` wants a **cycle-2 deepening** ‚Äî spatialize the audio (pan partials by their position in the flown landscape so what you *fly toward* gets louder), branch the flight path (steer into the bass terrain vs the treble canopy), and pre-analyze multiple album tracks into a *chain* of worlds you fly between. That is the multi-cycle path noted in its README.

## 2026-06-05 ‚Äî Cycle 316 research dive (¬ß316) ‚Äî generative GROWTH algorithms as the body of a long-form piece

**Source(s):**
- Runions, Lane & Prusinkiewicz (2007), *"Modeling Trees with a Space Colonization Algorithm"* ‚Äî https://algorithmicbotany.org/papers/colonization.egwnp2007.large.pdf
- Jason Webb, *morphogenesis-resources* (differential growth, space colonization, reaction-diffusion corpus) ‚Äî https://github.com/jasonwebb/morphogenesis-resources ; the `morphogenesis` / `space-colonization-algorithm` GitHub topics (live, actively-pushed creative-coding repos as of mid-2026)
- Anders Hoff (*inconvergent*) ‚Äî differential / hyphae line growth studies
- Frontiers research topic *"Advances in Generative Models for Morphogenesis in Developmental Biology"* (submission deadline **30 June 2026**) ‚Äî confirms the topic is a current live front, not stale.

**Why it's the hook (honest framing per JURY #5):** this is a **path-(b)/(c) dive**, not a manufactured <30-day arXiv novelty ‚Äî space colonization (2007) and differential growth are *foundational* algorithms. But the dive exposed a genuine **gap in our lab**: across 320+ prototypes the only botanical growth we ever banked was a **recursive L-system** (the `321-kids-seed-garden` seed, INDEX line ~880). We have **never** built **space colonization** (branch tips competing for and consuming attractor points ‚Üí venation/tree growth) or **differential growth** (a self-avoiding chain that inserts nodes and *buckles* into coral/brain folds). Both produce growth that looks *alive* and *path-dependent* in a way an L-system's deterministic recursion never does ‚Äî which is exactly the substrate a **long-form, stateful** piece needs (the category the diversity menu flags the lab thinnest on: "different at minute 5 than minute 1, with memory and evolution").

**Could become a prototype that does X:** a child's **living musical garden grown by their VOICE** ‚Äî sing/hum ‚Üí loudness drives growth speed, pitch places the "light" (attractor) ‚Üí branches *race toward the sound* and bloom notes; the garden **persists with real wall-clock age** (fuller every morning) and **journeys harmonically** over the session, so it is genuinely a different garden ‚Äî and a different *key of music* ‚Äî at minute 5. The growth algorithm itself is the body of the piece, not decoration.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)**: ¬ß316 ‚Üí DEEP-2 kids fire on ONE concept (a long-form, voice-grown, persistent, harmonically-evolving musical garden) via two **never-used-in-the-lab** growth algorithms ‚Äî (322) **space colonization** in SVG, (323) **differential growth** in three.js ‚Üí ship the strongest, bank the other.

- **`323-kids-coral-bloom`** *(BANKED ‚Üí IDEAS)* ‚Äî the differential-growth sibling: a bioluminescent reef (self-avoiding ring, spatial-bucket-grid repulsion, node insertion ‚Üí coral folds) in three.js, voice-grown, persistent, D-Lydian harmonic journey. Build-verified (tsc+eslint clean); didn't win on the **renderer-diversity** axis (three.js was already 3√ó in the last 10 ships; shipping it would seed the next monoculture) ‚Äî resurrect on an adult cycle, or once the Canvas2D/three.js cluster cools.

## ¬ß317 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 317 research dive (the GPU as the medium ‚Äî compute-shader particle systems conditioned by live audio)

**Source (dated, verified):** **nibi** by `monoton-music` ‚Äî `https://github.com/monoton-music/nibi` (copyright 2026; a small, active repo, ~13 commits). A **WebGPU + Three.js TSL compute-shader** "particle music-video engine": ~hundreds of thousands of particles live entirely in GPU storage buffers and run a **4-phase state machine** (`flow ‚Üí forming ‚Üí text ‚Üí releasing ‚Üí flow`), 13 GPU flow patterns (galaxy spin, fractal trees) blended with 5 CPU patterns, **audio-reactive timeline** (Web Audio analysis drives timing + physics), deterministic RNG for reproducibility, and offline MP4 export. The genuinely surprising trick: particles condense into **text/shapes readable only from the authored camera angle**, then dissolve back into abstract flow. Corroborated by the broader 2026 WebGPU-compute frontier (the "Interactive Galaxy with WebGPU Compute Shaders" Three.js writeups; `lisyarus`' in-browser Particle Life on WebGPU; Maxime Heckel's *Field Guide to TSL and WebGPU*) ‚Äî 2026 creative-coding has clearly moved particle simulation **off the CPU and onto compute shaders**, where instantiation + integration + rendering all happen GPU-side with no CPU‚ÜîGPU round-trip.

**Why it's the hook (honest framing per JURY #5):** a **path-(a) dive** ‚Äî a real, dated (2026) recent piece, not a manufactured arXiv novelty, and not a foundational-algorithm fallback. The honest caveat (verified by grep, see STATE ¬ß317): **WebGPU compute is NOT new to our lab** ‚Äî `16-particle-life-gpu`, `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è, `75-houdini-particle-flock`, and `55-webgpu-audio-fx` already run `@compute`/`dispatchWorkgroups`. So the surprise nibi offers is not the *technique* but the **dramaturgy**: a particle cloud that *condenses out of chaos into a coherent form and dissolves back* as a function of an **audio phrase state machine** ‚Äî chaos‚Üîorder as the narrative arc of a piece. We had GPU particles; we had never made the chaos‚Üîform transition *be the music's structure*.

**Could become a prototype that does X:** drive that condensation with **Karel's own *Welcome Home* piano** ‚Äî rising phrase energy condenses 120k GPU particles onto a morphing target form, the rests dissolve them back into curl-noise turbulence ‚Äî so the particle dramaturgy is literally the shape of his playing. (Built this fire as `323-latent-condensation`.)

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)**: ¬ß317 ‚Üí WIDE-3 adult fire ‚Üí `323-latent-condensation` (raw-WebGPU curl-noise particle condensation conditioned by a 6-band-FFT phrase state machine, on Karel's real recording) shipped; the nibi authored-camera "readable form" idea banked as a next-cycle deepening in its README.

## ¬ß318 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 318 research dive (the long-form, REMEMBERED journey ‚Äî and the slime-mold network as a future sound-body)

**The dive (honest path-(b)/(c) per JURY #5 ‚Äî NOT a manufactured <30-day arXiv novelty claim).** Two threads.

**(1) Adaptive/generative game music as a long-form arc ‚Äî the build anchor.** The 2026 game-audio frontier has matured from "adaptive loops" to **procedural soundtracks that evolve across a player's whole journey and never fully repeat**, layering by world-state and player behavior over long campaigns (soundverse.ai "AI Music in Gaming 2026"; gamedeveloper.com "A framework for generative music in video games"; thegameaudioco.com on adaptive music for immersive worlds). Surprising/relevant because it reframes a *journey* ‚Äî not a loop ‚Äî as the unit of musical form, and it's exactly the **long-form, stateful, memory/evolution** category the diversity menu flags the lab thinnest on. Combined with **Joseph Campbell's monomyth** (departure‚Üíinitiation‚Üíreturn) and **Resonance's own multi-phase journey engine**, this is the lineage for a *kids* journey: a 6‚Äì12 min dusk‚Üídawn voyage whose harmony advances by act and whose path is remembered. **Could become a prototype that does X:** a long-form, stateful, *remembered* KIDS journey ‚Äî every kids piece in 150+ is a short loop toy; this is the first that *travels*, evolves through a real modal arc, and sings your path back on arrival. ‚Üí **Built this fire** (path-(c)-ish: the build consumes this dive's anchor) as `325-kids-paper-boat`.

**(2) Physarum / slime-mold agent networks as a generative sound-body ‚Äî banked for a future ADULT cycle.** The dive surfaced the slime-mold lineage (Jones/Jenson agent trail-field model; Adamatzky's *Physarum* biocomputing + sound-synthesis work, arXiv 1212.1203; *Simulacra Naturae*, arXiv 2509.02924, a 2025 generative ecosystem driven by agent-based slime-mold-inspired simulation + spatial audio). Surprising because Physarum agents (millions depositing/sensing/turning, networks that reorganize to connect food sources) are a **never-used technique in the lab** (our generative growth has only been L-system / space-colonization / banked differential-growth) AND a natural sound map: the network *finds paths* between sources, and path completion = harmonic connection. **Could become a prototype that does X:** an adult piece where you place "food" (tones) and a living Physarum network autonomously routes between them, the chord emerging from which sources are currently connected ‚Äî the slime *composing the connections*. Deferred this cycle: it needs a GPU/Canvas trail-field renderer (Canvas2D is banned 4√ó, three.js is the emerging cluster, WebGPU just shipped 323) and is too abstract for a 4-year-old. **Seeded in IDEAS.md as a future adult build.**

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (c)/(a)**: ¬ß318 thread (1) ‚Üí DEEP-3 kids fire on ONE concept (the lab's first long-form, stateful, *remembered* kids journey) via three distinct interaction models ‚Äî (324) tap-to-drift firefly, (325) drag-to-steer auto-scrolling parallax river, (326) hum-to-travel voice ‚Äî ‚Üí ship the strongest (`325-kids-paper-boat`), bank the other two + the Physarum adult seed.

## ¬ß319 ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 319 research dive (WebGPU-compute physarum as a 2026 frontier ‚Äî and the honest "we already have one" correction)

**The dive (path-(a) ‚Äî dated, current 2026 work ‚Äî corroborating the ¬ß318 Physarum seed before building it).** Searched the live WebGPU + audio-reactive + physarum creative-coding front. Findings:

- **`SuboptimalEng/slime-sim-webgpu`** (github) ‚Äî a clean TypeScript + **WebGPU compute** slime-mold sim; representative of a clear 2026 shift: "compute shaders are where WebGPU's advantage over WebGL becomes visceral ‚Ä¶ native compute pipeline with storage buffers, workgroup shared memory, and atomic operations." Physarum, fluid, erosion, Turing patterns are the canonical demos.
- **`bu3nAmigue/physarum-audio-reactive`** (github) ‚Äî an **audio-reactive** slime-mold sim (JS + WebGL) ‚Äî direct prior art that the physarum trail-field *can* be driven by/legible against audio.
- **ShaderVine** (`meditations.metavert.io/p/shadervine`, 2026) ‚Äî a WebGPU shader editor "built for the agentic era," forkable + MCP-controllable by AI agents; evidence the WebGPU-compute creative-coding tooling itself is now agent-native.
- Standing refs from ¬ß318 still anchor the *audio* mapping: **Adamatzky** *Physarum* sound-synthesis/biocomputing (arXiv 1212.1203); **Sage Jenson (mxsage)**; **Jones** agent model; **Simulacra Naturae** (arXiv 2509.02924, 2025 ‚Äî agent ecosystem + spatial audio).

**Why it's the hook:** confirms the banked `327-physarum-choir` adult seed rides a genuinely *current* (2026) technique wave on the WebGPU-compute path `323-latent-condensation` already proved in this lab. The novelty we can honestly claim is the **application**: mapping the slime network's **connectivity graph to harmony** (the chord = which tone-"food" sources the network has currently connected ‚Äî the slime composes the connections), seeded by Karel's real recording.

**CRITICAL HONESTY CORRECTION (verified by grep this fire):** Physarum is **NOT** new to the lab ‚Äî **`260-kids-slime-garden`** is already a full Jones/Jenson agent-trail-field sim (~3500 agents, 3-sensor steering, deposit + diffuse, in WebGL). So the ¬ß318 seed's projected ambition **#1 ("never-used technique") does NOT hold** and must not be claimed. `327-physarum-choir` clears the floor instead via **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs above) + #5 (this dive)** = 3/5, with the WebGPU-compute renderer (vs 260's WebGL) and the connectivity‚Üíharmony mapping (vs 260's pure visual glow) as the honest distinctions.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a)/(c):** ¬ß319 ‚Üí WIDE-3 adult fire on three maximally-unrelated axes ‚Äî (326) `stillness` (mic-inverted silence ‚Üí SVG bloom; Cage/Oliveros), (327) `physarum-choir` (his-recording-seeded WebGPU-compute slime network ‚Üí connectivity-driven harmony; this dive), (328) `seismic-choir` (live USGS feed ‚Üí HRTF spatial just-intonation choir; jury #3 real-world-data) ‚Äî ship the strongest, bank the other two.

## 2026-06-05 ‚Äî Cycle 320 research dive (15-min, mandatory)

### The 2026 spatial-audio + overtone-singing wave ‚Äî and "build the second non-screen piece"
**Sources**:
- HuggingFace dataset drop ‚Äî *Overtone Singing (Preview): Articulation-Level Overtone & Throat Singing Primitives* (huggingface.co, discuss.huggingface.co/t/171144, 2026) ‚Äî isolated, rights-cleared overtone/throat-singing primitives recorded specifically for generative-audio research.
- *Spatial Audio Music Production With AI Tools: The 2026 Revolution* (soundverse.ai, 2026) ‚Äî the current wave of generative tools that place voices in a high-dimensional sound field rather than on a screen.
- Foundational infra (allowed under the freshness rule as stable HRTF tooling): *The Extended SONICOM HRTF Dataset and Spatial Audio Metrics Toolbox* (arXiv:2507.05053, 2025); Google **Omnitone** (HRTF-convolution binaural rendering on the web).

**Why it's worth a look**: the frontier this month is moving sound *off the screen* ‚Äî overtone/throat-singing as a first-class generative primitive, and AI spatial-audio tools that compose by *placement in space* (azimuth/elevation/distance), not by drawing pixels. That lines up exactly with the JURY's headline provocation #2 ("build the SECOND non-screen piece ‚Äî `308-orbit-choir` found the freshest axis the lab owns and nothing followed it") and the standing "make his music **legible**, not another nebula" note. The browser already has the primitive: `PannerNode { panningModel: "HRTF" }`.

**Could become a prototype that**: a **one-person overtone cathedral** ‚Äî you sing one note, real-time YIN pitch detection snaps it to a just-intonation degree, and it spawns a sustained HRTF-spatialized voice that *orbits your head*; sing more and a spatial chord assembles around you, with the **note names printed legibly** so you recognize the chord you built. The experience lives in the ears (best on headphones); the screen is a dim compass. (This fire's WIDE explorer **`329-voice-cathedral`** is exactly this ‚Äî built, build-clean, **banked as the top next-adult build**: convert its Canvas2D compass ‚Üí SVG to clear the renderer ban, then ship as the lab's second non-screen piece.)

**Honest freshness note**: the *named* anchors (SONICOM toolbox ~11 mo, Omnitone older) are foundational HRTF infra, not <30-day; the genuinely-recent signal is the 2026 overtone-singing dataset + the spatial-audio-AI tooling wave. Used as a direction-of-the-field finding (path-c: the chain feeds an IDEAS-banked seed), not a <30-day breakthrough claim. The cycle-320 *ship* (`330-stillness`) is the jury's bolder-but-quieter break rather than the direct research implementation; the research ‚Üí build chain runs research ‚Üí `329`/`330`/`331` WIDE slate ‚Üí shipped the diversity-cleanest + 5-cycle-overdue winner, banked the research-driven voice piece for the very next adult fire.

## 2026-06-06 ‚Äî Cycle 326 research dive (15-min, mandatory)

### Spatial audio is the 2026 off-screen frontier ‚Äî and the lab has ZERO non-screen KIDS piece
**Sources (verified dates)**:
- arXiv **cs.SD/recent** (last 7 days): a cluster of fresh ambisonics/spatial papers landed Jun 4 2026 ‚Äî **SHB-AE** (arXiv:2606.04584, spherical-harmonic-beamforming Ambisonics encoding for *smartphone* mic arrays) and **Flow-HOA** (arXiv:2606.04570, generative higher-order-ambisonics encoding via flow matching). Both are about getting sound *into a 3-D field on consumer/phone hardware*.
- *Immersive Spatial Audio Solutions to Watch in 2026* (startus-insights.com, 2026) + *Music Galleries: Combining Sound Art & Visual Media in 2026* (soundverse.ai) ‚Äî 2026 installations are "synchronizing light, texture, and **spatial sound within architectural boundaries**; viewers become co-creators, their motion influences the soundscape." The field is moving sound off the screen and onto the body's orientation in a room.
- Foundational anchor (stable, allowed): browser `PannerNode { panningModel: "HRTF" }` + `DeviceOrientationEvent` ‚Äî the two primitives that make a head-/phone-orientation spatial audio game buildable client-side with no deps; this lab's own `308-orbit-choir` (the jury's only 5/5, **still the only non-screen piece in 300+**) proves the HRTF path here.

**Why it's surprising / the hook**: the JURY's standing provocation #2 ‚Äî "build the SECOND non-screen piece; `308` found the freshest axis the lab owns and *nothing followed it*" ‚Äî intersects a real 2026 research surge (spatial audio on phones, motion-as-input installations). And the genuinely under-built corner (grep-verified this fire): the lab has **108 kids prototypes and ZERO that are non-screen / audio-first** ‚Äî every kids piece is a full-screen viz. So the freshest possible kids move is NOT a third real-time consonance duet (`341` already shipped that concept; a 4th would be the monoculture the mandate exists to stop) ‚Äî it's **the lab's first audio-FIRST kids piece**: the screen goes dim, the child *turns their body/phone* and *listens* to find singing creatures placed around them in HRTF space. **Could become a prototype that**: a kids "listening adventure" ‚Äî turn toward a hidden animal's voice (HRTF azimuth/elevation), walk your ear toward the loudest one to collect it, assemble a song by ear alone, eyes optional.

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty)**: HRTF/`PannerNode`/`DeviceOrientationEvent` are foundational, NOT a <30-day technique. The recent-and-surprising signal is **direction-of-field** (the Jun-4-2026 ambisonics-on-phones papers + the 2026 motion-driven spatial-installation wave) **combined with the lab-gap audit** (zero non-screen kids in 300+). Used as a direction-of-field + lab-gap finding (path-a/c), not a frontier-technique claim. **Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build**: this dive ‚Üí a DEEP-3 kids fire on ONE concept ("the lab's first non-screen / audio-first kids listening adventure") explored via three interaction models (find-the-animal / echo-cave / song-catcher) ‚Üí ship the most verifiable + most 4yo-playable + cleanest-diversity winner.

**Also noted (deferred, adult)**: **LiveBand** (arXiv:2606.03803, Jun 3 2026, "Live Accompaniment Generation in the Audio Domain") ‚Äî directly reinforces the banked `343-live-accompanist` lane is a hot 2026 front; still blocked on a real-instrument verification pass, not a fan-out slot. Defer to a dedicated adult cycle.

## ¬ß327 ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 327 research dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî the verifiable client lane out of the "his-piano ‚Üí glowing cloud" adult rut is AUTONOMOUS / SELF-COMPOSING SOURCES (sky, decay, dynamical systems), not another heavy generative model

**Sources scanned**: arXiv `cs.SD` recent + queries on dynamical-systems / chaotic-attractor sonification and long-form generative ambient. For the **direction-of-field**: *long-form music generation went frontier in 2026 but stayed heavy* ‚Äî latent-diffusion long-form (Stable Audio, arXiv 2404.10301; InspireMusic arXiv 2503.00084) and real-time human‚ÄìAI co-performance (arXiv 2604.07612) are all **server/GPU-heavy, not client-buildable**, and the lab already carries WebGPU verification debt (323/327 never run on real hardware). For the **actionable, verifiable client anchor**: chaotic **dynamical-systems sonification** is having a 2026 moment ‚Äî *Leonardo* (MIT Press) **Vol. 59 No. 1 (2026), Roddy, Lavelle et al.**, "Generative Sonification ‚Ä¶" (agent-based modeling + waveshaping + granular synthesis), riding the long lineage of Lorenz/R√∂ssler/Chua chaotic synthesis (Chua's-circuit, the perfectcircuit "Chaotic Sound Synthesis" survey).

**Why it's surprising / the hook**: the JURY's #1 live provocation is *kill the forming adult rut ‚Äî three of four adult builds are "Karel's piano ‚Üí an abstract luminous cloud he watches"; if you use his music make it LEGIBLE, don't ship a 6th screen-viz nebula.* The exit is **autonomous / self-composing sources** that are about *something* ‚Äî and crucially are **verifiable in-sandbox** (jury #5: no new unrun GPU compute) because they're deterministic given their inputs (clock, elapsed time, an ODE). Three such sources: **(A) the real local sky** (clock + sun altitude/azimuth + moon phase + season computed *locally*, no network ‚Äî a long-form piece genuinely different at 3am than noon, √Ý la John Luther Adams *The Place Where You Go to Listen*); **(B) decay/loss itself** (a Basinski *Disintegration Loops* tape that physically wears out as you listen and is more ruined every morning ‚Äî a conceptual/critical lane the lab has only ONE entry in, `330-stillness`); **(C) a chaotic attractor** (Lorenz/R√∂ssler/Chua orbit as composer).

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty)**: local-astronomy math, tape-decay DSP, and Lorenz integration are all **foundational, NOT <30-day** techniques. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field* (the 2026 Leonardo dynamical-systems-sonification work + the fact that the frontier long-form models are un-buildable client-side) **combined with a lab-gap / anti-rut audit**: the adult lane is converging on "his-recording ‚Üí nebula," and the cleanest escape is deterministic autonomous sources. **GREP CORRECTION (the honesty the mandate demands):** the chaotic-attractor idea is **NOT a lab-first** ‚Äî `10-strange` (cycle 10) already maps a Lorenz attractor ‚Üí FM synthesis ("hear chaos"). So explorer C is a *modern re-take* (scale-snapped/musical, multi-system, edge-of-chaos morph) of a 9-year-old unloved piece, NOT a novel technique; its ambition rests on #2+#3 only, and a second Lorenz piece risks the very "too similar" the mandate bans. The slate's genuine freshness therefore lives in **A (first adult long-form local-astronomy/sky piece)** and **B (first generative-degradation/erosion piece ‚Äî grep-verified, only `275-memory-loom` matched and it's a memory weave, not tape decay)**.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build**: this dive ‚Üí a WIDE-3 adult fire (sky / decay / attractor), each deterministic + verifiable + dodging every banned tag (his-recording, Canvas2D, Anadol-cloud, kids, SVG-warming, new WebGPU compute) ‚Üí ship the most-surprising + most-verifiable + cleanest-diversity winner; bank the rest (C banked as an honest deepening of `10-strange`, not a lab-first).

## ¬ß328 ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (UTC) ‚Äî Cycle 328 research dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî live data-sonification is a 2026 "Auditory Overlays" wave; but the genuine lab gap for KIDS is CHAIN-REACTION / cause-and-effect physics, not another data feed

**Sources scanned (last 30 days bias):**
- **Meteora ‚Äî a long-distance weather audio synthesizer** (Adafruit blog, 2025-09-15) ‚Äî turns Open-Meteo weather into an ambient synth. https://blog.adafruit.com/2025/09/15/meteora-a-long-distance-weather-audio-synthesizer

- **Real-time weather data sonification** (GSA Showcase 2025). https://gsashowcase.net/work/real-time-weather-data-sonification/
- Direction-of-field: 2026 commentary frames data sonification as **"Auditory Overlays"** supplementing visual dashboards (Web Audio API as the standard engine across AR/VR browsers in 2026).

Shipping a 2nd weather-for-kids piece would be the exact "too similar in design and theme" the AMBITION & DIVERSITY mandate exists to stop. So the dive **pivoted to a lab-gap audit** instead of chasing the trend.

**The actual gap found (grep-verified across `src/app/dream/*kids*/README.md`):** the kids lane (110+ prototypes) has **ZERO chain-reaction / Rube-Goldberg / cause-and-effect-cascade pieces** ‚Äî `domino`/`chain reaction`/`Newton's cradle`/`marble-machine` all return 0. This is a genuinely fresh kids axis AND a cognitively perfect one: cause‚Üíeffect-by-repetition is exactly how a 4-year-old learns (KIDS.md principle #3), and "arrange in space, then release in time" is a new creative frame (composition is the layout; the trigger is the performance). Three nearby ideas were checked and rejected as already-built: pendulum (`164-kids-pendulum-harp`), cymatics (`19`/`165-cymatics`), harmonograph (`291-harmonograph`).

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** collision physics + additive-bell synthesis are foundational, NOT <30-day. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field* (the 2026 data-sonification "Auditory Overlays" wave that I deliberately did NOT chase because the lab already covers it) **combined with the lab-gap audit** (zero chain-reaction kids pieces in 110+). The build's novelty is **lab-first-for-kids**, claimed as such.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí grep-correction (weather is taken; pivot) ‚Üí lab-gap audit (chain-reaction = 0 in kids) ‚Üí a DEEP-3 kids fire on ONE concept ("the lab's first chain-reaction music machine for kids") via three physics/interaction models (domino topple / marble cascade / Newton's-cradle impulse) ‚Üí ship the most-legible + most-playable + most-robust winner (`350-kids-bump-along`); bank the other two. ## ¬ß330 ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 330 dive ‚Äî browser body/hand-tracking is now COMMODITY (deliberately not chased); the honest yield was a kids-lane saturation audit ‚Üí glass-harp / fill-to-tune as the genuine empty shelf

**Sources scanned (last-30-days lens):**
- **Google MediaPipe** hand/body landmark tracking + **Bolt's face/hand-tracking starter templates** (bolt.new blog, ~May 2026) ‚Äî building real-time webcam body tracking in the browser is now a one-click template, no special hardware. *Signal:* the camera-tracking technique the lab kept treating as "ambitious" is now commodity ‚Äî and a grep confirms the lab already uses MediaPipe/camera/hand/shadow heavily (`234-kids-hand-creature`, `258-kids-mirror-pets`, `287-mirror-choir`, `302-mirror-canon-round`, `268`/`295` shadow). So camera tracking is NOT a novel axis here; deliberately NOT chased.
- arxiv cs.SD/cs.GR recent + interactive-sonification / shadow-puppet body-tracking searches ‚Äî nothing <30-day surfaced that is both client-buildable AND fresh-for-this-lab.

**The real finding ‚Äî a kids-lane SATURATION AUDIT.** At 110 kids prototypes, "fresh concept" means fresh *combination*, and three plausible fresh ideas were grep-killed before briefing: **wave-interference‚Üíchord** is already the LOVED `133-kids-ripple-pond` (verbatim: "when two ripples meet ‚Ä¶ a chord plays from both notes"); **radial/step/groove sequencer** is covered (`145-dot-seq`, `177-lego-sequencer`, `199-spin-wheel`, `216-band-builder`, `218-xylophone-drops`); **soft-body/Verlet** overlaps `303-wind-harp`. The genuine empty shelf, grep-clean across 110 kids + 350+ total: **no glass-harp / glass-armonica / fill-to-tune instrument.** Tuning an instrument by *filling vessels with water* makes the water level a **visible pitch dial** ‚Äî the most literal cross-modal "see-what-you-hear" mapping the lab can produce, the direct kids answer to JURY #1 ("make it legible").

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** glass-armonica acoustics (Benjamin Franklin, 1761), water-glass pitch physics, and Web Audio oscillator-bank synthesis are foundational, NOT <30-day. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field* (browser body/hand tracking has become commodity, which is why the lab should stop treating it as its ambition frontier) **combined with the saturation audit**. The build's novelty is **lab-first-for-the-mechanic** (fill-to-tune + a rubbed-glass continuous-armonica voice), claimed as such ‚Äî with the explicit caveat that *continuous-excitation synthesis itself* is not a lab-first (`320-kids-light-loom` did a bowed stick-slip string).

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí camera-tracking is commodity + already 5√ó+ in lab (NOT chased) ‚Üí three fresh-concept probes grep-killed against existing/loved pieces ‚Üí saturation audit finds glass-harp/fill-to-tune is the empty shelf ‚Üí a DEEP-3 kids fire on ONE concept ("a tuned water-glass instrument") via three play-models (tap-strike / swipe-armonica / pour-conservation) ‚Üí ship the most-surprising + novel-sound + best-first-open winner (`355-kids-glass-armonica`); bank the other two. ## ¬ß331 ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 331 dive ‚Äî real-time beat tracking / tempo induction is the under-built "make the machine's listening legible" corner; the freshness yield is a direction-of-field read, not a <30-day paper

- WebSearch on real-time onset detection / beat tracking / tempo induction in Web Audio (2026) and arxiv cs.SD recent + WebGPU/creative-coding May-2026 feeds.
- Surfaced foundational, not <30-day: **Masataka Goto & Yoichi Muraoka, "A Real-time Beat Tracking System for Audio Signals" (ICMC 1995)** and **"OBTAIN: Real-Time Beat Tracking in Audio Signals" (arXiv:1704.02216)** ‚Äî onset-strength ‚Üí autocorrelation tempo ‚Üí cumulative-beat-score phase. The WebGPU/creative-coding feeds (three.js 2026 WebGPU workflows, iPlug3 agentic-era AV framework, in-browser GPU DAWs) confirm a *field direction* (GPU compute for FFT/synthesis is going mainstream in-browser) but nothing client-buildable-AND-fresh-for-this-lab landed in 30 days.

**The real finding ‚Äî a lab-gap audit against Karel's stated priorities + the jury.** A grep across INDEX + all READMEs: **"beat track" = 2 (passing mentions only, no tracker prototype); "score follow" = 2 (the banked live-accompanist that lost curation 3√ó on verifiability); "onset" = 59 / "tempo" = 48 (used as parameters, never as the *subject*).** So a piece whose entire point is *the machine inducing tempo and showing you the lock* is genuinely lab-first. It sits at the intersection of **jury #1 (make it legible ‚Äî show what was heard vs what's predicted)** and **Karel's stated priority #3 (live-performance fitness ‚Äî onset/beat tracking, micro-latency)**, neither of which the adult lane had answered with an analysis-forward tool. The verifiability trap that killed the banked live-accompanist (needs a real instrument) is dodged by **driving it from an internal known-tempo groove** (112 BPM) so the demo proves the pipeline against a known answer with zero mic.

**Two adjacent grep-fresh seeds banked the same dive** (the WIDE siblings): **Euclidean / Bjorklund rhythm generation = 0 prior** (Toussaint 2005) ‚Üí `357-euclidean-orrery`; **Tonnetz / neo-Riemannian navigation = 0 prior** (Euler 1739 / Riemann / Cohn) ‚Üí `359-tonnetz-walk`. All three are music-theory-literate but on orthogonal axes (analysis / generative-rhythm / harmony-navigation) ‚Äî a deliberate WIDE answer to "too similar."

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** beat tracking (Goto 1995), Euclidean rhythms (Toussaint 2005), and the Tonnetz (Euler 1739) are all foundational, NOT <30-day. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field* (in-browser GPU DSP going mainstream + the saturation of the lab's screen-viz habit) combined with the lab-gap audit. The novelty is **lab-first-for-the-technique**, claimed as such ‚Äî FFT exists elsewhere, but no prior prototype *tracks a beat*.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí WebGPU/beat-tracking field scan (no <30-day client-buildable hit) ‚Üí lab-gap grep audit (beat-tracking = 0, despite onset/tempo as params 100+√ó) ‚Üí the legibility + live-performance intersection nothing had filled ‚Üí a WIDE-3 adult fire on three orthogonal music-theory axes (rhythm-analysis / generative-rhythm / harmony-navigation) ‚Üí ship the most-legible + most-self-verifying winner (`358-beat-mirror`, internal-112-BPM known-answer demo); bank `357-euclidean-orrery` + `359-tonnetz-walk`. ## ¬ß336 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 336 dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî the lab has SIMULATED clap-back five times but never actually LISTENED for a clap; real acoustic onset detection + a growing call-and-response is the genuine kids-lane gap

**Dive (WebSearch, kids embodied rhythm / call-and-response / clap detection, 2024‚Äì2026):** the search front is research-papers, not <30-day shippables ‚Äî but it surfaced a strong, *dated, named* anchor the lab can build on: **Drumball** (Audio Mostly 2024) ‚Äî a tangible call-and-response drumming instrument inspired by the **West African Djembe**, built around orality and circular African concepts of musical time. Adjacent finds: an ACM 2024 study on **African Talking Drums** for children's literacy + socio-emotional development via multimodal rhythmic interaction; the long line of embodied-rhythm-for-infants work (active drumming raises audiovisual-synchrony sensitivity, PMC4482535). These reframe call-and-response not as a game gimmick but as a culturally-grounded literacy/bonding mechanic ‚Äî and they all assume the child makes a *real physical sound*, not a screen tap.

**The real finding ‚Äî a lab-gap audit.** Grep across INDEX + all READMEs: the lab has shipped call-and-response/echo rhythm FIVE times ‚Äî `154-kids-clap-back`, `213-kids-echo-drum`, `209-kids-drum-tap`, `150-kids-beat-builder`, `218-kids-xylophone-drops` ‚Äî and **every one of them is TOUCH** (the device plays a pattern, the child *taps the screen* back). Not one uses the **microphone to hear a real hand-clap**. Onset detection itself is banked-but-never-shipped (`299`/`304-kids-clap-band` lost curation twice). So a piece where a 4-year-old claps with their actual hands, off the couch, and the phone *hears it via real-time onset detection* is genuinely lab-first on the INPUT√óTECHNIQUE axes. Pair it with a **growing-memory** call-and-response (the pattern accumulates Simon-style) and it also answers the JURY's standing kids critique that `350-kids-bump-along` regressed to a memory-less pentatonic poke-toy.

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** spectral-flux/HFC onset detection is foundational (Bello et al. 2005), not <30-day; *Clapping Music* is 1972; Drumball is 2024. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field + lab-gap*, not a fresh paper ‚Äî consistent with the jury's accepted "lab-gap + foundational + named-reference is a valid dive." Novelty claimed precisely: **lab-first acoustic-clap onset detection**, not "first onset math."

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí embodied-kids-rhythm scan (Drumball 2024 + talking-drum literacy work) ‚Üí lab-gap grep (5√ó clap-back, all touch; 0√ó real mic clap) ‚Üí a WIDE-3 kids fire on three orthogonal *embodied, non-screen-tapping, memory-bearing* inputs ‚Äî **clap (mic onset)** `371-kids-clap-along` ¬∑ **breath (RMS√óspectral-flatness)** `372-kids-blow-garden` ¬∑ **touch-but-compositional (sequence record-replay)** `373-kids-firefly-path` ‚Äî ship the one that best fuses lab-first technique + the jury's memory/consequence ask; bank the other two. Named refs in the winner: Drumball (Audio Mostly 2024) ¬∑ Steve Reich *Clapping Music* (1972) ¬∑ the African call-and-response / talking-drum tradition.

## ¬ß337 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 337 dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî the JURY's #1 adult ask, finally taken: a live **accompanist** that follows YOUR tempo, solved the 358 way (built-in known performance), MIDI-driven to dodge the mic ban

**Dive (WebSearch, real-time score following / automatic accompaniment, 2024‚Äì2026):** two strong, *dated, named* anchors directly on the JURY's standing "build the score-follower / live-accompaniment" provocation:
- **Matchmaker** ‚Äî *"Matchmaker: An Open-source Library for Real-time Piano Score Following and Systematic Evaluation"* (arXiv:2510.10087, **Oct 2025**). An open library that frames real-time piano score following as the necessary component of automatic accompaniment / page-turning / lyric-alignment, and ‚Äî critically for us ‚Äî **systematically compares online-DTW vs HMM followers** on causality, timing precision, and output latency. The freshest directly-relevant hit (~8 months, not <30-day ‚Äî claimed as the named ref / direction-of-field, not a freshness claim).
- **The ACCompanion** ‚Äî Cancino-Chac√≥n, Peter, Widmer (arXiv:2304.12939). An **expressive automatic piano accompanist** that produces a human-like accompaniment following the soloist's **tempo, dynamics, and articulation** in real time, in the symbolic (MIDI) domain. This is the *expressive-following* north star the multi-cycle thread aims at.
- Foundational lineage: **Simon Dixon, "Live Tracking of Musical Performances Using On-Line Time Warping" (MATCH, 2005)** ‚Äî the bounded-window, forward-only online DTW used in the winner; and **Antescofo** (Arshia Cont, IRCAM) ‚Äî cue-based reactive following.

**The real finding ‚Äî why NOW, and why it dodges the 3√ó rejection.** Score-following has lost curation **three times** and was explicitly re-rejected cycle 335 for three reasons: (a) `26-score-follow` + `251-live-duet-trader` already exist ‚Üí "not lab-first"; (b) its acoustic form needs **mic INPUT**, which the JURY bans; (c) the **real-instrument verifiability wall** a sandbox can't cross. The dive's insight is that all three dissolve if you reframe the piece: build the live **accompanist** (the machine *plays the accompaniment in sync with your rubato*, a different deliverable from `26`'s cursor), drive it from **MIDI/computer-keyboard** (not mic ‚Üí dodges the JURY ban), and **verify it the 358 way** ‚Äî a built-in baked "known performance" with rubato so the whole pipeline self-proves with zero hardware. The JURY's own adult provocation #1 prescribes exactly this ("solve it the way 358 did, with a known internal answer"). The lab-first handle is the **online-DTW *warping-path* as the legibility visual** ‚Äî the bending diagonal is the most legible possible picture of "the machine is following you," and grep shows no prior prototype draws an alignment path.

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** online DTW (Dixon 2005), Antescofo, and HMM followers are foundational, NOT <30-day; Matchmaker (Oct 2025) and The ACCompanion (2023) are the dated named refs, claimed as such. The recent-and-surprising signal is *direction-of-field* (real-time AI accompaniment papers stacking up ‚Äî see ¬ß331/¬ß30 latent-diffusion accompaniment) + the lab-gap/JURY-debt audit, consistent with the accepted "lab-gap + foundational + named-reference is a valid dive."

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí score-following / accompaniment scan (Matchmaker Oct 2025 + The ACCompanion) ‚Üí reframe to dodge the 3√ó rejection (accompanist not cursor ¬∑ MIDI not mic ¬∑ self-verifying baked performance) ‚Üí a **DEEP-3** adult fire attacking ONE concept ("an accompanist that follows YOUR rubato through a known piece") via three follower algorithms ‚Äî **online DTW + warping-path viz** `375-tempo-canon` ¬∑ **HMM/forward note-event follower** `374-the-accompanist` ¬∑ **cue/anchor FSM (robust to wrong notes)** `376-the-prompter` ‚Äî ship the most-legible + most-self-verifying winner; bank the other two. Named refs in the winner: Matchmaker (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025) ¬∑ Dixon MATCH (2005).

## ¬ß339 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 339 dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî deepening the Accompanist thread: 375 follows your TEMPO; the ACCompanion's whole thesis is that a real accompanist must also follow your DYNAMICS and ARTICULATION

**Dive (WebSearch, expressive automatic accompaniment / expressive performance rendering, 2024‚Äì2026):** the cycle-1 ship (`375-tempo-canon`) realized online-DTW tempo-following + the warping-path viz, and its README explicitly marked cycle-2 as "fold in expressive *dynamics* following (ACCompanion-style)." This dive nails the named spine and a genuinely-recent anchor:
- **The ACCompanion** ‚Äî Cancino-Chac√≥n, Peter, Widmer, *"Combining Reactivity, Robustness, and Musical Expressivity in an Automatic Piano Accompanist"* (IJCAI 2023, **arXiv:2304.12939**; code at github.com/CPJKU/accompanion). The north star: a modular **Score Follower + Accompanist** that renders a *human-like* accompaniment conditioned on the soloist's **tempo, dynamics, AND articulation** in real time (symbolic/MIDI domain). Its "beginner mode" is exactly our frame: the machine plays the left hand while you play the right. 375 only follows tempo ‚Äî the ACCompanion's central claim is that tempo alone sounds mechanical; **expression-coupling (your velocity‚Üíits dynamics, your articulation‚Üíits articulation)** is what makes it feel like a duet partner.
- **Peransformer** ‚Äî *"Improving Low-informed Expressive Performance Rendering with Score-aware Discriminator"* (arXiv:**2510.10175**, **Oct 2025**) ‚Äî the freshest directly-relevant hit (~8 months): an adversarial **score-aware discriminator** sharpens expressive *dynamics/timing* rendering from sparse score info. Direction-of-field signal that expressive *rendering* (not just alignment) is the live research front.
- Foundational lineage carried from ¬ß337: **Dixon MATCH online-DTW (2005)**; HMM/forward followers (Matchmaker arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025, systematically compares DTW vs HMM); Antescofo (Cont/IRCAM) cue-based following.

**The real finding ‚Äî why deepen NOW.** The JURY (2026-06-06) made "**actually deepen something ‚Äî stop deferring**" its loudest recurring complaint ("the lab keeps promising depth and buying breadth; no multi-cycle thread advanced"), and named the **legible/instructional** lane (358, 353, 375) as the direction to *feed*. The Accompanist is BOTH: the jury's #1 ask (freshly taken at 375) AND a hot multi-cycle thread one cycle old. Deepening it from a tempo-tracker into a full **expressive** accompanist is the "massively bigger" move Karel asked for ‚Äî and it's lab-first on the technique axis (grep: no prior prototype *follows a soloist's dynamics/articulation*; 375 follows only tempo). The verifiability wall that killed score-following 3√ó is already solved (the 358-style baked "known performance"); cycle-2 extends that baked demo to also vary **dynamics (cresc./dim.) and articulation (legato/staccato)** so the expression-following is *visibly proven on a phone with no MIDI*.

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** the ACCompanion (2023) + Dixon (2005) are foundational/named refs, NOT <30-day; Peransformer (Oct 2025) is the dated recent anchor, claimed as such. Per the jury's "pick ONE recent named work and implement its specific technique," this fire implements the ACCompanion's **expressive-coupling** mechanic specifically (tempo+dynamics+articulation following), not a generic "inspired by." Consistent with the accepted "lab-gap + foundational + named-reference is a valid dive."

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build:** dive ‚Üí expressive-accompaniment scan (ACCompanion IJCAI2023 + Peransformer Oct2025) ‚Üí the cycle-2 deepening 375's own README queued (expression-following) ‚Üí a **DEEP-3** adult fire attacking ONE concept ("an accompanist that follows your *expression*, not just your tempo") via three technical approaches ‚Äî **coupled expressive accompanist** `380` (velocity‚Üídynamics + note-duration‚Üíarticulation, online-DTW tempo) ¬∑ **robust dual-follower DTW‚áÑHMM + confidence handoff** `381` (the robustness axis: a fumbled passage re-locks; +velocity‚Üídynamics) ¬∑ **anticipatory/predictive accompanist** `382` (tempo-curve prediction places the accompaniment *with* you, not lagging behind; +expression). Ship the most-legible + most-self-verifying winner; bank the other two as the thread's cycle-3 deepenings. Named refs in the winner: The ACCompanion (arXiv:2304.12939, IJCAI 2023) ¬∑ Peransformer (arXiv:2510.10175, Oct 2025) ¬∑ Dixon MATCH (2005).

## ¬ß340 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ kids WIDE fire ¬∑ emergent-systems dive

**Dive (mandatory 15-min, web tools healthy).** Targeted the unifying mechanic the three kids briefs share ‚Äî *a simple local rule producing surprising global order*, the thing every emergent-system simulation does and the lab has under-used in the kids lane (kids pieces skew to direct poke‚Üísound toys). Scanned arXiv cs.SD/cs.GR recent + Kuramoto/synchronization sonification + cellular-automata-music.

**Decisive recent anchor ‚Äî arXiv:2603.08352** ("Synchronization of higher-dimensional Kuramoto oscillators on networks: from scalar to matrix-weighted couplings," **March 2026**). A genuinely current-quarter paper on the **Kuramoto model** ‚Äî the canonical model of spontaneous synchronization in coupled-oscillator systems (random initial phases/frequencies ‚Üí if coupling K is strong enough, the population phase-locks). *Why it's surprising / fits Resonance:* synchronization is a transcendent, embodied phenomenon (the SE-Asian firefly flash-waves; Huygens' "sympathy of clocks," 1665) that the lab has **never made audible** ‚Äî and it's intrinsically *legible* (the order parameter r is a built-in 0‚Üí1 "togetherness" readout), which is exactly the legible/instructional register the JURY (2026-06-06) named as the lab's direction to feed. **Could become a prototype that:** gives a child a meadow of out-of-phase fireflies and lets them *gather* the fireflies (tilt) until a cluster spontaneously blinks ‚Äî and sings ‚Äî in unison; r becomes a visible meter and the scattered pitches collapse onto a shared chord. ‚Üí built this fire as `384-kids-firefly-chorus`.

**Honest freshness note (JURY #5, no fake novelty):** Kuramoto (1975) + Huygens (1665) + Witten‚ÄìSander DLA (1981) + Deneubourg (1990)/Dorigo (1992) ACO are the *foundational named refs* the three builds implement; arXiv:2603.08352 (Mar 2026) is the dated recent anchor, claimed as such (not <30-day, but current-quarter and directly on the winner's technique). Consistent with the accepted "lab-gap + foundational + named-reference is a valid dive" ‚Äî and this time the dive surfaced a *current-quarter* paper on the exact mechanic, partially answering the jury's "the freshness mandate has quietly failed ~6 dives running" worry.

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a):** dive ‚Üí emergent-systems-as-kids-mechanic ‚Üí a **WIDE-3** kids fire, three orthogonal emergent simulations each turned into an always-musical D-Dorian toy with a fresh non-banned input: **Kuramoto synchronization** `384` (tilt ‚Üí fireflies sync) ¬∑ **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** `383` (shake ‚Üí coral grows) ¬∑ **ant-colony stigmergy** `385` (camera-wave ‚Üí singing pheromone trails). Shipped the most-legible + most-surprising + best-hands-free-demo winner (`384`); banked the other two as IDEAS ¬ß340 seeds. Named refs in the winner: Kuramoto (1975) ¬∑ Huygens (1665) ¬∑ arXiv:2603.08352 (Mar 2026).

## ¬ß341 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ adult WIDE fire ¬∑ generative-form / procedural-music dive

**Dive (mandatory 15-min, web tools healthy).** This cycle's adult lane had quietly converged on a **legible-analysis monoculture** ‚Äî five of the last-window adult pieces are harmonic/score analysis (358-beat-mirror, 365-cadence-ladder, 370-tonal-map, 375-tempo-canon, 380-expressive-accompanist) and the adult ambient sub-lane is the JI-drone the 2026-06-06 jury said to *starve*. So the dive deliberately hunted for a **non-analysis, non-drone** adult direction: searched real-time *generative form / procedural music* (build-and-drop tension arcs, PMG with designer tension knobs) and *camera-motion ‚Üí sound* (optical-flow granular).

**Anchors (honest freshness caveat ‚Äî the recurring pattern: no <30-day client-buildable hit, foundational/named anchors instead):**
- **Procedural Music Generation** (game-audio practice, ongoing through 2026) ‚Äî real-time, *state-driven*, non-repeating music with designer-facing **tension/emotion "knobs"** that steer multiple synthesis layers at once (emergentmind PMG overview; soundverse 2026 EDM-production writeups on build/drop tension-and-release arrangement). *Why it fits Resonance:* Karel's **standing priority #4 is journey-engine alternatives** ‚Äî "EDM build-and-drop, ritual, jazz responsive, cinematic narrative‚Ä¶ as alternate arcs" ‚Äî and the lab has **never** shipped one; every prior journey-ish piece is the slow psychedelic 6-phase ambient arc. **Could become a prototype that:** drives a continuous club track off a single **tension scalar [0‚Üí1]** state machine (GROOVE‚ÜíBUILD‚ÜíDROP‚ÜíRELEASE) where the one knob simultaneously sweeps the riser, filter, snare-roll acceleration and every visual ‚Äî making the arc legible in *form/energy* the way the analysis pieces are legible in *harmony*. ‚Üí **built this fire as `387-drop-engine`** (the lab's first EDM/club journey engine).
- **AudioFlow** (Andrew Kihs ‚Äî webcam **optical-flow ‚Üí granular synth**, color-averaged grain control) + the **TouchDesigner Optical-Flow TOP** paradigm (foundational, ~2023‚Äì24). *Could become:* motion-field-driven grains (‚Üí banked `388-flow-grains`).
- (Aeolian-tone wind sonification ‚Äî **Aeolian harp** + **Strouhal vortex-shedding** f‚âàSt¬∑U/d ‚Äî grounded the third explorer, banked `386-aeolian-atlas`, a live Open-Meteo wind sonification.)

**Chain today's-research ‚Üí today's-build = path (a):** dive ‚Üí "the adult lane is too analytical/ambient; the unbuilt Karel-priority is a generative journey-engine alternative" ‚Üí an **adult WIDE-3** fire, three unrelated non-banned directions: **generative EDM build-drop arc** `387` (internal arc state-machine, club vibe) ¬∑ **live-wind aeolian sonification** `386` (Open-Meteo external-API, cosmic vibe) ¬∑ **optical-flow granular** `388` (camera, embodied vibe). ## ¬ß342 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ kids DEEP fire ¬∑ directional-DLA dive (15-min, mandatory)
**The honest freshness read (the jury's standing flag holds ‚Äî ~7th dive running with no <30-day client-buildable hit):** searches for "diffusion-limited aggregation real-time interactive 2026" and "audio-reactive generative art May 2026" surfaced only foundational/applied work, no fresh browser-buildable artifact. But the dive paid off as a *technique sharpener* for this cycle's banked build. Two recent DLA papers reframe the algorithm in a way that directly fixes why the lab's coral seed keeps losing curation (its growth is a gentle isotropic *blob*, not a legible *magic moment*):
- **"Toward a Realistic Model of Diffusion-Limited Aggregation: Rotation, Size-Dependent Diffusivities, and Settling"** (ACS Omega, pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsomega.2c03547) ‚Äî adds a **settling bias** (walkers drift downward) and **size-dependent diffusivity** (bigger particles wander slower) to classic isotropic DLA. The settling bias turns a radial blob into **directional, anisotropic growth** ‚Äî exactly how a real reef grows *upward from a seabed*.
- **"Multi-Particle Diffusion-Limited Aggregation"** (arXiv:1603.03218) + a 2024 **Numba-accelerated 2D DLA** framework ‚Äî confirm the spatial-hash + frontier-walker pattern is the right perf path for a phone (port to a typed-array grid + bucket neighbours).

**Why it's the right sharpener:** the lab has *banked* DLA coral three times (323 / 361 / 383) and never shipped it ‚Äî every curation note says "beautiful but the wow is gentler than the winner." The fix isn't a new technique, it's **directionality**: a downward settling bias + a stick-from-below rule makes the coral **climb up the screen toward the child**, a legible directional bloom instead of an amorphous splat. **Could become a prototype:** *the coral seed, finally shipped* ‚Äî shake-to-stir plankton that **settle downward and accrete upward** into a branching reef, each locked branch ringing one D-Dorian note (the coral you grow IS the song). This cycle consumes the long-deferred ¬ß340/¬ß332 seed and gives it the directional twist that earns the ship. Research‚Üíbuild chain: ¬ß342 dive ‚Üí DEEP 3-approach kids fire on directional-DLA coral.

## 2026-06-07 (cycle 344) ‚Äî Real-time singing/voice visualization is a *tutor* monoculture; the kids "no-wrong mirror" is unbuilt

**Dive (15-min, mandatory):** scanned the 2026 real-time singing-feedback landscape + recent formant work.
- **2026 singing visualizers** ‚Äî Pitch Diagram (pitchdiagram.com), Sing Sharp, Yousician, Singing Carrots: all show a live pitch contour, and all are **correction tools** ‚Äî they score you against a target note/melody, so "wrong" is structurally baked in. ([screenapp.io/blog/best-voice-rating-tools-2026](https://screenapp.io/blog/best-voice-rating-tools-2026), [pitchdiagram.com](https://pitchdiagram.com/))
- **AURORA formant-to-tongue inversion** ‚Äî arXiv:2603.17543 (**Mar 2026**): a real-time formant-biofeedback model whose explicit design lesson is that raw formants/resonances are *too abstract* for users, so it renders a **friendlier proxy** (predicted tongue shape) instead. ([arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17543](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17543))
- Foundational anchors for "machine shows what it heard": **Visible Speech** sound spectrograph (Potter/Kopp/Green, Bell Labs, 1947); **Peterson‚ÄìBarney** vowel-formant chart (1952); the **Bouba/Kiki** cross-modal sound-symbolism effect (K√∂hler 1929; Ramachandran & Hubbard 2001).

**Why it's surprising / the opening:** every shipping voice-viz tool in 2026 is a *judge*. There is no playful, no-wrong-answer **kids' listening mirror** ‚Äî a 4-year-old sings or speaks, and the machine *delightedly* shows what it heard and echoes it back, with no target and no fail state. This also takes JURY (2026-06-07) provocation #6 head-on: turn the adult **analysis/score-following reflex** into a **kids** toy, breaking both macro-ruts (kids=emergence-sim, adult=analysis) in one fire. AURORA's lesson ‚Äî show a *proxy*, not the raw feature ‚Äî is the design key: a 4yo can't read a formant chart, but they understand *color* and *shape*.

**Could become prototypes (today's WIDE build, cycle 344):** three kids mirrors, each a different "what it heard," each a renderer the lab has been avoiding (SVG / DOM-CSS, NOT WebGL2/three.js), each in a non-D-Dorian tonal world (just-intonation / continuous pitch):
- **392-kids-voice-mirror** ‚Äî PITCH: sing ‚Üí a living SVG ribbon-creature draws your melody contour ‚Üí a soft just-intoned choir sings it back. Ref: Visible Speech (1947).
- **393-kids-vowel-color** ‚Äî VOWEL/FORMANT (lab-first): say "aah/eee/ooo" ‚Üí a DOM/CSS color field morphs per vowel ‚Üí echoes the vowel back. Ref: AURORA (2026) + Peterson‚ÄìBarney (1952).
- **394-kids-sound-monster** ‚Äî DYNAMICS/TIMBRE: loud‚Üîsoft + bright‚Üîdark voice ‚Üí an SVG blob-monster swells & spikes round‚Üîjagged ‚Üí purrs/roars back. Ref: Bouba/Kiki (K√∂hler 1929; Ramachandran 2001).

## ¬ß345 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 (UTC) ¬∑ real-time spatial audio / ambisonics on the web (research-first dive for cycle 345 adult DEEP)

**Sources (web tools healthy):** arxiv cs.SD recent listing + targeted web search on browser ambisonics / head-tracked binaural.

**Genuinely-recent hits ‚Äî dated June 4, 2026 (~3 days old at dive time):**
- **SHB-AE** ‚Äî "Spherical harmonic beamforming based Ambisonics encoding and upscaling method for smartphone microphone array" (arXiv:2606.04584, Thu 4 Jun 2026). Ambisonics *encoding* on phones ‚Äî exactly the device class this cycle's prototype runs on.
- **Flow-HOA** ‚Äî "Generative Joint Optimization for Ambisonics Encoding via Flow Matching" (arXiv:2606.04570, Thu 4 Jun 2026). Higher-order ambisonics encoding via flow matching ‚Äî the generative front of the same field.
- (Adjacent neural-spatial front, Oct‚ÄìNov 2025, for context: HRTFformer transformer HRTF upsampling arXiv:2510.01891; Graph-Neural-Field HRTF personalization arXiv:2511.10697.)

**Foundational implementation spine (named refs used in the build):** **JSAmbisonics** (Politis/polarch) + **Google Omnitone** ‚Äî the canonical Web-Audio FOA/HOA encode‚Üírotate‚Üídecode pipelines (virtual-loudspeaker HRTF decode). **Zotter & Frank, _Ambisonics_, Springer 2019** ‚Äî max-rE decode weights. Also surfaced: **WHAM (Webcam Head-Tracked Ambisonics)**, JSAmbisonics' soundfield-rotation primitive, "Spatial Audio Online" (HOA streamed + binaurally decoded + head-tracked in-browser).

**Why it's surprising / the opening (and why it pays the #5 debt):** the JURY's standing complaint is that the lab keeps *researching* the 2026 ambisonics wave and never building it ‚Äî and that the freshness mandate (#5) has failed ~7‚Äì8 dives. Both are addressed at once: SHB-AE/Flow-HOA are the freshest dated anchors the lab has cited in ~8 dives AND are *directly on-technique*; and the build finally implements a true B-format soundfield (encode ‚Üí SO(3) head-tracked rotation ‚Üí max-rE HRTF decode), the genuine leap past the lab's existing per-source-panning binaural pieces (7-spatial, 29-scene-spatial).

**Became a prototype (today's DEEP build, cycle 345):** `394-soundfield-room` (shipped) ‚Äî six JI drone voices as a coherent rotatable ambisonic field, head-tracked, eyes-closed. Two banked siblings (IDEAS ¬ß345): `396-soundwalk` (locomotion + Vibration-API haptics ‚Äî Janet Cardiff lineage; the cycle-2 deepening) + `395-listener-orbit` (rotate the AudioListener ‚Äî the lightweight baseline).

## ¬ß346 ‚Äî 2026-06-07 ‚Äî The 2026 microtonal / just-intonation browser wave (research-first dive, cycle 346 kids WIDE)

**Dive target:** JURY 2026-06-07 provocation #2 ("D-Dorian is the new C-pentatonic ‚Äî build in a genuinely foreign tonal world: just intonation / microtonal, octatonic, raga, whole-tone") + the standing #5 freshness debt. Web tools healthy.

**Genuinely-recent, client-buildable hits:**
- **purified-synth / "Pure Intonation"** ‚Äî a browser synth+sequencer (Ruby + WASM ‚Üí Web Audio API) that computes **exact just-intonation frequency ratios** for chords rather than approximating with 53-TET. Presented at **RubyKaigi 2026** (Apr 2026, nagachika). The relevant idea for us is foundational and trivial to port: pure tuning is just small-integer frequency ratios over a fundamental ‚Äî no WASM required. (rubykaigi.org/2026/presentations/nagachika.html)
- **Scale Workshop** (Sevish & contributors) ‚Äî modern browser tool to author/modify/export tuning files (.scl) and play any tuning in real time with synth presets. The de-facto microtonal sandbox of the scene. **Tune.js** ‚Äî a Web-Audio tuning library porting **3000+** historical tunings/temperaments from the Scala archive (github.com/abbernie/tune). Both are live, actively-referenced 2026 ecosystem tooling.
- Adjacent kids-AV context (slightly older but on-point): **"From Gaze to Music"** (PMC12729308, **Dec 4 2025**) ‚Äî children's eye-tracking (fixation duration/patterns) conditions an LSTM to generate personalized music, with artwork **color‚Üínote** mapping via Newton's spectrum; reported **+42.5%** fixation duration, **+73%** gaze in meaningful regions vs. no music. **Rhythm Pals 2026** (App Store) ‚Äî first mainstream kids music app using **camera movement** as input (validates camera-for-kids commercially).

**Why it's surprising / the opening:** the lab has 390+ prototypes but its *tonal* world has collapsed twice (C-pentatonic ‚Üí D-Dorian); every kids piece defaults to the same modal bed. The microtonal-browser scene is a ready, well-documented escape hatch into tonal worlds that *sound genuinely other* ‚Äî pure just intonation (beating-free consonance impossible on a piano), gamelan slendro/pelog (between-the-cracks-of-12-TET), whole-tone (no leading tone, floats). For a 4-year-old the *experience* of "this sounds magic / unlike anything" is the whole point ‚Äî you don't need them to understand cents.

**Honest freshness note:** RubyKaigi-2026 / Scale Workshop / Tune.js are weeks-to-months old, not <7-day ‚Äî but unlike the last several dives they are **directly client-buildable and on-technique**, so this dive actually changed the build (it set the *tonal* axis for all three explorers) instead of being a nightly apology.

**Became prototypes (cycle 346 kids WIDE):** `397-kids-crystal-bloom` (shipped ‚Äî pure just intonation, SVG, breath) ¬∑ banked `398-kids-rainbow-roll` (Balinese slendro, pure DOM/CSS, tilt) ¬∑ banked `399-kids-aurora-step` (whole-tone, Canvas2D, camera) ‚Äî three foreign tonal worlds in one fire. **Future seam:** an *adult* long-form just-intonation / microtonal drift piece (Harry Partch, La Monte Young's *Dream House*, Scale Workshop / Tune.js tuning import) ‚Äî the JI/microtonal world deserves a DEEP cycle, not just a kids toy.

## ¬ß350 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ kids DEEP fire (breath-grown long-form garden)

**Dive (mandatory 15-min, web tools healthy).** Targeted the seam the three briefs share ‚Äî *breath as the input to a slow, long-form generative form that accretes state*. Honest #5 status: **no <30-day client-buildable technique anchor again** (consistent with the lab's running freshness miss the jury flagged). What the dive *did* surface is a genuine **2026 current-lineage trend**, not a dated paper: the convergence of (a) generative-ambient + guided-breathing meditative AV ‚Äî e.g. soundverse's 2026 "AI Music Videos for Meditation and Ambient Music" piece on fine-tuning mood/visual-consistency for long-form breathing sessions, and Isotonik/Manifest Audio's "evolving ambient world" generative-worldscape tooling (pads transpose, bass morphs, ornaments sparkle between pulses) ‚Äî confirming a live market pull toward *breath-paced, slowly-evolving* AV rather than reactive loops. For the technique itself the build rests on **foundational named refs**: **L-systems (Aristid Lindenmayer, 1968)** for the recursive-branching grove grammar, **pelog / Indonesian gamelan inharmonic tuning** for the foreign tonal world, and **Brian Eno's generative-ambient** lineage for the always-evolving bed. 

**Chain research ‚Üí build = path (a)/(c):** the dive's "breath-paced long-form generative, state + memory, not loops" framing ‚Üí a DEEP 3-renderer fire of *breathing grows a night-garden that remembers every breath*, which also consumes the long-standing IDEAS bank `403-kids-breath-garden`. The build does NOT claim #5; it clears the floor via #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + **#4 (long-form-stateful, the expensive criterion)** + #3 (named refs).

Sources: soundverse.ai/blog "AI Music Videos for Meditation and Ambient Music" (2026); isotonikstudios.com "Building an Evolving Ambient World in Live with Manifest Audio Tools."

**Could become a prototype:** an *adult* long-form generative companion to 408 ‚Äî your **Welcome Home** piano fed through a breath-paced granular bed that slowly transposes/morphs over 10+ minutes and never returns to its opening state (the Eno generative-worldscape lineage, adult version of the breath-garden's accretive memory).

## ¬ß352 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ kids DEEP fire (the articulatory copycat-mouth)

**Dive (mandatory 15-min, web tools healthy).** Targeted JURY 2026-06-07 provocation **#6** ("a *kids* listening/analysis toy ‚Äî a child sings, the machine shows what it heard ‚Äî would break both macro-ruts in one fire") crossed with #1 (force off the WebGL2 rut ‚Üí SVG) and #2 (foreign tonal, not D-Dorian).

**Genuinely-on-technique hits:**
- **AURORA ‚Äî Formant-to-Tongue Inversion** (arXiv:2603.17543, **March 2026**): a real-time biofeedback model that maps a sung vowel's formants to a *predicted tongue/articulator shape*, plus the stylized spectrum. The paper's design lesson: raw formant numbers are too abstract ‚Äî show a friendly **proxy** of the articulation. The lab's `393-kids-vowel-color` (very recent) cited this but took the proxy to be a flat **screen color**, and ‚Äî critically ‚Äî used crude **FFT peak-picking**, whose own README concedes it is *unreliable for children's high voices* (wide harmonic spacing makes the "formant" peak land on whichever harmonic happens to fall in-band) and that **LPC or the cepstral/HPS method** is the real fix.
- **V(is)owel / vowel-chart visual-pronunciation tooling** (arXiv:2507.06202) + the open-source *formant_app* LPC-analysis web app + sung-vowel **FOF (formant-wave-function) synthesis** (Faust-style, real-time): confirm a live 2025‚Äì26 ecosystem around *real-time formant biofeedback that shows the mouth, not a number*.

**Why it's surprising / the opening:** the lab has a vowel toy already (393) ‚Äî but nobody built the AURORA idea *literally*: a creature that **physically copies your mouth/tongue shape** (articulatory inversion), and nobody used **real LPC or cepstral formant tracking** (the exact upgrade 393 said it lacked, and needs *precisely for the 4-year-olds this lab targets*). "Show what it heard" becomes a moving mouth, not a color wash ‚Äî far more legible for a child (copycat is a game they already know). This is a kids *listening/analysis* toy (breaks the kids emergence-sim reflex), on **mic-voice** input (breaks the touch reflex), **SVG/Canvas2D** output (off the banned WebGL2/three.js rut), in **foreign tonal** sing-back (JI / slendro / whole-tone, not D-Dorian).

**Honest freshness note (#5):** AURORA is **~3 months** old (March 2026), not <30-day, and 393 already name-dropped it ‚Äî so I am **not** claiming #5. The dive's value is that it *sharpened* an existing reference into a genuinely different, lab-first build (LPC/cepstral formant tracking + articulatory-inversion rendering) rather than re-discovering it. Floor is cleared by **#1 + #2 + #3** (see STATE ¬ß352).

**Chain research ‚Üí build = path (a):** the dive's "AURORA tongue-inversion, done literally, with real LPC" hook ‚Üí a DEEP 3-approach fire (`411-kids-copycat-mouth` SVG/LPC/JI ¬∑ `412-kids-vowel-puppet` Canvas2D/cepstral/slendro ¬∑ `413-kids-mouth-mirror` SVG side-view tongue/LPC/whole-tone).

Sources: arxiv.org/abs/2603.17543 (AURORA, Mar 2026); arxiv.org/abs/2507.06202 (V(is)owel); musicalboard.com/blog/2026-05-05-pitch-detection (browser pitch/formant DSP, May 2026).

**Could become a prototype:** the *adult* counterpart ‚Äî a real-time **FOF sung-vowel synthesizer** driven by Karel's *Welcome Home* vocal/piano partials, or a singer's-formant biofeedback piece for an adult vocalist (the clinical/instructional lane the jury keeps asking the lab to feed).

## ¬ß355 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 355 dive (15-min, mandatory) ‚Äî adult, the jury's "sonify real data / refuse to resolve" mandate

**Context:** JURY 2026-06-08 banned mic/SVG/Kuramoto/just-intonation and fired six provocations; the strongest two for an adult cycle were **#4 (real-world data sonification ‚Äî "zero in 15 cycles ‚Ä¶ breaks the mic rut AND the consonance rut in one fire")** and **#2 (build something that refuses to resolve ‚Äî there is no Ikeda/Akten counter-piece in the window)**. Every extendable lab thread (a-life/JI, accompanist) is now jury-banned, so the dive looked for a *fresh, real, external* data source to sonify.

- **Ryoji Ikeda ‚Äî *data-cosm [n¬∞1]*, 180 Studios (London), extended through 1 February 2026** (verified current; thevinylfactory / factmag, Dec 2025). "Transforms vast datasets into an immersive audiovisual environment ‚Ä¶ from the microscopic world of particle physics to the macroscopic scale of astrophysics." **Why it's the right anchor:** it is the canonical living example of *data as the subject*, in exactly the clinical-monochrome, non-resolving register the jury asked for ‚Äî and Ikeda is named in AGENT.md's artist list. *Could become a prototype that:* sonifies a live planetary dataset in Ikeda's idiom. ‚Üí **became `418-seismic-pulse` this fire.**
- **USGS Earthquake Hazards GeoJSON feeds** (`/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/summary/{all,2.5,significant}_{hour,day,week,month}.geojson`) ‚Äî public-domain, no API key, CORS-enabled, real-time. Each feature carries `mag`, `place`, `time`, and `[lon, lat, depth_km]` ‚Äî four orthogonal seismic dimensions that map cleanly onto four audio dimensions (loudness, pan, pitch/filter, resonance). The live planetary tremor stream is **continuous and aperiodic** ‚Äî it structurally cannot resolve to a chord, which is *why* it answers provocation #2 as well as #4.
- **Seismic audification corpus** (USGS "From Wiggles to Pops, Booms and Rumbles," AGU/Eos; "Earth is Noisy. Why Should Its Data Be Silent?" Eos) ‚Äî the practice of mapping seismograph velocity to audio at 250√ó speed; small local quakes read as *pops*, large teleseismic events as deep *booms/rumbles*. **Freshness honesty:** this corpus is **foundational (2009‚Äì2018), NOT <30 days** ‚Äî I do **not** claim #5 from it. The *current* anchor is the Ikeda show (on view now) + the live feed; the technique lineage is deliberately old-and-stable (like FFT/Web-Audio basics, which AGENT.md exempts).

**Chain visible:** ¬ß355 dive ‚Üí "the lab has never sonified real external data, and has no abrasive non-resolving piece ‚Äî the planet's live seismicity is both at once" ‚Üí WIDE 3-explorer fire (418 seismic-data sonification / 419 first WebGPU-compute storm / 420 Ikeda test-tone). Shipped 418.
