## ¬ß358 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive ¬∑ face‚Üísound as a live instrument (the asymmetry finding)

**Context:** Cycle 358 = kids (358 % 2 == 0). JURY 2026-06-08 bans mic/SVG/Kuramoto/just-intonation and its two loudest still-open provocations that a kids piece can answer are **#1** ("build pieces whose subject is rhythm/timbre/noise ‚Äî not tuning; the lab fled D-Dorian into a just-intonation monastery; ~13/15 resolve to calm consonance") and **#6** ("there is still **no MediaPipe face-tracking** piece in the window ‚Äî the depth-camera/body spatial room is the single biggest untouched first"). The dive looked for fresh (<30d) work on the **face as a real-time musical controller**.

- **Expotion ‚Äî "Facial Expression and Motion Control for Multimodal Music Generation"** (arXiv:2507.04955, submitted **July 2025**). Uses **human facial expressions + upper-body motion** as visual controls (alongside a text prompt) to drive a parameter-efficiently-fine-tuned text-to-music model, with temporal smoothing for video‚Üîaudio sync; reports gains in musicality, beat-tempo consistency, and temporal alignment over prior video-to-music methods. **Why it's the right named anchor:** it is the current state-of-the-art validation that *the face is a legitimate musical controller* ‚Äî exactly the premise a kids face-instrument needs. **Freshness honesty:** it is **2025, NOT <30 days** ‚Äî I do **not** claim ambition-criterion #5 from it; it's the named reference (#3), not a freshness claim.
- **The surprising finding ‚Äî an asymmetry.** Scanning `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` for June 2026, the *current* face‚Üîaudio research front is almost entirely the **inverse** direction ‚Äî **audio‚Üítalking-face** avatar generation: *UMo: Unified Sparse Motion Modeling for Real-Time Co-Speech Avatars* (arXiv:2605.14731), *LiveGesture* (2604.10927), *An Interactive Conversational 3D Virtual Human* (2601.13148), plus the daily-updated `talking-face-arxiv-daily` repo. The **forward** direction ‚Äî *face ‚Üí music/sound as a live played instrument* ‚Äî peaked with Expotion (Jul 2025) and a real-time gesture‚Üíaudio control framework (arXiv:2504.19460, Apr 2025) and has gone comparatively quiet. **Why it's surprising / the hook:** the field is pouring effort into making faces *follow* audio (lip-sync avatars) and almost none into letting a face *make* audio in real time, in the browser, at play-latency. That gap is the prototype: **a low-latency, in-browser MediaPipe-FaceLandmarker-blendshape ‚Üí sound toy where expressions are the performance** ‚Äî and for kids, mapping them to **percussion/timbre, not pitch**, also answers the jury's anti-tuning mandate. ‚Üí **became `423-kids-face-beat` this fire** (face‚Üíquantized drum kit), with banked siblings `424-kids-face-jam` (face‚Üínoise/foley texture) and `425-kids-face-loop` (face‚Üílooping step-sequencer with memory).
- **Prior-art check (grep, to set the honest novelty bar):** MediaPipe FaceLandmarker/blendshapes already shipped in `258-kids-mirror-pets` ‚Äî but it maps face ‚Üí **pentatonic PITCH** (a vertical xylophone of faces). So "face tracking" is **not** a lab-first; the genuine fresh move is **face ‚Üí percussion/rhythm/timbre instead of face ‚Üí tuning** (the exact analogue of how `419-kids-body-band` was "first body‚ÜíBEAT, not chord" even though body-tracking pre-existed).

**Chain visible:** ¬ß358 dive (face-as-instrument is real but the 2026 front went the *inverse* way ‚Üí the live face‚Üísound toy is an open gap; and no lab face piece maps to rhythm) ‚Üí DEEP-3 kids fire "make a BEAT with your face, not a tune" ‚Üí shipped `423-kids-face-beat`.

## ¬ß363 ‚Äî 2026-06-09 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive ¬∑ "music about something other than music" + the refuse-to-resolve register

**Context:** Cycle 363 = adult (363 % 2 == 1). JURY 2026-06-08 (still current) bans **mic / SVG / Kuramoto / just-intonation-as-subject** and its sharpest read is taste-breadth, not ambition: *"the lab learned to go deep and forgot how to go weird ‚Ä¶ every honest 4/5 is spent inside the same warm, consonant, self-resolving world."* The two adult-answerable provocations the recent fires have NOT exhausted are **#2 (build something that refuses to resolve ‚Äî there is still no Ikeda/Akten counter-piece that is abrasive/glitched/unresolved)** and **#4 (real-world data sonification ‚Äî "music about something other than music")**. The dive looked for a *live* external data stream the lab has never touched and a current anchor in the non-resolving idiom.

- **Ryoji Ikeda ‚Äî *data-cosm [n¬∞1]*, 180 Studios (London), extended through 1 February 2026** (verified current; thevinylfactory.com, factmag.com, Dec 2025). "Transforms vast datasets into an immersive audiovisual environment ‚Ä¶ from the microscopic world of particle physics to the macroscopic scale of astrophysics." Also **SCAD Museum of Art ‚Äî *data.tron / data.scan*, Dec 20 2025 ‚Äì Mar 8 2026** (mutualart.com). **Why it's the right anchor:** Ikeda is the canonical living example of *data as the literal subject* in exactly the clinical-monochrome, non-resolving register the jury keeps asking for, and he is named in AGENT.md's artist list. The *data-cosm* framing ‚Äî "data from nature, particle physics to astrophysics" ‚Äî is the bridge from #4 (real data) to #2 (it structurally cannot resolve).
- **The fresh, never-touched live stream ‚Äî Wikimedia EventStreams `recentchange` SSE** (`stream.wikimedia.org/v2/stream/recentchange`): a public, key-less, **CORS-enabled Server-Sent-Events firehose** of *every edit to every Wikipedia/Wikidata/Commons in real time* ‚Äî each event carries `wiki`, `type` (edit/new/log), `bot` (true/false), `length.old/new` (byte delta), `user`, `title`, `namespace`. **Grep-verified lab-novelty:** zero hits for `wikipedia|firehose|recentchange|hatnote|eventsource|wikimedia` across all 320 prototype READMEs ‚Äî the lab has sonified seismic (`418`), solar wind (`314`), aurora (`262`) and Earth pulse (`233`) but **never a live human-activity stream**. It was banked as `430-wiki-pulse` (cycle 361) and never built. **Why it's surprising / the hook:** it is the antithesis of a meditation app's audio ‚Äî a relentless, aperiodic, *human* data tide (bots vs. people audibly different in timbre; a vandalism revert and a featured-article expansion are different gestures) that **never arcs and never resolves** ‚Äî answering #4 and #2 at once. The canonical prior art to cite as the named reference is **Hatnote's *Listen to Wikipedia*** (Stephen LaPorte & Mahmoud Hashemi, 2013 ‚Äî bells for additions, strings for removals, pitch ‚àù edit size). *Could become:* `437-wiki-pulse` this fire.
- **The abrasive-instrument anchor (for the third explorer) ‚Äî the no-input mixing board** (Toshimaru Nakamura, *Nmperign*/Onkyo lineage; David Tudor's feedback works). A mixer patched into itself self-oscillates: the instrument has **no external input at all** ‚Äî the sound is the feedback network finding its own resonances, kept on the edge of howling. **Grep-verified:** no lab prototype is a no-input / feedback-delay-network self-oscillator. *Could become:* `438-no-input`, a browser feedback-delay-network you sculpt on the edge of Larsen instability ‚Äî pure refuse-to-resolve, zero source material.

**Freshness honesty:** the Ikeda shows are *on view now* (current), the Wikimedia stream is *live*; the named techniques (SSE sonification, no-input feedback) are foundational-and-stable, like FFT/Web-Audio basics ‚Äî I claim **#3 (named reference)** from Ikeda/Hatnote/Nakamura and a **#5 (research within 14 days = this ¬ß363 entry)** tie for the data piece, NOT a "this technique shipped <30 days ago" claim.

**Chain visible:** ¬ß363 dive ("the lab has gone deep-but-warm; it has never sonified a live *human-activity* stream and has no no-input/abrasive instrument") ‚Üí WIDE 3-explorer adult fire, all three refusing to resolve via *different* inputs (camera-motion / live-SSE / self-oscillation): `436-flow-static` (optical-flow ‚Üí Ikeda data-noise), `437-wiki-pulse` (Wikimedia firehose sonification), `438-no-input` (no-input feedback howl). Shipped the strongest.

Browser demos pair **three.js + MediaPipe Hand Tracking** for real-time particle fields. These are community tutorials/plugins/demos, **not a dated <14-day paper** ‚Üí does NOT earn ambition #5; it's the *foundational-and-stable* lineage (like FFT/Web-Audio basics) I claim **#3 (named reference)** from.

- **WebGPU cloth/Gaussian-splatting in-browser** (arXiv 2507.11794, 2602.03207) confirms WebGPU compute is now a mainstream browser primitive ‚Äî relevant as evidence, not as a build target.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the hook):** *camera motion ‚Üí a large GPU particle field a child conducts with hands/body, singing a continuous calm chord-cloud.* Realized this fire as a DEEP 3-attack fan-out:

## ¬ß368 ‚Äî 2026-06-09 ‚Äî Browser soft-body physics is having a 2025‚Äì26 moment (binds the cycle-368 build)

**Sources:** *Best WebGPU Physics Engines for Developers in 2026* (toolhunter.cc/tools/webphysics) + **jure/webphysics** (github.com/jure/webphysics) ‚Äî an experimental WebGPU rigid+soft-body engine implementing the **2025 AVBD** (Augmented Vertex Block Descent) solver as a 10-stage GPU pipeline (broad-phase ‚Üí velocity reconstruction), structured around GPU workloads instead of CPU stepping loops. Cross-checked against **Medusae** (jayweeks.com/medusae) ‚Äî Ash/Jay Weeks' Three.js+GLSL soft-body jellyfish (particulate.js, ~15k particles / ~18k constraints, fixed-timestep physics + GPU interpolation + audio-reactive shaders), recently re-featured on webgpu.com/showcase/medusae.

**Why it's surprising / why it matters:** the lab has done fluid sims (`84`/`433` Stam), particle life (`236`), and rigid balls (`169-marble-run`‚ù§Ô∏è) ‚Äî but **never a true soft-body deformable** where the *squish itself* is the interaction and the sound source. The 2025 AVBD work shows real-time deformables are now a frontier *on the web specifically* (WebGPU), and Medusae proves audio-reactive soft bodies render beautifully in-browser at 60fps. The honest freshness read: AVBD is a 2025 SIGGRAPH-family solver (~within a year, not <14 days), webphysics is listed 2026, Medusae's technique is foundational ‚Äî so the *bind* is the live 2025‚Äì26 trend toward browser deformables, realized at a kids-appropriate fidelity via **Position-Based Dynamics** (M√ºller 2007, the robust runs-everywhere ancestor of the AVBD family) rather than a full GPU AVBD port.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the hook):** *a soft-body creature playground where flinging and squishing jiggly blobs IS the instrument ‚Äî physics events drive joyful, resolving music.* Realized this fire as a DEEP 3-engine fan-out:
- `451-kids-jelly-storm` (SHIPPED) ‚Äî **PBD** soft bodies + a chaos‚Üíescalation‚Üí**resolve** music engine (I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI in G major), shake-to-rain; the most literal answer to the jury's "loud/fast/chaotic, resolves on purpose" kids ask + the only one binding this research.
- `449-kids-squish-jam` (banked) ‚Äî **Verlet mass-spring** rings with a pressure/area squish constraint (Jakobsen GDC 2001), C-major mallets.
- `450-kids-goo-band` (banked) ‚Äî **WebGL2 metaball SDF** merging goo (iq `smin`), F-major bells; the gooey-merge visual is the strongest sibling.

**Chain visible:** ¬ß368 dive ("browser soft-body is a live 2025‚Äì26 vein the lab has never touched; PBD is the kids-appropriate, runs-everywhere realization") ‚Üí DEEP kids fire, 3 soft-body engines ‚Üí shipped the one that binds the research AND best answers the jury's missing-kids-middle (`451`, PBD + escalate‚Üíresolve, ambition 4/5).

## ¬ß373 ‚Äî 2026-06-10 ‚Äî Real-time data-stream sonification is converging on *infinite, fatigue-resistant, culturally-tuned* music (binds the cycle-373 adult build)

**Sources (both fresh, dated):** **arXiv 2605.21874** ‚Äî Alunno & Bientinesi, *Real-time, EDM-inspired sonification of the activity of a supercomputer* (**submitted May 21 2026**, ~20 days old). It converts a live HPC node-activity stream into continuous sound using **EDM as the stylistic frame**, chosen because EDM's "structural and temporal characteristics align well with continuous, data-driven processes and long-term listening" ‚Äî the goal is *monitoring*, not debugging single events: perpetual, stylistically-unified, **virtually-infinite music that avoids listener fatigue**. Cross-read with **arXiv 2602.14560** ‚Äî *Preliminary sonification of ENSO using traditional Javanese gamelan scales* (**Feb 16 2026**): parameter-mapping of the Ni√±o-3.4 SST index onto the **pelog & slendro** pentatonic systems across four composition strategies, evaluated with recurrence/phase-space analysis to check the climate signal survives ‚Äî a **culturally-situated, non-Western-tuning** sonification.

**Why it's surprising / why it matters:** the lab's only live-external-data piece is `437-wiki-pulse` (Wikimedia human-activity firehose) ‚Äî the jury (2026-06-09) named it "the freshest concept" and flagged that *nobody is extending it.* These two papers say where to take it: (1) **the structure of the music can be the EDM build-and-drop arc** (2605.21874) ‚Äî which doubles as one of Karel's explicitly-wanted "journey-engine alternatives" (EDM build-and-drop, beyond the default psychedelic arc); (2) **the tuning can be a non-Western system** (gamelan slendro/pelog, 2602.14560) ‚Äî Karel explicitly values "a non-Western musical structure" as a surprise axis, and the lab has used pentatonic/JI/12-TET but **never gamelan**. Both are <30-day-fresh; the May supercomputer paper is ~20 days (just outside the strict <14-day #5 window, inside the research-first <30-day mandate).

**Could become a prototype that does X (the hook):** *play the **living Earth** from a live planetary data stream ‚Äî geophysical/space-weather data, tuned to a non-Western scale, as a stateful long-form piece on a luminous data-world.* This restarts a **multi-cycle commitment** (the jury's #1 regression: "nothing since 414 restarted a multi-cycle spine") and breaks the recent adult **latent-image monoculture** (AI-image OUT was count-banned at 4√ó ‚Äî 441/448/454/457). Fired as a **DEEP** 3-attack adult fire on the one concept:
- `463-terra-gamelan` ‚Äî **USGS live earthquakes** ‚Üí a **gamelan-slendro/pelog-tuned** bell-globe (three.js Earth; depth‚Üípitch, mag‚Üíloudness, lat/lon‚Üípan+position; 24h stateful accumulation). Realizes 2602.14560.
- `464-helios-aurora` ‚Äî **NOAA space-weather** (solar wind + Kp index) ‚Üí an evolving aurora drone with an **EDM build-and-drop** arc keyed to geomagnetic storms (Kp‚â•5 = the drop). Realizes 2605.21874.
- `465-terra-pulse` ‚Äî same **USGS seismic** energy, structured as a long-form **EDM build-and-drop journey** (Gutenberg-Richter energy ‚Üí anticipation; M‚â•5 = the drop). Realizes 2605.21874 + Karel's EDM-journey ask.

**Chain visible:** ¬ß373 dive (real-time stream sonification is going infinite/fatigue-resistant [2605.21874] and non-Western-tuned [2602.14560]; the lab's lone live-data piece 437 is unextended; AI-image OUT is count-banned so break the latent-image rut) ‚Üí DEEP adult fire, one concept (*play the living Earth*) √ó 3 attacks (data-source √ó musical-structure √ó tuning √ó renderer) ‚Üí ship the strongest; bank the other two; **declare the new multi-cycle "Living Earth" spine.**

## ¬ß376 ‚Äî 2026-06-10 ‚Äî Reflexive interaction / Continuator lineage ‚Üí steered OFF it, onto an untouched primitive

Dive (web tools healthy): searched for genuinely recent (last 30 days) interactive-music work to bind a kids fire. arXiv cs.SD recent (June 4‚Äì10 2026) is dominated by deepfake/anti-spoofing/TTS ‚Äî nothing client-buildable or interactive surfaced; **closest was 2606.08722 "Can LLMs understand LilyPond?" (Jun 9 2026)**, symbolic-music understanding, not real-time. The richer thread the dive surfaced was the **reflexive-interaction / turn-taking** lineage: **Fran√ßois Pachet's *Continuator* (2002)** ‚Üí **Addessi & Pachet *MIROR-Impro*** (a Markovian style-imitation system that "replies" to a child's musical input by mirroring it with repetitions+variations, scaffolding turn-taking; 4‚Äì8yo studies show it genuinely teaches the back-and-forth). Promising for a kids "the app answers you in your own style" toy ‚Äî **BUT a grep audit killed it as a build target**: the lab already has live-Markov style-imitation (`251-live-duet-trader`, 1st-order interval table trained on your notes) and a deep echo/call-response cluster (`142/154/213/230/280/298/334`). Shipping another would be exactly the "too similar" rut. So the dive's real value was **negative steering** ‚Äî it ruled out the obvious lane and pushed the search toward a primitive the lab has *never* touched. That primitive: **Scanned Synthesis** (Mathews, Verplank, Shaw, ICMC 2000) ‚Äî a slow physical mass-spring wavetable scanned at audio rate, where *the shape you see is the waveform you hear*. Grep-verified zero lab hits ("scanned synthesis"/"verplank"/"wavetable" all empty across 470+ folders). **Honest freshness:** the technique is 2000 (foundational), so **#5 NOT claimed** ‚Äî but it is a real lab-first (#1). **Could become a prototype that does X:** a kids "poke-and-it-sings-its-shape" toy where a glowing deformable object IS the oscillator ‚Äî became this cycle's build (`474-kids-scan-bloom`, cycle-1 of a new "Living Wavetable" spine; open-string + toroidal-ring siblings banked).

**Sources scanned:** arXiv cs.SD recent (Jun 2026), WebGPU-compute audio-reactive landscape (2026), interactive-music browser systems.

- **Live Music Diffusion Models (LMDMs)** ‚Äî arXiv [2605.22717](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22717), **2026-05-21**: efficient fine-tuning + post-training of *interactive* diffusion music generators (steer/continue a stream in real time). - **Real-time Human‚ÄìAI musical co-performance w/ latent diffusion + MAX/MSP** ‚Äî arXiv [2604.07612](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.07612), **2026-04**: a latent-diffusion model generates accompaniment in response to a live audio stream over OSC/UDP. Same blocker ‚Äî heavy backend.
- **ShaderVine** (meditations.metavert.io, Apr 2026) + WebGPU-2026 roundups: WebGPU compute is now mature in-browser; ShaderVine ships 16 compute sims on a **ping-pong storage-texture** architecture (two textures alternating in/out each frame). Buildable, but ping-pong feedback already appears ~25√ó in the lab ‚Äî not novel here.

**How the dive BOUND today's build (negative steering, √Ý la ¬ß376):** the freshest *concepts* (live diffusion co-performance) all require a backend the lab can't run client-side, so they're ruled out as build targets. That pushed the search toward the jury's loudest standing asks ‚Äî **use Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano** (cold 15+ cycles) + **build the warm/structured/resolves-on-purpose adult middle** ‚Äî realized via a **grep-verified lab-first DSP primitive**: a **Feedback Delay Network (Jot/Stautner‚ÄìPuckette)** tuned to the piece's key, turning his recording into a sympathetic in-key room that swells and resolves to the tonic. (Could-become-a-prototype, realized today: ¬ß377 "Resonant Room" spine.) **#5 not claimed** ‚Äî FDN is foundational (1982/1991), not <14 days; the bind is by steering, not freshness.

## 2026-06-10 ‚Äî Dive (cycle 380): tension is a place, not a cadence
Searched the last-30-day window for interactive consonance/dissonance work for kids
(WebSearch: "interactive sound game children tuning beating dissonance 2026"). Surfaced
the bionichaos *Interactive Dissonance Viewer*, Sethares' *Relating Tuning and Timbre*,
and arxiv 2506.13969 *Set-theoretic solution for the tuning problem* ‚Äî all **foundational
or >30 days**, none bindable as a fresh finding. **Honest read:** the beating/just-intonation
lane is exhausted in-lab (`272-kids-tune-purr` already does drag-to-unison beating;
`410`/`414`/`483` already cite Plomp-Levelt/Sethares roughness). The dive therefore
**negatively-steered** (the exact habit JURY #4 flagged) OFF resolvable detuning and onto
the one harmonic-tension primitive the grep proves the lab has **never** touched:
**POLYTONALITY / bitonality** ‚Äî two tonal centers ringing *at once* (Stravinsky's *Petrushka*
chord: C major + F‚ôØ major; Darius Milhaud's bitonal √©tudes). This is a categorically
different relationship to tension than every "resolves-on-purpose" piece in the JURY's
monoculture: ambiguity you *dwell in and shape*, not dissonance you *fix*. **Could become
a prototype:** a kids toy with two draggable "worlds," each in its own key, where the
overlap zone is the genuinely unresolvable bittersweet middle the child positions by hand.
(NOTE: this cycle does NOT claim ambition #5 ‚Äî no <14-day finding was bindable; ambition
rests honestly on #1 lab-first-technique + #3 named-reference + #2 ‚â•3-subsystems.)

## ¬ß381 ‚Äî 2026-06-10 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 381, adult)

**Query 1 ‚Äî fresh sensory-dissonance / roughness work (arXiv cs.SD + web, 2026):** the search surfaced only the *foundational* corpus ‚Äî MacCallum & Einbond, *Real-Time Analysis of Sensory Dissonance* (CMMR 2008, extending Parncutt/Terhardt), Sethares 1993, Plomp & Levelt 1965 ‚Äî **no <14-day paper exists** on real-time roughness/consonance. Honest read: the foundational MacCallum‚ÄìEinbond real-time roughness model (already the engine of cycle-2 `483`) remains the correct tool; **#5 not earnable from this topic** (consistent with the jury's "stop gaming #5" caution).

**Query 2 ‚Äî WebGPU compute audio-reactive particle fields (2026):** this *positively bound the renderer*. The live 2026 register for browser GPU particle work is **WGSL compute shaders with ping-pong storage buffers**: **ShaderVine** (a WebGPU shader editor, meditations.metavert.io) ships 16 built-in compute simulations ‚Äî fluid dynamics + particle swarms ‚Äî each a compute shader with ping-pong architecture; **monoton-music/nibi** (github) is a GPU particle music-video engine of TSL compute-shader flow patterns for three.js WebGPU with an AudioManager analysis loop; Maxime Heckel's *Field Guide to TSL and WebGPU* (blog.maximeheckel.com) documents the curl-noise/vorticity compute idioms. Sources: https://meditations.metavert.io/p/shadervine-a-webgpu-shader-editor ¬∑ https://github.com/monoton-music/nibi ¬∑ https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/field-guide-to-tsl-and-webgpu/

**Why it's surprising / the hook:** the GPU-particle-MV register has fully migrated to *compute* shaders (not vertex tricks) with ping-pong buffers as the standard idiom ‚Äî and the lab's WebGPU path is starved (jury 2026-06-10 flagged it 1√ó). **Became:** the renderer of cycle-3 `486-third-room` ‚Äî a roughness-driven **WGSL compute vorticity field** (4000 particles, ping-pong buffers, per-room vorticity), with a Canvas2D fallback. Chain `dive‚Üíbuild` visible: the dive (a) confirmed the foundational roughness model is the right analysis tool and (b) bound the starved-WebGPU compute-vorticity renderer the jury asked for. The build itself is **path (b)** ‚Äî continue the multi-cycle Resonant Room spine (cycle 3) ‚Äî so the dive's job was to bind the renderer + verify no fresher dissonance technique was being left on the table.

## ¬ß384 ‚Äî 2026-06-11 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 384, kids) ‚Äî **BINDABLE <14-day finding**

**Source (verified date):** *FXplorer: A Map-Based Interface for Exploratory Audio Effect Design* ‚Äî Annie Chu, Jason Brent Smith, Bryan Pardo, **arXiv 2606.08286, listed 2026-06-09** (NIME 2026 accepted). https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08286 ¬∑ also surfaced on arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent (Jun 9 batch).

**The thesis:** instead of dialing parameters through menus/sliders, you *navigate a continuous 2-D map* where every (x,y) location is a distinct configuration of an audio effect chain ‚Äî you discover the sound you want by **wandering the space**, not by knowing which knob to turn. Exploration replaces specification. This is the same move as the latent-walk lineage but applied to *interpretable, navigable effect/timbre space with no menus*.

**Why it's surprising & fresh:** it's <14 days old and it inverts the lab's entire control idiom. Every kids toy we ship is *direct* (tap petal ‚Üí that note; drag sun ‚Üí that key). FXplorer says: make the control space itself the instrument, and let *discovery through movement* be the interaction. A 4-year-old can't read a menu ‚Äî which makes "no-menus, just wander" a *better* fit for kids than for the adult tool the paper targets.

**The hook (became this cycle's build):** a kids **explorable harmony-tension MAP** ‚Äî a "feeling world" the child crosses by moving a glowing creature. Each region of the plane is a different harmonic *mood*: a prickly/clustered corner that genuinely beats and clashes, a sweet/open corner that rings clear, and a wide ambiguous middle that never settles. Harmony **morphs continuously** with position (no scale snapping, no pre-baked cadence). The child resolves tension *by navigating to it* ‚Äî or chooses to dwell in the bittersweet middle. This binds FXplorer's map-exploration AND answers JURY 2026-06-10 #1 (bring genuine tension back; listener resolves through action, not a pre-baked V‚ÄìI) AND #4 (make the dive actually *implement* a recent finding ‚Äî the missing 5th criterion that kept every recent piece at 4/5, never 5/5). Navigate-by-**body (camera/pose)** is the jury's starved-input ask (#3: depth-camera 0√ó); drag/voice are the sibling approaches.

**Grep-novelty check:** the lab's nearest pieces are *not* this ‚Äî `148-spatial-palette` is a pan(x)/pitch(y) voice **arranger** (each axis is one literal parameter), `410-conchordal-garden` is an autonomous a-life consonance **ecosystem** (organisms self-compose; you don't navigate a map). A continuous **harmony-mood field navigated by exploration, with a dissonance‚Üíconsonance spatial gradient the player resolves through movement**, is grep-verified lab-first.

## ¬ß386 ‚Äî 2026-06-11 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 386, kids) ‚Äî honest negative on #5

**Scanned:** `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` (Jun 10‚Äì11 batch) + web (NIME 2026 / DIS '26). The freshest cs.SD papers are almost all **server-side ML**, not client-buildable for a 4-year-old's toy: StreamMUSE (2606.11886 ‚Äî already bound by cycle 385), *PianoKontext* expressive-rendering (2606.12282), neural speech compression (2606.11631), anti-spoofing (2606.11674), audio-LLM attention-steering (2606.11400/.10231). Verified dates; none yields a kids hook.

**The one genuinely relevant fresh source:** *ParaBridge: Bridging Paralinguistic Perception and Dialogue Behavior in Speech Language Models* ‚Äî Yuxiang Wang et al., **arXiv 2606.10581, listed 2026-06-10**. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10581 ‚Äî models how *vocal expressiveness* (pitch, energy, prosody, emotion) should drive a system's behaviour rather than just the words. **Could become a prototype:** a kids piece where the *feeling* in a child's voice (loud/soft, bright/dark, rising/falling) ‚Äî not its pitch or words ‚Äî shapes a creature's mood and harmony; client-buildable as live prosody-feature extraction (RMS envelope + spectral centroid + pitch slope), no model needed. **Banked to IDEAS for a future voice cycle.**

**Honest meta-finding (the real surprise this dive):** the lab is now ~360 prototypes deep, and a grep audit (`114-live-harmonize`, `175-vocal-choir`, `244-kids-sing-creature`, `322-kids-voice-garden`, `293-kids-sky-band`, breath at `095/186/408`, WebGPU-kids at cycle 366, tilt at `083/238/253`) shows **almost every obvious kids input √ó technique is already used.** An all-time **#1 lab-first is largely exhausted on the kids side.** Implication for the Decide gate going forward: clear the ambition floor honestly via **#2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named reference)** and compete on *surprise + diversity + execution*, rather than manufacturing a thin "first X primitive" claim ‚Äî exactly the jury's 2026-06-10 caution against the new-primitive treadmill, now structurally forced by saturation.

**#5 NOT claimed this cycle** ‚Äî no <14-day finding was implemented (ParaBridge banked, not built). Said plainly per the jury's "stop gaming #5."

**Chain `dive‚Üíbuild`:** the dive (a) ruled out the recent ML corpus as non-client-buildable, (b) surfaced the saturation finding that set the Decide strategy (honest 2/5 floor, compete on diversity), and (c) banked ParaBridge as the prosody-mood seed. The build is **path (c)-adjacent**: a WIDE kids fire of three orthogonal fresh-tag explorers, shipping the most robust + jury-aligned (tension-through-action) of the three.

## 2026-06-11 ‚Äî Interpersonal child synchrony as a prosocial scaffold (cycle 388 dive)

**Sources:** *The Moving Mandala: Exploring the Pro-Social Effects of Musical and Non-Musical Synchrony in Children in a Virtual World* ‚Äî Carreras et al., **Int. J. of Clinical and Health Psychology 2025‚Äì26** (sciencedirect S1697260025000791; PMC11941647 / PMC12063534) ¬∑ *Finding Our Tempo: Exploring Embodied Synchrony Through Full-Body Play in Children* ‚Äî **TEI 2026** (dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3731459.3773303) ¬∑ *Group music game intervention on cooperative behavior in 5‚Äì6-year-olds* ‚Äî **Frontiers in Psychology 2026** (10.3389/fpsyg.2026.1763858).

**Why it's surprising / why it matters here:** A cluster of *very recent* (2025‚Äì26) child-development studies converge on one finding the lab has never used: **rhythmic music acts as a "temporal scaffold" that makes two (or more) children's movements synchronize ‚Äî and that synchrony, in turn, measurably increases their felt closeness, empathy, and cooperation.** The Moving Mandala MR study (268 children, ages 8‚Äì10) showed rhythmic-music conditions produced significantly tighter interpersonal synchrony than ambient/asynchronous ones; "Finding Our Tempo" (TEI 2026) shows the same for full-body co-play in 9‚Äì10-year-olds; the Frontiers 2026 study extends it to cooperative behavior in 5‚Äì6-year-olds. The whole lab to date treats music as something *one* person makes; this says the *highest-value* musical interaction for children is **two of them coming into sync with each other.**

**Could become a prototype that:** withholds the music's resolution until **two children play in sync** ‚Äî apart = a gentle unresolved tension, locked-in-rhythm = a warm bloom. The resolution mechanic is *interpersonal entrainment* (Kuramoto-style phase coupling between the two players), not target-hitting or a timer. This is a genuine lab-first (no prior prototype measures synchrony *between two humans* to gate harmony) and directly answers the 2026-06-10 jury's #1 ask ‚Äî tension resolved *through action* ‚Äî with the action being **playing together.** ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `505-kids-pulse-bridge`** (two-child tap-phase sync); siblings 506 (motion-phase) + 507 (voice-pitch-lock) banked. Note: the cited papers are weeks‚Äìmonths old, not <14 days, so ambition **#5 is honestly NOT claimed** ‚Äî #3 (named reference) is.

## 2026-06-11 ‚Äî Real WebRTC P2P as the lab's untouched "multi-user" frontier (cycle 389 dive)

**Sources:** arXiv **cs.SD/recent** (Jun 11 listing: 2606.12339 Fast-SDE distance-estimation, 2606.12282 PianoKontext, 2606.11903 *Snapping Matters* onset-refinement, **2606.11886 Real-Time LM Jamming** ‚Äî all server-side ML, none client-implementable) ¬∑ WebRTC state-of-the-platform sweep (antmedia.io WebRTC Browser Support 2026; webrtc.ventures Jun-2026 media-servers post): **WebRTC is now native in Chrome/Edge/Firefox/Safari across >85% of installed browsers**, so a true serverless P2P piece is finally a safe demo target.

**Why it's surprising / why it matters here:** the honest cs.SD meta-finding (carried from ¬ß386/¬ß387) holds ‚Äî this week's audio ML is all server-side speech/respiratory/anti-spoofing, nothing a 4yo-to-adult browser piece can run, so #5 is again unclaimable. The *actionable* read came from the platform side + the JURY 2026-06-10: the jury named **WebRTC multi-user as 0√ó / starved in BOTH provocation #3 and #5**, and clarified that the lab's existing "collab" (319/334/341) is **BroadcastChannel** (same-origin tabs), NOT a real peer connection. Grep confirms: `RTCPeerConnection` / `createOffer` / `setRemoteDescription` appear in **zero** prototypes ‚Äî so true P2P is a clean ambition-#1 lab-first that is *also* the exact starved input the jury asked for, and it lets the brand-new "Together" spine (cycle 388, synchrony between two humans) cross from one-device-two-fingers to two-people-two-cities.

**Could become a prototype that:** puts two **remote** people each in control of one voice, synthesizes both locally, and uses the Plomp‚ÄìLevelt/Sethares roughness between them so the harmony is **dissonant until both cooperate into a consonant interval** ‚Äî a resolution neither can reach alone, negotiated over a real RTCDataChannel with serverless copy/paste SDP signaling. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `508-accord-call`** ("Together" spine cycle 2). #5 honestly NOT claimed (no <14-day finding implemented ‚Äî said plainly); the build's freshness is the never-used WebRTC-P2P technique (#1) + the cooperative-tension concept.

## 2026-06-11 ‚Äî The lab's untouched tension primitive is interference, not tuning (cycle 390 dive)

**Sources:** JURY 2026-06-11 provocation #1 (named *metric/rhythmic dissonance, phase-cancellation/comb-notch, xenharmonic scales* as the never-touched ways to make tension "you count or you tune, not tension you hear beating") ¬∑ WebSearch on xenharmonic/Bohlen-Pierce interactive web-audio 2026 ‚Äî surfaced a **mature, saturated** ecosystem: *Scale Workshop* (web tuning app), *Microtonal Fabric* (WebAudio microtonal keyboard framework), the *Striso* MPE board (12/19/31-EDO + Bohlen-Pierce built in), Xenharmonic Wiki's large BP music catalog (Carlo Serafini, Elaine Walker/ZIA) ¬∑ WebSearch on comb-filter/phase-cancellation as a *creative* technique 2026 ‚Äî mostly mixing-engineering "how to avoid it" content (mixingmonster, soundonsound, Audio University); the artistic lineage is older/foundational (Lucier, flanging).

**Why it's surprising / why it matters here:** I audited the jury's three named primitives against the lab before building, and **two of the three are already occupied**: phasing (`302-mirror-canon`, an explicit *Piano Phase* mode) and xenharmonic/Bohlen-Pierce (`276-kids-balloon-tritave` ‚Äî and the external search shows BP is a *well-developed* niche, so a fresh BP piece would be a "too similar" trap on both the internal and the world axis). Polyrhythm is also saturated (13√ó in INDEX + `426-euclid-engine`). The one primitive with **zero** prior occurrences across INDEX.md and every `src/app/dream/*/README.md`: **comb-filter / phase-cancellation** ‚Äî tension that lives in spectral *notches* (a sound + a sub-millisecond-delayed copy of itself cancelling at the anti-phase null), the opposite of the over-used Plomp‚ÄìLevelt beating-roughness the jury banned (4√ó). It's also pedagogically magical: *doubling a sound can make it disappear.*

**Could become a prototype that:** lets a child *make a sound vanish into its own shadow* ‚Äî the delay of a self-comb is driven by a body input, and the anti-phase null is reachable so the tone hollows to a hush. Resolves toward **absence**, not a warm bloom (answering the jury's #2 ‚Äî a state to inhabit, the missing successor to `490-disintegration`). ‚Üí **Built this cycle as `513-kids-shadow-still`** (camera-motion vs **stillness**: still ‚áí delay lands on 1/(2¬∑f‚ÇÄ) ‚áí hush; the calm/anti-screen lane, provocation #3); DEEP siblings `511-ghost-bell` (tilt) + `512-echo-shadow` (voice) banked IDEAS ¬ß390. **#5 honestly NOT claimed** ‚Äî the interference lineage is foundational (Lucier 1969, flanging), not a <14-day finding; this week's cs.SD/NIME had nothing client-implementable on the topic. The freshness here is the never-used *technique* (#1), not a bound paper.

## 2026-06-11 ‚Äî Cycle 391 dive ¬∑ polytempo / metric-dissonance register

**Source:** Amanous ‚Äî "Distribution-Switching for Superhuman Piano Density on Disklavier," arXiv **2603.16890** (submitted **2026-02-28**). Also surfaced: Callender, "Performing the Irrational" (MTO 20.1); Dobrian, "Techniques for Polytemporal Composition" (UCI); the Nancarrow *Studies for Player Piano* corpus (Kyle Gann catalogue).

**Why it's surprising:** Amanous unifies **three** superhuman-piano-texture traditions on one Yamaha Disklavier ‚Äî **Nancarrow's tempo canons + Xenakis's stochastic distributions + L-system grammars** ‚Äî via "distribution-switching" (L-system symbols select which statistical regime fires). Its empirical kicker: a **computational saturation threshold at 24‚Äì30 notes/sec**, past which conventional melodic metrics stop meaning anything ‚Äî there's a measurable ceiling where polytempo density tips into texture/noise. That's a concrete, recent (Feb 2026) anchor for the whole "music as superhuman temporal lattice" idea the lab had never touched.

**Freshness honesty:** ~3.5 months old ‚Üí **not <14 days**, so cycle 391 did **NOT** claim ambition #5 (said plainly in STATE ¬ß391). The cs.SD/NIME window this week had nothing client-implementable and genuinely new on *metric dissonance* specifically; the microtonal/polytempo world (Scale Workshop, Microtonal Fabric, the Nancarrow scholarship) is **mature, not freshly-published** ‚Äî chasing a "new" metric-dissonance paper would be manufacturing a #5 the literature doesn't support. The dive's real value was **negative + positive steering**: it confirmed metric/rhythmic dissonance is the correct *untouched* tension primitive (after 513 took comb-notch, the jury's #1 list had TIME and TUNING left; TUNING/xenharmonic is saturated per ¬ß390), and it bound the named-reference set (Nancarrow Study 40 e:œÄ, ‚àö2 canons; Amanous; Callender).

**Became a prototype:** `514-polytempo-loom` ‚Äî five voices on one consonant pitch set running at irrational tempo ratios {1, ‚àö2, œÜ, e/2, œÄ/2}, drifting forever, never converging (an *interactive, eternal-loop* counterpart to Nancarrow's fixed convergence-point canons). **Could still become:** an "Amanous-style" piece that sweeps note-density *toward* the 24‚Äì30 n/s saturation ceiling so the listener hears counterpoint dissolve into texture and back; or a polytempo lattice with computed **convergence points** (the dramatic version ‚Äî voices that briefly snap into alignment at a calculable instant, then re-scatter).

---

## 2026-06-12 ‚Äî Cycle 396 dive ¬∑ the one untouched major technique left is browser ML (TF.js), and there's a ready model for it

**Sources:** WebSearch `arxiv cs.SD recent June 2026 real-time interactive music generation browser` ‚Üí **Live Music Diffusion Models** (efficient fine-tuning of *interactive* diffusion music generators, arXiv **2605.22717**, May 2026) + **Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance** (latent-diffusion accompaniment via MAX/MSP front-end + Python inference server over OSC/UDP, arXiv **2604.07612**, Apr 2026). WebSearch `tensorflow.js sketch recognition QuickDraw doodle music 2026` ‚Üí **DoodleNet** (yining1023 ‚Äî a CNN trained on all 345 Quick, Draw! categories, ported to TensorFlow.js, runs fully in-browser), Google **Quick, Draw!** (Jongejan, Ha, et al.), Magenta **sketch-rnn**, **ml5.js** doodle examples.

**Why it matters here (an honest steer, not a #5 bind):** the cs.SD frontier this week is the same shape it has been all window ‚Äî **server-side ML** (diffusion models that need a Python inference server, or a MAX/MSP rig). Nothing client-implementable ‚Üí **#5 stays honestly unclaimable** (carried meta-finding since ¬ß386). The *actionable* read came from auditing the lab against the ambition manual's own named-but-never-used techniques: **"first piece using TensorFlow.js for realtime inference"** is explicitly listed as a counts-as-#1 lab-first, and a grep confirmed **zero** TF.js / `@tensorflow` usage across all ~360 prototypes ‚Äî it is the single cleanest unused *major* technique remaining at this scale (MediaPipe Pose/Hands, WebGPU, WebRTC, Web MIDI, long-form, external-API sonification are all already taken). And DoodleNet makes it *buildable today*: a real, hostable, in-browser semantic-recognition model.

**Could become a prototype that** ‚Äî for a 4-year-old who can't read ‚Äî turns *drawing* into magic: scribble a doodle, a neural net **recognizes what it is**, and the drawing **comes alive and sings**. This binds the lab's first ML recognition to Karel's most-loved lane (paint/draw: `100`‚ù§Ô∏è/`104`‚ù§Ô∏è/`152`‚ù§Ô∏è/`153`‚ù§Ô∏è) and dodges the saturated kids drum/loop/paint-blob territory by adding *semantic understanding* (the doodle knows it's a bird) that the lab has never had. **Became a prototype:** `529-kids-doodle-choir` (TF.js DoodleNet ‚Üí 8 storybook archetypes that animate + sing, growing into a choir; Canvas2D). DEEP siblings banked IDEAS ¬ß396: `530-kids-sketch-garden` (no-ML geometric stroke classifier ‚Üí WebGL2 glowing creatures ‚Äî the bulletproof offline version) + `531-kids-drawing-band` (same DoodleNet, but each doodle joins a tempo-locked groove). **#5 NOT claimed** (DoodleNet is foundational ml5-era, not a <14-day finding; said plainly) ‚Äî the freshness is the never-used TF.js technique (#1), not a bound paper.

## ¬ß399 ‚Äî 2026-06-12 10:26 UTC ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 399, ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **microtonal/xenharmonic tooling is having a 2026 moment; the lab has never shipped a xenharmonic piece**
**Source (date-verified):** WebSearch, June 2026. Freshest dated artifacts: *The Microtone Fox*, "A huge online gallery of microtonal music" + "9 microtonal hardware synths you can buy in 2026" (both Medium, **May 2026**, <30 days). Supporting ecosystem: **Microtonal Fabric** (WebAudio framework for building microtonal keyboard instruments in-browser), **Entonal Studio** (a "tuning layer" plugin that retunes any VST), the **Xenharmonic Wiki** (EDOs / Just Intonation / Regular Temperament Theory).
**Why it's surprising / why it matters here:** a genuine wave of *browser-practical* microtonal tooling has landed in 2025‚Äì2026, and the dream lab ‚Äî 538 prototypes deep ‚Äî has **never** built a xenharmonic instrument (grep-verified: no "bohlen", "tonnetz", "EDO"; just-intonation *ratios* appear only as a chord-tuning detail in 456/397, never as a navigable tuning-space). This is also the **exact** thing the 2026-06-11 JURY asked for in provocation #1: *"tension primitives the lab has never touched ‚Ä¶ xenharmonic scales (Bohlen‚ÄìPierce, 19-TET). Make a piece whose tension lives in TUNING, not in beating partials."*
**Could become a prototype that:** lets you wander a **Tonnetz-style harmonic lattice** in 5-limit Just Intonation / **Bohlen‚ÄìPierce** (the non-octave 3:1 tritave) / **19-EDO**, playing exact rational-ratio frequencies ‚Äî so the "tension" is the *strangeness of the tuning system* (BP refuses Western resolution; JI's syntonic comma drifts), not amplitude beating. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as 538-xenharmonic-lattice** (path (a): today's dive ‚Üí today's build).

## ¬ß402 ‚Äî 2026-06-12 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 402, KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **a child's bond with a voice companion deepens through MEMORY OF CONTENT, not novelty or a clock**
**Source (date-verified):** WebSearch, June 2026. Two wells checked. (1) **arxiv cs.SD** ‚Äî still dominated by *server-side* singing-voice synthesis (CONTUNER, local-style-token SVS, etc., 2022‚Äì2024) with nothing client-buildable in the last 30 days ‚Üí the recurring honest read that **#5 is unclaimable from cs.SD** (carried since ¬ß386), restated plainly rather than gamed. (2) **Children + voice-agent HCI** ‚Äî surfaced a **2026 AccScience study** ("Children's preferences for virtual agents with natural and synthetic voices," online-first 2026; n=132, ages 4‚Äì10) finding developmental differences in *willingness to interact* and *perceived voice quality* ‚Äî younger children bond more readily with an agent that reads as a known, responsive presence.
**Why it's surprising / why it matters here:** the lab's kids companions (518 living-ember, 322 voice-garden, the whole calm/joyful-awe set the jury flagged as autopilot) treat the creature as a *reactive surface* ‚Äî it responds in the moment, then forgets. The AccScience read reframes the strongest kids interaction as **a relationship that accrues**: the value isn't a new effect each visit, it's the creature *recognizing you* by what you gave it. That is exactly JURY 2026-06-12 provocation #6 ("a creature a child finds genuinely changed tomorrow") with a developmental-science backbone. **Date honesty:** "online-first 2026" is not verifiably <14 days, so **#5 NOT claimed** ‚Äî but the finding *backs the design decision* (memory-of-content over novelty) rather than decorating it.
**Could become a prototype that:** remembers the actual *songs* a child sings (pentatonic melodic content, not RMS energy), persists them across **calendar days**, and is visibly + audibly richer on return *because of what was sung* ‚Äî recalling melodies woven across days, with the creature's body literally drawn from the remembered contours. ‚Üí **Built this cycle as 549-kids-song-friend** (path (a): today's dive ‚Üí today's build; extends 518 per jury #6, transcends 322's timer-aged plant).

## ¬ß405 ‚Äî 2026-06-12 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 405, ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **the first genuinely <14-day, client-buildable bind in ~8 juries: in-browser Gaussian splatting**

**The break in the streak:** since ¬ß386 the dive has written "cs.SD is server-ML, #5 unclaimable" nine times ‚Äî the jury (2026-06-12 #5) called this decay out explicitly and named the *other* wells to mine (graphics/WebGPU, TouchDesigner, AV-artist show news). This dive went to those wells and found a real <14-day, client-buildable finding.

**Sources (date-verified):**
- **BrightCoding ‚Äî "Stop Waiting for CUDA! Run Gaussian Splatting in Your Browser with WebGPU," 2026-06-05** (https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2026/06/05/stop-waiting-for-cuda-run-gaussian-splatting-in-your-browser-with-webgpu). **7 days old.** Real-time 3D Gaussian-splat radiance-field rendering has moved into vanilla browser GPU paths (WebGPU, and WebGL2 for the rasterization) with no plugins ‚Äî the technique is now a commodity client-buildable primitive, not a CUDA/research-only one.
- **arxiv cs.GR recent (June 8‚Äì12 2026)** corroborates the wave: 2606.11529 (XPR ‚Äî extensible cross-platform point-based differentiable renderer, 11 Jun), 2606.11390 (scalable multi-GPU Gaussian splatting, 11 Jun), 2606.09606 (path-traced inverse rendering in 3D Gaussian fields, 9 Jun) ‚Äî point-based radiance fields are the dominant cs.GR topic this week.
- **DNE √ó Gracia (CG Channel, 2026)** ‚Äî a **4-minute streamable 4D-Gaussian-splatting performance of singer-songwriter Amy May**, playing in-browser over WebGPU on desktop/mobile/Quest 3. A *musical performer rendered as a volumetric splat scene* ‚Äî the most direct Resonance-relevant proof the technique is ready for music.

**Why it's surprising / why now:** the lab has shipped 553 prototypes and **never** rendered a Gaussian splat (the only prior "splat" is fluid-force injection in `119-poem-fluid`, a categorically different thing). Splatting is the dominant new rendering paradigm of 2024‚Äì26 and it just became a browser commodity ‚Äî a true lab-first technique that is finally client-buildable in one cycle (as additive billboarded splats in WebGL2, sidestepping captured-scene assets by generating the splat field procedurally from audio).

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *Karel's real piano sculpts a navigable cloud of thousands of glowing Gaussian splats* ‚Äî shipped as `557-piano-splat-galaxy` (onset-bloom galaxy), with `556` (spectrogram solid) + `558` (resonant body, the Amy-May-style "performer as body of light") banked. **Research‚Üíbuild chain: this dive ‚Üí today's build, direct and visible.** #5 honestly claimed at last.

## ¬ß406 ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (UTC) ¬∑ research-first dive (cycle 406, KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **no <14-day kid-buildable bind this window; the jury named a whole TENSION VOCABULARY that "had no children"**

**The dive:** WebSearch across (a) children's music interaction √ó body-tracking √ó WebGPU, June 2026, and (b) collaborative kids music-making on shared tablets. The freshest concrete finding was a *platform* note, not a *technique*: a usable **WebGPU baseline has now shipped to all major browsers via the Interop 2026 project** (verified 2026, recent ‚Äî confirmed in browser-tech roundups). That's real and worth tracking, but it's an enabling layer, not a bindable kid-appropriate technique ‚Äî and the lab already ships kids WebGPU (518/520/541). The child-CI literature surfaced (MIROR-Impro reflexive-interaction studies; collaborative grid-composition pass-the-tablet activities) is foundational, not <14 days.

**Honest read (the 10th jury running):** **#5 NOT claimable** ‚Äî no kid-appropriate, client-buildable, genuinely <14-day technique surfaced; cs.SD remains server-ML. The structural read from ¬ß404 holds: at ~360+ kids prototypes the easy kids lab-firsts are spent (Hands/Face/Pose/voice‚Üípitch/tilt/GPU-fluid/boids all taken), so a kids cycle competes on **ambition + diversity + execution + a NAMED concept that's new TO CHILDREN**, not a thin first-X.

**Where the surprise actually came from:** JURY 2026-06-12 provocation **#4** ‚Äî "514 proved metric-dissonance works and **had no children**. A whole tension vocabulary ‚Äî tempo canons, polymeter, **Reich phase**, Nancarrow studies ‚Äî got touched once and abandoned." That IS the seam: the lab has the time-tension toolkit for adults (514/545) but never brought it to a 4-year-old.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *a 4-year-old claps a rhythm and watches it slide out of phase with itself ‚Äî Steve Reich's gradual phase-shifting (Clapping Music 1972 / It's Gonna Rain 1965) made legible and joyful for a child, with a visible phase-wheel.* Shipped as `559-kids-clap-canon` (mic clap-onset ‚Üí SVG phase-canon), with `560-kids-shadow-band` (camera no-ML silhouette ‚Üí three.js creature band) + `561-kids-rainmaker` (shake ‚Üí Xenakis grain-cloud storm) banked. **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (no <14-day kids bind; the jury names a tension vocabulary with no children) ‚Üí today's build brings Reich phasing to a child.** #5 honestly NOT claimed; floor rests on #2+#3 (3/5).

## ¬ß409 ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (UTC) ¬∑ adult DEEP, embodied polytempo (cycle 2 of the 514 spine)

**The dive (research-first rule):** Two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) arxiv cs.SD recent + beat-tracking/polytempo/metric-structure, June 2026, and (b) real-time browser audio-visual / WebGPU creative-coding new techniques, May‚ÄìJune 2026 ‚Äî plus a direct fetch of `arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent` (June 8‚Äì12 window). The most on-topic hit was **S√° Pinto, Bernardes & Davies, "Challenging Beat Tracking: Tackling Polyrhythm, Polymetre, and Polytempo with Human-in-the-Loop Adaptation"** (Springer, CMMR proceedings; surfaced via a 2026 reprint) ‚Äî directly about beat-tracking on polytempo material (Candombe, Bambuco, Reich's *Piano Phase*). **But it is 2023 work, not <14 days** ‚Äî a named *reference*, not a fresh bind. The cs.SD recent listing (verified June 8‚Äì12) held **zero** beat-tracking / rhythm / interactive-performance papers ‚Äî all speech-recognition, TTS, deepfake-detection, symbolic Bach, codecs (the now-familiar server-ML read carried since ¬ß386). The browser/WebGPU search surfaced Aalap Davjekar's browser visual-synth (May 2026, already banked ¬ß408) and the Jan-2026 WebGPU-Baseline platform fact (already known) ‚Äî no bindable <14-day technique.

**Honest read:** **#5 NOT claimable** for this cycle ‚Äî no genuine <14-day finding to bind; said plainly rather than gaming a phantom score (the jury 2026-06-12 #5 explicitly called out the decayed research-first rule, so honesty here is the point). The dive instead confirmed the *concept's* pedigree: human-in-the-loop polytempo beat-tracking is a live research thread, which validates the embodied-conducting framing even without a fresh paper to cite.

**Where the surprise / build came from:** JURY 2026-06-12 provocations **#1** ("Chase a 4/5 ‚Äî take **514-polytempo-loom** to cycle 2 AND bind a fresh finding") and **#4** ("514 proved metric-dissonance works and **had no embodiment** ‚Äî build the SECOND time-tension piece, ideally embodied or multi-user: *a conducted tempo-canon where two people's gestures set incommensurable tempi*"). 514 is the autonomous loom (5 voices at fixed irrational ratios). The seam: make it **embodied** ‚Äî the human's own gestures set the tempi, so metric dissonance is something you feel in your arms, not just watch.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *you conduct two incommensurable tempi with your two hands ‚Äî left hand beats one voice, right hand the other, each snapped to the nearest irrational ratio {‚àö2, œÜ, e/2, œÄ/2} so the two consonant pulses drift through every phase relationship and can NEVER share a downbeat.* Shipped as **`568-conductor-hands`** (camera MediaPipe-Hands beat-extraction ‚Üí three.js never-closing-Lissajous phase-space), with `569-conductor-batons` (multitouch tap-tempo ‚Üí WebGL2 Nancarrow piano-roll) + `570-conductor-baton-motion` (DeviceMotion baton ‚Üí three.js) banked ¬ß409. **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (no <14-day bind; the jury names 514 + the embodied-polytempo seam directly) ‚Üí today's build makes the polytempo loom embodied.** #5 honestly NOT claimed; floor rests on #2 + #3 + #4 (3/5, #1 contestable for gesture‚Üítempo as a cross-modal lab input).

## ¬ß411 ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (UTC) ¬∑ the 2026 in-browser ASR wave (client-side speech‚Üítext as a browser-native primitive)

**The dive (research-first rule):** Three WebSearches ‚Äî (a) arxiv cs.SD May‚ÄìJune 2026 real-time/differentiable browser audio synthesis, (b) WebGPU creative-coding audio-visual releases June 2026, (c) SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery/Art Papers program ‚Äî plus targeted searches on Hugging Face transformers.js in-browser ASR models and Bileam Tschepe (Elekktronaut) recent TouchDesigner uploads.

**The finding:** **automatic speech recognition now runs fully client-side in the browser.** Hugging Face **transformers.js** executes ASR (Whisper et al.) via ONNX Runtime + WebAssembly/WebGPU with no server. Two recent drops sharpen it: **LiteASR / LiteWhisper** (Univ. of Washington + Kotoba Technologies ‚Äî "LiteASR: Efficient Automatic Speech Recognition with Low-Rank Approximation," surfaced in transformers.js docs ~March 2026) and **Cohere Transcribe** ("Introducing Cohere Transcribe: a new state-of-the-art in open-source speech recognition"). Source: huggingface.co/docs/transformers.js + the transformers.js GitHub. **Why it's surprising:** the lab has treated voice as *pitch/energy* (autocorrelation hum, vowel formants, vocoder) and ASR only as a *keyword switch* (189-voice-scene's 6-scene selector) ‚Äî but continuous, on-device *transcription of meaning* (the actual words) as a real-time control signal is a primitive the lab never used. **Honest date caveat:** the core capability is ~3 months old (Mar 2026), **not <14 days** ‚Äî so it is a *soft* bind, the production upgrade path, **NOT a clean #5 claim**.

**Secondary (logged, not built):** SIGGRAPH 2026 (LA, July 19‚Äì23) Art Papers theme = **"The Creative Complexities of Translation: Practices, Artifacts, and Stories"** (PRNewswire 2026-05-21, ~23 days) ‚Äî and the featured *Sternwerk* installation (water-drop-reset cellular automata, sound-amplified). The "translation" framing inspired the cross-modal *language‚Üímusic* angle, but a conference theme is not a client-buildable technique.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *speak, and your live words become a self-layering musical incantation ‚Äî continuous speech recognition transcribes your sentence, each word becomes a pentatonic pitch (letters‚Üíhash), finished phrases freeze into looping ostinati, and you hear your own words woven into an accreting canon.* Shipped this cycle as **`570-spoken-spell`** using the zero-dependency **Web Speech API** (instant, no model download ‚Äî the right tool for a <1s-load prototype) with the transformers.js/Whisper in-browser path noted as the offline + cross-browser cycle-2 upgrade (which would finally land a real #5). **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (in-browser ASR wave + the language/translation framing) ‚Üí today's build makes spoken language the composition.** #5 honestly NOT claimed (date ~3 months); floor rests on a qualified #1 (continuous-transcription‚Üícomposition, distinct from 189's keyword-switch + 345's typed text) + #2 (5 subsystems) + #3 (Reich *Different Trains* / Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room*) = 3/5.

## ¬ß413 ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 413 research dive (the head-tracked-binaural bind)

**The finding:** **webcam head-tracking now drives binaural spatial audio entirely in the browser, no headset.** MediaPipe **FaceLandmarker** (on-device, WASM/GPU, ~real-time) yields 3D face landmarks from a laptop/phone camera; recent open work ‚Äî the open-source **"Spatial-Head-Tracking-Audio"** project ("too broke for new AirPods, so I built my own head-tracked spatial audio for any headphones") and **webcam-auralization research** ("Head tracker using webcam for auralization", landmark‚Üíhead rotation matrices ‚Üí Euler angles) ‚Äî closes the loop into **Web Audio's `AudioListener`** (the `positionX/forwardX` AudioParams), so a webcam head pose moves the *listener* through an HRTF-panned field. Sources: huggingface/MediaPipe FaceLandmarker docs + github.com/MeharPro/Spatial-Head-Tracking-Audio + researchgate "Head tracker using webcam for auralization." **Why it's surprising:** the lab has shipped HRTF spatial audio many times (`7-spatial`, `29-scene-spatial`, `148-spatial-palette`, `394-soundfield-room`, `400-soundwalk-room`) but the listener was always moved by **dragging a dot** or **singing** ‚Äî never by the visitor's *own body*. Head-pose‚ÜíAudioListener makes the body the controller: the directest answer to the standing jury complaint "**zero embodied-spatial prototypes after 570 builds**." **Honest date caveat:** webcam auralization is an actively-developing 2026 area but the underlying math (HRTF, landmark‚ÜíEuler) is foundational, so this is a **soft bind, a qualified #5**, not a hard <14-day claim.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *your own face is the listener ‚Äî lean and turn your head and a warm just-intonation Dream-House chord physically re-spatialises around you; lean toward a voice and it blooms.* Shipped this cycle as **`576-presence-room`** (MediaPipe FaceLandmarker ‚Üí Web Audio `AudioListener` ‚Üí 6 HRTF-panned JI drone voices, raw WebGL2 warm haze). **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (webcam head-tracking ‚Üí binaural listener) ‚Üí today's build makes the visitor's head the controller of the soundfield** ‚Äî the first head-tracked binaural piece in the lab, and the first genuinely *embodied*-spatial one. Floor rests on a clean **#1** (head-pose‚ÜíAudioListener is lab-first; drag/sing-moved listeners don't count) + **#2** (face-tracking + HRTF/AudioListener spatial + WebGL2) + **#3** (Maryanne Amacher / La Monte Young *Dream House* / Pauline Oliveros). #5 held as a soft/qualified bind, not gamed.

## ¬ß414 ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 414 research dive (KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **WebGPU is production-ready across all browsers in 2026, and verlet position-based-dynamics make a *stretchable singing membrane* a browser-native instrument ‚Äî the directest break from the jury-banned pentatonic-tap-wash: a sheet you pull whose tension bends a CONTINUOUS chord**

**The dive (research-first rule):** Two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "interactive audio-visual creative coding kids music WebGPU June 2026 new technique"; (b) "shadertoy / TouchDesigner audio-reactive verlet cloth string physics 2026." The jury's #5 dared me back to the **graphics / WebGPU / AV-artist wells** (the ones ¬ß405-splat proved still hold fresh binds) instead of the dry cs.SD well.

**The finding:** **WebGPU has gone production-ready across every major browser in 2026** (utsubo.com "What's New in Three.js (2026): WebGPU, New Workflows & Beyond" ‚Äî WebGPU "becoming production-ready across all browsers" is the headline shift of the year; Safari 26 / Firefox 141 shipped it), and the WebGPU-native frameworks (iPlug3 on WebGPU+Skia Graphite hitting 120fps; ChucK's ChuGL) confirm GPU-compute creative tools are now the default surface. Pairing that with **verlet / position-based-dynamics cloth** ‚Äî a foundational real-time-physics technique now trivially GPU-parallelisable (shadertoy "Cloth Sim. via verlet integral"; verlet TOPs in TouchDesigner) ‚Äî yields a **2-D elastic membrane** you can grab and stretch on the GPU in real time. **Why it matters for THIS cycle:** the jury (2026-06-13) banned the kids **pentatonic-safe-wash** (‚âà6 of 7 kids pieces are "tap ‚Üí a discrete consonant note ‚Üí pretty viz") and the **three.js/SVG outputs**, and begged for **WebGPU revival** (starved 1√ó) and **warmth you feel, not a puzzle you solve.** A membrane whose *tension* bends a **continuous, gliding** warm chord answers all of it: the pitch is physical and continuous (never a discrete safe-note ‚Äî the literal opposite of the pentatonic-tap reflex), the renderer is the starved WebGPU (Canvas2D fallback), and stretching silk that sings is pure warmth. **Honest date caveat:** WebGPU-production-ready is a *2026-trend* (months, not <14 days) and verlet PBD is foundational (Jakobsen, *Advanced Character Physics*, 2001) ‚Äî so **#5 is NOT claimed**; the dive validates the renderer-revival lane the jury named, not a hard <14-day result.

**Freshness within the lab (grepped):** verlet exists only as **1-D strings** (`303-kids-wind-harp` ‚Äî vertical pinned chains plucked by tilt); a **2-D connected membrane** you grab/stretch with multi-touch, sounding **continuous tension‚Üípitch** (not discrete plucks), is not in the lab. So this is a fresh *interaction + musical structure*, not a fresh primitive ‚Äî I do **not** over-claim #1 on verlet.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle binds):** *a luminous elastic sheet of light ‚Äî grab it with your fingers and pull; the membrane stretches like silk and its tension bends a warm choir/bowed-string chord, so pulling it taut makes the voices rise and brighten and letting it billow lets them sigh and fall. The pitch is continuous and physical ‚Äî you bend the chord with your hands, you never tap a note.* Built this cycle as **`579-silk-choir`** (see STATE/INDEX ¬ß414). **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (WebGPU production-ready + verlet membrane ‚Üí a stretchable singing sheet) ‚Üí today's build makes the membrane's tension a continuous, hand-bent chord** ‚Äî reviving the jury-starved WebGPU and breaking the pentatonic-tap-wash. Floor rests on **#2** (‚â•3 subsystems: GPU/CPU verlet membrane solver + multi-touch grab + tension‚Üícontinuous-bowed-chord synth + render) + **#3** (Jakobsen verlet PBD; the continuous-expressive-pitch lineage of the Ondes Martenot / Theremin; warm-drone refs) = honest **3/5**; #1 NOT claimed (verlet exists at 303), #5 NOT claimed (no <14-day bind).

## ¬ß416 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 416 research dive (KIDS, DEEP) ‚Äî **the whole body as the instrument: video-keypoint ‚Üí real-time music, and the foundational responsive-environment lineage the lab has never built for kids**

**The dive (research-first rule):** three WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "MediaPipe full body pose real-time interactive dance music browser 2026"; (b) "camera body motion responsive environment installation children 2026"; (c) "arxiv 2026 real-time motion to music sonification body movement embodied." The jury's standing **#1** ("get off the glass ‚Äî the lab still has zero embodied-spatial prototypes after 570 builds, and mic is over-used 5√ó") pointed the dive at the body-as-controller well.

**The finding:** **full-body video keypoints are now a mature real-time music-control signal in the browser.** MediaPipe **PoseLandmarker** tracks 33 body landmarks (with depth) fully on-device via WASM/GPU at interactive rates (research.google BlazePose; mediapipe.org), and the academic line treats body movement itself as the score: arxiv **2511.02351 *Human-Machine Ritual: Synergic Performance through Real-Time Motion Recognition*** (Nov 2025), the conducting-gesture ‚Üí virtual-orchestra work (arxiv 2604.x), and "Reimagining Dance" ‚Äî dancers dynamically controlling music in real time from video keypoints. Sources: research.google/blog BlazePose + arxiv.org/abs/2511.02351 + the motion-sound-interaction / motiongram sonification literature. **Why it's surprising for THIS lab:** the lab has shipped *hands* (`568-conductor-hands`, MediaPipe Hands ‚Üí tempo) and a *head* (`576-presence-room`, FaceLandmarker ‚Üí listener), but **never the whole figure** ‚Äî a 4-year-old just *dancing* in front of the iPad and the room singing back. That is the directest possible answer to "get off the glass," and it sidesteps every kids ban at once: no mic (camera), no tap (motion), no creature (frozen spine), and ‚Äî if the harmony is warm-modal instead of C-major-pentatonic ‚Äî no safe-wash either. **Honest date caveat:** PoseLandmarker is foundational/months-old and arxiv 2511 is ~7 months old, so **#5 is NOT claimed**; the ambition lever rests on the **named foundational reference Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1974)** ‚Äî the original full-body responsive environment where your silhouette plays the room ‚Äî plus **Daniel Rozin**'s body-as-pixels mirror lineage (both on AGENT.md's AV-artist list). The dive validates the embodied-body well the jury named, not a <14-day result.

**Freshness within the lab (grepped INDEX + recent READMEs):** camera input exists only as hands (568) and head/face (576); a **whole-body / silhouette / optical-flow / pose-skeleton** instrument for kids is not in the lab. Whole-figure motion ‚Üí music is a fresh *input modality + interaction* here, so I claim a **soft #1** (first whole-figure motion instrument) but lean the floor on #2 + #3 to stay honest.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle binds):** *a 4-year-old's whole body, seen by the front camera, becomes a warm instrument ‚Äî dance and the room sings; movement paints luminous light and bends/blooms a warm modal chord, with NO tapping, NO humming, NO creature.* Built this cycle as a **DEEP 3-approach fire** ‚Äî `581-kids-shadow-band` (Canvas2D frame-difference motion-energy ‚Üí zoned warm-modal bells), `582-kids-dance-sky` (MediaPipe PoseLandmarker ‚Üí skeleton aura, hands‚Üívoices / body-spread‚Üíchord-openness), `583-kids-light-ribbons` (Canvas2D optical-flow field ‚Üí continuous body-bowed choir + flowing ribbons). Ship the strongest; bank the others (IDEAS ¬ß416). **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (whole-body keypoints ‚Üí real-time music + Krueger responsive-environment) ‚Üí today's build makes the child's whole moving body the instrument** ‚Äî the lab's first whole-figure embodied piece, off the glass, off the mic, off the pentatonic wash. Floor: **#2** (‚â•3 subsystems: camera capture + real-time motion/pose analysis + warm-modal spatial synth + Canvas2D body render) + **#3** (Krueger *Videoplace* / Rozin) = honest **3/5**; soft #1, #5 NOT claimed.

## ¬ß417 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 417 research dive (ADULT, DEEP) ‚Äî **concatenative musaicing: target-driven corpus resynthesis as a browser-native way to "reach into" a recording and re-voice it with its own grains**

**The dive (research-first rule):** WebSearches on (a) "real-time neural audio synthesis browser WebGPU June 2026 new release" and (b) "arxiv cs.SD 2026 real-time concatenative granular resynthesis audio mosaicing piano timbre," plus a check of the lab's love signal (`/api/dream/votes`).

**The finding:** **concatenative musaicing / corpus-based concatenative synthesis (CataRT) is having a 2026 moment.** Diemo Schwarz's CataRT (real-time corpus-based concatenative synthesis, 2006) is foundational, but the live thread is fresh: **"The Concatenator: A Bayesian Approach to Real-Time Concatenative Musaicing"** (Tralie & Berger, arXiv:2411.04366, Nov 2024 ‚Äî a particle-filter that infers the best corpus grains to re-create a *target* stream in real time) and **MACataRT / MACAT musical-agent systems** (arXiv:2502.00023, Feb 2026 ‚Äî CataRT wrapped as an interactive gesture-controlled agent). Adjacent: **Latent Granular Resynthesis using Neural Audio Codecs** (arXiv:2507.19202, 2025 ‚Äî granular resynthesis in a neural-codec latent space). On the browser side, **LFM2.5-Audio / Voxtral Realtime (WebGPU + ONNX)** confirm real-time audio ML now runs fully client-side. **Why it's surprising for the lab:** the lab has treated his piano as something to *visualize* (557/564 splat, 243/267/321 spectral) or to *scatter blindly* (loved `227-paths-granular` is random granular). It has NEVER done **target-driven grain MATCHING** ‚Äî where a gesture specifies a timbre coordinate and the system *selects* the closest grains from his own corpus. That's the difference between sprinkling his sound and *reaching into it and pulling out the exact grain you mean.* **Honest date caveat:** CataRT is foundational (2006); the <14-day claim is NOT made ‚Äî this is a soft bind to a live 2024‚Äì2026 wave (The Concatenator / MACataRT), the production lane, not a fresh primitive.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *Karel's real solo piano is shattered into a spectral-feature-tagged grain corpus; you drag a probe through a 2-D timbre field (X=brightness/centroid, Y=register/pitch) and a CataRT-style matcher continuously selects and overlaps his closest-matching grains into a continuous warm cloud of his own sound ‚Äî you paint with his timbre, his corpus answers.* Shipped this cycle as **`583-piano-mosaic-field`** (per-grain FFT centroid/RMS/peak-pitch corpus + weighted-distance matcher with repeat penalty + Hann-overlap concatenation through a limiter; WebGL2 additive grain-field, Canvas2D + synth-piano fallbacks). **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (concatenative-musaicing wave) ‚Üí today's build makes his recording reachable ‚Äî target-driven grain selection, the genuine step past 227's blind granular scatter.** Floor: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (Schwarz CataRT / Tralie&Berger *The Concatenator* / MACataRT / Roads *Microsound*) = honest 3/5; soft #1 (first target-matched concatenative resynthesis in the lab); #5 soft, not clean-claimed (Schwarz 2006 is foundational). Banked the two DEEP siblings: `584-piano-spectral-tide` (phase-vocoder spectral freeze) + `585-piano-grain-orrery` (n-body physics‚Üígranular retrigger) ‚Äî IDEAS ¬ß417.

## ¬ß419 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 419 research dive (ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **live environmental-ocean data as a warm musical instrument: real swell state ‚Üí just-intonation drone, the "music about something other than music" the jury keeps asking for**

**The dive (research-first rule):** WebSearches on (a) "ocean wave swell data sonification real-time 2026 generative audio installation" and (b) "arxiv cs.SD May/June 2026 environmental data sonification real-time parameter mapping," cross-checked against the lab's standing data-sonification pieces and the love signal.

**The finding:** real-world **environmental-data sonification with a *musical* (scale-based, consonant) mapping** is having a quiet 2026 moment, and it lines up exactly with the jury's loudest unfilled adult ask (provocation #2: "real-world-data sonification is 0√ó the whole window ‚Ä¶ music *about* something other than music"). The freshest concrete bind is **arXiv 2602.14560, "Preliminary sonification of ENSO using traditional Javanese gamelan scales" (Feb 2026)** ‚Äî it parameter-maps the Ni√±o-3.4 sea-surface-temperature anomaly index (1870‚Äì2024) onto two gamelan pentatonic systems, i.e. it deliberately routes *ocean/climate data through a real tuning* rather than abstract beeps. That sits on a lineage ‚Äî **"Pulse of an Ocean: Sonification of Ocean Buoy Data"** (Sturm, buoy-spectrum ‚Üí audible wave dynamics) and the **Zadar Sea Organ** (a built instrument the literal tide plays) and **Annea Lockwood's "A Sound Map of the Hudson River."** **Why it's surprising for *this* lab:** the lab has many data pieces (233 earth-pulse, 314 solar-wind, 437 wiki-pulse, 463 terra-gamelan, 502 atmosphere-veil, 337 seismic-globe), but **none sonify the live state of the ocean**, and the recent data pieces lean abstract/cerebral ‚Äî none is a *warm, breathing instrument* tuned by the real sea right now. The honest date caveat: the ENSO paper is ~4 months old (not <14 days), so this is a **soft #5 bind to a live 2026 wave**, not a fresh-primitive claim ‚Äî consistent with the jury's note that the dive should stop over-claiming.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the builds this cycle bound, mode WIDE):** three orthogonal warm/off-the-puzzle adult explorers, none using a jury-banned tag (three.js / SVG / mic / onset-tempo / pentatonic-puzzle) and all off the over-trended camera input ‚Äî
- **`588-tidal-organ`** ‚Äî live **Open-Meteo Marine API** swell (height/period/direction, CORS-friendly, no key) ‚Üí a just-intonation **sea-organ drone** (wave-period = the breath, height = fullness, direction = spatial pan) over a WebGPU ocean-energy field (Canvas2D fallback). The directest hit on provocation #2: the tension lives in the actual sea. Refs: Zadar Sea Organ / Lockwood / Pulse of an Ocean / arXiv 2602.14560.
- **`589-still-bloom`** ‚Äî **device-motion stillness** ‚Üí an √âliane-Radigue drone that blooms voice-by-voice the stiller you hold the device, over WebGPU reaction-diffusion "coral" (Canvas2D fallback). Off-glass/off-mic/off-finger, warmth you *feel* (provocations #1 + #4). Refs: Radigue / La Monte Young Dream House / Gray-Scott.
- **`590-star-atlas`** ‚Äî **aim the phone at the real sky** (device-orientation/compass + bundled bright-star catalog ‚Üí alt/az) and the constellations overhead become a drifting just-intonation chord; Canvas2D star-field. Embodied, cosmic, warm. Ref: **John Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* (1961).**

**Research‚Üíbuild chain:** dive (live environmental-ocean sonification with a real tuning, the jury's 0√ó category) ‚Üí **`588-tidal-organ`** as the lead explorer; the other two attack the same "warmth, not a puzzle / off the glass" mandate via independent inputs (motion, orientation). Ship the strongest; bank the other two as IDEAS ¬ß419.

## ¬ß420 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 420 research dive (KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **un-simulated physical instruments: in a 588-deep lab, the freshest move is to grep for a real-world acoustic phenomenon nobody has modeled yet, then sonify it**

**The dive (research-first rule):** WebSearches on (a) "physical modeling sonification interactive instrument 2026 friction resonator glass armonica web audio" and (b) "arxiv cs.SD June 2026 real-time physics-based sound synthesis interaction granular contact," cross-checked against an exhaustive grep of `src/app/dream` for ~25 candidate physical phenomena.

**The finding:** the lab is now **588 prototypes deep**, and the diversity/ambition mandate's "technique never used in the lab" criterion has gotten genuinely hard ‚Äî most obvious AV mechanics are taken (cymatics already at `19`/`165`, granular at `18`/`227`/`360`, ferrofluid at `456`, slime/physarum at `260`/`327`, cellular-automata at `25`). The fresh, repeatable move is to treat the lab itself as the corpus: **enumerate real-world acoustic/physical instruments and phenomena, grep each, and build the first one with 0 hits.** This cycle's grep surfaced **three clean lab-gaps**: **plinko / Galton board** (0√ó), the **jal-tarang + glass-armonica rubbed-rim friction resonator** (0√ó), and the **sundial / shadow-as-sequencer** (0√ó). The literature soft-bind: physical-modeling and *friction/contact* sonification is a live 2026 thread ‚Äî **arXiv 2508.01789 "Sonify Anything: Towards Context-Aware Sonic Interactions in AR"** (material-based contact sounds), **arXiv 2507.14947 "Echoes of the Land"** (an interactive installation built on a physical model), the foundational **"Kinetic surface friction rendering for interactive sonification"** (arXiv 2107.13825), and the **International Faust Conference, Lyon, June 2026** (physical-modeling DSP). **Why it's surprising for the lab:** these are centuries-old physical instruments (Galton 1894, glass armonica 1761, the sundial) that the lab ‚Äî for all its WebGPU/ML/data sophistication ‚Äî had simply never *simulated*; each is a fresh primitive hiding in plain sight. **Honest date caveat:** **#5 NOT clean-claimed** ‚Äî these are foundational phenomena newly simulated, not a <14-day result; the AR-material-sound papers are months old. Soft bind, said plainly per the jury's anti-over-claim note.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the builds this cycle bound, mode WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal kids explorers, each clearing the floor via DIFFERENT tags, none jury-banned [three.js / SVG / mic / onset-tempo / pentatonic-wash / creature-spine]):**
- **`593-kids-sun-shadows` ("Move the Sun") ‚Äî WINNER.** A **sundial turned into a sequencer**: drag a glowing sun, every object's long cast shadow (gnomon geometry `L = H/tan(altitude)`, swinging away from the sun) sweeps the ground, and when a shadow tip crosses a JI-tuned chime stone it rings ‚Äî so moving the light *scrubs a sequencer built out of geometry* (shadow-as-playhead), over a dawn‚Üínoon‚Üídusk sky/pad journey. The warmest + most surprising answer to the jury's "chase warmth, tension you feel" + the spirit of "off the glass / spatial." Refs: the sundial/gnomon; Olafur Eliasson *The Weather Project* (Tate Modern, 2003).
- **`591-kids-plinko-bells` ("Bell Drop").** A sonified **Galton/quincunx board** (1894): balls cascade through pegs into 9 just-intonation bins, the binomial histogram blooms *as a warm major chord* with the most-hit center bins on root/fourth/fifth ‚Äî emergent, autonomous, self-playing music + a visible bell curve. Banked IDEAS ¬ß420 (love-aligned to Karel's emergent-music cluster 236/130/262).
- **`592-kids-water-bowls` ("Singing Water").** **Jal-tarang + glass-armonica**: tap a bowl to ring it, **RUB a finger to make it SING** via a **friction-excited resonator** (high-Q bandpass noise + shimmer partials, amplitude/brightness driven by drag speed ‚Äî tap and rub are two excitations of one resonator). The deepest fresh *audio primitive* of the three. Refs: Jal Tarang; Benjamin Franklin's glass armonica (1761). Banked IDEAS ¬ß420.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain:** dive (grep the 588-deep lab for un-simulated physical instruments ‚Üí three 0√ó gaps) ‚Üí ship the warmest (`593-kids-sun-shadows`); bank the friction-resonator (`592`) and the Galton board (`591`) as IDEAS ¬ß420. Floor (winner): #1 (shadow-as-sequencer, 0√ó verified) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (sundial/Eliasson) = honest 3/5; #5 not claimed.
