## ¬ß421 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 421 (ADULT, DEEP) ¬∑ the disintegration / self-overwriting-looper thread

**The dive.** Searching current AV/audio work for a genuinely *different register* than the lab's recent four warm reactive screen-toys (576/580/583/588), the freshest live thread is **audible decay / disintegration as a compositional process**. Two concrete, dated anchors: **Music Thing Modular "Degenerator"** ([Synthtopia, 2026-05-28](https://www.synthtopia.com/content/2026/05/28/make-your-own-disintegration-loops-with-the-music-thing-modular-workshop-system/)) ‚Äî a brand-new (~17 days) Workshop-System firmware that turns the Computer module into a **self-overwriting audio looper** "from slow ambient decay to saturated noise and collapse," explicitly built to recreate **William Basinski's _The Disintegration Loops_ (2002)** (tape oxide flaking off the head every pass, so the music crumbled as it was captured); and **arXiv:2511.17404 "The Artist is Present" (Nov 2025)**, which studies whether Basinski-style processual decay can be *induced* in text-to-audio AI via attribute-level prompting ‚Äî confirming disintegration is a live 2026 research object, not just a 2002 record.

**Why it's surprising for the lab.** The lab *cites* Basinski (banked seed `584-piano-spectral-tide` names him) but has **never implemented an actual decay process** ‚Äî 584 was spectral **freeze** (holding time *still*, the opposite of decay) and it was banked, not shipped. Across 593 prototypes the lab visualizes sound, scatters it (granular `227`), and re-tunes it, but **nothing it has built gets irreversibly worse as it plays** ‚Äî no piece where the material is *consumed by its own playback*. That's a whole unfilled emotional register: not reactive warmth, but warmth-as-loss, witnessed over a long arc.

**Honest date caveat.** **#5 NOT clean-claimed.** The Degenerator is real and current (<30 days, satisfies the research-first rule) but it's ~17 days, *not* <14, and the underlying technique (feedback re-recording, self-overwriting buffers) is foundational, not a fresh primitive. So the floor is cleared on **#2 + #3**, with #5 a soft current-month bind ‚Äî said plainly per the jury's anti-over-claim note.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode DEEP ‚Äî one concept, 3 techniques; ship the most literal + emotionally true):**
- **`594-disintegration-loop` ("The Disintegration Loop") ‚Äî WINNER.** A **self-overwriting "tape"**: a warm JI seed in a single mutable `Float32Array`, re-recorded onto itself each pass through a wear chain (downward-creeping lowpass + permanent accumulating oxide dropouts + wow/flutter + tanh saturation), so erosion is genuine compounding state. One lever between *hold on* and *let go* ‚Äî a moral choice, not a puzzle. The directest, most literal implementation of **both** named references (Basinski's literal tape + the Degenerator's self-overwriting looper). Canvas2D worn-tape strip driven by real decay state. Refs: Basinski 2002 + Music Thing Degenerator 2026-05-28.
- **`595-disintegration-spectral` ("Fading Photograph").** Same disintegration in the **frequency domain**: a persistent per-bin "oxide map" attenuates partials (highs first, spreading down) + adds irreversible detune drift; resynth via an oscillator bank; an eroding spectrogram fades like a photograph in the sun. Banked IDEAS ¬ß421. **Caveat that lost it:** spectral-domain erosion overlaps Karel's already-loved spectral cluster (`243`‚ù§Ô∏è/`267`‚ù§Ô∏è/`321`‚ù§Ô∏è) and the banked `584` ‚Äî too close to ship in the same window (the same reason `584` was banked).
- **`596-disintegration-dust` ("Dust").** Same idea **granular**: a monotonic `wear` variable climbing every grain drives density/duration/de-pitch/drop-out, so a continuous cloud thins to sparse motes; **WebGPU compute-particle** render (revives the starved renderer) with a Canvas2D fallback. Banked IDEAS ¬ß421. **Caveat that lost it:** richest visual + revives WebGPU, but granular-into-particles overlaps the loved particle/granular cluster (`227`‚ù§Ô∏è/`236`‚ù§Ô∏è/`262`‚ù§Ô∏è/`130`‚ù§Ô∏è) and is the least distinct on the TECH axis. The WebGPU-spectacle resurrection.

**Floor (winner):** #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: self-overwriting tape DSP + per-pass wear chain + long-form stateful pass controller + state-driven canvas) + #3 (Basinski _Disintegration Loops_ 2002 / Music Thing Degenerator 2026-05-28) = honest **3/5**; #1 soft (first audible-decay-as-state in the lab, but feedback re-recording isn't a grep-clean primitive); #5 soft (current-month Degenerator, foundational technique).

## ¬ß422 ‚Äî 2026-06-14 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 422 ¬∑ KIDS DEEP ¬∑ research dive

**Dive (friction / modal physical-modelling synthesis in the browser).** Searched arxiv cs.SD + physical-modelling-synthesis for a fresh <30-day bind on *friction-excited* sound. Findings cluster around **modal synthesis** (decompose an object into resonant modes; excite the bank) and **differentiable / neural modal resonators** ‚Äî e.g. *Rigid-Body Sound Synthesis with Differentiable Modal Resonators* (arXiv:2210.15306) and *Differentiable Modal Synthesis for Planar String Sound* (arXiv:2407.05516, Jul 2024). The **glass armonica** (rubbed nested glass bowls, Franklin 1761) and **jal tarang** (struck tuned water bowls) are the canonical *friction* and *strike* excitations of the same resonator family. **Honest freshness verdict:** no clean <30-day paper bind surfaced ‚Äî the technique is **foundational** (FFT-era modal synthesis), so **#5 stays soft** (consistent with the ¬ß420 seed's own soft framing). The genuinely lab-new thing is the *primitive itself*: a real-time **friction-excited resonator driven by drag speed** has never been built in this lab (grep-verified: 0√ó "friction"/"glass armonica"/"jal tarang"/"modal synthesis" across 594 prototypes). **Could become a prototype that:** lets a child *rub* a glowing bowl and have it SING, where rub speed maps to a continuous friction energy injection (amplitude + brightness + Q) ‚Äî exactly cycle 422's build. Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive confirms the friction resonator as the fresh primitive ‚Üí `597-kids-water-bowls` ships it (consuming the ¬ß420-banked, research-grounded seed `592`).

**Three approaches explored (mode DEEP ‚Äî one instrument, 3 synthesis cores; ship the most robust + truest):**
- **`597-kids-water-bowls` ("Singing Water") ‚Äî WINNER.** Friction-excited resonator (subtractive): per-bowl looping pink-ish noise ‚Üí **high-Q bandpass** (Q 14‚Üí26) tuned to the bowl pitch + 3 quiet inharmonic shimmer sines; drag speed (smoothed px/ms) drives amplitude + bandpass brightness + Q in real time; tap = struck inharmonic bell (1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2√ó). Truest to the ¬ß420 banked seed (which named exactly this primitive as "the genuinely new browser synthesis"), most robust (standard nodes only + a **separate audio-rate smoothing loop decoupled from the visual rAF** so the rub stays smooth even if the canvas hitches), kid-safe master chain (lowpass 7200 + comp ‚àí18/6/12, gains capped). Refs: Jal Tarang + Franklin glass armonica (1761).
- **`598-kids-water-bowls` (modal additive).** Per-bowl bank of 5 decaying sine modes (1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2, 5.4√ó); tap = impulse into all modes; rub = continuous stick-slip friction source (noise+saw, band-passed) re-energizing the bank proportional to speed. Banked IDEAS ¬ß422. **Caveat:** arguably the *truest glass timbre* (real inharmonic mode bank), but the doubled excitation path (friction source AND direct mode-gain lift) is muddier to tune and less faithful to the banked seed's literal "bandpass-noise" spec.
- **`599-kids-water-bowls` (bowed waveguide AudioWorklet).** Inline `AudioWorkletProcessor` (Blob URL): tuned waveguide delay-line + nonlinear stick-slip friction curve driven by a "bow velocity" param, with a DelayNode-comb fallback. Banked IDEAS ¬ß422. **Caveat that lost it:** the most physically *authentic* stick-slip, but an inline-Blob worklet is the **least robust** path for an unverifiable-in-sandbox kids ship that must "just work" at a silent 06:30 glance (addModule timing, prod Blob handling) ‚Äî banked as the eventual AudioWorklet-deepening.

**Floor (winner `597`):** #1 (first **friction-excited resonator / modal synthesis** primitive in the lab ‚Äî grep-verified 0√ó across 594 prototypes) + #3 (named refs that drive it: Jal Tarang + Franklin's glass armonica, 1761) = honest **2/5**; #5 soft (foundational technique, no <30-day bind).

## ¬ß430 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 430 ¬∑ KIDS WIDE ¬∑ research dive ‚Äî **emergent stochastic physics as a kids instrument: the grep-0√ó Galton board**

**Dive (research-first rule).** WebSearches on (a) "Galton board / quincunx sonification generative music emergent 2026 interactive" and (b) "arxiv cs.SD June 2026 stochastic physics emergent generative music sonification interactive," cross-checked against the lab's own 618-prototype corpus. **No clean <14-day primitive surfaced** ‚Äî the freshest *live* anchors are **Insook Choi, "Interactive Sonification Exploring Emergent Behavior" (2018)** (a formal model for sonifying emergent/stochastic systems with interactive auditory feedback ‚Äî the academic lineage for "let a chance process play music") and the active **International Faust Conference / IFC-26 (Lyon, June 2026)** physical-modeling DSP thread; the broader cs.SD front this quarter is generative/aleatoric music (stochastic, indeterminate, chance composition). **#5 NOT clean-claimed** ‚Äî said plainly, consistent with the ¬ß420/¬ß422 soft framing.

**The finding (surprising-for-the-lab).** A **Galton board / quincunx** (Sir Francis Galton, 1894) is a centuries-old *emergent-physics instrument*: marbles cascade through a peg lattice, each bounce a coin-flip, and the binomial ‚Üí normal distribution **draws its own bell curve** ‚Äî a visible, audible lesson in how order emerges from chance (central limit theorem). For all the lab's WebGPU/ML/real-data sophistication, this is **grep-0√ó across 618 prototypes** (confirmed against ¬ß420's physical-instrument sweep, which first flagged plinko/Galton as one of three clean 0√ó gaps and banked it as `591-kids-plinko-bells`). It is also a near-perfect *kids* instrument: drop-and-watch (no skill, no reading), nothing ever "wrong" (every bin tuned), and self-playing/mesmerizing ‚Äî and it is love-aligned to Karel's emergent-music cluster (`236-particle-life-song`‚ù§Ô∏è, `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è).

**Could become a prototype that:** lets a 4-year-old DROP a marble into a quincunx and hear **chance itself play a warm chord** ‚Äî the bins tuned symmetrically so the bell-curve peak IS the root/fifth/octave, so the emergent histogram is always a rooted, consonant chord, richest in the middle.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal kids explorers, fired to break the silly-googly-creature 3-peat 603/609/617; ship the most distinct + highest-ambition):**
- **`619-kids-plinko-bells` ("Marble Bells") ‚Äî WINNER.** Galton/quincunx stochastic physics ‚Üí emergent just-intonation bell histogram, WebGL2 instanced (Canvas2D fallback). Consumes the research-grounded banked ¬ß420 seed `591` (research-first rule path (c)). The only clean ambition-#1 of the three. Refs: Galton 1894 / Wintergatan Marble Machine / Choi 2018.
- **`620-kids-music-mill` ("Music Mill").** Tap-to-place pegs on a rotating music-box cylinder ‚Üí self-playing Karplus-Strong loop (WebGL2). Banked IDEAS ¬ß430. **Caveat that lost it:** a step-sequencer is not a lab-first (overlaps loved loop-builders `172`‚ù§Ô∏è/`111`‚ù§Ô∏è); honest 2/5.
- **`621-kids-duet-bloom`.** Two-finger cooperative consonance ‚Äî the meadow blooms only when two kids find a consonant interval together (Canvas2D). Banked IDEAS ¬ß430. **Caveat that lost it:** the freshest *interaction* (the thin cooperative-two-player social lane) but Canvas2D (jury-flagged) + cooperative-consonance overlaps adult `508`; honest 2/5.

**Floor (winner `619`):** #1 (first Galton-board / quincunx stochastic-physics instrument ‚Äî grep-0√ó across 618) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: quincunx physics + histogram accumulator + JI struck-bell synth + WebGL2 instanced render) + #3 (Galton 1894 / Wintergatan / Choi 2018) = honest **3/5**; #5 soft (foundational phenomenon, no <14-day bind).

## ¬ß431 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (UTC) ¬∑ adult cycle ¬∑ cellular automata as an autonomous composer (the 1D / Wolfram sub-lane)

**Dive (research-first rule).** The concept was seeded by Karel's loved emergent/autonomous-music cluster (`236-particle-life-song`‚ù§Ô∏è, `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è, `262-aurora-particle`‚ù§Ô∏è) plus STATE ¬ß430's adult standing-watch: the adult side had run **four edges/real-data builds in a row** (601 howl ¬∑ 606 piano-HPSS ¬∑ 613 seismic ¬∑ 618 solar) and was warned not to let edges/real-data become the new autopilot. A system that *plays itself* is off both the cozy and the edges autopilots. The **decisive research act was a grep audit of the lab's own 619-prototype corpus** ‚Äî and it surfaced a near-miss worth recording: an initial batch grep returned a **false negative** (a `grep -E` pattern using `\|` alternation, which `-E` treats as a literal, not OR), so a re-run with a correct pattern revealed CA is in fact *well-mined in 2D*: `25-cellular` + `180-cellular` are BOTH "Conway's Life as a musical composer," `264-kids-lenia-pond` is **Lenia** (continuous CA), and `360-kids-sand-choir` / `377-kids-cascade-bloom` are **sandpile** CA.

**The finding (surprising-for-the-lab).** Despite all that CA prior art, the **1D elementary cellular automaton** ‚Äî Wolfram's 256 rules over a left/self/right neighbourhood, the rules 30 (chaos) / 90 (Sierpinski fractal) / 110 (Turing-complete) ‚Äî is **grep-0√ó**. It is a uniquely musical CA: a *single 8-bit integer* defines the entire dynamical system, so "the rule number is the composition" is a genuinely novel one-lever interaction, and the three regime classes (periodic / fractal / chaotic / complex) map cleanly to three audibly distinct musical characters. Live anchors: **Stephen Wolfram, _A New Kind of Science_ (2002)**; **Matthew Cook, Rule-110 Turing-completeness (2004)**; the broader generative/aleatoric cs.SD front (Xenakis's CA-in-composition lineage). **#5 NOT clean-claimed** (foundational, no <14-day result ‚Äî said plainly, consistent with ¬ß420/¬ß422/¬ß430 framing).

**Could become a prototype that:** lets you turn a single integer 0‚Äì255 and hear how the universe of elementary CA rules sounds ‚Äî chaos, fractal, complexity ‚Äî the rule number as a knob on emergence itself.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode DEEP ‚Äî one concept, 3 CA paradigms; ship the only duplication-clean one):**
- **`622-wolfram-rhythm` ‚Äî WINNER.** 1D elementary CA / Wolfram-rule instrument, WebGL2 scrolling diagram (Canvas2D fallback). The only grep-0√ó paradigm of the three. Refs: Wolfram 2002 / Cook 2004 / Xenakis.
- **`620-life-engine` (Conway's Life ‚Üí playhead composer).** Rejected ‚Äî duplicates `25-cellular` + `180-cellular`. Banked CAUTIONARY IDEAS ¬ß431 (do not rebuild).
- **`622-quickening` (Lenia ‚Üí organism voice).** Rejected ‚Äî duplicates `264-kids-lenia-pond` (reused its code verbatim). Banked CAUTIONARY IDEAS ¬ß431 (do not rebuild).

**Floor (winner `622`):** #1 (first 1D-elementary-CA / Wolfram-rule instrument ‚Äî grep-0√ó) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: 1D-CA rule engine + stagnation/perturb long-form controller + CA‚Üípercussion synth + WebGL2 render) + #3 (Wolfram 2002 / Cook 2004 / Xenakis) = honest **3/5**; #5 soft.

**Meta (for future Decide steps):** in a 600+-deep lab the highest-leverage research is **duplication-checking the CONCEPT, not just the technique name** ‚Äî and a grep that uses `\|` under `-E` will silently lie. This cycle's DEEP umbrella ("CA-as-composer") was over-broad; the win was the *sub-lane* discovery.

## ¬ß435 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 ¬∑ the science of groove is *microtiming*, and the lab has never built danceable

**Dive (research-first rule).** Per JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 ("get off the doom autopilot ‚Äî build the missing middle: ecstatic, danceable, groovy, tender"), searched the current generative-rhythm / groove-perception front. The decisive, citable anchor is the **groove-as-microtiming** science: *Microtiming Deviations and Swing Feel in Jazz* (**Scientific Reports**, 2019, nature.com/articles/s41598-019-55981-3) and the broader **inverted-U finding** ‚Äî perceived "groove" (the urge to move) peaks at a *moderate* amount of systematic timing deviation off the metronomic grid, not at zero (robotic) and not at too much (sloppy). Cross-checked the 2026 production landscape (mixingmonster.com sidechaining guide 2026; artistsindsp.com "11 best generative music VSTs 2026" ‚Äî Euclidean rhythms + stochastic modulation are the current generative-plugin vocabulary). **Why it's surprising for THIS lab:** a grep audit of the corpus shows the lab has 630 prototypes and **zero** in a danceable/groove register ‚Äî everything adult is doom/austere or cozy/warm (the jury's exact diagnosis). The science says the thing the lab is missing (groove, body, the pocket) is *mechanically specific* ‚Äî it's microtiming + interlocking meter + sidechain energy ‚Äî not vague "make it fun." Foundational-not-breaking (tagged so), but it directly drives the build.

**Could become a prototype that:** makes an *invisible* musical mechanism (the gap between the grid and where a hit actually lands; the drift of two identical patterns out of phase) **visible and playable** ‚Äî so you can see groove being woven, not just feel it.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode WIDE ‚Äî 3 distinct missing-middle registers; ship the strongest):**
- **`632-polymeter-loom` ‚Äî WINNER.** Interlocking **Euclidean rhythms** (Bjorklund) at differing step-counts (polymeter) + a **Reich phasing** pair drifting in/out of unison over minutes; three.js woven loom. Refs: **Reich** (*Music for 18 Musicians* / *Clapping Music*) ¬∑ **Toussaint** (*The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms*, 2005). The hypnotic/trance-groove corner.
- **`631-drop-engine` (euphoric EDM build-and-drop arc).** Generative 4-on-the-floor with **sidechain ducking ("pump")** ‚Äî grep-0√ó in the lab ‚Äî auto-advancing BUILD‚ÜíDROP‚ÜíBREAKDOWN with one Energy lever; Canvas2D. Banked **IDEAS ¬ß435** (strong, non-duplicative ‚Äî a real journey-engine alternative Karel asked for by name; resurrect, do not discard).
- **`633-slow-burn` (tender neo-soul microtiming).** Lookahead scheduler + per-voice **microtiming offsets** scaled by a "feel/looseness" lever + FM Rhodes + reharmonization state machine; Canvas2D viz that draws **grid-vs-where-the-hit-landed**. Refs: Sci. Rep. 2019 swing study ¬∑ Charnas, *Dilla Time* (2022). Banked **IDEAS ¬ß435** (the lab's only tender/sensual candidate ‚Äî but watch it doesn't read as the jury-banned "warm/cozy"; resurrect when the rolling-10 is cold on warmth).

**Floor (winner `632`):** **#2** (‚â•3 subsystems: Bjorklund Euclidean generator + phasing clock/lookahead scheduler + multi-voice FM/pluck percussion synth + three.js loom = 4) + **#3** (two canonical named refs that *drive* the design: Reich phasing + Toussaint Euclidean) = honest **3/5**. #1 NOT claimed honestly ‚Äî euclidean/polymeter/Reich are all referenced earlier in the 630-deep corpus (grep: euclidean 5√ó, polymeter/polyrhythm 15√ó, Reich/phasing 19√ó); only `sidechain` was grep-0√ó (that was 631's, not the winner's). #4/#5 not claimed.

**Meta (for future Decide steps):** the missing register is mechanically specific. "Build something joyful" is unactionable; "build interlocking microtiming + sidechain energy + the pocket" is buildable. When the jury names a *vibe* gap, find the *mechanism* that produces it, then the brief writes itself. Also: in WIDE mode, picking three *registers* of one jury-named gap (euphoric/hypnotic/tender) is a cleaner fan-out than three unrelated concepts ‚Äî it maps the gap rather than scattering.

## ¬ß436 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 ¬∑ the kids "missing middle": gesture‚Üímusic is a live 2026 lane, and the lab's kids side is stuck warm-or-silly

**Dive (research-first rule).** Per JURY 2026-06-15 (kids side = "warm-or-silly with nothing between" ‚Äî four warm 593/597/619/624 vs. a three-in-a-row funny-creature streak 603/609/617 ‚Äî plus provocation #4: NO touch, the touch ban was ignored by 619/624). Searched the current browser gesture-to-music front. The live, citable anchors: **MediaPipe Hands** real-time 21-keypoint hand tracking in the browser (research.google/blog on-device hand tracking) is now routinely wired to **Tone.js / p5.js** music toys (digitreelab "Hand-Motion-Music"; three.js + MediaPipe "15k particles steered by hand gestures" demos circulating 2026); and the WebGPU agentic-shader scene continues (**ShaderVine**, meditations.metavert.io, April 2026 ‚Äî interactive uniform sliders + shader genetic-evolution). **Why it's relevant for THIS lab:** the kids side keeps reaching for the SAME two inputs ‚Äî mic (603/609) and camera-optical-flow (a heavily-mined lane: 221/248/295/317/419/513/582/617). The genuinely under-used kid-native, NON-touch inputs are **device tilt** (off-glass, loved via 83-tilt-rain / 169-marble-run) and **gamepad** (jury-blessed via 587, 0√ó recently) ‚Äî and the under-used *register* is the **active/wonder middle** (mesmerizing-flow, soaring-joy, arcade-play) that is neither the warm-drone autopilot nor the silly-creature autopilot. Foundational-not-breaking on the technique (pitch detection, flow fields, gamepad are all stable), so the build leans on **diversity** (fill the middle, vary the input) over raw novelty.

**Could become a prototype that:** lets a 4-year-old make an emergent melody with their WHOLE BODY off the glass ‚Äî tilt the iPad to pour a river of light through tuned chimes; sing to fly a kite up the sky; steer a ship through musical star-gates with a controller ‚Äî three different non-touch inputs, one active-wonder register.

**Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal NON-TOUCH kid explorers, one active-wonder register; ship the strongest):**
- **`634-kids-tilt-river`** ‚Äî INPUT device tilt ‚Üí curl/flow-field particle stream ‚Üí tuned chime-gates; three.js. Refs: Tyler Hobbs flow fields ¬∑ Wintergatan Marble Machine.
- **`635-kids-sing-kite`** ‚Äî INPUT mic voice-PITCH (autocorrelation/MPM) ‚Üí kite altitude + auto-harmony; Canvas2D. Refs: McLeod Pitch Method (2005) ¬∑ Iwai Electroplankton.
- **`636-kids-star-pilot`** ‚Äî INPUT Gamepad API ‚Üí flight through tuned star-gates + rhythmic loop-memory; Canvas2D. Refs: Iwai Electroplankton ¬∑ Mizuguchi Rez/Child of Eden.

(Winner + floor/diversity recorded in STATE ¬ß436 after curation.)

## RESEARCH ¬ß442 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ kids cycle dive ¬∑ movement-energy ‚Üí groove

- **IRCAM ISMM (Sound Music Movement Interaction group)** ‚Äî https://ismm.ircam.fr/ ‚Äî and the children's-spontaneous-movement sonification literature (e.g. *Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children ‚Äî Cross-Modal Mapping*, PMC5104747) map **movement QUALITIES** ‚Äî energy, fluidity, impulsivity ‚Äî to **granular synthesis** rather than mapping pose/skeleton to discrete notes. The surprising bit for the lab: you don't need a skeleton/ML model to make body‚Üímusic feel alive; a continuous *energy* signal is enough, and it's exactly what a 4-year-old's whole-body flailing produces in abundance. Meanwhile 2026 in-browser webcam mocap is now turnkey (**RADiCAL Live**, **Rokoko Vision** ‚Äî record/stream mocap straight from a browser webcam), confirming the camera-as-instrument lane is live and cheap.
- **Could become a prototype that:** lets a child make a band play by *dancing* ‚Äî measure global motion energy via plain frame-differencing (no ML), map energy ‚Üí number of active groove layers + brightness, quantize layer changes to the beat. Implemented this cycle as Builder B **652-kids-dance-band**. The granular-from-energy angle (ISMM) is a deeper cycle-2: replace the layered-loop engine with energy-driven granular density on Karel's own piano.
- **Bonus corpus note:** a banked seed already exists for the voice-pitch lane ‚Äî **`635-kids-sing-kite`** (RESEARCH ¬ß436: mic voice-PITCH via McLeod/autocorrelation ‚Üí auto-harmony, Canvas2D). Builder A **651-kids-choir-leader** this cycle is the realization of that seed (ambition path (c): consume an existing recent-research IDEAS entry).

## RESEARCH ¬ß447 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ adult cycle dive ¬∑ principled consonance for an *evolving* phrase

- **arXiv:2606.16412 (Jun 16, 2026) ‚Äî "An Asymmetric Formula for Interval Consonance and its Relation to Harmonic Coincidence", David De Roure** ‚Äî https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16412 . Dated *today* (verified on arxiv.org/list/cs.SD/recent). Proposes a closed-form, **asymmetric** consonance measure for musical intervals tied to harmonic coincidence ‚Äî asymmetric because the perceived roughness of an interval differs depending on whether you approach it from the flat or sharp side, which a symmetric ratio-distance model misses. **Why it's surprising:** the lab keeps hiding every harmonic choice behind pentatonic / scale-snap (the jury's named local minimum ‚Äî 6√ó on kids). A *continuous, asymmetric* consonance score is the principled alternative: instead of snapping to a safe scale, you can let a generative process **choose** which note to add by weighting candidates by how consonant they'd be against what's already sounding ‚Äî favoring consonance but with a controlled, asymmetric lean toward gentle tension, so growth is tasteful without being saccharine.
- **Could become a prototype that:** drives the *generative growth* of an evolving phrase ‚Äî when the piece decides to add a new note from a performer's own material, it scores every candidate pitch class by De Roure's asymmetric consonance against the currently-ringing notes and samples from the top of that distribution. This is the harmonic brain for **cycle-4 of the piano spine** (606‚Üí630‚Üí643): a looper/phrase-memory built from Karel's 12 isolated piano notes that grows itself over minutes in his own timbre ‚Äî tender, principled, NOT pentatonic-snap.
- **Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode DEEP ‚Äî cycle-4 of the piano spine, 3 technical approaches; ship the strongest):** the asymmetric-consonance growth rule is the shared harmonic engine; the three builders differ only in renderer/interaction (665 WebGL2 phrase-tape ¬∑ 666 audio-first+SVG still-room ¬∑ 667 three.js reflective tide). Winner + floor/diversity recorded in STATE ¬ß447 after curation.

## RESEARCH ¬ß448 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ kids cycle dive ¬∑ *which* harmonic event a 4-year-old can actually perceive

- **Developmental music-cognition literature on mode, tempo, and tonal closure** ‚Äî the consensus finding (Dalla Bella, Peretz, Rousseau & Gosselin, *"A developmental study of the affective value of tempo and mode in music"*, Cognition 2001; and the tonal-closure work, e.g. *"Sad syntax? Tonal closure affects children's perception of emotional valence"*, arXiv:2008.01810): **3‚Äì4 year-olds do NOT yet reliably map minor‚Üísad** ‚Äî the mode‚Üíemotion association emerges only around **age 7‚Äì8**. What young children *do* master first is **tempo** (faster=happier), and from ~7 they perceive **tonal closure and its denial** (a suspension created and then resolved) as emotional states. Sources: link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-540-76442-7_5 ¬∑ sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0010027700001360 ¬∑ arxiv.org/pdf/2008.01810 . **Tagged honestly as `[older, foundational]`** ‚Äî this is developmental-cognition consensus, not a last-30-days frontier find; per the freshness mandate's foundational carve-out it is included because it *drives* this cycle's build and curation, not as a "what shipped this week" claim.
- **Why it's surprising / the reframe for the lab:** we kept assuming "make a kid hear major vs. minor" is the harmonic event to ship. For the actual 4-year-old target it largely *isn't yet legible* ‚Äî the perceptible harmonic events at that age are (a) the **change itself** (a gliding third, a moving drone) and (b) **tension‚Üírelease / closure and its denial**. This directly reorders the cycle's three explorers: the suspension piece sits on the firmest developmental ground; the mode-flip piece is better aimed at older kids/family.
- **Could become a prototype that** (and did, this cycle): a child hums a note and an unseen friend answers with a **suspension chain that creates and resolves tension** ‚Äî closure and its denial, no screen. Shipped as **`669-kids-hum-answer`**.
- **Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal harmonic-event explorers; ship the strongest):** dive (which harmonic event a 4yo perceives: change + closure > mode) ‚Üí 668 mode-morph (tilt/three.js) ¬∑ 669 suspension-chain (mic/off-glass) ¬∑ 670 shifting-drone (gamepad/WebGL2) ‚Üí the dive's developmental finding (closure beats mode for the target age) + the jury's off-glass directive jointly selected **669**. Winner + floor/diversity recorded in STATE ¬ß448 after curation.

## RESEARCH ¬ß450 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ kids cycle dive ¬∑ sensory-SAFE harmonic tension ("a wrong note that isn't scary")

- **arXiv:2602.22813 ‚Äî "Input-Envelope-Output: Auditable Generative Music Rewards in Sensory-Sensitive Contexts" (Ye, Shang, Ma & Zhang, CHI 2026)** ‚Äî https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22813 . A **constraint-first** generative-music framework for neurodivergent / sensory-sensitive users (autism, hyperacusis): rather than optimizing musical "quality," it applies **envelopes** ‚Äî loudness ceilings (LUFS per ITU-R BS.1770-5), forbidden frequency bands, bounded temporal dynamics ‚Äî *before* any content is generated, and makes the constraint-satisfaction **auditable** (the system shows *why* each choice was safe). **Why it's surprising for the lab:** it's the missing companion to the jury's #1 kids mandate. The jury wants a child to "meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary" ‚Äî this paper is the engineering of *exactly that*: you can introduce real harmonic tension (a dissonance, a deceptive cadence, a drone that shifts under them) while a hard sensory envelope guarantees it can never become harsh, loud, or startling. Tension without threat is a designable constraint, not a hope.
- **Could become a prototype that:** any kids piece that introduces a genuine harmonic event runs its whole output through a fixed "kid-safe envelope" (master lowpass ~7.5kHz + fast compressor + gain cap + trigger rate-limit) so the dissonance is *felt* but never sharp ‚Äî turning the master chain from a safety afterthought into the *enabling* mechanism for harmonic risk. Applied to all three explorers this cycle.
- **Carried-forward developmental anchor (from ¬ß448, `[older, foundational]`):** 3‚Äì4 yos do NOT yet reliably map minor‚Üísad (that emerges ~age 7‚Äì8; Dalla Bella/Peretz 2001); the harmonic events they *do* perceive are (a) **change itself** ‚Äî a gliding third, a moving drone ‚Äî and (b) **closure and its denial** (tension‚Üírelease). This ordered today's curation: a *moving-drone* and a *deceptive‚Üíauthentic cadence* sit on firmer ground for the 4yo than a static major/minor flip (the flip is better aimed at older kids/family, and only works here because it's rendered as a continuous *glide*, i.e. "the change itself").
- **Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal harmonic-event explorers; ship the strongest):** dive (sensory-safe envelope = the enabler of un-scary harmonic risk) + ¬ß448 anchor (change + closure > mode for a 4yo) ‚Üí 674 mode-glide (tilt/three.js, cosmic) ¬∑ 675 shifting-drone (touch/WebGL2, oceanic) ¬∑ 676 deceptive-cadence (rock-motion/off-glass audio-first, night-voyage) ‚Üí all three carry a real harmonic event behind a hard kid-safe envelope, none use pentatonic scale-snap. Winner + floor/diversity recorded in STATE ¬ß450 after curation.

## RESEARCH ¬ß461 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (UTC) ¬∑ adult cycle dive ¬∑ the audio‚Üíimage‚ÜíAUDIO closed loop (cross-modal round-trip)

- **arXiv:2512.08951 ‚Äî "AI Co-Artist: A LLM-Powered Framework for Interactive GLSL Shader Animation Evolution" (Dec 2025)** ‚Äî https://arxiv.org/html/2512.08951 . An LLM *generates and evolves* a population of distinct audio-reactive GLSL fragment shaders; the human acts as a **curator**, selecting the visually compelling ones, and the system breeds the next generation from those choices. **Why it's surprising:** it inverts the usual "one artist hand-tunes one shader" loop into a *generate‚Üícurate‚Üíevolve* loop ‚Äî which is structurally the same choreography the dream lab itself now runs (planner‚Üíparallel makers‚Üícritic). It also points at a frontier the lab hasn't touched: the visual layer as a *searchable population*, not a fixed program.
- **The image-as-spectrogram lineage (foundational anchor, drives today's build):** spectrogram‚Üîwaveform synthesis (the convolutional spectrogram‚Üíwaveform line) makes the *return trip* ‚Äî turning a 2D image back into sound by reading it as a time√ófrequency grid ‚Äî a stable, decades-deep technique (Xenakis **UPIC** 1977, the Russian **ANS synthesizer** 1958, MetaSynth, the modern neural spectrogram-vocoder stack). Tagged `[older, foundational]` per the freshness carve-out: included because it *enables* this cycle's build, not as a last-30-days claim.
- **Could become a prototype that** (and did, this cycle): a generative sound dreams an austere data-image (flux), then the image **becomes the next instrument** ‚Äî re-sonified column-by-column as a spectrogram while a scan-line sweeps it, so you literally see and hear the picture played. Shipped as **`704-spectral-seance`**. (The fresher AI-Co-Artist *evolve-a-shader-population* hook is banked for a future "the visuals are a searchable population" piece ‚Äî not built today.)
- **Research‚Üíbuild chain (mode DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept [the music dreams a picture and the dream changes the music], 2 technical approaches to closing the loop; ship the strongest):** path (c) ‚Äî the build consumes the closed-loop seeds **687-latent-oracle + 688-spectral-seance**, both banked from **RESEARCH ¬ß455**'s same-day AI-pipeline dive that produced ceiling-build 689; JURY 2026-06-17 #4 ("extend the ceiling builds, not a sixth tonal-tension toy") selected this concept over a fresh build. Approaches: 703 closes via **color**‚Üísynth (Anadol soft nudge) vs 704 closes via **literal re-sonification** (Ikeda image-as-instrument). Winner + floor/diversity recorded in STATE ¬ß461 after curation.

## ¬ß467 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ ADULT dive: interactive machine co-improvisation (the "duet partner's mind")

- **arXiv:2502.00023 ‚Äî "Musical Agent Systems: MACAT and MACataRT" (Feb 2025)** ‚Äî https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.00023 . Two human-in-the-loop co-creative musical agents: **MACAT** (real-time generation/analysis/curation) and **MACataRT**, which builds a *creative space* for human-AI co-improvisation via **concatenative synthesis** ‚Äî it answers by retrieving and recombining grains from an audio *corpus* rather than synthesizing tones. **Why it's surprising/useful:** it reframes "the machine's reply" as a *retrieval* problem over a real recording, not a synthesis problem ‚Äî so a duet partner can literally be made of *someone's actual playing*. Tagged `[recent ‚Äî Feb 2025]` (>30d but the freshest concrete co-improv-agent paper; the concatenative-co-improv lineage Diemo Schwarz **CataRT** + IRCAM **OMax/Somax2** is the `[older, foundational]` anchor).
- **Somax 2 (IRCAM) / Pachet Continuator (2002) / George Lewis *Voyager* (1980s‚Äì)** ‚Äî the named lineage of *listen ‚Üí infer style ‚Üí answer in turn*. Somax2 = real-time machine-listening + stylistically-coherent response; Continuator = a variable-order Markov model that learns *your* phrase vocabulary live and continues in your style; Voyager = a rule-based interactive improviser that "trades" with the soloist. These are the three distinct *minds* a call-and-response partner can have: **rule-based taste**, **learned style**, **corpus retrieval**.
- **Could become a prototype that** (and did, this cycle): **Shadow Duet** ‚Äî you play a phrase (MIDI / keyboard / on-screen keys) and a machine improviser **answers in the gaps**, trading fours like a partner with taste. Directly serves Karel-the-pianist + the jazz-responsive *alternate journey-arc* he asked for + live-performance fitness. Built `mode: DEEP` ‚Äî ONE concept, the three minds above as three parallel approaches: **716-shadow-duet** (Voyager-style heuristic taste), **717-duet-mirror** (Continuator-style live style-learner), **718-duet-paths** (MACataRT-style concatenative shadow of Karel's *real* Welcome Home piano). Ship the strongest; bank the rest.
- **Honesty correction to the banked `713-shadow-duet` seed:** its "lab's FIRST Web MIDI" claim is **false** ‚Äî `navigator.requestMIDIAccess()` already appears in 4-operator, 291-harmonograph, 365-cadence-ladder, 375-tempo-canon, 391-resilient-accompanist, 483-two-rooms-apart, 486-third-room. MIDI is not a #1. The genuine novelty is the *responsive jazz call-and-response improviser arc* + (for 718) answering with his real recording. Floor claimed honestly at #2+#3.

## ¬ß468 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ KIDS dive (cycle 468, WIDE) ‚Äî **468 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí kids. The binding directives are JURY 2026-06-17 #2 ("the next off-glass swing should be embodied-spatial/installation/haptic, NOT another eyes-closed glow-screen") + #5 ("MAKE A KID LAUGH ‚Äî the silly pole is 0√ó, the kids side has gone uniformly solemn") + #1 ("groove/play, NOT a cadence/voice-leading ‚Äî banned on kids for a week"). The question: what shipped in the last 30 days in embodied/movement interaction, and what's the named-reference grounding for whole-body ‚Üí sound for children?**

- **Ghost Arcade ‚Äî open-source projection-mapping / VJ software (live, June 2026)** ‚Äî https://ghostarcade.live/ . Its newest layer type runs **WebGPU compute shaders directly, zero-copy from compute output into the compositor**, with audio-reactive parameter modulation, 316+ GPU shaders, Spout/Syphon, MIDI clock, and a mobile companion. **Why it's surprising:** it's the first *consumer/VJ-grade* tool treating a WebGPU compute pass as a first-class, audio-reactive composite layer with no readback round-trip ‚Äî exactly the TouchDesigner-TOP paradigm Karel keeps asking to port to the browser, now with a working reference implementation. Tagged `[recent ‚Äî June 2026]`. **Could become a prototype that:** a WebGPU-compute particle/feedback field whose simulation parameters are modulated live by Karel's piano FFT (an adult/installation fire ‚Äî the genuine port of the `714-physarum-choir` WebGPU deepening + the JURY-#2 embodied-spatial lane). *Banked as an adult seed; not this cycle's kids build.*
- **BodyBeats ‚Äî whole-body musical interfaces for children (foundational)** ‚Äî research mapping children's whole-body movement qualities to sound in real time via **Rudolf Laban's Theory of Effort** (weight/space/time/flow); kids "play" by mimicking objects/characters and the movement *quality* drives the synthesis. Cross-referenced with the Kinect skeletal-tracking ‚Üí note-generation lineage (velocity/acceleration/limb-position ‚Üí pitch/density). Tagged `[older, foundational]` ‚Äî it's the academic grounding the lab's off-glass kids pieces have lacked a citation for. **Why it matters here:** it's the named reference that makes "stomp in front of the camera ‚Üí percussion" a principled mapping (movement effort ‚Üí sonic gesture), not an ad-hoc gimmick.
- **This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** path **(c)** ‚Äî consumed the ¬ß466-banked `715-kids-stomp-zoo` seed (itself born of RESEARCH ¬ß466's VTech-2026 "your-movement-is-the-controller" thesis) as the lead brief, now grounded by BodyBeats/Laban. Ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out of three laugh-first explorers on three *embodied non-glass inputs* so they can't collapse into one another: **SEE** (`719-kids-stomp-zoo`, webcam whole-body stomp ‚Üí tuba-parade) ¬∑ **SHAKE** (`720-kids-shake-pop`, devicemotion ‚Üí fizzy-bottle build-and-erupt) ¬∑ **BLOW** (`721-kids-blow-balloon`, mic-breath ‚Üí balloon hum + raspberry). All three clear every JURY rolling-15 ban (groove/joke not cadence ¬∑ embodied not touch-on-glass ¬∑ Canvas2D not glow/WebGL2 ¬∑ silly not hushed). **Winner: 719** (most embodied = truest answer to JURY #2's named embodied-spatial lane, + the standing RESURRECT-FIRST seed). Banked 720 ‚≠ê + 721 as IDEAS ¬ß468.

## ¬ß469 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ ADULT dive (cycle 469, DEEP) ‚Äî **the navigable corpus map: "fly inside your own recording."** Binding directives: JURY 2026-06-18 #3 ("stop banking ceilings and walking away ‚Äî push 710/704 to a cycle-2, reward DEPTH over breadth"), #1 ("ban Canvas2D ‚Äî it's the new 9√ó monoculture; 710 proved a WebGPU-compute stack degrades cleanly, port that"), #5 ("use Karel's REAL music ‚Äî it's in only 1 of the last 15"). The question: what's the named-reference + recent grounding for turning his real recording into a *spatial map you navigate to re-sound it*?

- **Diemo Schwarz ¬∑ CataRT ‚Äî real-time corpus-based concatenative synthesis (foundational anchor; drives the build)** ‚Äî https://www.researchgate.net/publication/224927716 . The recording is segmented into grains, each analyzed for descriptors (energy, brightness/spectral-centroid, pitch); grains are laid out in a **2D descriptor space** and a cursor *navigates* that space, selecting + concatenating the nearest grains in real time. Tagged `[older, foundational]` per the freshness carve-out ‚Äî it's the technique the build *is*, not a last-30-days claim. The point: "the machine's voice = retrieval over a real recording" (the same reframing ¬ß467 found in MACataRT, now turned from a duet-partner into a navigable *place*).
- **TU-Berlin (DAFx 2025) ‚Äî "Pitch-Conditioned Instrument Sound Synthesis from an Interactive Timbre Latent Space"** + **"Audio Latent Space Cartography" (arXiv:2212.02610)** `[recent-ish ‚Äî 2025 / older-foundational]` ‚Äî the live edge of the idea: a 2D/3D *map* of timbre you move through to synthesize, rather than triggering discrete events. The freshest concrete primary source in this niche is 2025 DAFx; I could **not** find a <30-day primary source for navigable-corpus-cartography specifically (honest note ‚Äî the field's cutting edge here is months, not weeks, old; logged as such rather than padded).
- **Why it's surprising / the hook:** the lab has a navigable WebGPU particle field (`710-presence-bloom`, its only new ceiling build) and concatenative grains of Karel's real piano (`718-duet-paths`) ‚Äî but **neither lets you fly through his recording as a place.** Fusing them: every grain of *Welcome Home* becomes one GPU particle positioned by its sonic character (time √ó brightness), and dragging a cursor through the cloud re-sounds the nearest grains, Hann-windowed and panned. **Could become (and did, this cycle): `720-paths-grainfield`** ‚Äî descriptor-space cartography of his real piano on a WebGPU compute particle field.
- **This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** path **(b)/(c)** ‚Äî extends the ceiling builds 710 (WebGPU compute) + 718 (his-piano concatenative grains) per JURY #3, grounded by CataRT + Audio Latent Space Cartography. Ran a **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept ("fly inside his recording"), 3 technical approaches ‚Äî **720-paths-grainfield** (raw WebGPU compute, descriptor map) ¬∑ **721-paths-constellation** (three.js Points galaxy, attractor steering) ¬∑ **722-paths-spectral-cloud** (three.js STFT sculpture you orbit/fly ‚Äî Xenakis UPIC / Ikeda). **Winner: 720** ‚Äî the only one on WebGPU compute (the lab's scarcest renderer, 1√ó; JURY #1's literal ask) and the truest cycle-2 of 710. Banked 722 ‚≠ê (the most novel concept ‚Äî navigate the *spectral shape*) + 721 as IDEAS ¬ß469.

## ¬ß470 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ KIDS dive (cycle 470, WIDE) ‚Äî **470 % 2 == 0 ‚Üí kids. JURY 2026-06-18 is the binding verdict, and it is severe: it bans **Canvas2D** (exploded 2√ó‚Üí9√ó ‚Äî "the new monoculture renderer"), bans the **shared-clock loop / step-sequencer groove as the core kids idea** ("the new pentatonic / the new harmonic-event"), bans **touch/on-glass-primary input** (5√ó, two juries running) and the **silly/goofy-comedy kids vibe** (6√ó). Provocation #1 is explicit: *"Ban Canvas2D for a cycle. 710 proved a WebGPU-compute + MediaPipe stack degrades cleanly ‚Äî port that **to a kids piece**. A kid deserves the scarce renderer too."* Provocation #5: *"Use Karel's real music ‚Äî it's in exactly 1 of the last 15."* The question this cycle: is the scarce renderer (WebGPU) actually shippable on the tablets a 4-year-old holds yet, and can a kids piece be built on Karel's REAL piano instead of a fresh oscillator bank?**
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, two WebSearches):**
- **WebGPU is production-ready across ALL browsers as of the 2026 window ‚Äî including iOS** (`utsubo.com/blog/frontier-web-apis-2026-production-ready`, `programming-helper.com/tech/webgpu-2026‚Ä¶`, `utsubo.com/blog/threejs-2026-what-changed`): Chrome + Firefox + **Safari 26 all ship WebGPU**, covering "the vast majority of web users"; three.js r171+ (Sep 2025) made WebGPU its production path. GPGPU compute (particle sims) runs **10‚Äì100√ó faster than WebGL transform-feedback** and enables compute scenarios WebGL couldn't express. Showcase confirmations of the live register: a *Party* WebGPU particle-physics playground, an audio-reactive WebGPU slime-mold with 19 presets. **Surprising bit:** the historical blocker on giving a *kid* the scarce renderer was always **iOS Safari had no WebGPU** ‚Äî so every kids WebGPU attempt fell back to Canvas2D on the actual iPad. That blocker **closed in the 2026 window**: WebGPU compute now runs on the iPad a 4-year-old holds, so JURY #1's "a kid deserves the scarce renderer" is, for the first time, *literally shippable* rather than aspirational.
- **The lab's own proven real-piano loader** (grep across `718-duet-paths` / `720-paths-grainfield`): `/api/audio/<id>` GET (recording id `549fc519-‚Ä¶`) ‚Üí `fetchPianoBuffer` ‚Üí `buildGrainCorpus` (CataRT-style ~90ms grains, RMS + ZCR-brightness descriptors) ‚Üí `selectNearest`, with an offline-rendered harmonic fallback so the corpus is never empty. **Surprising bit:** this entire CataRT pipeline ‚Äî built for *adult* navigable-corpus pieces (718/720) ‚Äî drops straight into a *kids* toy with one substitution: replace the cursor with a child's **breath**. A 4-year-old humming becomes the descriptor-space navigator of Karel's actual *Welcome Home* performance.
- **No-screen / embodied kids-music register (2026)**: the dominant award-winning kids-audio products (Yoto, Toniebox) are **screen-free / embodied** ‚Äî squeeze, tap, move ‚Äî confirming the design pull *away* from finger-on-glass that JURY's touch-input ban also names. Backs a breath/voice-driven input over a touch one for the kids lane.
- **Why it's surprising / the hook:** the lab's only recent *ceiling* build (710-presence-bloom, WebGPU compute) and its only recent use of Karel's real music (718/720) are **both adult**. JURY #1 + #5 asked for exactly their fusion *on the kids side* ‚Äî and the iOS-WebGPU news makes it real. **Could become (and did): `721-kids-piano-garden`** ‚Äî a 4-year-old hums and Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano blooms as a WebGPU-compute particle garden (Canvas2D fallback), breath energy ‚Üí grain count, breath pitch ‚Üí grain brightness. No beat, no loop, no groove, no silly ‚Äî a tender luminous texture.
- **This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** path **(a)** ‚Äî the iOS-WebGPU-now-shippable finding + the proven CataRT piano loader ‚Üí a **kids WIDE** fan-out of 3 explorers, each on a *scarce non-banned renderer* and each dodging all four JURY bans (no Canvas2D-primary, no loop-groove, no touch-primary, no silly): **721-kids-piano-garden** (mic-breath ‚Üí **WebGPU** compute ‚Üí Karel's-real-piano concatenative grains ‚Üí tender) ¬∑ **722-kids-star-scoop** (MediaPipe hands ‚Üí **three.js** 3D starfield scoop ‚Üí awe) ¬∑ **723-kids-aurora-sail** (device-tilt ‚Üí **WebGL2** GLSL aurora ‚Üí dreamy). **Winner: 721** ‚Äî the only candidate hitting *two* jury provocations at once (#1 scarce-renderer-for-a-kid + #5 his real music) and chained to this cycle's research; banked 722 + 723.

## ¬ß471 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 (UTC) ¬∑ ADULT dive (cycle 471, DEEP) ‚Äî **the room that re-sounds his own performance.** Binding directives: JURY 2026-06-18 #3 ("stop banking ceilings and walking away ‚Äî push **710** to a cycle-2, reward DEPTH over breadth; the 2-of-15 ceiling count hasn't moved in two windows because nobody returns to extend"), #5 ("use Karel's REAL music ‚Äî it's in only 1 of the last 15, all the rest synthesize fresh audio"), #1 (ban Canvas2D ‚Äî use 710's proven WebGPU-compute stack). The question: what's the named-reference + last-30-days grounding for turning his real recording into **spatialized grains you sculpt with your body in 3D**, fusing the lab's only new ceiling build (710: WebGPU + MediaPipe pose + HRTF) with its concatenative-piano work (718/720)?
**Sources (date-checked this cycle, WebSearch on "concatenative synthesis spatial audio HRTF interactive installation 2026 body tracking corpus grain"):**
- **`Synaptic Sanctum Neuroscape` (2026, ResearchGate publication 400907113)** ‚Äî a real-time immersive VR installation using a **CataRT-MuBu concatenative granular synthesiser** that produces **spatialised audio grains** whose duration scales with the participant's alert/relaxed state; **IRCAM Spat5 renders each grain with per-source higher-order ambisonics (HOA) binaural decoding locked to head-tracking quaternions.** This is, almost line-for-line, the architecture of this cycle's concept: a body/state signal drives *which* grains of a corpus play and *where they sit in 3D around the listener's head*, binaurally. **Surprising bit:** the freshest installation work (2026) is converging on exactly the fusion the lab has been building in two separate halves ‚Äî concatenative corpus grains (718/720) + per-source binaural spatialization (710's HRTF voices) ‚Äî but driven by the *body* as the navigator. Nobody in the lab had yet put the two halves in one piece.
- **Corpus-based concatenative synthesis as a *spatial instrument*** (Schwarz et al., "The Sound Space as Musical Instrument: Playing Corpus-Based Concatenative Synthesis", ResearchGate 265258205; CataRT overview 241312117): grains are selected by **proximity to a target position in descriptor space**, and "by mapping movements in real space to descriptor space, sound segments are triggered continuously, producing a natural and immersive auditory experience." Direct grounding for mapping limb position / body energy ‚Üí descriptor-space grain selection.
- **The Concatenator (arXiv 2411.04366)** ‚Äî a Bayesian real-time concatenative *musaicing* approach, confirming the corpus-navigation register is live and being pushed on right now.
- **Why it's surprising / the hook:** the lab BANKS ceilings and walks away (JURY's exact charge) ‚Äî 710 (the only new ceiling build) sings *synth* D-Dorian voices, and 718/720 (his real piano) live in a *flat 2D* grain map you fly a cursor through. **Could become (and did): a cycle-2 of 710 where the persistent accreting HRTF voices are no longer oscillators but GRAINS OF HIS REAL `Welcome Home` RECORDING** ‚Äî so the spatial architecture you build with your body around your own ears is made of his actual performance. Three body‚Üígrain mapping/temporal models explored.
- **This cycle's chain (research ‚Üí build):** path **(b/c)** ‚Äî extend a ceiling build (JURY #3) using this dive's CataRT-spatial grounding ‚Üí an **adult DEEP** fan-out of 3 approaches to the ONE concept: **722-presence-paths** (descriptor-space *reach* ‚Äî hand height‚Üíbrightness, openness‚Üítime, dwell-flick places a persistent grain-voice) ¬∑ **723-presence-tide** (whole-body *conductor* ‚Äî stillness‚Üísparse/distant/wet grains, vigour‚Üídense/near/dry, no placement) ¬∑ **724-presence-drift** (each placed voice owns a slow **moving read-head** through his recording, so the spatial chord re-voices itself over minutes ‚Äî long-form/state). **Winner: 724** ‚Äî keeps 710's exact placement gesture (a true cycle-2 deepening) AND adds the one axis neither 710 nor 720 had (long-form temporal re-voicing of his real performance); banked 722 + 723.

## ¬ß473 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 ‚Äî WebRTC networked music performance, browser, low-latency (cross-device jam)

Dive (WebSearch "WebRTC networked music performance browser low latency jam 2026") for the cycle-473 adult build (jury #4: make a multiplayer piece *actually* cross-device). Surfaced the **AES e-Lib paper "Web-Based Networked Music Performances via WebRTC: A Low-Latency PCM Audio Solution"** (https://www.aes.org/e-lib/download.cfm?ID=22017) ‚Äî 16-bit PCM stereo over `RTCDataChannel` + Web Audio/AudioWorklet, **40 ms** between two MacBooks on a LAN ‚Äî and **JackTrip WebRTC** (HTML5 JackTrip-for-the-browser, in development; https://github.com/omarcostahamido/awesome-networked-media). Context from the 2026 WebRTC-trends pieces (dacast/antmedia/dev.to): the delay-tolerance threshold for networked music is **20‚Äì30 ms**, and the standard move is peer-to-peer UDP/data-channel to dodge codec/relay latency.

**Honesty on freshness:** the *primary* sources in this specific niche (the AES PCM paper, JackTrip-WebRTC) are not <30 days old ‚Äî they're the foundational `[older, foundational]` anchors for browser networked-music; the genuinely <30-day signal is the general 2026 WebRTC-growth/edge-latency commentary, which is directional, not a primary implementable artifact. Logged plainly rather than padded.

**The surprise / the hook actually built:** every prior Resonance "multiplayer" is same-browser `BroadcastChannel` ‚Äî the jury flagged it three windows running. The fresh inversion of the AES/JackTrip approach is: **don't stream audio at all.** If both peers already hold Karel's recording (his Welcome Home piano, sliced into the same grain corpus), you send only tiny note-**events** `{p,x,y,t}` over the data channel and each peer re-sounds the *same grain locally* from the identical corpus ‚Äî near-zero bandwidth, no codec latency, full fidelity. ‚Üí **became `729-piano-portal-jam`** this cycle (zero-server shareable-link WebRTC handshake, three.js two-constellation field), with siblings `728` (copy-paste codes + WebGL2) and `730` (room-code serverless relay + WebGPU) banked. Research‚Üíbuild chain RESEARCH ¬ß473 ‚Üí 729 is direct (path (a)).

## ¬ß474 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ KIDS dive (cycle 474, DEEP) ‚Äî bare-hands gesture ‚Üí glowing particle swarm, the live 2026 frontier

**Source:** [Gesture-Particles (GitHub, Feb 2026)](https://github.com/Krishna71340/Gesture-Particles) ‚Äî real-time MediaPipe hand-tracking driving a swarm of glowing 3D particles (three.js + GLSL); plus the broader cluster of <30-day browser builds doing the same: [Bolt "Build Rebuilds: hand-tracking particle effects"](https://bolt.new/blog/build-rebuilds-interactive-particle-effects-with-hand-tracking), [collidingScopes/threejs-handtracking-101](https://github.com/collidingScopes/threejs-handtracking-101), [DEV "real-time hand-tracking platform entirely in the browser"](https://dev.to/quochuydev/how-i-built-a-real-time-hand-tracking-platform-that-runs-entirely-in-the-browser-en1).

**Why it's surprising / worth it:** the *technique* (21-landmark MediaPipe hands ‚Üí additive glow-point swarm, fully in-browser, zero special hardware) has crossed from research demo into a copy-pasteable 2026 commodity ‚Äî multiple fresh repos this quarter. But every one of them stops at the *same* idea: **the hand is a gravity well; particles are pretty.** They are silent, adult/showpiece, and goalless. The genuinely unoccupied corner is the one Resonance owns: **make the swarm a musical instrument a 4-year-old plays off-glass** ‚Äî a closed fist *scoops* a handful of stars into a consonant bell cluster, an open hand *spills* them into a rising glissando, hand-height sets register, and there is no wrong note. Same commodity input, but mapped to gather‚Üíchord / openness‚Üíarc instead of gravity‚Üíspectacle.

**Could become a prototype that does X:** ‚Üí **became `731-kids-star-reach` this cycle** ‚Äî but deliberately on **raw WebGL2** (not the repos' three.js) for renderer-scarcity + iOS reliability + zero CDN renderer dep. Research‚Üíbuild chain is **path (c)**: consumes the twice-banked ‚≠ê IDEAS seed `726-kids-star-scoop` (¬ß472, itself born of RESEARCH ¬ß470's iOS-WebGPU finding), and this dive confirms the gesture‚Üíparticle-swarm input is current/commodity while the *musical-scoop* mapping is the still-open Resonance-shaped move. Two siblings ‚Äî `730` (three.js, the repos' renderer) + `732` (WebGPU compute, 12k particles) ‚Äî banked.

### ¬ß475 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 ‚Äî research-first dive (last-30-day scan)

**Search 1 ‚Äî WebGPU compute, audio-reactive (last 30d).** [WebSearch] Surfaced a **WebGPU Physarum slime-mold** generative-art app released **2026-06-18** (19 presets, audio-reactive routing across 10 targets, kaleidoscope, long-exposure trails; PWA + macOS app) and **Ghost Arcade**'s new WebGPU-compute layer (zero-copy compute-output ‚Üí compositor for bespoke particle/fluid sims). Confirms WebGPU achieved **Baseline (Jan 2026)** and three.js/Babylon have mature WebGPU renderers ‚Äî agent-based compute audio-reactive art is now production-feasible in the browser. *Hook considered ‚Üí REJECTED as a banned variant:* a piano-fed slime-mold network is **already in the lab** (`327-physarum-choir` = WebGPU Physarum on Karel's recording; `260-kids-slime-garden`). Useful negative result ‚Äî saved a redundant ship and confirmed WebGPU-compute is safe to lean on (informed banking `733-paths-disintegrate`'s 40k-particle WebGPU path).

**Search 2 ‚Äî long-form generative music with state + memory (2026).** [WebSearch] The live trend: diffusion long-form models (Stable Audio 2.0 = diffusion-U-Net + VAE for coherent long-form), **Vocos** removing the upsampling bottleneck for real-time reconstruction, "Generative Streams" tracking biometric state, and the granular framing *"a single note can evolve for five minutes without repeating itself exactly."* **Why surprising / why it matters here:** it names the lab's actual gap ‚Äî not a new model call, but **long-form STATEFUL pieces with memory and evolution** (the categorical-diversity-menu category we're thinnest on; the jury keeps asking for *depth*, and almost every lab piece is a short loop or a reactive instrument, not a 6‚Äì8 min arc that is *different at minute 6 than minute 1*). **Became a prototype:** `734-paths-tape-erosion` (this cycle's winner) ‚Äî Karel's real recording driven through a 5-movement disintegrate‚Üíreform arc with smoothstep memory, never looping; conceptually anchored to **Basinski *The Disintegration Loops*** + **Eno *Reflection***. Chain visible: *today's research ‚Üí today's build*.

## ¬ß481 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ ADULT dive (cycle 481, WIDE) ‚Äî **real-time score-following / automatic accompaniment is a live 2026 area, and the lab has never once used his recording as a *score you play along with***

**The dive (research-first rule):** two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "real-time score following onset detection browser web audio 2026 piano accompaniment"; (b) "arxiv cs.SD June 2026 symbolic music reharmonization real-time generative accompaniment." Pointed by JURY 2026-06-19 provocation #1 ("his recording can be used WITHOUT shattering it into grains: **play it and score-follow it**; harmonize a live voice against it; let its structure conduct a generative system").

**The finding:** **real-time score-following / automatic accompaniment is an actively-pushed 2026 research lane.** The freshest anchors: **Matchmaker** ‚Äî an open-source library for real-time piano score following + systematic evaluation (arXiv **2510.10087**, Oct 2025); **"Pairing Real-Time Piano Transcription with Symbol-level Tracking for Precise and Robust Score Following"** (arXiv **2505.05078**, May 2025); **"Structure-Aware Piano Accompaniment via Style Planning and Dataset-Aligned Pattern Retrieval"** (arXiv **2602.15074**, Feb 2026); and **"Real-Time Symbolic Music Accompaniment Generation for Edge Devices"** (Springer 2026) ‚Äî the on-device/edge framing that matters for a browser build. The foundational lineage is Dannenberg/Vercoe (1980s synthetic performers), Christopher Raphael's *Music Plus One* (statistical accompaniment), and Arshia Cont's *Antescofo* (anticipatory score follower). **Why it's surprising for the lab:** across 740+ prototypes, Karel's *Welcome Home* recording has been used exactly two ways ‚Äî **visualized** (spectral/splat) or **scattered into grains** (the 7√ó-overused CataRT cluster the jury just banned). It has NEVER been used as a **performance you play along with** ‚Äî a recording that *follows YOU*. Score-following inverts the relationship: instead of the human chasing a fixed track, the recording re-times itself to the human's pulse. **Honest date caveat:** automatic accompaniment is foundational (1980s) and Matchmaker is ~8 months old; this is a *soft* bind to a live 2024‚Äì2026 lane (Matchmaker / Structure-Aware Accompaniment / edge-device accompaniment), **NOT a clean <14-day #5 claim** ‚Äî the ambition floor rests on #1 (lab-first technique) + #2 + #3 + a qualified #5.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *you keep the pulse ‚Äî tap a beat ‚Äî and Karel's real recording becomes an accompanist that LISTENS, estimating your tempo from inter-onset intervals and driving the playbackRate of his actual performance to follow your groove toward the next musical landmark.* **Research‚Üíbuild chain: dive (real-time score-following / automatic accompaniment) ‚Üí today's build makes his recording the follower and you the conductor** ‚Äî shipped as **`748-duet-follower`** (WIDE-fire winner). The two divergent siblings explored the *same* "use his music a non-grain way" mandate via different inputs/techniques: **`749-spoken-libretto`** (speech-ASR words select & sequence whole real-recording PHRASES onto a typographic SVG staff ‚Äî the Reich/Lucier speech-melody lineage) and **`750-sky-almanac`** (locally-computed sun position + moon phase conduct a long-form stateful arrangement of his real-recording phrases on an SVG sun-arc dial ‚Äî Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* / Finer *Longplayer*). Both banked (IDEAS ¬ß481).

## ¬ß482 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ KIDS dive (cycle 482, DEEP) ‚Äî **the Web Speech API just reached a fresh spec milestone, and the lab has never once let a recognized SPOKEN WORD become music for a child**

**The dive (research-first rule):** two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "browser Web Speech API kids music creative coding 2026"; (b) "on-device speech recognition real-time word to sound 2026 arxiv." Pointed by JURY 2026-06-19 provocation #5 ("¬ß411's in-browser client-side ASR / speech‚Üítext wave is grep-0√ó ‚Äî the lab has never once turned a real *recognized spoken word* into music. A 4-year-old who *says a word* and hears it become a sound is a genuine first. Build it.").

**The finding (dated, fresh):** the **Web Speech API specification was re-published as a W3C/WICG Draft Community Group Report on 2026-05-21** (29 days ago ‚Äî inside the 30-day window) ‚Äî the spec is actively maintained, not abandoned. More importantly the *platform reality* finally favors a kids build: `SpeechRecognition` (`webkitSpeechRecognition`) is now supported across **Chrome, Edge, Safari (incl. iOS/iPadOS Safari 14.5+), and Opera** ‚Äî so live in-browser recognition runs on the exact device KIDS.md targets (the iPad), no install, no API key, no server. On the research side, on-device/edge streaming ASR is an active 2024‚Äì2026 lane (light-weight streaming-ASR punctuation models arXiv 2407.13142; SAS device-addressed speech detection Apr 2026; PI-Whisper edge-ASR) ‚Äî confirming "recognize a word locally, instantly" is a commodity capability, not a research gamble. **Why it's surprising for the lab:** across 750+ prototypes, real `SpeechRecognition` appears only twice (`570-spoken-spell` phoneme‚Üípitch incantation, `189-voice-scene` word‚Üíscene trigger) ‚Äî **both adult, neither a kids piece, and neither maps a child's spoken word to a playable instrument.** Voice-as-music for kids has always meant *pitch/RMS* (`88`/`158`/`179`/`210` hum-to-paint / voice-monster) ‚Äî never the *recognized word itself*. A 4-year-old saying "banana" and hearing it become music is genuinely un-built.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *a child SAYS a word and it instantly becomes music + a bright joyful visual.* **Research‚Üíbuild chain: ¬ß411 (in-browser ASR, 2026-06-13) + ¬ß482 (Web Speech spec milestone + iOS-Safari support, today) ‚Üí today's DEEP kids fire** ‚Äî three different speech‚Üímusic mappings, ship the best. Shipped **`752-kids-word-band`** (phonetic: spoken word's letters ‚Üí a bright SVG rainbow-xylophone pentatonic riff that joins a look-ahead groove ‚Äî and the deliberate **zero-GPU-shader, SVG/DOM-only** answer to JURY #2). The two banked siblings: **`751-kids-say-a-zoo`** (semantic: each spoken word ‚Üí a unique bouncing Canvas2D creature that sings; word-length‚Üísize, hash‚Üíhue+pitch, vowel-count‚Üíphrase length ‚Äî accreting chord-garden) and **`753-kids-spell-parade`** (rhythmic/stateful: each word's letters ‚Üí drum-pad steps in an accumulating Canvas2D marching-band groove that is different at minute 5 than minute 1). All three dodge every JURY 2026-06-19 ban (speech INPUT not mic-hum/camera; SVG-DOM or Canvas2D OUTPUT not GPU-shader-field; word‚Üímusic not grain-cloud; bright-daylight VIBE not dark-glow) and hit provocation #4 (the bright joyful active-middle kids register).

## ¬ß483 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 (UTC) ¬∑ ADULT dive (cycle 483, DEEP) ‚Äî **browser networked-music has quietly become a real lane, and the framework for "agents you conduct" just got published**

**The dive (research-first rule):** two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "networked music performance WebRTC ensemble conducting browser 2026"; (b) "browser conducted ensemble generative music multiplayer 2026 tempo gesture". Pointed by JURY 2026-06-19 provocation #3 ("protect the one habit that worked ‚Äî DEPTH; return to a ceiling nobody extended ‚Äî take **729-piano-portal-jam** to 3+ players / a conducted room") fused with #1 ("use his music a way that ISN'T a grain cloud").

**The finding (dated, fresh):** **"A Design Space for Live Music Agents"** (arXiv **2602.05064**, submitted **2026-02-04**, verified via abstract) surveys **184 live-music-agent systems** and maps real-time human‚ÄìAI music co-creation across usage contexts, interactions, technologies, ecosystems ‚Äî i.e. it's the first taxonomy of *agents that play alongside a human leader in real time*, exactly the framing for a conducted ensemble of ghost/remote performers. Around it the search surfaced a live 2024‚Äì2026 browser lane I'd underweighted: the AES paper **"Web-Based Networked Music Performances via WebRTC"** (RTCDataChannel + AudioWorklet, ~40ms LAN latency), **Fourhands** (WebRTC 2-person MIDI jamming), the **Intersymmetric Sequencer** (real-time multiplayer browser drum machine), and a **Springer** web-distributed *choral* system where a **conductor directs with gestures and selects BPM + harmonic content** for mobile choristers ‚Äî a near-exact precedent for conducting a browser ensemble. **Why it's surprising for the lab:** across 750+ prototypes, real multi-user is essentially one piece (`729`, a 2-player duet) and it used his piano *as grains*; a **conducted N-seat ensemble** that uses his recording as **phrases** and lets a human bias a long-form arc is un-built ‚Äî and it lands off the grain-cloud (JURY #1) and off the GPU-shader field (JURY #2) in one move.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *Karel's recording becomes a long-form CONDUCTED ensemble ‚Äî seats that replay his phrases, an arc he biases with tempo/dynamics, chairs friends fill from their phones.* **Research‚Üíbuild chain: JURY 2026-06-19 #3 (extend the 729 ceiling) + #1 (non-grain his-music) ‚Üí ¬ß483 (live-music-agents taxonomy + browser networked-ensemble precedents) ‚Üí today's DEEP adult fire** (one "Conducted Room" concept, three renderer √ó conducting-interaction attacks). Shipped **`754-conducted-table`** (Canvas2D top-down score table; seat-mixer + tempo/dynamics dials; look-ahead scheduler w/ memory arc; star WebRTC topology) ‚Äî the deliberate **Canvas2D, zero-GPU-shader** answer to JURY #2 (Canvas2D fell to 3√ó and is scarce again). Banked siblings: **`753-conducted-orbit`** ‚≠ê (three.js concert-in-the-round, baton gesture) + **`755-conducted-stage`** (SVG/DOM dashboard with a long-form timeline ribbon). All three dodge every JURY 2026-06-19 ban (phrase-remix not grain-cloud ¬∑ non-shader output ¬∑ seat/peer/slider input not mic-hum/camera ¬∑ bright concert-hall not dark-glow).

## ¬ß490 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 490 research dive (KIDS, WIDE) ‚Äî **konnakol: spoken SYLLABLES are percussion, and the lab has used ASR only as pitch ‚Äî never as rhythm**

**The dive (research-first rule):** two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "konnakol spoken rhythm syllable beatboxing interactive music children 2026"; (b) "arxiv 2026 speech rhythm to music sonification syllable onset spoken word percussion." The kids cycle + JURY 2026-06-19 #4 ("the un-built kids register is the active/joyful MIDDLE ‚Äî bright, bold-primary, fast legible cause-and-effect, neither solemn-glow nor slapstick") pointed the dive at *rhythm/groove* rather than the perennial pentatonic-melody-tap.

**The finding:** **konnakol** ‚Äî the South Indian Carnatic art of vocal percussion, where spoken syllables (*ta, ka, di, mi, tom, nam‚Ä¶*) literally ARE the rhythm ‚Äî is an established **children's** rhythm pedagogy: the SaPa academy (Subramaniam Academy, "Indian beatboxing") takes learners **as young as three**, and parents report kids "becoming obsessed with konnakol" as the gateway to feeling a beat (splice.com konnakol packs; udemy SaPa course; en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Konnakol). Adjacent academic anchor: speech-rhythm research treats the **syllable onset** (valleys in the sonority/theta envelope) as the unit of rhythmic structure (arXiv 2401.14416 "Acoustic characterization of speech rhythm"; biorxiv syllabic-rhythm modulation-spectrum work). **Why it's surprising for THIS lab:** the lab has shipped speech‚Üímusic several times ‚Äî `752-kids-word-band` and `570-spoken-spell` both run live Web-Speech-API ASR ‚Äî but **both map a recognized word to PITCH** (letters‚Üípentatonic bars; phonemes‚Üípitches). **No prototype has ever mapped speech to RHYTHM** ‚Äî a word's *syllable count + stress* becoming a drum/clap pattern that loops. That's the konnakol premise, and it sidesteps every kids ban at once: it's percussion not pentatonic-melody-wash (JURY's standing kids crutch), Canvas2D not GPU-shader (JURY #2), speech not mic-hum/camera (JURY diversity), and bright parade not dark-glow (JURY #4). **Honest date caveat:** konnakol is ancient/foundational and the syllable-onset papers are 2022‚Äì2024 ‚Äî **#5 NOT claimed.** The dive validates the *speech-as-rhythm* lane (fresh WITHIN the lab, grepped 0√ó), not a <14-day primitive.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle bound):** *a 4-year-old SAYS words and they march in as a parade of percussion ‚Äî each word's syllable rhythm ("el-e-phant" = a 3-hit gallop, "cat" = one BOOM) becomes a looping drum pattern, and words accrete into a band you build by talking.* **Research‚Üíbuild chain: JURY #4 (active/joyful kids middle) + the grep that ASR-in-lab is pitch-only ‚Üí ¬ß490 (konnakol = spoken syllables ARE percussion) ‚Üí today's WIDE kids fire**, one approach of which (`773-kids-parade-caller`, Canvas2D, ASR‚Üísyllable-rhythm groove, ref Konnakol/Reich speech-melody) makes spoken language a *beat* for the first time. The two siblings keep the bright/active/non-shader register without ASR: `774-kids-marble-symphony` (SVG/DOM physics marble-machine, ref Wintergatan) + `775-kids-stamp-band` (Canvas2D step-sequencer with looping memory, ref Toshio Iwai *SimTunes*/*Electroplankton*). All three dodge every JURY 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-cloud ¬∑ no GPU-shader ¬∑ no mic-hum ¬∑ no dark-glow). Floor for each rests on a clean **#2** (‚â•3 subsystems) + **#3** (named reference); ASR-as-rhythm is a soft #1 (the primitive exists, the *use* is lab-first).

## ¬ß491 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (UTC) ¬∑ cycle 491 research dive (ADULT, WIDE) ‚Äî **the self-similarity matrix: see a song's whole ARCHITECTURE at once, and the lab has never drawn one of his recording**

**The dive (research-first rule):** two WebSearches ‚Äî (a) "real-time audio visualization web audio 2026 new technique generative music browser"; (b) "arxiv cs.SD June 2026 music structure score-following self-similarity matrix." JURY 2026-06-19 #1 ("use his music a NEW way ‚Äî not grains; let its STRUCTURE conduct / be read") + #2 ("rotate off GPU-shader fields entirely; build one piece with no shader, prove the idea carries it") aimed the dive at *structural analysis* of his recording rendered on a non-shader surface.

**The finding:** the **self-similarity matrix (SSM)** ‚Äî Jonathan Foote's *"Visualizing Music and Audio using Self-Similarity"* (ACM Multimedia 1999/2000), the foundational technique ‚Äî is having a 2026 ML revival as the structural backbone of music-structure-analysis and structure-aware generation: **SSM-Net** (arXiv 2211.08141, learns features so the resulting SSM matches a ground-truth structure map) and **"Generating Music with Structure Using Self-Similarity as Attention"** (arXiv 2406.15647) which feeds a user-supplied SSM into the attention layer to *impose* form on generated music. The SSM is an N√óN grid where cell (i,j) is the feature similarity (here: cosine of per-frame chroma) between moment i and moment j: the hot diagonal is "now = now," and **off-diagonal bright stripes are recurrence** ‚Äî exactly where a phrase or theme RETURNS. It draws a song's whole architecture as one legible image. **Why it's surprising for THIS lab:** grep is decisive ‚Äî `545-motion-loom` uses self-similarity only as a **1-D autocorrelation for tempo induction**, and `370-tonal-map` reads his harmony as a moving comet, but **no prototype has ever computed and rendered the full 2-D SSM of his recording** ‚Äî the picture of his *form* (where Welcome Home repeats itself, where it turns). It dodges all four JURY 2026-06-19 bans at once: it *uses* his recording without shattering it into a grain cloud (chroma analysis, the audio plays whole), it renders on **Canvas2D** (the scarce non-shader surface JURY #2 begged for), no mic, and it's a **bright analytical heatmap**, not a dark-glow field. **Honest date caveat:** Foote's SSM is foundational (1999) ‚Äî like FFT, a stable primitive, so explicitly fair under the research rules' "foundational technique" carve-out; the 2024‚Äì2026 SSM-Net / SSM-as-attention work is the live revival framing, not a strict <14-day artifact. **#5 not hard-claimed**; the value is the lab-first *use* (grep 0√ó) + the named foundational reference.

**Could become a prototype that does X (the build this cycle binds):** *his Welcome Home recording plays WHOLE while the browser computes its chroma-SSM and draws it as a luminous heatmap ‚Äî a playhead crosshair sweeps the matrix as he plays, and CLICKING a bright off-diagonal cell plays the two similar moments back-to-back so you HEAR why the machine drew them as a repeat.* You watch, then confirm with your ears, the architecture of his own piece. **Research‚Üíbuild chain: JURY #1 (his music a new non-grain way) + JURY #2 (off the shader, onto Canvas2D) + the grep that SSM-of-his-recording is 0√ó ‚Üí ¬ß491 (Foote SSM, revived 2024‚Äì2026) ‚Üí today's ADULT WIDE fire**, lead approach `777-song-architecture` (Canvas2D chroma-SSM heatmap + playhead + click-to-hear-the-repeat, ref Foote 1999). Two divergent siblings explore off his music to spread the fire: `778-markov-mirror` (SVG transition-graph, a live-learning variable-order Markov chain that improvises in your style, ref David Cope *EMI* / Hiller *Illiac Suite*) + `779-ink-score` (SVG, an autonomous long-form generative graphic score the machine draws AND plays with motif memory, ref Cardew *Treatise* / Earle Brown). All three off every JURY 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-cloud ¬∑ no GPU-shader-field ¬∑ no mic-hum ¬∑ no dark-glow); floor for each rests on a clean **#2** (‚â•3 subsystems) + **#3** (named reference), with `779`'s long-form state reaching toward **#1**.

## ¬ß492 ‚Äî 2026-06-20 (cycle-492 kids DEEP research dive: drawing-as-musical-score for children)

**The dive.** Two WebSearches: (1) interactive sound for children / gesture sonification (2026); (2) drawing-as-musical-score, Xenakis UPIC, web-interactive.

**What surfaced ‚Äî fresh + foundational.**
- **CHI 2026 ‚Äî "From Movement to Sound and Back: A Workshop on Movement-Based and Sonification Design Approaches"** (dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3772363.3778764) and new 2026 real-time head-movement / head-gesture sonification methods for non-experts. *Fresh (2026 program), in-window.* Confirms movement‚Üísound for non-experts is an active 2026 lane.
- **Xenakis's UPIC** (1977, CEMAMu ‚Äî *Unit√© Polyagogique Informatique du CEMAMu*) ‚Äî freehand drawing directly controls pitch + dynamics; Xenakis 1979: **"The pedagogical interest is obvious: with UPIC, music becomes a game for children. They draw. They hear."** *Foundational.* Modern living descendants verified current: **UPISketch** (iOS/Win/Mac, still distributed), **SonicSketch** ("live out your Xenakis fantasies, free in your browser" ‚Äî CDM, recent), JeongHo Park's image‚Üíscore instrument, and "Draw and Listen!" (a 2024+ sketch-based MIR music-inpainting system, transactions.ismir.net). The ZKM/OAPEN volume *From Xenakis's UPIC to Graphic Notation Today* shows the technique is actively re-examined now.

**Why surprising for the lab.** Across 770+ prototypes the lab has many paint‚Üísound pieces (loved 100/104/158/160/152) but every one is either **per-stroke loops in fixed timbre columns** (160) or **splat/triggers** (100) or **draws FROM voice** (158). **None implements the actual UPIC model: a single global traveling reader (x/angle=time) that sounds ALL drawn strokes by their y/radius=pitch as one continuous, evolving score.** That continuous-drawn-curve-as-pitch-read-by-a-playhead is grep-0√ó ‚Äî and Xenakis literally designed it as a children's game, so it lands squarely on the un-built bright/joyful/active kids register (JURY 2026-06-19 #4) AND off every 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-cloud, no GPU-shader-field, no mic-hum, no dark-glow).

**Could become a prototype that does X (built this cycle).** ‚Üí `781-kids-paint-conductor`: a 4yo paints freehand on a bright cream canvas; a glowing playhead sweeps L‚ÜíR forever; x=time, y=pitch (pentatonic-snapped, never wrong), color=one of five friendly timbres, thickness=loudness; the picture they draw becomes the song they hear, accumulating richer over minutes (long-form stateful). The research‚Üíbuild chain is visible: ¬ß492 (UPIC "music becomes a game for children") ‚Üí 781. Banked siblings: `782-kids-sun-clock-song` (radial/cyclic UPIC), `783-kids-melody-rider` (a character rides the drawn line). Sources: dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3772363.3778764 ¬∑ en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UPIC ¬∑ iannis-xenakis.org/en/dictionary-upic ¬∑ cdm.link/sonicsketch ¬∑ transactions.ismir.net/articles/10.5334/tismir.128
